  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 fontbox `GlyfSimpleDescript`/`GlyfCompositeDescript`/`GlyfCompositeComp`, `PDEncryption`/`AccessPermission`
  reason: all pinned both-sides; no production change in these two surfaces.

- **Wave 1544 (agent B) — PDFunctionType2/Type3 interpolation/stitching: 3 real bugs fixed.** Built `oracle/probes/FunctionType23FuzzProbe.java` (31 cases): Type2 `/N` fractional/negative/zero/huge, `/C0`/`/C1` mismatched/missing/non-numeric, reversed/odd `/Domain`; Type3 `/Functions`/`/Bounds`/`/Encode` count mismatches, NaN inputs/bounds, zero-width edges, Type-4 child. **Real bugs fixed:** (1) `pd_function_type2.py` eval read `/C0`/`/C1` via tolerant `to_float_array` (non-numeric→0.0); upstream `getC0().toFloatArray()` casts to COSNumber and throws — added strict `_eval_c0`/`_eval_c1` so a non-numeric coefficient raises. (2) `pd_function_type2.py` exponentiation returned a finite double where Java's `(float)` cast overflows to `Infinity` (e.g. `0.5**-1000`) — added `_narrow_to_float` (overflow-boundary only, in-range precision preserved). (3) `pd_function_type3.py` partition selection used `if x < partition[i]: continue` which isn't NaN-equivalent to upstream's `x >= partition[i]` entry test — a NaN input/bound evaluated a NaN interval instead of raising "partition not found"; switched to `if not (x >= partition[i])`. 7 tests; full function suite 1163 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.function.PDFunctionType2` / `PDFunctionType3`
  reason: Type2 coefficient cast now strict + float-overflow→Infinity; Type3 NaN partition selection matches upstream's `>=` entry test.

- **Wave 1544 (agent A) — Type2CharString standalone path rendering fixed.** Built `oracle/probes/Type2CharStringFuzzProbe.java` (33 cases): every curve operator, flex family, hintmask byte-skipping, callsubr/callgsubr inlining, width-on-first-operator, 255 fixed-point, seac-via-endchar, stack underflow. **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/type2_char_string.py`):** a `Type2CharString` built from raw `bytes`/program list always rendered an empty path — the bare fontTools `T2CharString` had no `.private`/`.globalSubrs`, so `draw()` raised `AttributeError` (swallowed→`[]`). Attached a minimal `_Type2Private` shim (glyph widths + empty Subrs, default `globalSubrs=[]`) and lowercased `CharStringCommand` operator mnemonics to fontTools' casing. CFF-sourced path untouched. Pinned both-sides: trailing zero-length endchar moveto, `flex1` last-point bias, missing-endchar auto-close. 33 tests; CFF suite green.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.fontbox.cff.Type2CharString#getPath`
  reason: standalone-charstring path now renders (private-dict shim) instead of silently empty; minor flex/endchar geometry differences pinned both-sides.

- **Wave 1544 (agent C) — COSFloat(String) accepts Java Float.parseFloat special spellings.** Built `oracle/probes/CosStringNumberFuzzProbe.java` (82 cases: COSString UTF-16/PDFDocEncoding decode + toHexString, COSFloat literals, COSInteger int32-narrowing). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/cos/cos_float.py::_parse_float`):** now mirrors Java `Float.parseFloat` — accepts case-sensitive `NaN`/`Infinity`/`-Infinity`/`+Infinity` (with surrounding whitespace) and hex floats with a binary exponent (`0x1p4`→16.0), while still rejecting the lenient Python spellings Java rejects (`nan`, `inf`, `NAN`, bare `0x10`). Previously these raised `OSError` where upstream returns NaN/MAX_VALUE. `COSNumber.get` dispatch unaffected. Pinned both-sides the existing UTF-8-BOM divergence. 82 cases; full cos suite 1921 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSFloat` (`Float.parseFloat`)
  reason: leaf-constructor special-value parsing now matches Java's `Float.parseFloat` accept/reject set.

- **Wave 1544 (agent E) — PDInkAppearanceHandler emits /AP for an empty /InkList.** Built `oracle/probes/AnnotAppearanceHandlerFuzzProbe.java` (40 cases over Line/Square/Circle/Polygon/PolyLine/Ink/Highlight/Underline/StrikeOut/Squiggly with malformed `/C`/`/IC`/`/BS`/`/L`/`/Vertices`/`/InkList`/`/LE`/`/RD`/`/QuadPoints`/`/Rect`). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/pd_ink_appearance_handler.py`):** the Ink handler early-returned (no `/AP`) on absent/empty `/InkList`; upstream calls `getInkList()` unconditionally and still emits an `/AP /N` stream (stroke colour + line width, no path, `/Rect` unchanged). Removed the `if not path_infos: return` guard. Retargeted two stale tests in `test_pd_ink_appearance_handler_coverage.py`. Pinned both-sides the Highlight transparency-group-XObject divergence (pypdfbox lite surface inlines `gs rg … f`). 40 cases; full annotation suite 2570 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.handlers.PDInkAppearanceHandler`
  reason: empty `/InkList` now still emits the appearance stream like upstream instead of skipping it.

- **Wave 1544 (agent D) — AcroForm appearance generation: 0 real bugs; pinned negative-`/DA`-size divergence.** Built `oracle/probes/AppearanceGenFuzzProbe.java` (30 cases: degenerate `/DA` shapes, g/rg/k colour, newline values, `/Q` quadding, missing `/DR` font, comb `/MaxLen`). Pinned both-sides: for a negative `/DA` font size upstream carries the raw negative size into `Tf` (degenerate) whereas pypdfbox guards `size <= 0` as auto-size (strictly more robust); and a benign colour-encoding difference (upstream `cs <DeviceX> sc` vs pypdfbox literal `g`/`rg`/`k`, same colour family). No production change. 158 tests.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.form` appearance generator
  reason: pinned both-sides; pypdfbox's `size<=0`→auto-size guard is deliberately safer, no production change.

- **Wave 1543 (agent E) — FlateDecode/LZWDecode predictor now routes through PredictorOutputStream.** Built `oracle/probes/PredictorFilterFuzzProbe.java` (45 differential cases): PNG predictors 11–14 with Colors 3/4 + BitsPerComponent 1/2/4/16, wrong `/Columns`/`/Colors`, absent `/DecodeParms`, truncated final row, unknown PNG filter tag, unknown `/Predictor`, TIFF predictor 2 multi-colour/sub-byte/16-bit, LZW EarlyChange 0/1 + short stream, ASCII85/ASCIIHex/RunLength edge cases. **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/filter/flate_decode.py`, `pypdfbox/filter/lzw_decode.py`):** decode reversed the predictor via a divergent one-shot `unpredict()` instead of routing inflated bytes through `PredictorOutputStream` (`Predictor.wrap_predictor`) as upstream `FlateFilter`/`LZWFilter` do. The one-shot path raised on unknown `/Predictor` (upstream passes rows through via `decodePredictorRow`'s `default: break`), raised on `Colors=0` (upstream yields 0 bytes), and mis-counted partial final rows / wrong-`/Columns`. Both filters now route through `PredictorOutputStream`, byte-for-byte with upstream. Retargeted `test_flate_decode.py::test_unsupported_predictor_raises`→`test_undefined_predictor_passes_through` and `test_predictor_wave285.py::test_flate_decode_rejects_zero_color_predictor_params`→`..._yields_empty`. 47 tests; full filter suite 1665 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.filter.FlateFilter`/`LZWFilter` (+ `Predictor.wrapPredictor`)
  reason: predictor reversal now matches upstream's streaming `PredictorOutputStream` (undefined predictor passes through, `Colors=0` empty, correct partial-row byte counts) instead of a stricter one-shot path.

- **Wave 1543 (agent C) — PDType0Font code→CID→GID→width pipeline fuzz (test-only; pins existing no-substitute divergence).** Built `oracle/probes/CidWidthFuzzProbe.java` (13 cases × 12 codes × 4 accessors): predefined Identity-H + custom embedded CMap streams, `/CIDToGIDMap` Identity/absent/stream, range `/W`, omitted `/Encoding`. `code_to_cid` + `get_width` match upstream token-for-token. Pinned both-sides: with no embedded program upstream substitutes a system font (LiberationSans — non-zero `getWidthFromFont`, substitute identity GID overriding an explicit `/CIDToGIDMap`) whereas pypdfbox's `DefaultFontMapper` has no CID-font scanner (`get_width_from_font`=`0.0`, `code_to_gid` honours the dict's own `/CIDToGIDMap`); `no_encoding` throws upstream vs lenient pypdfbox. No production change.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType0Font`/`PDCIDFontType2#findFontOrSubstitute`
  reason: no on-disk CID-font substitute scanner (fail-soft) — font-program-dependent accessors answered from `0`/the dict's `/CIDToGIDMap` instead of a substituted system font.

- **Wave 1543 (agents A, B, D) — xref-stream recovery, COSWriter full save, mesh shadings 4–7: 0 real bugs; parity/divergences pinned.** Agent A (`oracle/probes/XrefRecoveryFuzzProbe.java`, 30 cases): PDF 1.5+ xref-STREAM read path end-to-end through the loader (malformed `/W`/`/Index`/`/Size` geometry, truncated bodies, broken `/Prev` chains, missing/dangling `/Root`, startxref recovery) — pinned that on a *located-but-malformed xref stream* pypdfbox is intentionally MORE STRICT than upstream (propagates the structural error per its documented contract; upstream brute-force rebuilds) and never fabricates a wrong page count. Agent B (`oracle/probes/CosWriterSaveFuzzProbe.java`, 22 docs): full-save structural shape (object count, trailer `/Size`, `/Root`/`/Info`/`/Prev`, xref style, string round-trip) byte-structurally matches; pinned two divergences both producing valid re-readable PDF — re-save object renumbering (upstream mints fresh numbers from loaded `/Size`; pypdfbox compacts/reuses keys, only trailer `/Size` differs) and a qpdf zero-page-tree limitation present on both sides. Agent D (`oracle/probes/MeshShadingFuzzProbe.java`, 42 cases): PDShadingType4/5/6/7 metadata accessors (bits-per-*, vertices-per-row, decode, background, bbox, anti-alias, function) all match; pinned the eager-vs-lazy `get_decode_for_parameter` range difference. No production changes in these three surfaces.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `COSParser`/`PDFXrefStreamParser`, `COSWriter`, `PDShadingType4..7`
  reason: all pinned both-sides; the xref-stream strictness and writer-renumbering divergences are deliberate, no production change.

- **Wave 1542 (agent C) — PDFStreamParser content-stream array/dict recovery now delegates to BaseParser.** Built `oracle/probes/ContentStreamParseFuzzProbe.java` (74 differential blobs + 5 pins): pathological numbers, name `#`-escapes, literal/hex strings, unterminated arrays/dicts, no-whitespace adjacency, comments mid-token, null bytes, mid-token truncation. **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdfparser/pdf_stream_parser.py`, `pypdfbox/pdfparser/base_parser.py`):** the content-stream array/dict parsers used the *strict document-loader* recovery overrides instead of upstream `BaseParser.parseCOSArray`/`parseCOSDictionary` that Java's `PDFStreamParser` uses directly. `parse_cos_array`/`parse_cos_dictionary` now delegate to `BaseParser`; `parse_dir_object` mirrors upstream's `this instanceof PDFStreamParser ? null : COSNull.NULL` (returns `None` for a skipped element under the content-stream parser). Fixes recovery for `[1 0 R]` (whole-token discard on an unresolvable indirect ref with no bound document), `[1 2 3 q`/`[1 R 2]` (corrupt element skipped, partial array kept), and `<< /A 1 /B 2 q`/`<< /A` (partial dict recovered). Retargeted `test_pdf_stream_parser_wave526.py::test_wave526_malformed_dictionary_closes_parser` → `..._recovers_partial`. Pinned both-sides: `IOException` (Java) vs `PDFParseError` (pypdfbox) on EOF, normalized to bare `ERR`. 79 tests; broad parser/cos/contentstream/text suites green.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdfparser.PDFStreamParser` / `BaseParser#parseCOSArray`/`parseCOSDictionary`/`parseDirObject`
  reason: content-stream recovery now matches upstream's `BaseParser` path (partial-container recovery, unresolvable-ref discard) instead of the strict document-loader override.

- **Wave 1542 (agent E) — PDFTextStripper emits the per-page empty-article wrap for glyph-free pages.** Built `oracle/probes/TextStripperOptionFuzzProbe.java` (33 option/edge combinations over a 4-page synthetic doc — page-range bounds, sort-by-position, separator/marker overrides, separate-by-beads, add-more-formatting, suppress-duplicate, whitespace-only page). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/text/pdf_text_stripper.py`):** a glyph-free page (whitespace-only or no content stream) returned `""`, dropping the per-page empty-article markers that upstream `writePage` brackets each `charactersByArticle` slot with via `startArticle()`/`endArticle()` — under `add_more_formatting` (article markers promoted to the line separator) this lost the per-page newlines Java emits. Added `_empty_article_wrap()`; routed both empty-position paths and the no-content-stream path through it; `process_page` on a contentless page now records the empty slot as `[[]]` (not `[]`). Retargeted `test_pdf_text_stripper_wave488.py::test_wave488_process_page_without_contents_resets_walk_state` (`get_characters_by_article() == [[]]`). 4 tests; full text suite 1183 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.text.PDFTextStripper#writePage`/`processPage`
  reason: glyph-free pages now bracket the empty article slot like upstream (per-page newlines under add-more-formatting) instead of returning bare `""`.

- **Wave 1542 (agent B) — fontbox CmapSubtable format-13 reverse lookup matches upstream.** Built `oracle/probes/CmapSubtableFuzzProbe.java` (12 cases splicing hostile cmap subtable bodies into a real SFNT; forward `getGlyphId` + reverse `getCharCodes` over formats 0/4/6/12/13/14). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/cmap_subtable.py::process_subtype13`):** built a multi-mapping reverse table with `glyph_id >= num_glyphs → continue`; upstream writes the gid slot directly inside the per-code loop (last code wins, no multi-mapping) and uses `glyph_id > num_glyphs → break` checked *before* char-code validation. Fixed to mirror upstream exactly (`getCharCodes(5)` now `[0x45]`, was `[0x41..0x45]`). Retargeted `test_cmap_subtable_formats.py` (`test_format_13_many_to_one` reverse → `[0xE004]`; `test_format_13_invalid_glyph_id_skipped` → `..._breaks`). 12 tests; full ttf+cmap suite 3168 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.CmapSubtable#processSubtype13`
  reason: format-13 reverse map now last-code-wins with a break-on-out-of-bounds-gid, matching upstream.

- **Wave 1542 (agent A) — PDField.get_fully_qualified_name returns None for a fully missing /T.** Built `oracle/probes/FieldValueFuzzProbe.java` (42 cases: typed flag predicates, FQN over pathological `/T` chains, typed `get_value` vs `get_value_as_string`). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_field.py`):** `get_fully_qualified_name()` coerced a missing `/T` to `""` (`get_partial_name() or ""`); upstream returns `None` when no `/T` exists anywhere in the self→parent chain. Rewrote to mirror upstream exactly (return type `str | None`); a present-but-empty `/T ()` still yields `""`. No stale test retargeted. 42 cases; full form suite 1571 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.form.PDField#getFullyQualifiedName`
  reason: missing `/T` chain now returns `None` like upstream (empty `/T ()` still `""`).

- **Wave 1542 (agent D) — PDPageLabelRange.get_style reads /S via getNameAsString.** Built `oracle/probes/PageLabelFuzzProbe.java` (31 cases: tree resolution below/between/past ranges, out-of-order keys, all styles + unknown/missing/wrong-type, `/St` 0/negative/float, roman subtractive boundaries incl. the Acrobat m-per-thousand quirk, alphabetic doubling/tripling). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_label_range.py`):** `get_style()` used `get_name()` (→`None` for a non-COSName `/S`); upstream uses `getNameAsString`, so a `/S` stored as a COSString is honoured as the style code. A wrong-type `/S "D"` now renders decimal `1|2|3` instead of prefix-only. No stale test retargeted. 3 tests; 149 page-label tests pass.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDPageLabelRange#getStyle`
  reason: `/S` now read via `getNameAsString` (string-typed style honoured) instead of name-only.

- **Wave 1541 (agent E) — PDSeparation / PDDeviceN `to_rgb` short-tint-output arity guard.** Built `oracle/probes/SeparationDeviceNFuzzProbe.java`, constructing `PDSeparation`/`PDDeviceN`/`PDDeviceNAttributes`/`PDDeviceNProcess` over 20 malformed/edge cases (colorant name/string/missing, `/All`+`/None`, alternate valid/missing/wrong-type, tint function-dict/stream/missing/wrong-arity, DeviceN colorant arrays empty/single/many/non-name, `/Attributes` missing/non-dict, `/Process` component mismatch). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_separation.py`, `pd_device_n.py`):** `to_rgb`/`to_rgb_with_tint_transform` crashed with `IndexError` when a tint transform produced fewer components than the alternate colour space needs (e.g. a 1-output tint feeding a 4-component DeviceCMYK unpack). Added a defensive arity guard returning `None` — the lazy-path equivalent of PDFBox's eager-constructor rejection. Pinned both-sides the central divergence: PDFBox's constructors are eager (resolve alternate+tint and validate arity at construction, raising on malformed pairings) whereas pypdfbox is lazy/lenient (constructs fine, accessors return `None`/skip). 20 tests; full color tree 1864 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.color.PDSeparation` / `PDDeviceN`
  reason: lazy `to_rgb` no longer raises `IndexError` on a too-short tint output (returns `None`); the eager-vs-lazy constructor-validation difference is pinned both-sides.

- **Wave 1541 (agents A, B, C, D) — PDAction subtypes, PDAbstractPattern.create, PDInlineImage, PDExtendedGraphicsState copy-into: 0 real bugs; parity confirmed.** Agent A (`oracle/probes/ActionSubtypeFuzzProbe.java`, 49 cases): JavaScript/URI/Named/Launch/GoToR/GoToE/ImportData/SubmitForm/ResetForm typed accessors (JS string/stream decode, `createFS` dispatch, OpenMode tri-state, fields/flags) — byte-exact (`IOException`→`OSError` is the documented porting mapping). Agent B (`oracle/probes/PatternFuzzProbe.java`, 33 dispatch cases): `PDAbstractPattern.create` `/PatternType` dispatch + tiling/shading bodies — parity; pinned the wave-1521 `tiling-bbox-short` divergence (short `/BBox` → `None` via `size()<4` guard vs upstream zero-padded rect) and `IOException`→`OSError` for unknown type. Agent C (`oracle/probes/InlineImageApiFuzzProbe.java`, 44 cases): PDInlineImage getter surface (abbreviated/full keys, `/CS`+`/F` abbreviations, BPC/W/H/IM/Decode) — 43/44 byte-exact; pinned one filter-module-level divergence (empty data + `/CCF` throws upstream but pypdfbox's CCITT filter zero-fills and constructs). Agent D (`oracle/probes/ExtGStateCopyFuzzProbe.java`, 34 modes): `PDExtendedGraphicsState.copy_into_graphics_state` spec-default-push + null-overwrite matrix onto a real `PDGraphicsState` — full line-for-line parity, no pins needed. No production changes in these four surfaces.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `PDAction*`/`PDActionFactory`, `PDAbstractPattern`, `PDInlineImage`, `PDExtendedGraphicsState`
  reason: all pinned both-sides; no production change in these four surfaces.

- **Wave 1540 (agent A) — PDFunctionType0 (sampled function) reversed-clip + empty-`/Range` fixed (resolves the wave-1539 follow-up).** Built `oracle/probes/FunctionType0FuzzWave1540Probe.java`, running `PDFunctionType0` over 35 batteries (~210 eval cases): reversed/short/long/empty/non-numeric `/Domain` `/Range` `/Encode` `/Decode`; `/Size` zero/negative/wrong-arity; `/BitsPerSample` sweep 1–32 + off-spec; sample stream too short/long; nearest-vs-linear at and between grid points; multi-input/output. **2 real bugs fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type0.py`):** (1) `eval` clipped inputs (to `/Domain`) and outputs (to `/Range`) via the base normalising `clip_input`/`clip_output`, which swap a reversed `(min,max)` pair; upstream `PDFunctionType0.eval` uses the non-normalising `clipToRange(x,min,max)` per dimension (confirmed by jar disassembly). Added a Type-0-local non-normalising clip (mirroring Type 3/4); reversed `/Domain`/`/Range` now match Java. (2) `eval` raised `ValueError` for an empty `/Range` (num_out==0); upstream's output loop runs zero times and returns `[]` — removed the guard (num_in==0 guard kept, matching Java's ERR). Retargeted `test_pd_function_type0_wave280.py` (error message "/Domain and /Range" → "/Domain"). Pinned both-sides: `/BitsPerSample`=64 (Java EOF→0.0 vs pypdfbox ValueError) and non-numeric arrays (Java ClassCastException vs lenient `to_float_array`→0.0, a shared COS-layer contract). 5 oracle tests; full function suite 1156 passed.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.function.PDFunctionType0#eval` / `PDFunction.clipToRange`
  reason: reversed-pair clip now matches upstream's non-normalising clamp and empty-`/Range` returns `[]`; residual lenient families pinned both-sides.

- **Wave 1540 (agent C) — PDOutputIntent `get_dest_output_intent()` non-stream `/DestOutputProfile` fixed.** Built `oracle/probes/OutputIntentFuzzProbe.java`, running `PDOutputIntent` over 63 cases (string/subtype fields × 11 malformed shapes + `/DestOutputProfile` × 8). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_output_intent.py`):** the upstream-named alias `get_dest_output_intent()` raised `TypeError` on a non-stream `/DestOutputProfile` (dict/name/int/COSNull); upstream resolves via `COSDictionary.getCOSStream`, which returns null for any non-stream and never raises. Fixed the alias to return `None`; the stricter pypdfbox-enrichment accessors `get_dest_output_profile()`/`get_dest_output_profile_cos()` keep their documented TypeError contract. Pinned both-sides: `get_subtype()` reads `/S` as a name (string-valued `/S`→`None` vs upstream `getNameAsString`→`"sRGB"`). 127 tests; all existing PDOutputIntent tests still pass.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.color.PDOutputIntent#getDestOutputIntent`
  reason: alias now mirrors upstream's `getCOSStream` null-on-non-stream resolution instead of raising; the `/S`-as-name read is pinned both-sides.

- **Wave 1540 (agents B, D, E) — PDViewerPreferences, PDStructureElement/PDMarkInfo, PDComplexFileSpecification: 0 real bugs; parity confirmed.** Agent B (`oracle/probes/ViewerPrefsFuzzProbe.java`, 50 cases): every boolean flag/name-enum/`/NumCopies`/`/PrintPageRange`/`/Enforce` byte-exact vs the jar; pinned the enrichment-getter divergence (`get_num_copies`/`get_print_page_range`/`get_enforce` have no upstream 3.0.7 counterpart — decoded value identical, only getter presence differs). Agent D (`oracle/probes/StructElementFuzzProbe.java`, 28 cases): `/C` class names (stateful revision-interleaved parse), `/Pg` non-dict shapes, `/R` max-int, single-hop `/RoleMap` resolution, float kids, `PDMarkInfo` booleans — all byte-exact. Agent E (extended the shared wave-1514 `oracle/probes/FileSpecFuzzProbe.java` grammar with `dos`/`mac`/`unix`/`vol` fields, 19 new cases): `get_file_dos`/`mac`/`unix` return null for wrong-typed slots and `is_volatile` coerces non-boolean `/V` to spec-default false — all matching upstream; retargeted `test_filespec_fuzz_wave1514.py` to project the new fields. No production changes in these three surfaces.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `PDViewerPreferences`, `PDStructureElement`/`PDMarkInfo`, `PDComplexFileSpecification`
  reason: all pinned both-sides; no production change in these three surfaces.

- **Wave 1539 (agent A) — PDFunctionType4 (PostScript calculator) reversed-`/Domain`/`/Range` clip fixed; lenient base-class families pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/FunctionType4FuzzWave1539Probe.java`, running `PDFunctionType4` over 71 cases (malformed function dicts: missing/empty/odd-length/single-entry/non-numeric/reversed/not-an-array `/Domain` & `/Range`; plus operator/execution corners: sin/cos cardinals, atan quadrant sweep, int/float tag preservation, `roll` boundaries, bitshift at ±31/±32/33, deep brace/if nesting, comparison-tag handling, multi-input clamping). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type4.py`):** input/output clip used the base `clip_input`/`clip_output`, which *normalise* a reversed `(min,max)` pair (swap lo/hi); upstream `PDFunction.clipToRange(x,min,max)` is non-normalising (`if x<min→min; if x>max→max`), verified by jar disassembly. Added a Type-4-local non-normalising clip override (mirroring the existing PDFunctionType3 override); a reversed `/Domain`/`/Range` now produces the jar's clamped value. Retargeted the stale `test_pd_function_type4_wave638.py` assertion (input 1.0 → 4.0, the Java-correct value). Pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/common/function/oracle/test_function_type4_fuzz_wave1539.py` (6 tests).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.function.PDFunctionType4` / `PDFunction.clipToRange`
  reason: reversed-pair clip now matches upstream's non-normalising clamp; the residual lenient families (whole-stack return when `/Range` declares 0 outputs, `to_float_array` coercing non-numbers to 0.0, missing/short `/Domain` treated as no-clip) are the project-wide permissive-factory contract, pinned both-sides. **Follow-up:** PDFunctionType0 likely shares the same base reversed-clip path — worth a dedicated check.

- **Wave 1539 (agent E) — PDTransition `/S`,`/M`,`/Dm` string-valued read fixed.** Built `oracle/probes/TransitionFuzzProbe.java`, running `PDTransition` over 51 cases (every `/S` style + unknown/string/int/null; `/D` zero/negative/huge/fractional/name/string; `/Dm` H/V/garbage; `/M` I/O/garbage; `/Di` ints/`/None`/garbage/float; `/SS`, `/B`). **Real bug fixed (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/pagenavigation/pd_transition.py`):** `get_style`/`get_motion`/`get_dimension` used `get_name` (COSName-only), dropping a string-valued `/S`/`/M`/`/Dm` to the default; upstream uses `COSDictionary.getNameAsString` (verified by bytecode), which returns the decoded string. Switched all three to `get_name_as_string`. Retargeted the stale `test_navigation_helpers_wave281.py` pin. Pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/interactive/pagenavigation/oracle/test_transition_fuzz_wave1539.py` (17 tests).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.pagenavigation.PDTransition#getStyle/getMotion/getDimension`
  reason: name-only read dropped string-typed `/S`,`/M`,`/Dm` values; now mirrors upstream `getNameAsString` (non-name/non-string shapes still fall back to defaults).

- **Wave 1539 (agents B, C, D) — OCMD membership, PDDestination family, PDAppearanceCharacteristics `/MK`: 0 real bugs; parity confirmed.** Agent B (`oracle/probes/OcmdMembershipFuzzProbe.java`, 21 cases): `PDOptionalContentProperties.is_group_enabled` resolution precedence — `/ON` wins over `/OFF`, name-overload short-circuits on first enabled match; reaffirms the existing deliberate divergence that `get_base_state()` is lenient on unknown `/BaseState` (faithful throwing accessor is `get_base_state_enum()`). Agent C (`oracle/probes/DestinationFuzzProbe.java`, 36 cases): full `PDDestination.create` dispatch + all subclass coordinate getters; pinned one honest divergence — out-of-bounds coordinate reads on a too-short array (e.g. `[page /XYZ]`) throw `IndexOutOfBoundsException` upstream but return `None` in pypdfbox (deliberate graceful-None guard in `PDPageDestination._get_float`). Agent D (`oracle/probes/AppearanceMkFuzzProbe.java`, 41 cases): `PDAppearanceCharacteristicsDictionary` read surface byte-exact vs the jar across rotation/`/BC`/`/BG` arity/captions/icons; noted `get_text_position`/`get_icon_fit`/`PDIconFit` are pypdfbox extensions absent from upstream 3.0.7 (no oracle counterpart, pinned Python-side). No production changes.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `PDOptionalContentProperties`, `PDDestination` family, `PDAppearanceCharacteristicsDictionary`
  reason: all pinned both-sides; the OCMD lenient-BaseState and destination graceful-None divergences are pre-existing project-wide contracts; no production change in these three surfaces.

- **Wave 1538 (agent A) — PDLab (CIE L*a*b*) malformed `[/Lab << … >>]` dictionaries fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: 0 real bugs; 2 documented divergence families pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/LabColorSpaceFuzzProbe.java`, constructing `new PDLab(array)` over 31 malformed/edge cases (missing dict, missing/short/long/empty/non-numeric/zero `/WhitePoint`, `/BlackPoint` variants, missing/short/long/empty/reversed/zero `/Range`, `COSNull` entries, a stream where an array is expected) and projecting `getName` / `getNumberOfComponents` / `getWhitepoint` / `getBlackPoint` / `getARange`+`getBRange` / `getInitialColor` / `toRGB` at the L* extremes and a*/b* corners. **No production bug found** — every divergence falls into one of two pre-existing, documented families: (1) **Permissive accessor contract** — a short/empty `/WhitePoint` makes upstream's eager whitepoint-cache fill throw `IndexOutOfBoundsException` *in the constructor*, and a short/empty `/BlackPoint` or `/Range` makes the corresponding `PDTristimulus`/`PDRange` accessor throw on access, whereas pypdfbox reads what is present and falls back to the documented defaults (`WhitePoint [1 1 1]`, `BlackPoint [0 0 0]`, `Range [-100 100 -100 100]`); (2) **JVM-CMM colour math** — `toRGB`'s final XYZ→sRGB step goes through the JVM colour-management module (D50 PCS) upstream vs pypdfbox's explicit IEC 61966-2-1 D65 matrix (the Lab→XYZ companding is identical, so only the last step diverges; deltas reach tens of 255). Both families are pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/color/oracle/test_lab_color_space_fuzz_wave1538.py` (31-case corpus + completeness + CMM-honesty + leniency-honesty pins, 34 tests). Notably a *short* (2-element) `/WhitePoint` / `/BlackPoint` matches both sides (pypdfbox returns the 2-element partial, which projects to the same `ERR` upstream's `getZ()` throws), and the all-zero `/WhitePoint` collapses `toRGB` to black on both sides — those are asserted to match exactly, not pinned as divergences. No stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.color.PDLab` (+ `PDCIEDictionaryBasedColorSpace` whitepoint-cache fill)
  reason: pypdfbox's lenient `/WhitePoint`·`/BlackPoint`·`/Range` reads (defaults instead of throwing on short/empty arrays) are the project-wide permissive-factory contract; the `toRGB` XYZ→sRGB delta is the same JVM-CMM vs explicit-D65-matrix divergence already pinned for CalRGB/CalGray/Lab-clamp. Both pinned both-sides; no production change.

- **Wave 1538 (agent B) — PDCalRGB / PDCalGray (CIE calibrated colour) malformed `[/CalRGB << … >>]` / `[/CalGray << … >>]` dictionaries fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: 0 real bugs; 3 documented divergence families pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/CalColorSpaceFuzzProbe.java`, constructing `new PDCalRGB(array)` / `new PDCalGray(array)` over 35 malformed/edge cases (21 CalRGB + 14 CalGray: empty dict, missing/short/long/empty/non-numeric/scalar `/Gamma`, missing/short/empty/scalar `/Matrix` (CalRGB), short/non-numeric/zero/negative/long `/WhitePoint`, short/long `/BlackPoint`) and projecting `getNumberOfComponents` / `getGamma` (CalRGB via `PDGamma.getR/G/B`, CalGray single float) / `getMatrix` / `getWhitepoint` / `getBlackPoint` / `getInitialColor` / `toRGB` (only on the non-unit-white-point pass-through branch — the unit-white-point CIE path's XYZ→sRGB CMM divergence is already pinned by `test_cal_color_oracle.py`, so it is emitted as a `CMM` marker, not compared). Every case keeps a dictionary present in slot 1 so both sides construct (the slot-1-non-dict ctor NPE is already pinned by wave 1512). **No production bug found** — every divergence falls into one of three pre-existing, documented families: (1) **Permissive ctor contract** — upstream's `PDCIEDictionaryBasedColorSpace` ctor eagerly reads `/WhitePoint` via `fillWhitepointCache(getWhitepoint())` (number-casting each element), so a short / non-numeric `/WhitePoint` throws *during construction*; pypdfbox reads the dict lazily, constructs, and surfaces the defect on access; (2) **`/Gamma` numeric/length leniency** — upstream `PDGamma.getR/G/B` casts each element to `COSNumber` (throws on non-numeric) and an empty `/Gamma` array yields an empty `PDGamma` whose index-0 read throws, whereas pypdfbox's `get_gamma` goes through `to_float_array` (coerces non-numerics to `0.0`) and treats an empty array as absent (falls back to `[1,1,1]`); (3) **`/BlackPoint` length leniency (CalGray)** — upstream `getBlackPoint` wraps a short array verbatim and the `getZ()` read throws, whereas pypdfbox's CalGray `_read_tristimulus` falls back to `[0,0,0]` on a short array (CalRGB happens to pass the short array through, so it throws like Java). All pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/color/oracle/test_cal_color_space_fuzz_wave1538.py` (35-case corpus + completeness pin = 36 tests, 28 byte-exact, 7 pinned). The accessor defaults (missing `/Gamma` → `[1,1,1]`/`1.0`; missing `/Matrix` → identity; missing `/BlackPoint` → `[0,0,0]`; missing `/WhitePoint` → `[1,1,1]`), short-`/Matrix` verbatim passthrough, gamma 4-element truncation, initial colour, and the non-unit-white-point pass-through `toRGB` are all byte-parity and asserted to match exactly. No stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.color.PDCalRGB` / `PDCalGray` (+ `PDCIEDictionaryBasedColorSpace` whitepoint-cache fill, `PDGamma`, `PDTristimulus`)
  reason: pypdfbox's lenient `/Gamma`·`/WhitePoint`·`/BlackPoint` reads (coerce non-numerics to `0.0`, default on short/empty, lazy ctor) are the project-wide permissive-factory contract; the unit-white-point `toRGB` XYZ→sRGB delta is the same JVM-CMM vs explicit-D65-matrix divergence already pinned for CalRGB/CalGray. All pinned both-sides; no production change.

- **Wave 1536 (agent E) — PDAnnotationWidget non-`/AP`/non-`/MK` accessors fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 over malformed widget dicts: 1 real bug fixed; 1 divergence pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/WidgetAccessorFuzzProbe.java`, projecting `getHighlightingMode` / `getAppearanceState` / `getBorderStyle` presence / `getActions` presence / `getAnnotationFlags` + the `/F` bit predicates / raw `/Parent` across 38 in-memory malformed cases (the `/AP`, icon and `/MK` surfaces are already owned by `WidgetApProbe` / `WidgetIconProbe` / `WidgetMkProbe`). **Real bug (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/pd_annotation_widget.py::get_highlighting_mode`):** it read `/H` via `get_name`, which returns `None` for a non-name value and therefore discarded a *string*-typed `/H`, falling back to the default `I`. Decompiled bytecode shows upstream reads `getCOSObject().getNameAsString(COSName.H, "I")` — a name returns its name, a string returns its decoded value, any other shape (int / array / null / absent) falls back to `I`. Switched to `get_name_as_string(_H, "I")`; now a `/H = (O)` string yields `O` to the bit, matching the oracle. The `/AS` (name-only, `get_name`), `/BS` // `/AA` presence (dict-only type check), and the `/F` flag predicates (incl. non-int → 0, float-truncation, negative bit patterns) were already at parity. **Pinned divergence (`parent_nondict`):** PDFBox 3.0.7 has NO `getParent()` on `PDAnnotationWidget` (only `setParent`), so a raw `(COSDictionary)` cast of a non-dict `/Parent` throws `ClassCastException`; pypdfbox adds a tolerant `get_parent()` that type-checks and returns `None`, never raising. Pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/oracle/test_widget_accessor_fuzz_wave1536.py` (38 oracle cases + 2 regression pins). No stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.PDAnnotationWidget#getHighlightingMode`
  reason: `/H` highlighting-mode read used `get_name` (drops string-typed values) instead of upstream's `getNameAsString` default-`I` semantics — now aligned; pypdfbox's tolerant `get_parent()` returning `None` for a non-dict `/Parent` is pinned both-sides (upstream has no such accessor, a raw cast throws).

- **Wave 1536 (agent B) — xmpbox STRUCTURED property types (`Dimensions`/`Job`/`Layer`/`ResourceRef`/`Thumbnail`/`Version`) field-access fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: 1 real bug fixed; surface otherwise at parity.** Built `oracle/probes/XmpStructuredTypeFuzzProbe.java`, which constructs each `AbstractStructuredType` subclass in-memory and projects the typed-getter outcomes across 36 cases: every getter `None` on an empty struct; set/get round-trips through the `_FIELD_TYPES` registry (incl. empty-string, NaN/Infinity, zero/negative); `addProperty` replace-not-append (`getAllProperties` size stays 1 after a second `setId`); `getAllProperties` insertion order; child-field namespace (`None`) + preferred prefix (`stJob`/`stRef`); `DimensionsType.toString`; `ResourceRefType.getAlternatePaths` absent/seeded/empty-element; wrong-type field rejection. **Real bug (`pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/dimensions_type.py::to_string`):** rendered missing `w`/`h`/`unit` fields as Python `None` (`DimensionsType{None x None None}`), but upstream `DimensionsType.toString()` concatenates the (null) `Float`/`String` field references, which Java renders as the literal `null` (`DimensionsType{null x null null}`). Switched `to_string` to render `None` fields as `null`. The empty-struct null returns, set/get round-trips, replace-on-duplicate, ordering, and child namespace/prefix were all already at parity. Parity locked in `tests/xmpbox/oracle/test_xmp_structured_type_fuzz_wave1536.py` (36 oracle cases). One stale unit test (`tests/xmpbox/type/test_dimensions_type_wave1273.py::test_to_string_empty`, which pinned the old `None` rendering) retargeted to the oracle-proven `null`. No pinned-both-sides items on this surface (the `IllegalArgumentException`→`ValueError` and `String.valueOf` float/bool/null spelling differences are the project-wide cross-language conventions, normalized in the oracle test, not behavioral divergences).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.xmpbox.type.DimensionsType#toString`
  reason: `DimensionsType.to_string()` rendered absent fields as Python `None` instead of the literal `null` Java string-concatenation produces for a null reference — now aligned.

- **Wave 1535 (agent B) — marked-content operator processors (`BMC`/`BDC`/`EMC`/`MP`/`DP`) fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: 3 real bugs fixed; surface now at parity.** Built `oracle/probes/MarkedContentOpFuzzProbe.java`, which synthesizes a one-page document per fuzz case (content stream + a `/Properties` resource so the `/Name`→resource-lookup branch resolves), drives it through `PDFMarkedContentExtractor.processPage`, and projects the resulting marked-content tree (depth / tag / mcid / children) plus the thrown-exception class across 23 cases (BMC with no operand / non-name tag / trailing-name junk; BDC inline-dict / named-props / unknown-name / missing-props / no-operands / wrong-props-type / non-name-tag; EMC underflow / double-underflow; unbalanced BMC; MP / DP equivalents). Decompiled the 3.0.7 `BeginMarkedContentSequence`, `BeginMarkedContentSequenceWithProperties`, and `MarkedContentPointWithProperties` bytecode to confirm exact operand semantics. **Real bug 1 (`_props.extract_tag`, used by `begin_marked_content.py` / `marked_content_point.py`):** it returned `operands[0]` cast to `COSName`, but upstream `BMC`/`MP` iterate the WHOLE operand list and keep the LAST `COSName` (`for (b : args) if (b instanceof COSName) tag = b`) — so `1 (x) /Span BMC` must yield `/Span` (pypdfbox gave `None`) and `/A /B BMC` must yield `/B` (pypdfbox gave `/A`). Rewrote `extract_tag` to scan for the last name. **Real bug 2 (`begin_marked_content_with_props.py`, `BDC`):** the registered processor called the engine hook UNCONDITIONALLY, even when the property list resolved to `None` and even with `<2` operands — upstream raises `MissingOperandException` on `<2` operands, returns silently when `operands[0]` is not a `COSName`, and (critically) returns WITHOUT opening a sequence when the property list cannot be resolved (unknown `/Name`, wrong operand type, null lookup). It pushed spurious marked-content nodes for every malformed BDC. Rewrote to mirror the bytecode exactly: tag is `operands[0]` (NOT the last name — the property operand of the `/Name` form is itself a `COSName`), arity-check raises, unresolved-props returns. **Real bug 3 (`define_marked_content_point_with_props.py`, `DP`):** same three defects as BDC; rewritten identically (DP's no-callback-on-unresolved was already proven by the wave-1469 `MarkedPointDispatchProbe`, but the registered processor still diverged). Parity locked in `tests/contentstream/oracle/test_marked_content_op_fuzz_wave1535.py` (23 oracle cases + an `extract_tag` regression). Six stale unit tests across `test_begin_marked_content_with_props*.py`, `test_define_marked_content_point_with_props*.py`, `test_props.py`, `test_extra_operators.py`, and `test_pdf_stream_engine_wave1369_marked_content_balance.py` that pinned the old "lenient fires-with-None" contract were retargeted to the oracle-proven strict behaviour. No pinned-both-sides items on this surface. (Flagged, NOT fixed — out of agent-B zone: `pypdfbox/text/pdf_marked_content_extractor.py::_dispatch_marked` and `pdf_text_stripper.py` reimplement BDC dispatch inline and still push a node for unresolved BDC props + take `operands[0]` as the BMC tag — same class of divergence, a follow-up for whoever owns `text/`.)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.markedcontent.BeginMarkedContentSequence#process`, `BeginMarkedContentSequenceWithProperties#process`, `MarkedContentPointWithProperties#process`
  reason: BMC/MP tag selection took the first operand instead of the last `COSName`; BDC/DP fired the engine hook for malformed/underflow/unresolved-property operands instead of raising `MissingOperandException` (arity) or returning silently — both now aligned, zero remaining divergence on the registered operator-processor surface.

- **Wave 1535 (agent D) — PDFunctionType0 (sampled) eval fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 over malformed dictionaries: 2 real bugs fixed; 2 divergences pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/FunctionType0SampledFuzzProbe.java` projecting `PDFunctionType0.eval` outputs (or `ERR`) across 21 malformed-dict batteries (/Size missing/wrong-length/zero/negative/huge/non-numeric, off-spec /BitsPerSample 0/3/64, truncated/empty body, present-but-short /Encode & /Decode, boundary-vs-interpolated inputs, 1-D/2-D shapes, Domain/Range clipping). **Real bug 1 (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type0.py` — /BitsPerSample validation):** pypdfbox rejected any width not in the Table-38 set `{1,2,4,8,12,16,24,32}` with a `ValueError`, but upstream PDFBox does NOT validate `/BitsPerSample` — its eval reads each sample with `MemoryCacheImageInputStream.readBits(bits)`, which accepts any width in `[0, 64]`, so off-spec widths like 3/5/7 (or 0) are read bit-for-bit, not rejected. The oracle showed `bits=3` returning real interpolated codes (`0.571429` at x=1.0) and `bits=0` returning `0.0`, both of which pypdfbox threw on. Replaced the `frozenset` membership check (in `eval`, `get_samples`, `decode_sample_grid`) with a `0 <= bits <= 32` range guard — the determinate range pypdfbox reproduces to the bit (verified by this oracle for widths 0/3/5/7/24/32). **Real bug 2 (same file — present-but-short /Encode // /Decode):** `_get_encode_pairs` / `_get_decode_pairs` (and `get_encode_for_parameter` / `get_decode_for_parameter`) default-filled the missing pairs (`(0, Size[i]-1)` for Encode, the `/Range` pair for Decode) when the array was present but too short. Upstream's `getEncodeValues()` / `getDecodeValues()` fill the spec default ONLY when the array is **absent** — a present-but-short array is returned verbatim, so `getEncodeForParameter` / `getDecodeForParameter` return `null` past its length and eval NPEs. Rewrote both accessors to delegate to `get_encode_values()` / `get_decode_values()` and return `None` when `size() < 2*n+1` (no default fill-in for a partial array), and made `_get_encode_pairs` / `_get_decode_pairs` raise `ValueError` on that `None` — mirroring upstream's hard failure. **Pinned divergence 1 (`size_huge`, /Size = 1e9):** PDFBox eagerly allocates the full `int[product][]` sample grid → OutOfMemoryError / NegativeArraySizeError (not an IOException, so it propagates) → ERR; pypdfbox reads samples lazily per-eval so it never allocates the grid and returns `0.0` (strictly more robust — matching PDFBox would mean deliberately allocating a billion-cell array). **Pinned divergence 2 (`bits64`, /BitsPerSample 33..64):** PDFBox accepts these (returns `0.0` here) but its output depends on a Java `(int)` long-truncation of the `readBits` result plus a stateful "first eval throws on the over-32 cast, the cached sample grid is left zeroed, later evals return 0" quirk that is not bit-reproducible in Python; pypdfbox raises `ValueError` for widths > 32. Both pins asserted both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/common/function/oracle/test_function_type0_sampled_fuzz_wave1535.py`. Retargeted 6 stale hand-written tests that pinned the pre-fix contract (off-spec-bits-reject in `test_pd_function_type0_bits_per_sample_matrix.py` / `test_pd_function_type0_eval.py` / `test_pd_function_type_0.py`, partial-encode/decode-default in `test_pd_function_type0_wave280.py` / `test_pd_function_type0_wave407.py`) to the oracle-proven behaviour.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.function.PDFunctionType0#eval` / `#getEncodeForParameter` / `#getDecodeForParameter` (and the private `getEncodeValues` / `getDecodeValues`)
  reason: /BitsPerSample was over-validated against the Table-38 set (upstream reads any [0,64] width via readBits) and present-but-short /Encode//Decode was silently default-filled (upstream NPEs) — both now aligned for the [0,32] determinate range; /Size=1e9 lazy-read 0.0 and /BitsPerSample 33..64 are pinned both-sides as unalignable Java allocation/int-cast quirks.

- **Wave 1535 (agent C) — xmpbox simple-property TYPE value-conversion classes fuzzed against Apache xmpbox 3.0.7: 2 real bugs fixed; surface now at parity.** Built `oracle/probes/XmpPropertyTypeFuzzProbe.java`, constructing `org.apache.xmpbox.type.IntegerType` / `RealType` / `BooleanType` / `TextType` / `DateType` with a raw (often malformed) value and projecting `getStringValue` (or the exception class) across 75 cases. **Real bug 1 (`IntegerType`):** `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/integer_type.py` accepted Python's unbounded `int`, so `"99999999999"` / `"2147483648"` / `"-2147483649"` (and the equivalent direct-`int` values) parsed fine while upstream's `Integer.parseInt` / Java `int` field overflows the signed 32-bit range → `NumberFormatException`. Added a `[-2147483648, 2147483647]` range guard on both the string-parse and direct-int paths (raises `ValueError`). **Real bug 2 (`RealType`):** `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/real_type.py` stored a Python `double` and parsed with bare `float()`, diverging from Java's single-precision `float` field + `Float.parseFloat` grammar three ways: (a) it rejected the Java type suffix (`2.5f`/`2.5d`/`3.0F`) and hex floats (`0x1.8p1`) that `Float.parseFloat` accepts; (b) it accepted lower-case `inf`/`nan` and digit underscores (`1_000.0`) that Java rejects; (c) it kept full double precision so `"1234567.89"` stored/rendered as `1234567.89` (Java rounds to the float32 `1234567.9`), `"1e40"` stayed finite (Java overflows to `Infinity`), and `getStringValue` used Python `str(float)` instead of Java `Float.toString`. Rewrote the parser to mirror the `Float.parseFloat` grammar exactly (regex-gated, with `float.fromhex` for hex literals), narrow every value to single precision via the existing `cos_float._float32_or_inf`, and render `getStringValue` byte-for-byte via the existing `cos_float.float_to_string` (the proven Java `Float.toString` port). The oracle also confirmed `BooleanType` (case-insensitive + whitespace-trimmed `True`/`False`, rejects `1`/`0`/`yes`), `TextType` (any string incl. empty/whitespace), and `DateType` (rejects unparseable strings, stores `None` for the empty/whitespace string, rejects `None`) already match. Parity locked in `tests/xmpbox/oracle/test_xmp_property_type_fuzz_wave1535.py` (75 cases). No pinned-both-sides items on this surface; the only comparison caveat is that `DateType.getStringValue` for partial / zone-less inputs renders the JVM's local zone (environment-dependent) so those date cases compare OK/ERR + null-vs-non-null classification only.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.xmpbox.type.IntegerType#setValue` and `org.apache.xmpbox.type.RealType#setValue`/`#getStringValue`
  reason: `IntegerType` accepted out-of-int32 magnitudes upstream overflows on, and `RealType` stored/parsed/rendered as `double` instead of Java's single-precision `Float.parseFloat`/`Float.toString` — both now aligned, zero remaining divergence on this surface.

- **Wave 1534 (agent A) — `sh` (shading fill) operator processor fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: 1 real bug fixed (non-`COSName` operand now raises `MissingOperandException`); surface now at parity.** Built `oracle/probes/ShadingFillFuzzProbe.java`, exercising `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.graphics.ShadingFill` two ways across 8 malformed cases (no operand, extra leading numbers `1 2 /Sh1 sh`, non-name operand `42 sh`, unknown shading name, missing `/Shading` sub-dict, wrong-typed `/Shading` entry, null page resources, normal): an ENGINE path (a minimal `PDFGraphicsStreamEngine` recording each surviving `shadingFill(COSName)` call) and a direct `process()` path (capturing the raw operand-gate result). **Real bug fixed:** `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/shading_fill.py` silently returned for a non-`COSName` leading operand, justified by a "leniency precedent of the path operators". Decompiling the 3.0.7 `ShadingFill.process` bytecode showed `sh` has NO such leniency — a non-name operand 0 explicitly throws `MissingOperandException` (the path operators differ because they use `checkArrayTypesClass`). The operator now raises `MissingOperandException` for a non-name operand 0, matching upstream exactly. Because that exception is logged + swallowed by `PDFStreamEngine.operatorException`, the engine-path observable behaviour is unchanged (no `shadingFill` invocation either way) — only the direct operator contract is corrected. The oracle also confirmed `ShadingFill` does NO resource lookup itself: an unknown name, a missing/wrong-typed `/Shading` dict, and even null resources all still invoke `shadingFill(name)` (lookup/skip happens in the downstream hook). Parity locked in `tests/contentstream/oracle/test_shading_fill_fuzz_wave1534.py`; the two stale `test_shading_fill.py` cases that pinned the old silent-skip contract were retargeted to the raise.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.graphics.ShadingFill#process`
  reason: non-name operand 0 was incorrectly skipped; upstream raises `MissingOperandException` — now aligned, zero remaining divergence on this surface.

- **Wave 1534 (agent D) — graphics `cm`/`Do`/`gs` operator processors fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: 2 real bugs fixed; surface now at parity.** Built `oracle/probes/GraphicsOperatorFuzzProbe.java`, which instantiates the graphics `Concatenate` (`cm`), `DrawObject` (`Do`) and `SetGraphicsStateParameters` (`gs`) processors with a constructor-injected recording `PDFGraphicsStreamEngine` and calls each `process(Operator, List)` DIRECTLY (routing through `processOperator` would swallow the arity/type exception into `operatorException`). 29 malformed cases: `cm` with <6 / non-number-in-first-six / huge values / 7 operands (all-number vs trailing non-number); `Do` with no operand / non-name / form / image / unknown name / non-stream `/XObject` entry / trailing extra; `gs` with no operand / non-name / good / unknown / non-dict `/ExtGState` entry / trailing extra. **Real bug 1 (`cm`):** `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/concatenate_matrix.py` guarded only `operands[:6]` for `COSNumber`-ness, while upstream `Concatenate.process` calls `checkArrayTypesClass(arguments, COSNumber.class)` over the WHOLE operand list — so a malformed `a b c d e f /Name cm` (7 operands, trailing non-number) made pypdfbox apply the matrix while upstream silently dropped it (identical class of bug as `Tm`/wave 1525). Fixed to guard the full list. **Real bug 2 (`Do`):** `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/invoke_named_xobject.py` painted a resolved image XObject unconditionally, missing upstream graphics `DrawObject`'s `if (!image.isStencil() && !context.isShouldProcessColorOperators()) return;` guard — a non-stencil image with colour-operator processing suppressed (uncoloured tiling pattern / Type3 `d1` charproc) must be SKIPPED, not drawn. Fixed with the same stencil + `is_should_process_color_operators` guard. The oracle also confirmed parity for the no-bug paths: `Do` empty → `MissingOperandException`, non-name → silent skip, unknown name → `MissingResourceException`, non-stream `/XObject` entry → `OSError`(↔`IOException`) from `getXObject`; `gs` empty → `MissingOperandException`, non-name → silent skip, unknown / non-dict `/ExtGState` → clean return (no throw). Parity locked in `tests/contentstream/oracle/test_graphics_operator_fuzz_wave1534.py`. The only pinned-both-sides item is the documented `PDFStreamEngine._should_process_color_operators` default divergence (pypdfbox defaults `True`, upstream `false` outside an active stream run) — the test drives the pypdfbox engine with the flag forced `False` so the `Do` image comparison is apples-to-apples; during a real render both engines set it `True` and paint the image identically.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.state.Concatenate#process` and `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.graphics.DrawObject#process`
  reason: `cm` ignored non-numbers past the first six (upstream guards the whole list) and `Do` drew images unconditionally (upstream skips a non-stencil image when colour-op processing is suppressed) — both now aligned, zero remaining divergence on this surface.

- **Wave 1535 (agent E) — `BT` / `ET` (begin/end text object) operator processors + text-matrix reset fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: parity-exact, no behavioral divergence; one cosmetic faithfulness fix to ET's clear order.** Built `oracle/probes/TextObjectFuzzProbe.java`, which calls `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.text.BeginText` / `EndText`'s `process(Operator, List)` DIRECTLY on a recording `PDFStreamEngine` subclass (the stock base engine discards text-matrix writes — text-state tracking lives in the rendering cluster, so a bare engine would show nothing) and snapshots `getTextMatrix()` / `getTextLineMatrix()` + begin/end call counts after every step. 16 cases: BT with no / one / many extra operands (all ignored, both matrices reset to identity, never throws); ET with no / extra operands (both matrices cleared to null, never throws); ET-without-BT underflow (tolerated, clean null-clear); BT→BT nesting/re-open (second BT re-resets to identity); double ET (each clears, increments end count); and BT→Tm-style-positioning→ET (positioning state survives until ET nulls it). **No real bug:** pypdfbox's `BeginText` already resets both matrices to the identity `[1,0,0,1,0,0]` and `EndText` clears both to `None`, with no operand validation on either — byte-for-byte parity across all 16 cases. **One cosmetic fix:** `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/text/end_text.py` now clears the text-line matrix *before* the text matrix to match upstream `EndText.process`'s exact statement order (`setTextLineMatrix(null); setTextMatrix(null)`); observably identical (both end null) — two stale tests that pinned the old text-matrix-first order were retargeted to the upstream order. Parity locked in `tests/contentstream/oracle/test_text_object_fuzz_wave1535.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.text.BeginText#process` / `EndText#process`
  reason: no behavioral divergence found; ET's matrix-clear statement order was reordered to mirror upstream source exactly (no observable effect) — surface confirmed at parity.

- **Wave 1534 (agent B) — `GlyphPositioningTable` (GPOS) leaked fontTools decode exceptions on a malformed GPOS body where upstream never throws: REAL bug fixed; surface now at parity.** Built `oracle/probes/GposTableFuzzProbe.java`, which splices a hostile GPOS *body* into a canonical SFNT (DejaVuSans re-serialised through fontTools) and projects, via `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.TTFParser`, whether `GPOS` survives in `getTableMap()` plus the GPOS table object's class + `getInitialized()`. **Load-bearing oracle finding:** Apache FontBox 3.0.7 has **no `GlyphPositioningTable` class at all** — `TTFParser.readTableDirectory` only instantiates typed tables for cmap/glyf/head/hhea/hmtx/loca/maxp/name/OS-2/post/DSIG/kern/vhea/vmtx/VORG/GSUB; the `GPOS` tag falls through to a generic, never-decoded `TTFTable` (offset/length only). FontBox is therefore completely **insensitive** to GPOS-internal corruption: wrong major version, version 1.1 featureVariations, out-of-bounds ScriptList/FeatureList/LookupList offsets, an all-zero header, and a truncated body all parse without throwing (`hasGPOS=true`, `gposClass=TTFTable`, `gposInit=false`). **Real bug fixed** in `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/glyph_positioning_table.py`: pypdfbox delegates GPOS decoding to fontTools (library-first), whose lazy decompile raised raw `struct.error`/`AssertionError`/etc. out of `populate_from_fonttools` and the structural accessors (`get_lookup_count`, `get_lookup_types`, `get_script_list`/`get_feature_list`/`get_lookup_list`, `get_lookup`/`get_lookup_subtables`, `get_feature_record`, `get_lookup_indices_for_feature`, `_build_kerning_pairs`) on a corrupt body — i.e. pypdfbox **threw where FontBox silently succeeds**. The wrapper now swallows the fontTools decode-error family per access and degrades a present-but-corrupt GPOS to an empty (but present) inventory, restoring the upstream "GPOS present, never structurally parsed, never throws" contract. Parity locked in `tests/fontbox/ttf/oracle/test_gpos_table_fuzz_wave1534.py` (8 GPOS-internal cases × strict/embedded + an accessors-never-raise guard). The table-directory "Skip table" cases (GPOS entry length 0 / offset past EOF, where FontBox drops the entry but fontTools keeps it) are the already-documented PDFBOX-5285 directory-level library-gap pinned in `test_ttf_parse_fuzz_oracle.py` (the TTFParser surface), so they are excluded from this GPOS-internal module.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.TTFParser#readTableDirectory` (no `GlyphPositioningTable` class exists upstream)
  reason: GPOS-internal corruption made pypdfbox raise a fontTools decode exception where upstream never parses GPOS and so never throws — now aligned, zero remaining divergence on this surface.

- **Wave 1533 (agent A) — `PDMarkInfo` (`/MarkInfo`) malformed boolean reads fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: parity-exact, no production change, zero real bugs; the `set_suspect` mutator divergence re-pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/MarkInfoFuzzProbe.java`, which drives `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.documentinterchange.logicalstructure.PDMarkInfo` `isMarked()` / `isSuspect()` / `usesUserProperties()` (over `/Marked`, `/Suspects`, `/UserProperties`, each a bool defaulting false) across 13 value shapes (absent, present true/false, wrong type int/name/string/array/dict/null, indirect-ref → bool/false/null), the empty/default constructor, and setter round-trips. **All reads match upstream byte-for-byte**: every non-`COSBoolean` value (int 0/1, name `/true`, string `(true)`, array, dict, null, indirect-null) coerces to the default `false` exactly as PDFBox's `COSDictionary.getBoolean` does, and an indirect reference resolving to `COSBoolean.TRUE` dereferences to `true` on both sides. No production change in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_mark_info.py` — the existing accessors and `COSDictionary.get_boolean` already match. **Re-pinned both-sides:** upstream `setSuspect(boolean)` ALWAYS writes `false` regardless of the argument (a longstanding PDFBox bug — verified by decompiling 3.0.7: the bytecode emits `iconst_0`); pypdfbox's `set_suspect` deliberately writes the real argument. The probe exercises upstream `setSuspect` and the parity test mirrors that upstream-bug result (writing `/Suspects = false` directly) so the read/coercion surface under test is what's compared, not the already-documented mutator divergence. Parity locked in `tests/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/oracle/test_mark_info_fuzz_wave1533.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.documentinterchange.logicalstructure.PDMarkInfo#isMarked` / `#isSuspect` / `#usesUserProperties` / `#setSuspect`
  reason: `setSuspect` divergence is an intentional fix of an upstream always-writes-false bug; the read coercion is fully alignable and needs no change.

- **Wave 1533 (agent B) — `PDStructureTreeRoot` non-`/ParentTree`/`/RoleMap` accessor reads fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7: parity-exact reads, no production change, zero real bugs; the `getClassMap()` null-vs-empty-Map return shape re-pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/StructureTreeRootFuzzProbe.java`, which constructs 35 malformed `/StructTreeRoot` dictionaries in-memory and projects each OTHER accessor independently — `getK()` COS shape, `getKids()` count + per-kid tag, `getIDTree()` / `getParentTree()` presence, `getParentTreeNextKey()`, `getRoleMap()` size + `Custom` lookup, `getClassMap()` entry shape — over `/K` absent/single-dict/array/empty-array/int/string/name/mixed, `/IDTree` absent/dict/array/int, `/ParentTree` dict/array/int, `/ParentTreeNextKey` absent/int/float/string/name/negative, `/RoleMap` array/int/empty/name-valued, and `/ClassMap` absent/array-typed/int/single-attr/array-of-attrs/wrong-type-entry/empty. **All reads match upstream byte-for-byte**: `getParentTreeNextKey()` defaults to `-1` and truncates a `COSFloat` to int and rejects a `COSString`/`COSName` to the default exactly as `COSDictionary.getInt` does; `getK()`/`getKids()` reproduce upstream's kid-filtering (a bare `COSInteger` `/K` surfaces as `int:5`, a `COSString`/`COSName` `/K` yields zero kids, an array drops non-structural slots); `getIDTree()`/`getParentTree()` return `None` for a non-dictionary entry; `getRoleMap()` is empty for a non-dict `/RoleMap` and resolves a name- or string-valued `/Custom` entry to text; `getClassMap()` drops a wrong-type (`COSString`) entry and exposes single-dict vs array-of-dicts entries identically. No production change in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_structure_tree_root.py`. **Re-pinned both-sides:** upstream `getClassMap()` returns a `Map<String,Object>` and is never `null` (an absent/non-dict `/ClassMap` yields an empty Map), whereas pypdfbox's typed `get_class_map()` returns a `PDStructureClassMap` wrapper or `None`; the probe and parity test normalise the empty/absent container to a shared `empty` tag and compare the observable surviving-entry content, which is identical. Parity locked in `tests/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/oracle/test_structure_tree_root_fuzz_wave1533.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.documentinterchange.logicalstructure.PDStructureTreeRoot#getK` / `#getKids` / `#getIDTree` / `#getParentTree` / `#getParentTreeNextKey` / `#getRoleMap` / `#getClassMap`
  reason: `getClassMap()` return type is a typed wrapper (or `None`) vs upstream's `Map<String,Object>` (never null); the entry content under test is fully alignable and needs no change.

- **Wave 1533 (agent C) — `PDTrueTypeFont` encoding-selection + `codeToGID` + width fuzz against PDFBox 3.0.7: embedded fonts parity-exact, no production change; the non-embedded substitute-font program is an unported, environment-dependent divergence pinned both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/PdTrueTypeFontFuzzProbe.java`, which loads PDFs carrying malformed simple-TrueType font dicts and projects, per code, the resolved `getEncoding().getClass().getSimpleName()`, `codeToGID(code)`, `getWidth(code)`, `getWidthFromFont(code)`, and the per-code glyph name, plus per-font `isSymbolic`/`isEmbedded`/`isDamaged`. The fuzz matrix (synthesised TTFs via fontTools) covers: symbolic flag set vs clear; `/Encoding` absent / WinAnsi name / MacRoman name / dict-with-`/Differences` (no `/BaseEncoding`); a NON-symbolic font carrying only a `(3,0)` cmap; the symbolic `(3,0)` `0xF000` PUA offset and `(1,0)` Mac-Roman cmap; `/Widths` present / absent (program fallback) / window-mismatched with `/FirstChar`,`/LastChar`; a missing `/FontDescriptor`; and a descriptor with no embedded `/FontFile2`. **For every EMBEDDED font (7 cases) all five columns match upstream byte-for-byte** — the symbolic-vs-non-symbolic encoding selection (`WinAnsiEncoding`/`MacRomanEncoding`/`StandardEncoding` default/`DictionaryEncoding`/`BuiltInEncoding`), the `(3,1)`/`(3,0)`/`(1,0)` + `0xF000` codeToGID chain, and the `/Widths`-window-vs-`hmtx`-program width path are all parity-exact. No production change in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_true_type_font.py`. **DIVERGENCE pinned both-sides:** for a NON-embedded simple TrueType font (no `/FontFile2`), upstream's `PDTrueTypeFont` constructor calls `findFontOrSubstitute()` to load a host system substitute (Arial/Liberation) so `getWidthFromFont`, `codeToGID`, and the out-of-`/Widths` `getWidth` resolve through that substitute program; pypdfbox does NOT wire `FontMapperImpl`'s substitute path into `get_true_type_font()`, so those three columns return `0` / GID `0`. The substitute's GIDs/widths are host-font-dependent and so unalignable for an oracle byte-diff; the parity test (`tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_pd_true_type_font_fuzz_wave1533.py`) pins only the substitute-independent facets for the non-embedded fonts (encoding class, per-code glyph name, and in-`/Widths`-window `getWidth`), which DO match upstream. Tracked as a deferred feature in `DEFERRED.md`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDTrueTypeFont#getEncoding` / `#codeToGID` / `#getWidth` / `#getWidthFromFont` / `#findFontOrSubstitute`
  reason: the non-embedded substitute-font program is environment-dependent (resolves to a host system TTF) and so cannot be byte-diffed against the live oracle; pypdfbox has not ported the `PDTrueTypeFont` substitute-loading path. All embedded-font behaviour is parity-exact and needs no change.

- **Wave 1533 (agent D) — `PDSimpleFont.to_unicode` dropped upstream's Identity-as-`/ToUnicode` fixup: REAL bug fixed.** Built `oracle/probes/PdSimpleFontFuzzProbe.java`, which drives `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDSimpleFont` over 17 malformed simple-font dicts and projects, per code, the resolved encoding class, `getGlyphList()` flavour (AGL vs ZapfDingbats), the encoding's glyph name, and `toUnicode(code)`. The matrix covers: named encodings (WinAnsi/Standard) feeding `toUnicode`; `/Differences` glyph names that are synthetic `uniXXXX` / `uXXXXXX` names, a standard `Euro`, an AGL-absent name, explicit `.notdef`, a malformed `uniXXXXzz`, and a dotted-suffix `A.sc`; `/ToUnicode` CMap override + fallback; `/ToUnicode` of the wrong COS type (a `COSName`, a `COSInteger`); `/ToUnicode` filling an encoding `.notdef` slot; the `getGlyphList()` flavour for Standard-14 `Symbol`/`ZapfDingbats` (incl. a ZapfDingbats with an ignored non-embedded name encoding); and no `/Encoding` on a non-embedded Standard 14. **Real bug fixed** in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_simple_font.py`: `PDSimpleFont.to_unicode` inlined `cmap.to_unicode(code)` for the `/ToUnicode` step, which silently dropped the base `PDFont.to_unicode` Identity fixup (PDFBOX-3123/4322/3550). A `/ToUnicode` that is an Identity CMap — a `COSName` `/Identity-H`/`/Identity-V` or a stream whose CMap has no Unicode mappings — must map each code to itself (`chr(code)`); pypdfbox instead fell through to the encoding glyph (e.g. code `0x80` → `U+20AC` instead of `U+0080`). Now delegates the CMap step to `super().to_unicode(code)` exactly as upstream `PDSimpleFont.toUnicode` does (which calls `super.toUnicode` first), so the fixup fires. All 17 cases now match upstream byte-for-byte (uniXXXX→codepoint, dotted-suffix→base glyph, AGL-absent/.notdef→null, ToUnicode override/fallback, Symbol→AGL, ZapfDingbats→Zapf list). Parity locked in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_pd_simple_font_fuzz_wave1533.py`; zero remaining divergences.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDSimpleFont#toUnicode` / `PDFont#loadUnicodeCmap`

- **Wave 1532 — `PDXObject.create_x_object` /Subtype dispatch fuzz: fixed two real divergences (both removals of a divergence) + pinned the `Unexpected object type` Java-FQN message both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/XObjectDispatchFuzzProbe.java`, which drives `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.PDXObject.createXObject(COSBase, PDResources)` over 28 cases and projects the dispatched concrete class (`PDImageXObject`/`PDFormXObject`/`PDTransparencyGroup`/`PDPostScriptXObject`), `null`, or the thrown `IOException` message. **Fixed two real divergences** in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/pd_x_object.py`: (1) `/Subtype` was read via `get_name` (name-only), but upstream reads `COSStream.getNameAsString(SUBTYPE)`, so a `/Subtype` stored as a `COSString` `(Image)` dispatches to `PDImageXObject` exactly as a name `/Image` would — pypdfbox previously rejected the string and raised; now uses `get_name_as_string`. (2) The `Invalid XObject Subtype: …` message rendered an absent/non-name subtype as Python `None`, while upstream's Java string concatenation renders a null name as the literal `null`; the message now emits `null` (this is plain formatting, not a Java FQN, so exactly alignable). Two stale assertions that pinned the old `…Subtype: None` text were retargeted to the oracle-proven `…Subtype: null`. The Form→`/Group /S /Transparency` sub-dispatch (transparency vs other vs missing-`/S` vs non-dict vs null vs indirect-group vs `/S`-as-string), the null/non-stream/indirect-stream base cases, and the `null`-return on a null base all already matched. Parity pinned in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/oracle/test_xobject_dispatch_fuzz_wave1532.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.PDXObject#createXObject`
- **Wave 1532 — `PDXObject.create_x_object` non-stream-base `Unexpected object type` message uses the Python short class name, upstream uses the Java fully-qualified name; pinned both-sides.** Upstream's `IOException("Unexpected object type: " + base.getClass().getName())` embeds `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSDictionary`; pypdfbox emits the short name `COSDictionary` (Python class objects carry no `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.` package prefix). Unalignable Java idiom; the oracle test strips the package prefix from the Java side before comparing the simple class name both runtimes produce. Pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/oracle/test_xobject_dispatch_fuzz_wave1532.py` (`base-dict`/`base-name`/`base-int`/`base-array`/`base-ind-stream` cases).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.PDXObject#createXObject`
  reason: Java `Class.getName()` returns the package-qualified name; Python has no equivalent package prefix, so only the simple class name is reproducible.
- **Wave 1532 (agent A) — typed `PDNameTreeNode` subclass leaf value-wrapping fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7. EmbeddedFiles is byte-exact (parity-lock); the JavaScript str-body divergence is re-pinned BOTH sides with its full malformed-leaf cascade; no production change, zero real bugs.** Built `oracle/probes/TypedNameTreeNodeFuzzProbe.java`, which drives the per-subclass `convertCOSToPD` of the two typed name-tree subclasses that exist in the 3.0.7 jar — `PDJavascriptNameTreeNode` (→ `PDActionJavaScript`) and `PDEmbeddedFilesNameTreeNode` (→ `PDComplexFileSpecification`) — over a malformed leaf battery (Python `null` / `COSNull` / `COSString` / `COSName` / `COSInteger` / `COSArray` / empty dict / well-formed dict / dict with wrong `/S` / dict missing `/S` / dict missing `/JS` / `/JS`-as-stream), projecting the wrapped object's class or the thrown exception. **EmbeddedFiles matches upstream byte-for-byte** and is parity-locked: a Python `null` leaf (the `[String, null]` `testNullEmbeddedFile` shape) yields a `PDComplexFileSpecification` around a fresh dict; a `COSNull` value or any non-dict (`COSString`/`COSName`/`COSInteger`/`COSArray`) raises (upstream `IOException` → pypdfbox `OSError`); a dict yields the wrapper and round-trips by identity. **JavaScript is the documented `PDNameTreeNode[str]` divergence**, re-pinned both sides here with its full cascade: upstream wraps via `PDActionFactory.createAction` + cast to `PDActionJavaScript`, so a `/S != /JavaScript` dict throws `ClassCastException`, a dict with no `/S` (and the empty dict) returns `null`, and a `/S=/JavaScript` dict with no `/JS` body still wraps fine; pypdfbox instead reads the `/JS` body as a Python `str` (decoding a `/JS` stream too), so it ignores `/S` and returns the body for the wrong-`/S` case, and raises `OSError` for the no-`/JS`/empty-dict cases (no body to read). Non-dict JS leaves raise on both sides. The six pypdfbox-only subclasses (`PDURLSNameTreeNode`/`PDIDSNameTreeNode`/`PDPagesNameTreeNode`/`PDTemplatesNameTreeNode`/`PDRenditionsNameTreeNode`/`PDAlternatePresentationsNameTreeNode`) have **no upstream class in the 3.0.7 jar** and are hand-tested for convert-pair consistency (dict-typed: identity passthrough + identity round trip, `OSError` on any non-dict leaf; `PDIDSNameTreeNode`: `COSString`↔`bytes` round trip, `OSError` on any non-string leaf). 54 tests in `tests/pdmodel/oracle/test_typed_name_tree_node_fuzz_wave1532.py` (incl. 1 live differential that re-asserts the upstream projection so an oracle-jar swap can't silently move the both-sides pin).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDJavascriptNameTreeNode#convertCOSToPD` / `PDEmbeddedFilesNameTreeNode#convertCOSToPD`
  reason: `PDJavascriptNameTreeNode` leaf type is the Python `str` JS source body (read from `/JS`), not the `PDActionJavaScript` wrapper upstream returns via `PDActionFactory` — a pre-existing documented design choice (`PDNameTreeNode[str]`); EmbeddedFiles and the pypdfbox-only dict/bytes subclasses carry no divergence.
- **Wave 1530 — Launch / URI action accessor fuzz: fixed one real `PDURIDictionary.get_base` divergence (removal of a divergence) + pinned the upstream `setOperation` /D-vs-/O bug both-sides.** Built `oracle/probes/LaunchUriActionFuzzProbe.java`, which drives the typed accessors of `PDActionLaunch` (`get_file`/`get_f`/`get_d`/`get_o`/`get_p`, `get_win_launch_params`, `get_open_in_new_window` `OpenMode` tri-state), `PDWindowsLaunchParams` (`get_filename`/`get_directory`/`get_operation`/`get_execute_param`), `PDActionURI` (`get_uri` text-decode, `should_track_mouse_position`), and `PDURIDictionary` (`get_base`) over a 39-case malformed corpus saved to one `corpus.pdf`. **Fixed one real divergence** in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/action/pd_uri_dictionary.py` `PDURIDictionary.get_base`: it had decoded a non-BOM `/Base` byte string as UTF-8 (via `_decode_uri_cos_string`, shared with `PDActionURI.getURI`) and added a `COSName` fall-through that returned the name text. Upstream `PDURIDictionary.getBase` is plain `COSDictionary.getString(Base)` → only a `COSString` decodes (PDFDocEncoding / UTF-16-BOM via `COSString.getString`) and a `COSName` (or any other shape) returns `null`. For `/Base = (https://x/caf<0xC3><0xA9>/)` pypdfbox emitted the UTF-8 `…café/`; PDFBox emits the PDFDocEncoding `…cafÃ©/`; for a name `/Base` pypdfbox returned the name, PDFBox returns `null`. `get_base` now delegates to `get_string(Base)`, matching PDFBox byte-for-byte (the `get_base_as_cos_string` raw-inspection helper is unchanged). Five stale unit tests that pinned the old UTF-8/name contract were retargeted to the oracle-proven PDFDocEncoding / `None` values. The launch / URI / `OpenMode` tri-state cases all already matched. Parity pinned in `tests/pdmodel/interactive/action/oracle/test_launch_uri_action_fuzz_wave1530.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.action.PDURIDictionary#getBase`
- **Wave 1530 — `PDWindowsLaunchParams.set_operation` writes `/O` (correct), whereas upstream `setOperation` writes `/D` (a latent bug); pinned both-sides.** Upstream `PDWindowsLaunchParams.setOperation(String)` mistakenly stores the value under `COSName.D` (the working-directory key), not `COSName.O`, so a `setOperation("print")` followed by `getOperation()` (which reads `/O`) falls through to the `"open"` default and the written value is stranded on `/D`. pypdfbox's `set_operation` correctly writes `/O`, so a round-trip returns the value set — a deliberate correctness divergence from the upstream bug. Pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/interactive/action/oracle/test_launch_uri_action_fuzz_wave1530.py` (`setop_*` cases: pypdfbox `afterset_o=print raw_O=str raw_D=null`; PDFBox leaves the value on `/D` so `getOperation` reads `/O` → `afterset_o=open` with `raw_D=str`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.action.PDWindowsLaunchParams#setOperation`
  reason: upstream `setOperation` targets `COSName.D` instead of `COSName.O` (round-trip with `getOperation` is broken); pypdfbox stores `/O` so the setter/getter pair is self-consistent.
- **Wave 1530 — `TrueTypeCollection` (.ttc) malformed collection-header fuzz: fixed one real divergence (removal of a divergence) + pinned one library-gap divergence.** Built `oracle/probes/TrueTypeCollectionFuzzProbe.java`, which constructs `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.TrueTypeCollection(File)` over a fuzzed `.ttc` container and projects (public API only — `getFontAtIndex`/`numFonts` are private upstream) `ok`, the count + postscript names visited by `processAllFonts`, and whether `getFontByName("__nope__")` returns null. The 18-case battery covers wrong scaler magic (not `ttcf`), empty input, truncation before/within `numFonts` and the offset array, `numFonts == 0`, `numFonts` over the upstream 1..1024 bound, a `numFonts` whose offset array runs past EOF, and a font offset pointing past EOF / into the header / to non-SFNT bytes — all of which already matched PDFBox byte-for-byte. **Fixed one real divergence** in `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/true_type_collection.py`: FontBox reads the 4-byte TTC version via `read32Fixed` and the only decision it drives is `version >= 2` (consume the trailing DSIG tag/length/offset); it never gates on the version DWORD being one of the two canonical values, so a header carrying `0x00000000` / `0xFFFFFFFF` / any other version still parses and yields its fonts. pypdfbox re-materialises the container for fontTools' `TTCollection` slicer, which asserts `version in (0x00010000, 0x00020000)` and crashed (`AssertionError: unrecognized TTC version`). New `TrueTypeCollection._normalise_ttc_version` rewrites the version DWORD to the canonical value matching the DSIG decision pypdfbox already made (it had already parsed/consumed the real version + DSIG fields in the header parse) before handing the bytes to fontTools, so the spurious version gate FontBox does not impose no longer leaks through — `version0`/`version_huge` now match PDFBox. 18-case parity + a version-fix regression guard pinned in `tests/fontbox/ttf/oracle/test_true_type_collection_fuzz_wave1530.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.TrueTypeCollection` (private constructor TTC header parse, lines 70-98)
