- **Wave 1530 — `TrueTypeCollection` DSIG-shifted v2 header: fontTools tolerates a layout FontBox rejects (pinned both-sides, library-gap).** A `version == 2` header that carries the 6 trailing DSIG bytes shifts the SFNT payload: FontBox reads `version == 2` → consumes the three DSIG shorts → then the per-font offset lands on the now-misaligned payload and `processAllFonts` throws. pypdfbox parses TTC fonts through fontTools' `TTCollection` (library-first per CLAUDE.md), whose container reader re-derives the per-font slices with its own offset interpretation and tolerates the layout, yielding a font with a null postscript name. Matching FontBox here would mean abandoning fontTools' container reader and re-slicing every SFNT by hand off the already-cached offsets — out of scope and against the library-first rule. Same eager-validation-vs-lazy-tolerance root cause as the wave-1506 TTF lazy/eager library-gap. Pinned both-sides in `tests/fontbox/ttf/oracle/test_true_type_collection_fuzz_wave1530.py::test_true_type_collection_dsig_shifted_library_gap`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.TrueTypeCollection` (`version >= 2` DSIG consumption + per-font offset seek)
  reason: per-font SFNT slicing is delegated to fontTools' `TTCollection` (library-first); fontTools' offset interpretation tolerates a DSIG-shifted layout that FontBox's sequential read rejects.
- **Wave 1530 — `PDOptionalContentMembershipDictionary` (OCMD) malformed `/OCGs` / `/P` / `/VE` fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (no divergence).** Built `oracle/probes/OcmdFuzzProbe.java`, which loads one PDF per case (the fuzzed OCMD installed as the first page's resource `/Properties /MC1`) and projects the wrapper class, `getOCGs().size()` + each entry's name (or wrapper class name for a non-OCG member), `getVisibilityPolicy().getName()`, and the `/VE` shape (top-array operator name / `absent` / `scalar` / `empty` / `noop`). The 38-case battery covers `/OCGs` absent, a single OCG dict, a single non-OCG dict (nested OCMD / plain — still counted as 1 via `singletonList(PDPropertyList.create(...))`), an array of OCGs, an array with non-dict members (skipped), an array of non-OCG dicts (wrapped by class), and `/OCGs` as a non-dict/non-array scalar (→ empty list); `/P` absent (default `AnyOn`), each spec name, an unknown name (`Bogus`, returned verbatim), an empty name, and non-name values (string/int/array — all fall back to `AnyOn`); `/VE` absent, `[/And ...]`/`[/Or ...]`/`[/Not ...]` (incl. nested), an unknown operator (`Xor`, reported verbatim — the wrapper does not validate), an empty array, a non-name head, and non-array scalars; and `/Type` absent / wrong (→ plain `PDPropertyList`) / `/OCG` (→ `PDOptionalContentGroup`). **All 38 cases match Apache PDFBox 3.0.7 byte-for-byte — no production divergence; no fix needed.** Confirmed from the 3.0.7 bytecode that `getVisibilityPolicy` is `getCOSName(P, ANY_ON)` (no `VisibilityPolicy.valueOf` enum lookup — an unknown `/P` name does **not** throw) and that the single-dict `/OCGs` branch wraps via `PDPropertyList.create` without an OCG-type filter; `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/optionalcontent/pd_optional_content_membership_dictionary.py` (`get_ocgs_property_list` / `get_visibility_policy_name` / `get_visibility_expression`) already mirrors all three exactly. 38-case parity pinned in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/optionalcontent/oracle/test_ocmd_fuzz_wave1530.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.optionalcontent.PDOptionalContentMembershipDictionary` (`getOCGs` / `getVisibilityPolicy` / `/VE`)
- **Wave 1529 — `/MK` appearance-characteristics malformed-entry fuzz surfaced + fixed a `PDColor` colour-component positional-alignment bug (removal of a divergence).** Built `oracle/probes/AppearanceCharacteristicsFuzzProbe.java`, which drives `PDAppearanceCharacteristicsDictionary` over fuzzed `/MK` dictionaries and projects `getRotation`, `getBorderColour`/`getBackground` (colour-space name + `getComponents`), the three captions, and the three typed icons. **Fixed one real divergence** in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/pd_color.py` `PDColor._parse_cos_array`: a non-numeric colour component in the *middle* of a `/BC`//`/BG` array was dropped and the later numeric components were packed left, whereas upstream `PDColor.initComponents` writes by index into a pre-zeroed `float[]`, so a skipped component leaves a `0.0` *at that index*. For `/BC = [0.1 /X 0.3]` pypdfbox emitted `DeviceRGB[0.1, 0.3, 0.0]`; Apache PDFBox emits `DeviceRGB[0.1, 0.0, 0.3]`. Now appends a `0.0` placeholder for each skipped index, preserving positional alignment (a too-short tail is still padded by `get_components`), matching PDFBox byte-for-byte. The remaining 30 cases (`/R` int/float/name/string/45/360/neg/huge, colour arrays of arity 0/1/2/3/4/5, `/BC` not-an-array/null/dict/indirect, wrong-typed captions, icon stream/dict/name/null/indirect) already matched. The stale wave-257 unit `test_parse_cos_array_warns_on_non_numeric_component` was retargeted to the oracle-proven `[0.5, 0.0, 0.25]`. 31-case parity pinned in `tests/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/oracle/test_appearance_characteristics_fuzz_wave1529.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.interactive.annotation.PDAppearanceCharacteristicsDictionary`, `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.color.PDColor#initComponents`
- **Wave 1529 — `PDFontDescriptor` malformed-dictionary leniency fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (no divergence).** Built `oracle/probes/FontDescriptorFuzzProbe.java`, which builds deliberately malformed font-descriptor `COSDictionary`s in memory and projects every `/Flags` bit-predicate, the typed `getFontBoundingBox`, the full 12-entry numeric-metric block, `getFontName`/`getFontFamily`/`getFontStretch`/`getCharSet`, and `/FontFile`/`/FontFile2`/`/FontFile3` presence. The 29-case battery covers `/Flags` as float (incl. truncate-toward-zero), string, bool, a huge int past the signed-32 range (wraps), and negative (all bits — reserved bits must not leak into named predicates); `/FontBBox` short (2/3), over-long (5), non-numeric (coerced to 0), and non-array (→ `None`); metrics missing / non-numeric / integer-typed; PDFBOX-429 negative `/CapHeight`+`/XHeight` (read back absolute); `/FontName` string-vs-int; `/FontStretch` string; `/FontWeight` non-numeric; `/CharSet` string-vs-name. **All 29 cases match Apache PDFBox 3.0.7 byte-for-byte — no production divergence; no fix needed.** `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_font_descriptor.py` already mirrors the Java `getInt`/`getFloat`/`getNameAsString` coercion + signed-32 bit-extraction semantics exactly. Parity pinned in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_font_descriptor_fuzz_wave1529.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDFontDescriptor`
- **Wave 1529 — pdmodel `PDType1Font` Standard-14 / AFM-backed metric surface fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (no fix; `getWidth` confirmed at full parity).** Built `oracle/probes/PdType1FontFuzzProbe.java`, which drives the *pdmodel*-level `PDType1Font` (distinct from the fontbox-level `Type1Font*` PFB/charstring probes) over non-embedded font dicts — Helvetica / Times-Roman / Symbol / ZapfDingbats / Courier-BoldOblique bare dicts, a `/Widths` override, a `/FirstChar`//`/LastChar` short-array mismatch, an unknown base-font name, `/Encoding` as WinAnsi/MacRoman/Standard names, a `/Differences` dict overlay, and a missing `/FontDescriptor` — and projects `getWidth`, `getWidthFromFont`, `isStandard14`, `isEmbedded`, the typed `getEncoding()` class, and `getEncoding().getName(code)` over 11 probe codes each. **`getWidth(code)` — the layout-driving advance — matches Apache PDFBox byte-for-byte for every Standard-14 font across all encodings, the `/Widths` override, and the `/FirstChar`//`/LastChar` mismatch; `isStandard14`/`isEmbedded`/encoding-class/glyph-name all match. No production change (zero real bugs).** Two systematic divergences are pinned BOTH-sides (next bullet). 5 tests / 143-code corpus in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_pd_type1_font_fuzz_wave1529.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font.getWidth` / `getStandard14Width` / `getWidthFromFont`
- **Wave 1529 — `PDType1Font.getWidthFromFont` returns the bundled Adobe AFM advance (not a machine-dependent substitute-program advance) for non-embedded fonts (pinned both-sides).** Upstream `PDType1Font.getWidthFromFont(code)` reads the *substitute* FontBox program the platform `FontMapper` loads for a non-embedded font (e.g. Helvetica → a system Liberation/Arial face), so it returns that program's scaled advance (`666.9922` for `A`, `744.1406` for the WinAnsi `Euro`); for an unknown non-Standard-14 name `getWidth` itself follows the same substitute path and reports its advances. pypdfbox deliberately keeps machine-dependent substitute-program resolution in the renderer (see DEFERRED.md wave 1488: "substitute-GID resolution in the renderer (documented divergence)"), so `getWidthFromFont` instead returns the bundled Adobe AFM *integer* advance for the resolved glyph (`667.0` for `A`), or `0.0` when the AFM lacks the glyph (e.g. `sfthyphen`/`Euro` under some encodings) or the font is the unknown non-Standard-14 name (no AFM). The integer magnitude agrees; only the sub-unit substitute remainder (and the AFM-absent glyphs / unknown font) differ. Pinned both-sides in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_pd_type1_font_fuzz_wave1529.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font.getWidthFromFont`
  reason: substitute-font (FontMapper) program loading is machine-dependent and intentionally lives in the renderer, not the model layer; the deterministic AFM advance is used for non-embedded Standard-14 widths.
- **Wave 1528 — `PDCIDFont` `/W` `/W2` `/DW` `/DW2` width-table parsing fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7.** Built `oracle/probes/CidFontWidthFuzzProbe.java`, which builds malformed descendant-CIDFont dictionaries in memory (wrapped in a minimal Identity-H `PDType0Font` so `codeToCID(code) == code`) and projects `getWidth`/`hasExplicitWidth`/`getVerticalDisplacementVectorY`/`getPositionVector` + the `/DW` default across both `/W` run forms (`c [w1 w2 ...]` list, `c1 c2 w` range), mixed forms, and a malformed-run battery (non-numeric c/w, array-vs-number swaps, odd trailing tokens, premature end, `c2 < c1`, negative/huge CIDs, `null` entries, overlapping runs). **Fixed one real divergence** in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_cid_font.py`: `get_position_vector` treated its argument as an already-resolved CID and skipped the `code -> CID` step, whereas upstream `PDCIDFont.getPositionVector(int code)` calls `codeToCID(code)` first (confirmed against the 3.0.7 bytecode) — so for a code whose CMap mapping is not the identity (exposed by a negative probe code) the default position-vector `v_x = widthForCID(cid)/2` was computed from the wrong CID. Now resolves `code -> CID` first, matching `get_width`/`get_vertical_displacement_vector_y`. The well-formed `/W` list/range parsing, `/DW` fallback (missing/non-numeric → 1000), and `/DW2` defaults already matched. Four malformed-`/W2`/`/DW2` cases are pinned BOTH sides (next bullet). 31 cases (27 byte-exact parity + 4 pinned) in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_cid_font_width_fuzz_wave1528.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDCIDFont.getPositionVector(int)` / `readWidths` / `readVerticalDisplacements`
- **Wave 1528 — malformed `/W2` / one-element `/DW2` leniency (pinned both-sides).** Apache PDFBox 3.0.7's `PDCIDFont.readVerticalDisplacements` (run from the CIDFont constructor) casts `/W2` elements to `COSNumber` *without* an `instanceof` guard and reads `/DW2` `getObject(0)`/`getObject(1)` unconditionally, so a `/W2` with a non-number leading CID or a non-number range metric throws `ClassCastException`, a `/W2` whose inner triple array is ragged (size not divisible by 3) throws `IndexOutOfBoundsException`, and a one-element `/DW2` throws `IndexOutOfBoundsException` — all from font construction, which propagates out of `new PDType0Font(...)`. pypdfbox guards every access in `_parse_w2_array` / `get_default_position_vector`, so construction succeeds and the malformed `/W2` simply contributes no vertical metrics (falls back to `/DW2` defaults). Deliberate hostile-producer robustness: one broken `/W2` run must not abort loading the whole composite font. Pinned both-sides (4 cases: `dw2_short`, `w2_nonnumeric_c`, `w2_ragged_inner`, `w2_range_nonnumeric`) in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_cid_font_width_fuzz_wave1528.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDCIDFont.readVerticalDisplacements`
  reason: hostile/buggy-producer robustness — a malformed `/W2`/`/DW2` must not crash font construction.
- **Wave 1528 — `/DCTDecode` (JPEG) decode fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`DCTFilter.decode`).** A 30-case DCT-specific differential (probe `oracle/probes/DctDecodeFuzzProbe.java`) confirms byte-for-byte parity (identical decoded-raster `len` + `sha`) across the malformed/forged JPEG surface: empty / SOI-only / garbage / junk-prefix / double-SOI / trailing-garbage, truncated baseline / grayscale / progressive scans, grayscale / RGB / CMYK / Adobe-APP14-stripped-CMYK / progressive variants, `/DecodeParms ColorTransform` 0 vs 1 (upstream's DCTFilter ignores it and relies on the JPEG markers — both engines wrap libjpeg-turbo), and corrupt / zero-dimension SOF (both throw). One genuine **library gap** is pinned both-sides, not fixed: a forged maximum-width SOF (declared 0xFFFF on an 8×8 scan) makes pypdfbox's imagecodecs/libjpeg-turbo path pad the exhausted scan out to the declared geometry and return success, whereas Java ImageIO (PDFBox) — and, independently, Pillow — reject the premature end-of-data and throw. A milder forged-oversize SOF that both sides tolerate (e.g. 8×128) yields the same decoded length but engine-dependent padding bytes (hence a differing `sha`). No clean invariant separates the forged-oversize case from a genuinely valid highly-compressible large image (a solid-colour 2000×2000 JPEG has the same decoded/encoded size ratio), and Pillow itself diverges from PDFBox in the opposite direction on a barely-truncated tail (rejects a 99%-truncated stream PDFBox accepts), so forcing parity would introduce a *new* divergence. Pinned in `tests/filter/oracle/test_dct_decode_fuzz_wave1528.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.filter.DCTFilter#decode`
- **Wave 1528 — `PDFStreamParser` now recovers a partial array/dictionary operand at EOF instead of raising (removal of a divergence).** The content-stream parser inherits `COSParser`'s `parse_cos_array`/`parse_cos_dictionary`, which raise `unterminated array`/`unterminated dictionary` when the closing `]`/`>>` is missing before EOF — correct for the strict document loader, but upstream's content-stream path (`org.apache.pdfbox.pdfparser.PDFStreamParser`, which inherits `BaseParser.parseCOSArray`/`parseCOSDictionary`) is lenient: at EOF it returns the elements / name-value pairs gathered so far. Previously a content stream ending mid-container (`[1 2 3` or `<< /A 1 /B 2`) was caught by `parse_next_token`'s `except PDFParseError: close(); return None`, dropping the operand AND closing the parser. `PDFStreamParser` now overrides both methods to return the partial container at EOF, matching Apache PDFBox token-for-token. The document-loader strictness in `COSParser` is untouched. Oracle: `oracle/probes/StreamParserFuzzProbe.java` (43-case token-sequence corpus), pinned in `tests/pdfparser/oracle/test_stream_parser_fuzz_wave1528.py`; the two stale unit tests that pinned the old stop-and-close behaviour were retargeted in `tests/pdfparser/test_pdf_stream_parser.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdfparser.PDFStreamParser#parseNextToken` (via `org.apache.pdfbox.pdfparser.BaseParser#parseCOSArray`/`parseCOSDictionary`)
- **Wave 1528 — `COSWriter.visit_from_stream` now hoists `/Length` to the FIRST stream-dictionary entry (removal of a divergence).** Upstream `COSStream()` (PDFBox 3.0.7) seeds `setInt(COSName.LENGTH, 0)` in its constructor, so `/Length` is always the leading dictionary key; the parser updates that seeded entry IN PLACE, so even a source file that spells `/Type` before `/Length` round-trips as `<< /Length N /Type ... >>` (oracle-verified). pypdfbox's `COSStream` deliberately carries no `/Length` until the body is committed (the absent state is load-bearing — `get_filtered_stream_length()`/`get_length()` rely on it, and the parser commit hooks key off it), so a freshly-built or parsed stream could emit `/Length` in a trailing position. The writer now removes and re-inserts `/Length` first at serialization time, matching PDFBox byte-for-byte without touching the model's `/Length`-absent invariant. Surfaced and pinned by the per-object writer fuzz oracle `oracle/probes/CosWriterObjectFuzzProbe.java` (84-case battery: number/float/bool/null/string/array/dict/stream serialization) in `tests/pdfwriter/oracle/test_cos_writer_object_fuzz_wave1528.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdfwriter.COSWriter#visitFromStream`, `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSStream#<init>`
- **Wave 1526 — invented `PDPageFitBoundingBox*` destination subclasses removed to restore exact PDFBox-3.0.7 structural parity.** A very early wave (35) wrongly invented `PDPageFitBoundingBoxDestination` / `PDPageFitBoundingBoxWidthDestination` / `PDPageFitBoundingBoxHeightDestination`; Apache PDFBox 3.0.7 has NO such classes. The `/FitB`, `/FitBH`, `/FitBV` bounded-fit types are represented by the SAME real classes (`PDPageFitDestination` / `PDPageFitWidthDestination` / `PDPageFitHeightDestination`) via each class's `TYPE_BOUNDED` flag (`"FitB"`/`"FitBH"`/`"FitBV"`) and `set_fit_bounding_box(True)`. `PDDestination.create()` already dispatched the bounded types to the real classes. The three invented class files and their three dedicated unit-test files were deleted, all consumers retargeted to the real class + bounded flag, and the upstream-collapse remapping in the destination-type oracle removed (the Python class name now equals the upstream Java class name with no remapping). This is a *removal of a divergence* — pypdfbox's destination class set now matches upstream exactly.
- **Wave 1526 — `PDOutlineItem.get_destination` over a malformed `/Dest` no longer diverges.** Wave 1519 made `PDDestination.create` propagate the conversion `OSError`, so the previously-pinned `outline:dest_bad_array|dest_bad_dict|dest_bad_int` cases now CONVERGE with upstream (both raise `IOException`). Removed from the `_KNOWN_DIVERGENCES`/`_PYTHON_TOLERANT` pins in `tests/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/oracle/test_doc_nav_fuzz_oracle.py`; they are compared directly now.
- **Wave 1526/1527 — `page_float` outline-destination divergence (pinned both-sides), and a CORRECTION of its diagnosis.** Under shared-tree reuse (an out-of-bounds outline lookup immediately preceding a float `/D[0]` lookup on the same document), pypdfbox raises `RuntimeError("Possible recursion found")` for `page_float` while upstream returns the page. The wave-1526 explanation ("upstream allocates a fresh guard set per top-level `get()`") was **WRONG**: wave 1527 decompiled PDFBox 3.0.7 `PDPageTree.get(int,COSDictionary,int)` and confirmed upstream uses a **per-instance `pageSet` field** (cleared only on the recursion path and on leaf-success) — which pypdfbox's `PDPageTree` already mirrors faithfully. Changing pypdfbox to a fresh-set-per-call DIVERGES (it flips the malformed-`/Count` count-gate cases P04/P05/P16/P23 in `test_page_tree_cycle_fuzz_wave1520` from upstream's `IllegalStateException` to `IndexError`), so that change was reverted. The real cause is in the destination page-resolution path (`PDOutlineItem.find_destination_page` / `retrieve_page_number`), not the page-tree guard set; tracked in DEFERRED.md (pdmodel/page) for a dedicated investigation. Pinned both-sides in `test_outline_destination_fuzz_wave1519.py` until then.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageTree#get(int)`, `PDOutlineItem#findDestinationPage`
- **Wave 1525 — contentstream text-state / text-positioning OPERATOR operand handling fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`SetCharSpacing` / `SetWordSpacing` / `SetTextLeading` / `SetTextHorizontalScaling` / `SetTextRise` / `SetTextRenderingMode` / `SetFontAndSize` / `MoveText` / `MoveTextSetLeading` / `SetMatrix` / `NextLine`).** Built `TextStateOperatorFuzzProbe.java`, which calls each operator processor's `process(Operator, List)` DIRECTLY with hand-built malformed operand windows (empty / too-few / too-many operands, `COSName`/`COSString`/`COSArray`/`COSNull` where a number is expected, out-of-range `Tr` modes, zero/negative `Tz`) — direct invocation is required because `PDFStreamEngine.processOperator` swallows `MissingOperandException` into `operatorException`, hiding the arity/type contract. The probe projects per case `ERR:<class>` or `OK|<text-state fingerprint>` (Tc/Tw/TL/Tz/Ts/Tr/Tf + text matrix) so a silent-ignore is distinguishable from an applied mutation. **Fixed one real divergence** in `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/text/set_matrix.py` (`Tm`): pypdfbox type-guarded only `operands[:6]` for `COSNumber`-ness, while upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.state.SetMatrix` calls `checkArrayTypesClass(operands, COSNumber.class)` over the WHOLE operand list — so a malformed `a b c d e f /Name Tm` (seven operands, trailing non-number) made pypdfbox APPLY the matrix while upstream silently drops it. Changed to `check_array_types_class(operands, COSNumber)` over the full list. Confirmed against the 3.0.7 bytecode that the single-operand setters match exactly (`Tc` uses `arguments.get(size-1)` / empty→throw; `Tw` and `Ts` empty→silent-return; `TL`/`Tz`/`Tr` empty→`MissingOperandException`; `Tr` range-check `<0 || >= RenderingMode.values().length`→return; `Tf`/`Td`/`TD` `<2`→throw). 67 cases (66 byte-exact parity + 1 Tm regression pin) in `tests/contentstream/oracle/test_text_state_operator_fuzz_wave1525.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.text.*` and `org.apache.pdfbox.contentstream.operator.state.SetMatrix`
  note: pypdfbox's text-state notification hooks (`set_character_spacing` etc.) are cluster-#2 no-ops by design — Tc/Tw/TL/Ts/Tr/Tf state-tracking lives in the rendering cluster, not the base engine, so the fuzz harness records the mutations through a test-only recording engine. `MoveTextSetLeading` (`TD`) carries a deliberate divergence already noted in source: upstream always re-enters via `processOperator("TL", ...)` (a no-op when no `TL` handler is registered), whereas pypdfbox falls back to a direct `set_text_leading` when `TL` is unregistered so subclasses still observe the change; in the realistic engine config (TL registered) both paths set the leading identically and the probe confirms parity.
- **Wave 1525 — TrueType `glyf` GLYPH-DECODE fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`GlyfSimpleDescript` / `GlyfCompositeDescript` / `GlyfCompositeComp`).** Built `GlyfDecodeFuzzProbe.java`, which splices one HOSTILE glyph's bytes into the bundled `DejaVuSans.ttf` in place (padded/truncated to the original length so `loca` and all downstream tables stay valid) and projects the decoded `numberOfContours` / resolved contour count / point count / bbox (or an error/`null` marker) per gid. **No production change** — `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/glyph_data.py` decodes the `glyf` payload through fontTools (library-first per CLAUDE.md), and every divergence found is rooted in fontTools' strict decoder vs FontBox's deliberately lenient hand-rolled reader, so matching them would mean reimplementing the byte-level glyf readers (out of scope, same precedent as the wave-1505 CCITT and wave-1506 lazy-vs-eager TTF-parse library-gaps). TEN mutations where the two engines agree on the full projection are pinned as exact-match parity (empty glyph `numberOfContours == 0`, all-zero glyf bytes, header-only truncation, non-monotonic `endPtsOfContours`, truncation to the 2-byte count, a clean composite, `MORE_COMPONENTS` set with no follower, a self-referencing component that borrows the half-built outline, an out-of-range component glyph index, all-zero composite bytes). NINE library-gap mutations are pinned BOTH-SIDES as divergences (negative `numberOfContours` other than -1, which FontBox treats as composite while fontTools rejects; simple- and composite-glyph streams truncated mid coordinate/component, which FontBox zero-pads while fontTools raises; a true cyclic composite, which FontBox detects and returns a zero-point outline while fontTools errors; huge / `Integer.MAX` contour counts, where both fail at different stages). Every mutant TERMINATES on both engines (no hang/crash). 20 cases in `tests/fontbox/ttf/oracle/test_glyf_decode_fuzz_wave1525.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.GlyphData` / `GlyfSimpleDescript` / `GlyfCompositeDescript` / `GlyfCompositeComp`
- **Wave 1524 — `StandardSecurityHandler` key-derivation / password-auth algorithms fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`computeEncryptedKey` / `isUserPassword` / `isOwnerPassword` / `getUserPassword`).** Built `StandardSecurityHandlerFuzzProbe.java` driving the instance-level algorithm methods with deterministic malformed `/O` `/U` `/OE` `/UE` byte strings, out-of-range `/R` (0/7/99), odd `/Length` (0/5/16), short/empty/over-long entries and >32-byte passwords. Fixed **three real divergences** in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/standard_security_handler.py`, all rooted in pypdfbox using `>=` revision tests where the 3.0.7 bytecode uses exact equalities: (1) `compute_encrypted_key` dispatched `revision >= 5` to the AES-256 Rev56 path, so revisions 7/99 wrongly entered Rev56 (absent `/OE` `/UE` → empty result); upstream dispatches only `== 5 || == 6` and falls every other revision through to Algorithm 2 (Rev234), which derives a key — changed to `revision in (5, 6)`. (2) `_compute_encryption_key` (Algorithm 2) ran the 50-round MD5 re-hash on `revision >= 3` and the `0xFFFFFFFF` metadata mix on `revision >= 4`; upstream `computeEncryptedKeyRev234` gates them on `== 3 || == 4` and `== 4`, so an out-of-range revision reaching Algorithm 2 now derives the plain rev-2 key. (3) `get_user_password` ran the r3/r4 RC4 unwind for any revision below 5; upstream `getUserPassword234` returns empty bytes for any revision not in {2,3,4} — guarded the whole derivation on the {2,3,4} set. Remaining divergences pinned both-sides: Java `IOException` ↔ pypdfbox `OSError` (unknown-revision validators, short r6 `/O`/`/U`), Java `IllegalArgumentException` ↔ `ValueError` (zero-length RC4 key), and Java `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` (unguarded array-slice crash on too-short `/O` / empty `/U` in the r6 path) ↔ pypdfbox's lenient slice. 55 cases (43 byte-exact parity + 12 pinned) in `tests/pdmodel/encryption/oracle/test_standard_security_handler_fuzz_wave1524.py`.
- **Wave 1524 — TrueType cmap subtable FORMAT-body parsing fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`CmapSubtable.processSubtype4` / `processSubtype6`).** Fixed two real divergences in `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/cmap_subtable.py`: both the format-4 (`processSubtype4`) and format-6 (`processSubtype6`) body readers were filtering out any glyph id `>= numGlyphs` (logging "Format N cmap contains an invalid glyph index" and dropping the mapping). Upstream PDFBox 3.0.7 has **no such num_glyphs bound** in either method — whatever the segment arithmetic / glyph-id array yields is stored verbatim (confirmed by bytecode: neither `processSubtype4` nor `processSubtype6` compares the gid against `numGlyphs`). A malformed `/idDelta` or out-of-range glyph-id-array entry therefore now keeps the resulting (possibly out-of-range) gid, matching `get_glyph_id` byte-for-byte. The other fuzzed format bodies (0, 2, 12, unknown/truncated/zero-length, odd/zero `segCountX2`, `startCode > endCode`, `endCode` not ending 0xFFFF, `idRangeOffset` OOB, huge `entryCount`, `nGroups` overflow, overlapping/surrogate format-12 groups) already matched. Five stale non-oracle tests that pinned the old filtering behaviour were retargeted to the oracle-proven contract. Pinned both-sides (20 cases) in `tests/fontbox/ttf/oracle/test_cmap_subtable_format_fuzz_wave1524.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.CmapSubtable.processSubtype4` / `processSubtype6`

- **Wave 1524 — COSDictionary typed-accessor coercion fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (no divergence; parity pinned).** Built `CosDictionaryAccessorFuzzProbe.java` (fuzz complement to `CosDictAccessorProbe.java`) driving `get_int/get_long/get_float/get_boolean/get_cos_name/get_name_as_string/get_string/get_dictionary_object/get_item` over the corners the basic probe omits: a wrong-type coercion matrix (each accessor over string / numeric-string / name / both booleans / array / sub-dict / explicit `COSNull` / absent → sentinel or default for every mismatch; `get_name_as_string` coerces a `COSString` but `get_string` never coerces a `COSName`), signed-32-bit `get_int` wrap and `f2i`/`f2l` saturation over `COSInteger`/`COSFloat` at `2**31`, `Long.MAX/MIN`, ±1e30, ±0.4, indirect-`COSObject` resolution with the two-key fallback through both direct and indirect `COSNull`, and `COSName`-key vs `str`-key overload equivalence. pypdfbox already matches Apache PDFBox byte-for-byte across the whole matrix — **no production change**; parity is pinned both-sides for regression protection in `tests/cos/oracle/test_cos_dictionary_accessor_fuzz_wave1524.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSDictionary` (`getInt`/`getLong`/`getFloat`/`getBoolean`/`getCOSName`/`getNameAsString`/`getString`/`getDictionaryObject`/`getItem` + COSName/String overloads), `COSInteger.intValue`, `COSFloat.intValue`/`longValue`

- **Wave 1523 — malformed Type 3 (stitching) function dictionaries fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`PDFunctionType3.eval`).** Rewrote `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/pd_function_type3.py::eval` as a faithful port of upstream's bytecode, fixing four real divergences: (1) the single-subfunction path now dispatches straight to `functions[0]` and interpolates over the *whole* `/Domain` into `/Encode` pair 0, ignoring `/Bounds` entirely (pypdfbox previously rejected a single function carrying any `/Bounds` with a "too many partitions" `ValueError`). (2) `/Bounds` length is never validated up front; an over/under-long `/Bounds` either selects the wrong interval or indexes past the function array (`IndexError`, mirroring upstream `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`). (3) `/Encode` is read through PDRange-equivalent COSNumber access (`_encode_pair`), so an absent / too-short / non-numeric `/Encode` pair now raises instead of silently substituting a `[0, 1]` default. (4) the input is clipped to `/Domain` with the non-normalising upstream `clipToRange(float, float, float)` (`_clip_to_range_scalar`), so a reversed `/Domain` yields the same "partition not found" failure as upstream rather than evaluating against a normalised interval. Five stale non-oracle tests retargeted to the oracle-proven contract.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.function.PDFunctionType3.eval` / `getEncodeForParameter`, `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRange`, `PDFunction.clipToRange(float, float, float)`

- **Wave 1522 — malformed Type 3 font dictionaries / char-procs fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7.** Fixed two real divergences in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type3_font.py`: (1) `get_font_bbox` required a 4+-entry numeric `/FontBBox` array and otherwise returned `None` (or raised `TypeError`/`ValueError` via `PDRectangle.from_cos_array` on short / non-numeric arrays); upstream `getFontBBox` builds a `PDRectangle` from *any* COSArray — short arrays are zero-padded to four (`Arrays.copyOf(toFloatArray(), 4)`), long arrays keep the first four, non-numeric entries coerce to `0`, and corners normalise via min/max. Now mirrored locally (returns `None` only when `/FontBBox` is absent or not an array). (2) `get_char_proc(int)` special-cased `.notdef` and returned `None` even when a `.notdef` char proc existed; upstream `getCharProc(int)` has no such filter — it resolves the code to a glyph name and looks that exact name up in `/CharProcs`. Now the `.notdef` short-circuit is removed; only a `None` name or a missing/non-stream entry yields `None`. One stale non-oracle test retargeted to the padded-bbox contract.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType3Font.getFontBBox` / `getCharProc(int)`, `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle(COSArray)`
- **Wave 1522 — Type 3 char-proc `getWidth` leniency (pinned both-sides).** `PDType3CharProc.get_width()` / `parse_width()` return `0.0` for a char proc whose leading operator is not `d0`/`d1` (a garbage proc) or whose first operand is missing/non-numeric (and the font-level `get_width(code)` likewise yields `0.0` for a no-`/Widths` garbage glyph), where upstream raises `IOException`. Deliberate robustness choice so the text-extraction / rendering loop keeps walking the font's other glyphs instead of aborting on one broken proc; the well-formed path is byte-exact. Pinned both-sides (3 cases) in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_type3_font_fuzz_wave1522.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType3CharProc.getWidth` / `parseWidth`
  reason: hostile/buggy-producer robustness — one malformed char proc must not abort the whole font.

- **Wave 1522 — malformed PDF date-string parsing fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`DateConverter.toCalendar`, the parser behind `COSDictionary.getDate`).** Fixed a real leniency bug: pypdfbox used Python `str.strip()` / `str.lstrip()` in `to_calendar`, `_try_parse_date_fallback`, and `_from_iso8601`, which strip *all* Unicode whitespace and so silently accepted tab / newline / CR / vertical-tab / form-feed bracketed inputs (e.g. `"\tD:20240315120000"`, `"D:20240315120000\t"`, `"2024-03-15T12:00:00Z\t"`) that PDFBox rejects (returns `null`). Upstream's tokenizer treats only the ASCII space (0x20) as skippable and enforces `where.index == text.length()`, so non-space whitespace fails the residue check. The parser core was already correct; the divergence was confined to the wrapper pre-strip. Now strips only spaces, matching PDFBox: a surrounding space is tolerated, any other whitespace is rejected.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.util.DateConverter.toCalendar(String)` / `parseDate` / `fromISO8601`

- **Wave 1523 — malformed LZW-compressed streams fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`LZWFilter.doLZWDecode`).** Verification-only: 42 hand-crafted bit-level LZW code streams stressing the codec's own state machine — reserved codes (`CLEAR_TABLE`/`EOD`), the 9→10→11→12 variable-width transitions, the `/EarlyChange` boundary (1 vs 0), KwKwK valid vs out-of-range codes, premature EOF (missing EOD), EOD mid-stream, no-leading-CLEAR, table growth past 4096 with no intervening CLEAR, and `/Predictor` (TIFF 2 / PNG 12) over the LZW core. pypdfbox's `LZWDecode` already matched upstream byte-for-byte on every case (lenient EOFException-as-stop contract, 258-entry initial table with null placeholders at 256/257, post-growth `calculate_chunk` width). No production change; the parity is now pinned exact (ok + len + sha) so a future LZW refactor cannot silently regress malformed-input behaviour.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.filter.LZWFilter.doLZWDecode` / `calculateChunk` / `createInitialCodeTable`

- **Wave 1523 — COSName `#XX` hex-escape decode + byte→String round-trip fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7 (`COSName.getName` / `BaseParser.parseCOSName`).** Fixed a real divergence in `pypdfbox/cos/cos_name.py::get_name`: the non-UTF-8 fallback decoded with **Latin-1**, but upstream's name decoder uses `ALTERNATIVE_CHARSET = Charset.forName("Windows-1252")` (a strict UTF-8 decoder first, then a replacing Windows-1252 decoder on failure). Latin-1 and cp1252 agree on 0x00–0x7F and 0xA0–0xFF but diverge across 0x80–0x9F: a name byte `0x80` decodes to the euro sign U+20AC (not U+0080), `0x9F` to U+0178, and the five cp1252-undefined slots (0x81/0x8D/0x8F/0x90/0x9D) to U+FFFD. `get_name` now does strict UTF-8 then `bytes.decode("cp1252", errors="replace")`, matching `new String(bytes, Windows-1252)` byte-for-byte over the full 0x00–0xFF range (both raw high bytes and `#XX` escapes). 413-case sweep (including all 256 byte values via escape + raw, multi-byte UTF-8 via consecutive `#XX`, malformed `#`-recovery, and the empty name) is now zero-divergence. One stale hand-written test (`test_cos_name_wave1368.py`) retargeted from "Latin-1" to the cp1252 contract.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName.getName`, `org.apache.pdfbox.pdfparser.BaseParser` (`ALTERNATIVE_CHARSET` = Windows-1252)

- **Wave 1521 — five malformed model-dictionary surfaces fuzzed against PDFBox 3.0.7.** Viewer-preference name getters now preserve an explicitly stored empty `COSString` instead of replacing it with the spec default. `PDFormXObject.get_b_box` and `PDThreadBead.get_rectangle` now zero-pad missing/non-numeric array coordinates like upstream `PDRectangle(COSArray)`. `PDSoftMask` now mirrors upstream lazy accessor caching, returns only transparency groups from valid `/G` XObjects, propagates malformed-XObject errors, threads the resource cache, and rejects dictionary-backed Type-4 transfer functions that require a stream. The tiling-pattern dictionary surface was already production-correct.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/viewerpreferences/PDViewerPreferences.java`, `interactive/pagenavigation/{PDThread,PDThreadBead}.java`, `graphics/form/PDFormXObject.java`, `graphics/pattern/PDTilingPattern.java`, `graphics/state/PDSoftMask.java`

- **Wave 1521 — retained malformed-model compatibility differences.** Thread-bead null `/N`/`/V` links return `None` instead of exposing PDFBox's null-backed `PDThreadBead` wrapper, and `append_bead` repairs a missing `/N` link instead of throwing `NullPointerException`. `PDTilingPattern` keeps the shared strict malformed-`/BBox` contract and permits `set_matrix(None)` as an entry-clear operation. `PDSoftMask.get_group` keeps pypdfbox compatibility for caller-built bare streams and dictionary-shaped group values where PDFBox errors. Viewer-preference `PickTrayByPDFSize`, `NumCopies`, `PrintPageRange`, and `Enforce` helpers remain pypdfbox enrichment APIs absent from PDFBox 3.0.7. Every difference is pinned with live two-sided output.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 classes listed above
  reason: preserve established pypdfbox safety/clear/enrichment contracts while making the upstream surface explicit

- **Wave 1520 — recursive/deep resource lookup and cache parity.** A 24-case live PDFBox 3.0.7 graph audit now covers direct/indirect entries, indirect chains/nulls, malformed Font/XObject/ColorSpace/Pattern/ExtGState values, nested/self-referential resource dictionaries, cache eligibility, create-over-wrong-type behavior, and page inheritance through depth 64, shadowing, wrong types, and parent cycles. `PDResources` now caches direct fonts by name, excludes context-sensitive images and Pattern color spaces from the shared cache, resolves null `has_color_space` entries as absent, and threads the cache into ExtGState wrappers; `PDPage.get_resources` now reuses its wrapper like upstream. One existing permissive-color-factory divergence remains pinned: a null color-space entry returns `None` where PDFBox raises `MissingResourceException`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDResources.java`, `PDPage.java`, `graphics/color/PDColorSpace.java`

- **Wave 1520 — embedded-file/file-spec malformed dictionaries moved to parity.** A 44-case live PDFBox 3.0.7 fuzz audit now covers wrong/null/indirect `/EF` and `/Params` values, all alternate-platform embedded-file keys, malformed `Size`/dates/`CheckSum`, subtype name/string coercion, and legacy Mac parameter accessors. `PDEmbeddedFile.get_size` now applies Java signed-`int` narrowing to wide integers, and `get_mac_res_fork` / `set_mac_res_fork` now expose upstream's string contract while the separate `*_mac_resource_fork` stream helpers remain available.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/filespecification/PDComplexFileSpecification.java`, `PDEmbeddedFile.java`

- **Wave 1520 — malformed annotation appearance dictionaries/streams pinned to parity.** Live PDFBox 3.0.7 fuzz coverage now exercises `PDAppearanceDictionary` `/N` `/R` `/D` stream vs subdictionary vs scalar/null/indirect entries, malformed state names, missing states, `PDAppearanceEntry` accessor exception messages, `PDAppearanceStream` defaults, and appearance setter storage canonicalization. The audited surface was already convergent; no production change or active divergence was added.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/annotation/PDAppearanceDictionary.java`, `PDAppearanceEntry.java`, `PDAppearanceStream.java`

- **Wave 1519 — five FDF/linearization/outline malformed-input surfaces moved toward parity.** Live PDFBox 3.0.7 fuzz probes fixed 12 grouped divergences: `COSDictionary.get_int` and `PDLinearizationDictionary.get_hint_table` now narrow wide integers like Java `int`; `FDFAnnotation.create` uses name-as-string subtype dispatch, recognizes canonical `Polyline` only, and returns `None` for unknown/missing malformed subtypes; `PDOutlineItem` propagates malformed `/Dest` conversion errors and positive out-of-range destination-page indexes; `FDFDocument.load` routes through the dedicated FDF parser, rejects non-FDF headers, and falls back to version `1.7` for malformed `%FDF-` versions; XFDF import now raises on invalid annotation `page` / hex `color` and skips bad-rectangle annotations at the dictionary layer.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSDictionary.java`, `pdmodel/fdf/FDFAnnotation.java`, `FDFDocument.java`, `FDFDictionary.java`, `pdfparser/FDFParser.java`, `pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/PDOutlineItem.java`

- **Wave 1518 — five malformed-input parse/read surfaces moved toward parity.** Live PDFBox 3.0.7 fuzz probes fixed 10 real divergences: predictor `calculateRowLength` now uses Java signed-`int` overflow and truncating division; literal strings at EOF return accumulated bytes and a trailing escape writes `0xFF`; `PDDestination.create` maps `FitB`/`FitBH`/`FitBV` to the same wrapper classes as upstream; `PDPageLabels` raises on a non-dictionary `/Nums` value; structure-tree reads skip unknown `/K` entries, accept string-valued `/RoleMap`, and default `/ParentTreeNextKey` to `-1`. pypdfbox-only allocator helpers still start at 0 by normalizing the raw `-1` sentinel.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `filter/Predictor.java`, `pdfparser/BaseParser.java`, `pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/destination/PDDestination.java`, `pdmodel/common/PDPageLabels.java`, `pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/PDStructureNode.java`, `PDStructureTreeRoot.java`

- **Wave 1512 — encryption R5/R6 auth fix (toward parity, not a divergence).** `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/standard_security_handler.py::_compute_encryption_key_r5_r6` now decides owner-vs-user from the `/O`,`/U` hashes alone (mirroring upstream `computeEncryptedKeyRev56`), then requires only the *authenticating* role's encryption key — raising `OSError("/Encrypt/OE entry is missing")` / `"…/UE…"` when that role's key is absent — instead of the prior wave-1511 draft that gated the hash branches on `/OE`/`/UE` presence and silently degraded a missing matched-role key to `InvalidPasswordException`. Validated against the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle (`EncryptDictFuzzProbe`).
- **Wave 1512 — malformed `/Encrypt`-dict open contract.** `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/standard_security_handler.py`: a malformed `/Encrypt` dict opens fail-closed with a clean `InvalidPasswordException` (or warn-and-open for an absent/short `/Perms` post-auth check) where PDFBox 3.0.7 throws `NullPointerException`/`IllegalArgumentException` (missing `/O` `/U`, `/Perms`, `/Length 0`) or refuses to open; an unknown `/V` on a legacy file is rejected up front rather than opened to undecryptable garbage. Wrong-password rejection is intact on both sides everywhere.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/StandardSecurityHandler.java`
  reason: hostile/buggy-producer robustness — never NPE on a malformed encryption dict, never silently fail-open. Pinned both-sides (15 cases) in `tests/pdmodel/encryption/oracle/test_encrypt_dict_fuzz_wave1511.py`.
- **Wave 1512 — `PDColorSpace.create` fuzz parity.** The construction-leniency surface is pinned both-sides against the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle (`ColorSpaceFuzzProbe`, 83 cases — 38 byte-exact, 45 documented divergences, no production change). Three pre-existing divergence families confirmed: (1) `create()` is permissive — upstream throws `IOException`/`MissingResourceException` on malformed/unknown input where pypdfbox returns `None` (structural dispatch) or constructs the array form leniently and surfaces the defect at `nc`/`init`/`toRGB`; short device names `G`/`RGB`/`CMYK` resolve in the bare factory; (2) DeviceCMYK/Cal*/Lab/Separation+DeviceN `toRGB` use explicit deterministic colour math vs PDFBox's JVM colour-management module; (3) `PDPattern.get_number_of_components`/`get_initial_color` return `0`/empty rather than throwing `UnsupportedOperationException`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/PDColorSpace.java`, `PDPattern.java`
- **Wave 1512 — XMP parser is a `TypeMapping`-free subset; strict-mode and unknown-schema contracts diverge.** `pypdfbox/xmpbox/dom_xmp_parser.py` has not ported upstream's `TypeMapping` / `PropertiesDescription` type system, so it lacks the per-namespace `isDefinedSchema` NoSchema gate, the per-property `checkPropertyDefinition` type lookup, and the mandatory leading `<?xpacket?>` PI. Consequences (validated + pinned both-sides in `tests/xmpbox/oracle/test_xmp_parse_fuzz_wave1512.py`): unknown schema namespaces are kept as free-form `XMPSchema` rather than raising `NoSchema`; unknown properties on a known schema are kept rather than raising `InvalidType` (strict); cardinality mismatches and non-rdf `parseType` are tolerated/over-strict differently; the `<?xpacket?>` envelope is optional; a non-rdf:RDF child of `x:xmpmeta` surfaces `NoRootElement` rather than `Format`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `xmpbox/src/main/java/org/apache/xmpbox/xml/DomXmpParser.java`
  reason: the rich typed-property infrastructure is not yet ported; the subset parser favours metadata recovery over upstream's PDF/A-grade strictness.
- **Wave 1512 — XMP XML-level parse failures map to `ErrorType.Undefined` (was `Format`).** `pypdfbox/xmpbox/dom_xmp_parser.py::parse` now wraps any malformed/truncated/not-XML/empty/DOCTYPE input as `ErrorType.Undefined` ("Failed to parse: ..."), matching upstream which wraps the DOM-build SAXException at this point (the structural FORMAT checks run only after a successful DOM build). Corrects pypdfbox's prior invented `Format` mapping; 4 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `xmpbox/src/main/java/org/apache/xmpbox/xml/DomXmpParser.java` parse(InputStream)
  reason: align the error-type discriminator with upstream (validated against the live xmpbox 3.0.7 oracle).
- **Wave 1512 — xref-stream decode fuzz parity.** A 34-case differential fuzz of `PDFXrefStreamParser` (`/W` arity & widths, PDFBOX-6037 sum>20 cap, `/W[0]==0` type-default, `/Index` missing/empty/odd/overrun/multi-subsection, free/in-use/compressed/unknown(≥3) entry types, body truncation, >32-bit offsets); pypdfbox is byte-identical to PDFBox 3.0.7 on every recovered table, no production change. Malformed-dict throws diverge only in exception class name (`PDFParseError` vs `IOException`/`NumberFormatException`) — pinned both-sides as throw-boolean parity per the wave-1505 filter-fuzz convention.
- **Wave 1513 — shading construction fixes (toward parity).** `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/pd_shading.py::PDShading.create` now constructs the mesh shading types 4-7 from a plain `COSDictionary` (parity with upstream `PDShading.create` / `PDShadingType4..7(COSDictionary)`; removed a non-upstream "requires a stream" guard), and a missing/invalid `/ShadingType` now defaults to `0` and raises the upstream wording `"Error: Unknown shading type <n>"` (matching `getInt(SHADING_TYPE, 0)`). 5 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/PDShading.java`
- **Wave 1513 — `PDCIDSystemInfo.get_supplement` default fix (toward parity).** Now defaults to `-1` (not `0`) for an absent/non-numeric `/Supplement`, matching upstream's `COSDictionary.getInt` one-arg default (oracle-verified, e.g. `Adobe-GB1--1`). 6 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/font/PDCIDSystemInfo.java`
- **Wave 1513 — image-construction fuzz divergences (pinned both-sides).** `PDImageXObject.get_color_space()` / `PDColorSpace.create()` are more lenient than upstream on a malformed `/ColorSpace` — upstream throws `IOException` for an absent (non-stencil), unknown-name, empty-array, or non-name/array color space; pypdfbox returns `None` (and accepts the inline `/G` `/RGB` `/CMYK` abbreviation on the XObject path), because the image-decode pipeline (`decode_pdimage_to_pil` / `sampled_image_reader`) is built around None-tolerance and degrades to a best-effort raster. A corrupt inline-image DCT payload fails on both sides with differing exception class (Java `IIOException` vs pypdfbox `OSError`). No production change; 81-case fuzz (76 byte-exact, 5 pinned) in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/image/oracle/test_image_fuzz_wave1513.py`. (Same permissive-`create` design pinned in wave 1512; also the root of a wave-1513 shading color-space divergence.)
- **Wave 1513 — AcroForm field-dict fuzz divergences (pinned both-sides).** 61-case fuzz of `PDField`/`PDTextField`/`PDChoice`/`PDButton`/`PDSignatureField` value+option+flag accessors (55 byte-exact, 6 pinned, validated against upstream bytecode): `ft_missing` — pypdfbox keeps a `PDFieldStub` generic-terminal wrapper for a `/T`-present-but-`/FT`-absent dict where upstream `PDFieldFactory.createField` returns `null` (the stub is a load-bearing field-tree scaffold; pypdfbox only widens what it surfaces, never mis-types); pypdfbox tolerates a COSName-valued choice `/V` and skips non-numeric `/I` entries where upstream returns `[]`/`null`; `getFieldFlags` returns `0` on a float/string `/Ff` where upstream `checkcast COSInteger` throws `ClassCastException`; and `/Ff = 2^32` stays unbounded in Python vs Java 32-bit narrowing (low 32 bits identical). No production behavior change (comment-only note in `pd_field_factory.create_field`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/`
- **Wave 1513 — Type0/CID composite-font fuzz divergence (pinned both-sides).** pypdfbox's `PDType0Font` constructs over a missing/empty/non-array/dict `/DescendantFonts`, an unknown/missing descendant `/Subtype`, and an unknown/unparseable `/Encoding` (returning a null descendant / no CMap), whereas upstream's eager constructor raises `IOException`. Deliberate lazy-construction robustness choice; extends the wave-1510 lazy-Type0 family. 41-case fuzz in `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_type0_font_fuzz_wave1513.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/font/PDType0Font.java`
- **Wave 1514 — ExtGState `/RI` string resolution + `PDResources.get_x_object` error class (toward parity).** `PDExtendedGraphicsState.get_rendering_intent`/`get_rendering_intent_typed` now resolve `/RI` via `get_name_as_string` (accepts `COSName` *or* `COSString`, e.g. a `/RI` stored as `(Perceptual)`), mirroring upstream `getRenderingIntent()`/`getNameAsString`; previously a string-valued `/RI` returned `None`. `PDResources.get_x_object` now raises `OSError` (was Python `TypeError`) for a non-stream `/XObject` entry, mirroring upstream `PDXObject.createXObject`'s `IOException` (2 stale tests retargeted). Both oracle-validated.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/state/PDExtendedGraphicsState.java`, `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/PDResources.java`
- **Wave 1514 — ExtGState `PDFontSetting.get_font_size` leniency (pinned both-sides).** Returns `0.0` for a `/Font` array of size < 2 (or a non-numeric size slot) where upstream does a raw `get(1)` + `(COSNumber)` cast and throws `IndexOutOfBounds`/`ClassCast`. The font setting itself resolves identically. Pinned in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/state/oracle/test_extgstate_fuzz_wave1514.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/PDFontSetting.java`
- **Wave 1514 — `PDResources.getColorSpace` malformed-entry leniency (pinned both-sides).** When a `/ColorSpace/<name>` entry is an unrecognised non-name spec (array-of-numbers/string/number), pypdfbox's shared permissive-None `PDColorSpace.create` returns `None` where upstream raises `IOException`; the rest of the resources-lookup contract is byte-identical. Rooted in the wave-1512 permissive-create design, not the resources layer. Pinned (3 cases) in `tests/pdmodel/oracle/test_resources_lookup_fuzz_wave1514.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/PDColorSpace.java`
- **Wave 1514 — name/number-tree traversal robustness (pinned both-sides).** `PDNameTreeNode`/`PDNumberTreeNode` generic-traversal fuzz (39 cases): (1) `PDNumberTreeNode.get_numbers` recurses through `/Kids` where upstream `getNumbers` is non-recursive (null on an intermediate node); (2) a kid missing `/Limits` (or a non-dict kid) makes pypdfbox `get_value` guard the null limit and fall through where upstream stops early (name tree) or throws `NullPointerException` (number tree); (3) malformed `/Limits` — pypdfbox is bounds-safe + type-strict (returns `None`) where upstream `getUpperLimit` throws `IndexOutOfBoundsException` on a <2-element `/Limits` and returns the `-1` sentinel for a non-number entry. No production change. Pinned in `tests/pdmodel/common/oracle/test_name_number_tree_fuzz_wave1514.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/common/PDNameTreeNode.java`, `PDNumberTreeNode.java`
- **Wave 1515 — annotation geometry-accessor fixes (toward parity).** `PDAnnotation.get_rectangle` now gates on `/Rect` `size()==4` AND every entry a `COSNumber`, returning `None` (never a partial rect, never a raised `TypeError`) — matching upstream `getRectangle()`. `PDAnnotationLine.get_line` returns the whole `/L` array via `to_float_array()` with no arity check/slicing (was slicing to 4 and requiring size≥4). `PDAnnotationFreeText.get_rect_difference` wraps any `/RD` array in a tolerant `PDRectangle` (pad-to-4 with 0.0, clamp, normalize) like upstream `PDRectangle(COSArray)`, instead of returning `None` on short/non-numeric `/RD`. The pypdfbox-specific strict accessors keep their `None`/`[]` contract. 5 stale tests retargeted. Also pinned: `create_annotation` dispatch is a deliberate superset (Movie/Screen/PrinterMark/Redact/Watermark/3D/TrapNet → typed subclasses vs upstream `PDAnnotationUnknown`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/PDAnnotation.java`, `PDAnnotationLine.java`, `PDAnnotationFreeText.java`
- **Wave 1515 — PDPage inheritance + box-parse fixes (toward parity).** `PDPage` inheritable-attribute resolution now only ascends to a parent whose `/Type` is `/Pages` (was: any dictionary parent) — matching upstream `PDPageTree.getInheritableAttribute`; a page parented to a non-`/Pages` node no longer wrongly inherits `/MediaBox`/`/CropBox`/`/Rotate`/`/Resources`. The five box accessors route through a private upstream-faithful `_rect_from_cos_array` (zero-pads short arrays, coerces non-numeric to 0, clamps to float32 `(float)Integer.MAX_VALUE`, never raises) instead of the strict shared `PDRectangle.from_cos_array` (kept stricter for its ~40 other call-sites — handoff for a future alignment wave). `get_annotations` now skips only `null` members and dispatches the rest to `PDAnnotation.create` (raises on a non-dict member, `TypeError` vs upstream `IOException` — same control-flow position and raise-vs-swallow behavior, only the exception class differs by language). Multiple stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/PDPage.java`, `PDPageTree.java`, `common/PDRectangle.java`
- **Wave 1515 — `PDFunctionType0.get_bits_per_sample` default fix + construction divergences.** Now defaults to `-1` (not `0`) for an absent `/BitsPerSample`, matching upstream's single-arg `getInt` default (2 stale tests retargeted). Pinned both-sides (pypdfbox more lenient than upstream's accidental faults): `create(None)` / `create(unresolved COSObject)` return `None` where upstream throws `IOException`; `get_number_of_input_parameters` returns 0 for an absent `/Domain` where upstream NPEs (it guards `/Range` but not `/Domain`); `PDFunctionType4` parses its PostScript body lazily at eval where upstream parses eagerly in the constructor.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/PDFunctionType0.java`, `PDFunction.java`
- **Wave 1515 — `PDDocumentCatalog.get_dests` wrapper-type fix (toward parity).** Now returns `PDDocumentNameDestinationDictionary` (matching upstream `getDests()`'s declared return type) for the legacy flat `/Dests` catalog entry, instead of the modern name-tree `PDDestinationNameTreeNode`. `pd_action_embedded_go_to._resolve_named_destination` updated to call `get_destination()` on the legacy wrapper. 5 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/PDDocumentCatalog.java`
- **Wave 1515 — `PDBorderStyleDictionary.get_style` malformed-`/S` coercion (toward parity).** Now reads `/S` via `get_name_as_string` (matching upstream `getStyle()`'s `getNameAsString(COSName.S, STYLE_SOLID)`), so a malformed `COSString` `/S` coerces to its text instead of falling back to the `S` default. Oracle-validated (61 cases byte-exact).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/PDBorderStyleDictionary.java`
- **Wave 1516 — classic xref-table leniency + brute-force recovery (toward parity).** The classic `xref`-table parser now mirrors upstream `COSParser.parseXrefTable`: a malformed subsection header / entry row (orphan entry from a wrong `/count`, non-numeric header, unknown entry-type char) is no longer a hard error — it breaks the subsection loop and recovers the (possibly incomplete) object set via a brute-force merge, and a cleanly-parsed table whose `/Root` reference is missing/mislabelled-free is relocated by the same scan. **Regression fix (same wave):** the relocation trigger `_located_root_needs_relocation` was firing on *any* PDF whose catalog lives in an object stream (it checked `/Root` resolvability before compressed members were loadable), spuriously pulling the `/XRef`-stream object into the pool — now guarded to skip relocation when the `/Root` key already carries a usable (non-free) xref entry. Validated vs the live oracle (`XrefTableFuzzProbe` 30 cases + `test_cos_graph_oracle.py[PDFBOX-6049]`). Pinned divergences: a non-reference / dangling `/Root` opens with a synthesised empty catalog (pages=0) where upstream throws `IOException` (deferred-`initialParse` design); all-free-entries recovery opens identically but the public xref object count differs (recovery-completeness nuance).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`parseXrefTable`/`checkXrefOffsets`/`bfSearchForObjects`)
- **Wave 1516 — object-stream (/ObjStm) resolution realigned (toward parity).** `PDFParser._load_compressed_object` / `_read_object_stream_offsets` now mirror upstream `COSParser.parseObjectStreamObject` / `PDFObjectStreamParser`: a malformed `/Type /ObjStm` resolves its member to `null` in lenient mode (default) instead of raising; `/Type` is no longer validated (upstream checks only `/N` and `/First`, via `getInt` `-1`-default); members resolve by stored object NUMBER (not the xref positional index); header pairs read from the full decoded buffer (not a `/First`-truncated slice). Pinned divergences (both-sides): a `/First` smaller than the real header parses the member at the spec-literal offset vs upstream's forward-only skip; a member body truncated/malformed resolves to `null` (lenient catch) vs upstream's partial-dict recovery — the gap is base-parser COS-token EOF leniency. Related cross-module: `PDDocumentCatalog` synthesises an empty catalog on a null `/Root` so `load_pdf` succeeds where PDFBox's eager validation fails (2 mutation-fuzz xfails).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdfparser/COSParser.java`, `PDFObjectStreamParser.java`
- **Wave 1516 — simple-font /Encoding resolution fixes (toward parity, divergences collapsed).** `PDSimpleFont.get_encoding_typed` lazy name-only branch now falls back to the font program's built-in encoding for an unknown `/Encoding` name (matching `readEncoding`) instead of `None`; `PDTrueTypeFont.read_encoding_from_font` now returns `Type1Encoding(afm)` for non-embedded Standard 14 (the AFM branch had been stubbed `None`). Full convergence — collapsed the wave-1510 `truetype_no_widths`/`truetype_fontfile2_garbage` 250.0-substitute-width pins (now real AFM 667/278).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/font/PDSimpleFont.java`, `PDTrueTypeFont.java`
- **Wave 1516 — `PDDocumentInformation` date accessors delegate to `COSDictionary.get_date` (toward parity).** `get_creation_date`/`get_modification_date`/`set_*` now delegate to the faithful oracle-pinned `DateConverter.toCalendar` port (matching upstream's `getDate`/`setDate`) instead of a private narrower `_PDF_DATE_RE` regex that rejected the broader lenient date shapes and clamped a 60-second value to `:59` rather than rejecting it. Module-level `_parse_pdf_date`/`_format_pdf_date` retained as thin delegates for the signature/annotation/form/embedded consumers. 36-case fuzz, 0 divergences.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/PDDocumentInformation.java`
- **Wave 1516 — COS object body-parse fuzz (rebaselined Wave 1520).** Differential fuzz of `COSParser.parse_direct_object` + container/number readers (81 cases). Wave 1520 aligned `COSObject.get_object` with upstream's parser-I/O swallowing, closing 6 old fail-fast pins; 73 cases are now byte-exact. Eight recovered-value differences remain pinned for malformed multi-dot/sign-only numbers and partial container/string framing.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdfparser/BaseParser.java`, `COSParser.java`
- **Wave 1517 — COSStream `/Length` + endstream recovery fixes (toward parity).** `pypdfbox/pdfparser/pdf_parser.py` stream-body recovery now mirrors upstream `COSParser.parseCOSStream` / `getLength` / `readUntilEndStream`. Five real bugs fixed (validated against the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle, probe `StreamLengthFuzzProbe`): (1) a **float `/Length`** is honoured as a `COSNumber` (its int value) instead of being treated as malformed; (2) a **negative `/Length`** falls through `validate_stream_length` to the endstream recovery scan instead of raising a fail-fast `PDFParseError` (upstream's negative `longValue()` simply fails `validateStreamLength`); (3) **endstream omitted but endobj present** recovers by scanning to `endobj` (the recovery scanner now matches `endstream` *or* `endobj`); (4) **both endstream and endobj omitted** recovers by scanning to EOF (and to the next document object's `endobj`) instead of raising; (5) a **missing trailing `endobj`** (file truncated right after the stream body) is tolerated in lenient mode. `_resolve_stream_length` now returns `int | None` (None = no usable length → recover) and distinguishes the wrong-type/unread-indirect error messages from the missing-length case. Strict mode keeps its fail-fast contract (missing `/Length` → raise). 9 stale tests across waves 322/405/484/583/593/644/1352 retargeted to the corrected behaviour.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`parseCOSStream`, `getLength`, `validateStreamLength`, `readUntilEndStream`)
- **Wave 1517 — wrong-typed `/Length` null-resolution divergence closed (Wave 1520).** A name, direct `null`, or missing indirect `/Length` target now resolves the stream object to null exactly like PDFBox: the parser failure crosses the lazy-loader boundary as `OSError`, and `COSObject.get_object` logs and swallows it once. The 31-case `StreamLengthFuzzProbe` corpus is fully converged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/cos/COSObject.java`, `COSParser.java`
- ~~**ICU bidi reordering not ported.**~~ **Closed wave 1387 (agent A)** — `pypdfbox/text/bidi.py` ships a stdlib-only UAX #9 implementation (`BidiResolver`, `reorder_visually`, `reorder_runs_visually`, `get_paragraph_direction`); `pypdfbox/text/pdf_text_stripper.py::handle_direction` now routes through it (P2/P3 paragraph-direction detection + explicit embedding/override/isolate stack + W1-W7 weak resolution + N0-N2 neutrals + I1-I2 implicit levels + L1-L2 reorder + L4 mirroring). Per-word aggregation in `_emit_group` matches upstream's per-word `handleDirection` contract. No new dependencies.
- ~~**`SimpleDateFormat` locale-sensitive parsing not ported.**~~ **Closed wave 1387 (agent B)** — bundled CLDR-derived month + weekday name tables for the 10 most common locales (en, fr, de, es, it, pt, ja, zh, ko, ru) in `pypdfbox/util/locale_data.py`; new `pypdfbox/util/date_util.py::parse_with_locale` ships a stdlib-only Java SimpleDateFormat tokeniser (yyyy / yy / MMMM / MMM / MM / M / dd / d / EEEE / EEE / HH / H / hh / h / mm / m / ss / s / a / z / quoted-literal) wired into the existing `_SIMPLE_FORMAT_HANDLERS` dispatch table on `DateConverter`. Module-level `to_calendar` falls through to `parse_date` on PDF-regex miss so alpha-start fixtures (`"Friday, January 11, 2115"`, `"Sun, Jul 6, 1980 at 4:23pm"`, `"Friday July 6 17:22:1 GMT+08:00 1979"`, …) parse end-to-end. Diacritic-insensitive (`fevrier` matches `février`) + case-insensitive (`JANUARY` matches `January`) via `unicodedata.normalize` + `casefold`. No new dependencies.
- ~~**Slash-separated `MM/dd/yyyy` date forms not handled.**~~ **Closed wave 1388 (agent A)** — `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py::_SIMPLE_FORMAT_HANDLERS` now carries explicit `MM/dd/yyyy`, `MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm`, `MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss` entries matching upstream `Locale.ENGLISH` US-default month-first semantics (the existing `M/d/yy` handler already covered the 4-digit-year shapes via a lenient regex; the new aliases make the dispatch table 1:1 with the commented-out upstream format list at `DateConverter.java` lines 136-142). `D:`-prefix strip routes through the existing `to_calendar` pre-handler. The previously-skipped `test_extract_slash_separated_date_with_optional_time` in `tests/util/upstream/test_date_util.py` now passes.
- **`split_on_space` / `tokenize_on_space` Python regex semantics.** `pypdfbox/util/string_util.py`: `split_on_space("   ")` returns `["", "", "", ""]` (Python's `re.split`); Java's `String.split("\\s")` strips trailing empties and returns `[]`. `tokenize_on_space("   ")` returns `["", " ", " ", " ", ""]`; Java's `Pattern.split` of zero-width lookarounds collapses adjacent matches and returns `[" ", " ", " "]`. Both shapes communicate the same tokens; we record the Python shape.
- **`PDFRenderer` pixel-exact parity not portable.** Upstream JUnit comparisons against stored TIFF/PNG references are *not* ported. Pypdfbox uses Pillow + a skia-backed `_aggdraw_compat` rasteriser; byte-equivalent raster output across the Java AWT and the Python pipeline is unachievable. Affected paths use structural parity (page count, MediaBox, Rotation, Contents shape, Resources keys, save-reload round-trip) anchored to the bundled reference PDFs. `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py`.
- **Skia anti-aliasing vs upstream Java2D AA.** The `_aggdraw_compat` skia path may render edge pixels differently from upstream Java2D in low-resolution rasters. Recorded as a known limitation; full pixel parity is not in scope (see preceding bullet).
- **Deeply nested direct containers raise `PDFParseError`, not `RecursionError`.** `pypdfbox/pdfparser/base_parser.py::parse_dir_object`: a malformed PDF nesting direct `[...]` / `<<...>>` past Python's recursion limit (~498-deep arrays at the default limit of 1000) raises the parser's own `PDFParseError` instead of leaking `RecursionError`. Upstream PDFBox is likewise recursive and would throw `StackOverflowError`; it tolerates deeper nesting only because the JVM stack is larger than CPython's recursion limit. Legitimate PDFs nest direct containers only a few levels (logical depth — page/outline/structure trees — uses *indirect* references, which do not recurse), so this guard fires only on hostile input. Found by the wave-1404 malformed-input fuzz harness.
- **Overlong integer literals raise `PDFParseError`.** `pypdfbox/pdfparser/base_parser.py::parse_cos_number`: an integer literal longer than `sys.get_int_max_str_digits()` (4300 by default) raises `PDFParseError` instead of leaking CPython's int-string-conversion `ValueError`. No Java analogue (this is a CPython CPU-DoS guard); pypdfbox-side hardening only.
- ~~**Re-encrypting a loaded-encrypted document double-enciphers stream bodies on save.**~~ — **FIXED wave 1418** (`COSStream.ensure_decrypted()` called from `COSWriter.visit_from_stream`; the 4 double-hop `xfail`s now pass). Original report: Found wave 1409 by the encryption cross-library oracle (`EncryptProbe`/`DecryptProbe`). When a document is loaded encrypted, lazily decrypted (each `COSStream` gets a security handler with `_decrypted=False`), and then re-protected + saved, `pypdfbox/pdfwriter/cos_writer.py::visit_from_stream` snapshots the body via `create_raw_input_stream()` — which returns the still-ciphertext bytes (only `create_input_stream()` runs the in-place decrypt pass) — and then enciphers it a second time. The reloaded file's stream bodies are double-encrypted → FlateDecode garbage. Reproduces on flat (non-object-stream) files too, so it is independent of the object-stream decrypt fix in this same wave. **Root cause is in `pypdfbox/pdfwriter`, not encryption** — the writer must decrypt-on-read (or consume the decrypted body) before re-enciphering. The single-hop interop directions (Java-encrypts→pypdfbox-decrypts and pypdfbox-encrypts→Java-decrypts) are unaffected and pass for all four algorithms; only the decrypt-then-re-encrypt double-hop is impacted. Pinned strict-`xfail` in `tests/pdmodel/encryption/oracle/test_encryption_interop_oracle.py::test_double_hop_java_to_py_to_java_preserves_content`.
- ~~**Inline dicts/arrays promoted to indirect objects on full save (object-count inflation).** Found wave 1408 by the writer live-oracle (`SaveRoundTripProbe`). The pypdfbox parser does not call `set_direct(True)` on dictionaries/arrays parsed as *inline* values, whereas upstream `BaseParser.parseCOSDictionary` / `parseCOSDictionaryNameValuePair` do (verified against the pinned 3.0.7 bytecode). Because `COSBase.is_direct()` defaults to `False`, the writer — whose `_write_array` / `_write_dictionary` `is_direct()` branch is byte-for-byte identical to upstream's `writeArray` / `writeDictionary` — emits every inline dict/array as a free-standing indirect object on a full save, inflating the trailer `/Size` (e.g. 347 vs PDFBox's 56 on `unencrypted.pdf`).~~ **Closed wave 1409.** See the Wave 1409 entry below: the parser now flags inline dicts/arrays direct and `COSArray` defaults `direct = True` (mirroring upstream), so the writer keeps them inline; `/Size` now tracks PDFBox within a couple of objects (was ~6×).

- **`PDFont.encode(str)` substitutes `?`/CID 0 for unencodable glyphs; upstream raises `IllegalArgumentException`.** (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/`.) Per-glyph widths match Java exactly (font-metrics oracle, wave 1408); only the unencodable-string error contract differs — `get_string_width` returns a number where PDFBox throws. **Permanent intentional divergence by user decision (2026-06-05, wave 1488): "keep it."** Downstream writer/appearance/render callers rely on the substitution; do not align to upstream in a future wave.

## Wave 1511 — document-navigation parsing-leniency differential fuzz (destinations / outlines / page labels)

Differential leniency fuzz of three document-navigation parsing surfaces vs the live Apache PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle (`oracle/probes/DocNavFuzzProbe.java` + `tests/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/oracle/test_doc_nav_fuzz_oracle.py`, 56-case seed-free corpus). All happy-path dispatch (`PDDestination.create` for every fit type, named string/name, integer/null/dict page slots, wrong arity, type-name case/whitespace variants, non-name type element, nested arrays; outline `/Dest` vs `/A` precedence; page-label number styles, `/St` 0/negative, missing/empty/odd-size `/Nums`, non-integer/negative keys, duplicate starts, ranges beyond page count) matches the jar token-for-token. The fuzz surfaced **no new bugs** — three pre-existing, already-documented robustness divergences (pypdfbox is the more-lenient superset) are now pinned both-sides:

- **`PDOutlineItem.get_destination` falls closed (returns `None`) on a malformed `/Dest` where upstream propagates the `IOException`.** (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/pd_outline_item.py`.) Upstream `PDOutlineItem.getDestination()` (PDOutlineItem.java) is `return PDDestination.create(getCOSObject().getDictionaryObject(DEST))` and `throws IOException`; a size-1 `/Dest` array, a bare integer, or a bare dictionary makes `create` throw and the exception escapes the getter. pypdfbox catches the `OSError` and returns `None`. Intentional since the wave-344-era outline-robustness pass (HISTORY): a corrupt bookmark target should not abort outline traversal. Pinned via `outline:dest_bad_array` / `outline:dest_bad_int` / `outline:dest_bad_dict` (Java `EXC:IOException` ⇔ pypdfbox `null`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/PDOutlineItem.java`
  reason: fail-closed robustness — a malformed bookmark destination must not break outline iteration.

- **`PDPageLabels` tolerates a non-dictionary `/Nums` value (skips it) where upstream throws `IOException`.** (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_labels.py`.) Upstream's `PDNumberTreeNode.getNumbers()` passes each value through a reflective `convertCOSToPD` that constructs `PDPageLabelRange(COSDictionary)`; a non-dictionary value (e.g. a bare integer in the value slot) fails the reflective constructor and `PDPageLabels(PDDocument, COSDictionary)` (which `throws IOException`) propagates it out of `PDDocumentCatalog.getPageLabels()`. pypdfbox's inlined `_find_labels` requires `isinstance(value, COSDictionary)` and silently skips a malformed entry, leaving the default labelling intact. Pinned via `labels:nondict_value` (Java `EXC:IOException` ⇔ pypdfbox tolerant `[1,2,3,4,5]`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/PDPageLabels.java` + `pdmodel/common/PDNumberTreeNode.java`
  reason: a single corrupt label range must not make the whole catalog `/PageLabels` lookup throw.

- **`PDPageLabels` recovers a same-node sibling `/Nums` when `/Kids` carries only junk (non-dictionary) children, where upstream drops the `/Nums`.** (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page_labels.py`.) Upstream `findLabels` is `if (getKids() != null) { recurse } else if (getNumbers() != null) { ... }`; a present-but-junk `/Kids` array makes `getKids()` return a non-null list of placeholder empty nodes (it logs `Bad child node at position N` and substitutes a fresh empty `PDNumberTreeNode`), so the else-if never reads the sibling `/Nums` and the document falls back to default labels. pypdfbox only takes the `/Kids` branch when a *real* dictionary child is found (`found_child`), otherwise it reads the sibling `/Nums` — recovering a valid label tree the jar discards (wave 310: "malformed or empty `/Kids` arrays no longer hide valid same-node `/Nums` label trees"). Pinned via `labels:kids_nonchild_with_nums` (Java `[1,2,3,4,5]` ⇔ pypdfbox `[I,II,III,IV,V]`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/PDPageLabels.java` + `pdmodel/common/PDNumberTreeNode.java#getKids`
  reason: recover a valid `/Nums` label tree from a document whose `/Kids` array is corrupt.

## Wave 1510 — FreeText cloudy-border (`/BE`) wired through `CloudyBorder` (backlog audit wave)

- **FIX — `PDFreeTextAppearanceHandler.generate_normal_appearance`: a `/BE` cloudy border now draws the cloud via `CloudyBorder` instead of falling back to the plain rectangle.** The port previously omitted the upstream `borderEffect != null && STYLE_CLOUDY` branch entirely (`PDFreeTextAppearanceHandler.java:186-204`) and always emitted the straight-edged padded rectangle — a documented lite-surface deviation. Ported the branch verbatim, mirroring the Square/Circle/Polygon handlers: build `CloudyBorder(cs, intensity, line_width, rect)`, call `create_cloudy_rectangle(rect_difference)`, write the expanded `/Rect` + fresh `/RD` back onto the annotation, and re-stamp the appearance stream's `/BBox` + `/Matrix` from the cloud. The non-cloudy path is unchanged (`border_box` still drives the text layout and a single `draw_shape` paints either result). New live-oracle probe `oracle/probes/FreeTextCloudyProbe.java` + `tests/.../oracle/test_free_text_cloudy_oracle.py` pin the cloudy `/AP /N` op-skeleton, `/BBox`, `/Matrix`, `/Rect` and `/RD` byte-exact vs PDFBox 3.0.7 (`/BE /S C /I 2` + `/RD [5 7 9 11]`). (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/annotation/handlers/PDFreeTextAppearanceHandler.generateNormalAppearance` (`/BE` STYLE_CLOUDY branch via `CloudyBorder`)
  reason: the plain-rectangle fallback was a draw-form divergence; `CloudyBorder` is already ported and used by the sibling handlers, so the cloudy FreeText path ports cleanly to operand-level parity

## Wave 1509 — Text-annotation ZapfDingbats/Symbol glyph icons operand-exact (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — `PDTextAppearanceHandler`: Cross//Star//Check//RightPointer (ZapfDingbats) + CrossHairs (Symbol) now emit the exact upstream Standard-14 glyph outlines; /AP token stream byte-identical.** `drawZapf` (ZapfDingbats `a22`//`a35`//`a20`//`a174`) and `drawCrossHairs` (Symbol `circleplus`) previously rendered hand-built path approximations (a documented lite-surface divergence) because pypdfbox has not ported `Standard14Fonts.getGlyphPath`. Embedded the exact upstream glyph outlines as pre-flattened `(op, coords)` paths (canonical to the 3-decimal operand precision Apache PDFBox emits, in the font's own units) and wired them through the existing `add_path` helper. Also corrected the fontMatrix-derived scale: ZapfDingbats//Symbol use a `1/2048` (`0.00048828125`) em, not the `1/1000` the old approximation assumed — `drawZapf` scales by `fontMatrix·minDim/0.8`, `drawCrossHairs` by `fontMatrix·minDim·1.3333` (both verified against the `PDTextAppearanceHandler` bytecode + the live `getFontBoxFont().getFontMatrix()`). The full operand stream — operators, operands, scale/translate `cm`, paint order — is now byte-exact vs the jar for all 16 standard icons. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/annotation/handlers/PDTextAppearanceHandler.java` (`drawZapf`//`drawCrossHairs` via `Standard14Fonts.getGlyphPath`)
