  reason: the hand-built glyphs were a draw-form divergence; the upstream glyph outlines are static point lists and port cleanly to operand-level parity, completing the text-icon byte-parity set begun in wave 1508

## Wave 1509 — merge writer-level byte residuals closed (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — `COSWriter.visit_from_dictionary`: nested `/XObject` / `/Resources` sub-dictionaries are now written INLINE (direct) on a full save (PDFBOX-3684).** pypdfbox's dictionary visitor was missing upstream's size-optimisation block: when a dictionary value itself carries an `/XObject` or `/Resources` sub-dictionary, upstream flips that sub-dict `setDirect(true)` (skipping the dict's own self-named key, to avoid a self-referential inline) before writing it, so it serialises inline rather than as a free-standing indirect object. Without it a merged form-XObject's `/Resources` was emitted as an indirect ref. Ported verbatim, guarded by `not self._incremental_update` exactly as upstream guards on `!incrementalUpdate`. Closes wave-1508 merge residual (1). (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfwriter/COSWriter.visitFromDictionary` (PDFBOX-3684 setDirect block)
  reason: writer-faithfulness — the inline-XObject/Resources optimisation is part of upstream's full-save shape; omitting it inflated merged output with extra indirect objects
- **FIX — `PDFMergerUtility._finish_struct_tree_merge`: the rebuilt struct-tree ParentTree leaf now carries `/Limits [lower upper]` ahead of `/Nums`, matching upstream `setNumbers`.** Upstream `PDNumberTreeNode.setNumbers` unconditionally materialises `/Limits` (via `setUpper/LowerLimit`) *before* `setItem(NUMS, …)`, even on the tree root the merger builds — so a single-leaf merged ParentTree serialises `/Limits [0 0]` then `/Nums`. pypdfbox's `PDNumberTreeNode` deliberately strips `/Limits` from a root node (documented spec-compliant DIVERGENCE A, oracle-pinned in `test_number_tree_setter_oracle` — intentionally left intact). Rather than perturb the general number-tree contract, the merger now re-stamps `/Limits` (in upstream insertion order, re-keying `/Nums` after it) only on the ParentTree it rebuilds. Closes wave-1508 merge residual (2). With both fixes, all four merge scenarios are byte-identical to PDFBox 3.0.7 modulo `/ID`. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `multipdf/PDFMergerUtility.mergeStructureTree` + `pdmodel/common/PDNumberTreeNode.setNumbers`
  reason: merge byte-faithfulness without weakening the (more spec-compliant) root-no-`/Limits` number-tree divergence pypdfbox keeps everywhere else

## Wave 1508 — CMap parse-fuzz differential (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — `CMapParser.create_string_from_bytes`: the empty `<>` token maps to U+0000 like upstream `getMapping(byte[0])`.** A `beginbfchar <01> <>` entry (or an empty array entry `[<0060> <> <0062>]`) yields a zero-length dst byte sequence; upstream routes `length <= 2` through `CMapStrings.getMapping(byte[])`, and `getMapping(new byte[0])` indexes `twoByteMappings.get(0)` returning `"␀"` — NOT the empty string a naive UTF-16BE decode of zero bytes would give. The port now returns `"\x00"` for the `len == 0` case. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/cmap/CMapParser.createStringFromBytes` + `CMapStrings.getMapping`
  reason: zero-length dst tokens are a lenient real-world ToUnicode quirk; the upstream lookup-table path resolves them to NUL, not empty
- **PIN (documented both-sides divergence, no code change) — no-codespace `CMap.readCode(InputStream)`: upstream throws, pypdfbox reads one byte.** When a CMap is truncated/corrupt such that no `begincodespacerange` block is parsed, `CMap` keeps the 3.0.7 constructor defaults `minCodeLength=4` / `maxCodeLength=0` (verified vs the `CMap.<init>` bytecode). Upstream `readCode` then does `new byte[maxCodeLength]` → `new byte[0]` followed by `in.read(bytes, 0, minCodeLength)` = `read(byte[0],0,4)`, throwing `IndexOutOfBoundsException` (the parse-fuzz probe reports `len=-1`). pypdfbox **deliberately** hardens `_read_code_from_stream` to short-circuit `max_len <= 0` to "read one byte" (the long-standing `test_cmap.test_empty_cmap_read_code_reads_one_byte_without_crashing` contract). Intentional robustness divergence — pinned both-sides by the new fuzz oracle (header + CID value + Unicode parity asserted; the `len` suffix divergence pinned explicitly), not aligned to the upstream crash. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/cmap/CMap.readCode(InputStream)` + `CMap.<init>` defaults
  reason: crashing on a no-codespace CMap is an upstream edge bug; pypdfbox's one-byte fallback is the more robust contract and is already relied upon
- **FIX — `PDTextAppearanceHandler`: Help//Paragraph//NewParagraph now emit the exact upstream Standard-14 glyph outlines; /AP token stream byte-identical.** `drawHelp` (Helvetica-Bold `question`), `drawParagraph` (Helvetica `paragraph`/pilcrow) and `drawNewParagraph` (Helvetica-Bold `N`+`P`) previously rendered hand-built path approximations (a documented lite-surface divergence) because pypdfbox has not ported `Standard14Fonts.getGlyphPath`. Embedded the exact upstream glyph outlines as pre-flattened `(op, coords)` paths (canonical to the 3-decimal operand precision Apache PDFBox emits) and wired them through the existing `add_path` helper with the upstream scale (`0.001*minDim/2.25`, `0.001*minDim/3.0`, constant `0.004`) + translate (`500,375`/`850,900`/`200,0`+`1300,0`) `cm` matrices. The operand stream — operators, operands, scale/translate, halo `q/Q`, outer-circle fill/stroke order — is now byte-exact vs the jar. New operand-level probe `TextAnnotationIconProbe` + oracle test pin Note//Comment//Key//Help//Paragraph//NewParagraph//Insert byte-exact (the ZapfDingbats//Symbol glyph icons Cross//Star//Check//RightPointer//CrossHairs were closed the same way in wave 1509). (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/annotation/handlers/PDTextAppearanceHandler.java` (`drawHelp`//`drawParagraph`//`drawNewParagraph` via `Standard14Fonts.getGlyphPath`)
  reason: the hand-built glyphs were a draw-form divergence; the upstream glyph outlines are static point lists and port cleanly to operand-level parity
- **FIX — `PDTextAppearanceHandler`: a set `/C` colour now emits `/DeviceRGB cs r g b sc` like upstream, not the device shorthand `rg`.** Upstream `generateNormalAppearance` passes the full `PDColor` from `getColor()` to `setNonStrokingColor(PDColor)`, which writes the colour-space name + `cs` + components + `sc`. pypdfbox passed the raw `/C` float list, routing through the `rg`/`g`/`k` shorthand path. The handler now wraps the components via `_pd_color_from_components` (the helper that already exists for exactly this); the `/C`-absent case still emits the gray shorthand `1 g` (upstream `setNonStrokingColor(1f)`). Pinned both directions by the new icon oracle (red Note, green Insert). (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/annotation/handlers/PDTextAppearanceHandler.java` `generateNormalAppearance` + `PDAbstractContentStream.setNonStrokingColor(PDColor)`
  reason: the raw-component shorthand path diverged from upstream's colour-space-qualified `sc` emission

## Wave 1507 — compressed-save complex fixtures + cff//type1 fuzz + annot icons + blend modes (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — `PDFileAttachmentAppearanceHandler`: exact upstream SVG-derived glyph paths ported; /AP token stream byte-identical for all four standard icons.** Replaced the wave-1285 stylized-icon approximations (paperclip from two pills, push-pin triangle+needle, graph `re` bars, tag polygon+circle — a documented lite-surface divergence) with the exact upstream `drawPushPin`/`drawPaperclip`/`drawGraph`/`drawTag` point lists (same CC0/Apache SVG sources, `0.022` scale + translate matrices, Tag's two `q…Q`-wrapped sub-shapes). Also bridged `PDAppearanceContentStream.transform`'s 6-float signature via the `_apply_matrix` helper the Text handler uses. New operand-level probe `FileAttachmentIconProbe` pins all four icons byte-exact (RECT+BBOX+every operand). Sweep verdict: the rest of the non-widget appearance surface was already pinned (Line//Poly//Ink//Link//Cloudy//StrikeOut//Squiggly//Highlight//Caret operand-byte; FreeText skeleton+colour exact with the text-layout divergence documented; Text icons keyword+bbox — hand-off recorded in DEFERRED). 1 stale stylized-icon test retargeted. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/annotation/handlers/PDFileAttachmentAppearanceHandler.java` (`drawPushPin`//`drawPaperclip`//`drawGraph`//`drawTag`)
  reason: the lite stylized icons were a draw-form divergence; the upstream paths are static point lists and port cleanly to operand-level parity
- **FIX — compressed full-save now raises the PDF version to the compression minimum (1.6) like upstream; complex-fixture sweep byte-identical.** Upstream `COSWriter.doWriteHeader` bumps to `max(current, COSWriterCompressionPool.MINIMUM_SUPPORTED_VERSION=1.6f)` when `isCompress()` — header becomes `%PDF-1.6` and, for sources already ≥1.4, `/Version /1.6` is written into the catalog via `PDDocument.setVersion`. pypdfbox emitted the source version verbatim (a 1.4 doc compressed-saved as `%PDF-1.4`, no catalog `/Version`) — latent divergence the ObjStm-body pins missed. Ported as `_bump_version_for_compress` in `_do_write_header`, gated to the compressed path, skipped under an explicit `set_pdf_version` override; non-compressed//incremental saves unaffected, 1.7 stays 1.7. SWEEP RESULT: compressed save is byte-identical to PDFBox on PDFA3A (full struct tree), with_outline (after the bump), AcroFormForMerge, and two MERGED deep graphs when both sides compress the same intermediate — the wave-1506 "object-renumbering on deep struct trees" observation was the MERGER's intermediate-graph difference, NOT a COSWriter traversal divergence (no traversal change needed; wave-1501/1502 pins green). New `FullSaveMergedObjStmParityProbe` + 4 oracle tests (header bump, catalog /Version, complex packing shape, merged compress). (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` `doWriteHeader` + `COSWriterCompressionPool.MINIMUM_SUPPORTED_VERSION` + `PDDocument.setVersion`
  reason: object streams + xref streams require ≥1.5; pypdfbox kept the source header verbatim on compressed save
- **FIX — renderer non-separable HSL blend modes (Hue//Saturation//Color//Luminosity) now compose via the integer 8.16 fixed-point `BlendMode` helpers, bit-identical to upstream `BlendComposite`.** The render path used the float `SetLum`//`SetSat`//`ClipColor` form (PDF §11.3.5.3 pseudocode) while upstream `BlendComposite.compose` dispatches to `BlendMode.getSaturationRGB`//`getLuminosityRGB` (integer `0x80`//`>>8`//`<<16` arithmetic, BlendMode.java L286-388) — off-by-one byte divergence (Luminosity red 188 vs 189). The four `_compose` closures now call the already-ported, oracle-tested integer helpers with upstream's src//dest order (Hue chains Sat→Lum per the `fHue` lambda). Audit verdict on the rest: all 12 separable modes + luminosity//alpha SMask + /TR transfer + /BC backdrop (BT.601 luma exact) + /Matte un-premultiply already at parity; SoftMask `Math.round`-vs-Python-`round` checked exhaustively — the identity-transfer `g/255` path never lands on a .5 boundary across all 256×256 pairs. Float `_hsl_*` helpers retained (test consumers) but no longer drive rendering. 1 bit-exactness regression test added. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `graphics/blend/BlendComposite.java` `compose` + `BlendMode.java` L124-388
  reason: float HSL arithmetic rounded differently from upstream's fixed-point pipeline
- **FIX — CFF + Type1 parse-fuzz (56 pins): `Type1Font.create_with_pfb` empty//truncated segment-2 contract aligned to upstream.** A .pfb with an empty eexec block raised `ValueError` from the eexec decryptor; upstream `Type1Parser.decrypt` returns empty plaintext for empty//short ciphertext (`cipherBytes.length == 0 || n > cipherBytes.length`) and parses to an empty font. Fixed at the call site (`type1_parser.py`: `len(eexec_cipher) < 4` → empty decrypted binary; the intentionally-strict `Type1FontUtil.eexec_decrypt` untouched) + `type1_font.py` `_charstrings_dict` now defaults to `{}` (upstream's always-initialised LinkedHashMap). Fuzz corpora (seed 1507): CFF 24 mutation classes pinned (header//Name INDEX//Top DICT//CharStrings//Private//GSubr//truncations//flips) with the fontTools-vs-FontBox library gaps characterized not pinned (header version//hdrSize//offSize validation polarity; eager geometry validation vs full-recompile tolerance — the wave-1505//1506 carve-out precedent); Type1 25 classes pinned (PFB segment headers//truncations//eexec flips//token corruption) with the pre-existing tolerant-binary-parse divergence documented. New probes `CffParserFuzzProbe` + `Type1ParserFuzzProbe`. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/cff/CFFParser.java` + `fontbox/type1/Type1Parser.java` (`decrypt`) + `Type1Font.createWithPFB`
  reason: pypdfbox's strict eexec rejection and missing-key propagation diverged from upstream's lenient empty-segment-2 contract
- **FIX — CCITTFaxDecode `/Rows==0` with no `/Height` now emits zero bytes, mirroring upstream exactly; row discovery relocated to the decoder-stream API.** Upstream `CCITTFaxFilter.decode` never estimates rows — `arraySize = rowBytes * max(rows,height) == 0` → 0-byte result; pypdfbox's filter-level `_estimate_rows` was an invention. Blast radius: the real image path always carries `/Height` (estimation never fired in production); the one genuine consumer was the standalone `CCITTFaxDecoderStream` API, whose row-discovery contract is preserved by relocating `_estimate_rows` into `_ensure_decoded` (feeds the filter an estimated `/Rows` up front). Oracle `ccitt_g4_zero_rows` flipped ok-only → EXACT (byte-identical zero-length both sides); +1 hand-written height-from-dict test. Closes the wave-1506 deferral. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `filter/CCITTFaxFilter.java` `decode`
  reason: filter-level row estimation diverged from upstream's empty allocation; estimation belongs only to the row-discovering stream consumer
- **FIX — `PDShadingType6`//`PDShadingType7` now extend `PDMeshBasedShadingType` (was `PDShading` directly), restoring the upstream hierarchy; `PatchMeshesShadingContext` was dead//broken code and now works.** The concrete types didn't inherit the base (a hierarchy divergence vs upstream `PDShadingType6/7 extends PDMeshBasedShadingType`), so `collect_patches` existed only as a `NotImplementedError` stub the render context called into — the Type 6/7 patch-mesh render path via `Type6/7ShadingPaint.create_context` could never produce patches. Implemented concrete `collect_patches` on the base mirroring upstream `collectPatches` (decodes through the wave-1504-pinned `parse_patch_stream`, applies matrix-then-xform device transform, builds triangulated `CoonsPatch`//`TensorPatch`); concrete types reparented to inherit it; `parse_patches` preserved as the geometry-only lite path. Verified end-to-end (real Type 6 → populated pixel table); wave-1504 `PatchMeshDecodeProbe` oracle stays green (parser untouched). 6 hand-written tests + 1 retargeted. Closes the wave-1504 deferral. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `graphics/shading/PDMeshBasedShadingType.java` `collectPatches` + the `PDShadingType6`//`PDShadingType7` ancestry
  reason: collapsed hierarchy left the patch-mesh shading context unreachable — a CLAUDE.md hierarchy-preservation violation latent since the original port

## Wave 1506 — jbig2 CX fix + ccitt//dct closures + flatten//merger audits + ttf fuzz (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **PARITY — TTF/OTF parse-fuzz: 37 SFNT-mutation fingerprints pinned identical to Apache FontBox 3.0.7; 0 pypdfbox bugs; 3 library-gap divergences characterized.** Corpus (seed 1506, base `DejaVuSansMono.ttf`): header numTables//searchRange//entrySelector//rangeShift corruption, 10 truncation shapes, directory bad-tag//checksum corruption, head version//magic//flags//unitsPerEm 0//1//0xFFFF//macStyle//glyphDataFormat, cmap numSubtables=0, maxp numGlyphs shrink, OS/2 version//weight, 6 deterministic bit flips — projection ok+numGlyphs+unitsPerEm+tables+advance widths+cmap(0x41) via new `TtfParserFuzzProbe` (non-embedded arm, matching pypdfbox's default). NOT pinned (characterized in docstrings, wave-1505 CCITT//libtiff precedent): (1) fontTools `lazy=True` defers table decode where FontBox `parseTables` force-loads — malformed glyf//loca//hmtx//bogus-maxp//OOB-cmap throw at parse upstream but at access in pypdfbox (matching would defeat the lazy-loading behavioral-compat rule); (2) fontTools hard-rejects an unknown sfntVersion where FontBox walks on without validating; (3) PDFBOX-5285 drops a past-EOF non-required directory entry where fontTools keeps it. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/ttf/TTFParser.java` + `parseTables`
  reason: font-parser robustness contract had no differential pin; the three gaps follow from the library-first fontTools backend
- **FIX — CCITTFaxDecode libtiff-strictness gap + DCTDecode empty-body divergence closed (filter lenient-decode contract).** (1) PDFBox's pure-Java `CCITTFaxDecoderStream` NEVER throws on a malformed body — it pre-allocates `(cols+7)/8*rows` (zero-filled), decodes what it can, swallows the EOF on the rest, and inverts the whole buffer when `/BlackIs1` is false. pypdfbox decoded via libtiff (Pillow), which raised `OSError` on truncated//garbage//empty input. `CCITTFaxDecode.decode` now wraps libtiff failures (and the empty-body case, which previously short-circuited to 0 bytes) in a deterministic `_zero_fill_result`: `rows*rowBytes` of `0xFF` (default) // `0x00` (`/BlackIs1`), empty when the row count is unknown — reproducing upstream's "decoded 0 rows" outcome byte-for-byte. (2) `DCTFilter.decode` feeds an empty stream to ImageIO and throws (no SOI marker); pypdfbox short-circuited to ok//0 bytes — early-return dropped, empty//unreadable DCT body now raises `OSError` (image read path unaffected — XObjects stop the chain before `/DCTDecode`). The 4 CCITT + 1 DCT fuzz mutants wave 1505 omitted are restored: libtiff-failure zero-fill cases pinned EXACT (pypdfbox-constructed buffer, not subject to the libtiff EOD carve-out), partial-decode + `/Rows==0` row-discovery cases ok-only. Fuzz corpus 75→82 mutants at parity. 3 stale pins retargeted; 2 hand-written zero-fill cases added. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `filter/CCITTFaxFilter.java` + `CCITTFaxDecoderStream.java` (`decode`//`readFromDecoderStream`//`fetch`) + `filter/DCTFilter.java`
  reason: libtiff is stricter than PDFBox's pure-Java fax decoder (which zero-fills instead of throwing), and PDFBox's DCT decode throws on an empty body where pypdfbox returned 0 bytes
- **FIX — `PDAcroForm.flatten` baked hidden//invisible widgets into page content + left nested fields dangling in `/Kids` (2 bugs).** (1) `_render_widget_appearance` never checked the widget `/F` Invisible//Hidden flags, so a hidden field's appearance was drawn into the visible page where upstream's `isVisibleAnnotation` (`!isInvisible() && !isHidden()`) skips the draw — the widget is still removed from `/Annots` either way; gate added at the top of the render helper. (2) Partial `flatten(fields, ...)` removed fields only from the root `/Fields` array, leaving a nested terminal field dangling (widget-less) in its parent's `/Kids` after its widget was stripped — now routed through the upstream-faithful `remove_fields` (parent `/Kids` or root `/Fields` via `COSArray.remove_object`); the empty-terminal early-return dropped (upstream removes passed fields regardless of whether a widget rendered). Rest of the audit at parity: refresh-appearances arm, `resolveTransformationMatrix` transformed-BBox math, `/AS` state-dict selection, `Fm<n>` resource naming, content-stream APPEND array promotion, `/XFA` removal + `/SigFlags` clear, dynamic-XFA early-out. 4 tests added (incl. the previously-skipped upstream `testFlattenSpecificFieldsOnly` count invariant); 3 tests corrected from pinned-buggy to upstream-faithful. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/form/PDAcroForm.java` `flatten()` L259-310 (`isVisibleAnnotation` gate) + `removeFields()` L853
  reason: hidden-field appearances leaked into flattened output and partial flatten corrupted nested field trees
- **FIX — `PDFMergerUtility` catalog-scalar merges aligned to upstream gating: `/MarkInfo` now merged, `/Lang` + `/ViewerPreferences` moved into the structure-tree block, invented `/PageLayout` merge dropped.** Upstream `appendDocument` runs `mergeMarkInfo`/`mergeLanguage`/`mergeViewerPreferences` as the final statements INSIDE the `if (mergeStructTree)` block (L848-850) — they fire only when the structure tree is merged; there is NO `/PageLayout` merge arm. pypdfbox merged `/Lang` + `/ViewerPreferences` inline (unconditional first-source-wins), merged an invented `/PageLayout`, and never merged `/MarkInfo` (the method existed since the struct-tree wave but was never called). Oracle-confirmed both arms: untagged merge → none of the four carried; tagged merge (PDFA3A) → `/MarkInfo {Marked true, Suspects false}`, `/Lang (de-DE)`, `/ViewerPreferences {DisplayDocTitle true}` — pypdfbox now matches exactly. `/PageMode` merge kept (documented enhancement: upstream's `getPageMode()` bakes the spec default making its guard dead code). ALSO PINNED — merge OUTPUT byte geometry: under a matched save strategy (NO_COMPRESSION both sides) the merged object numbering//types//header are byte-identical to PDFBox for plain concatenation, isolating the default-merge byte diff entirely to the documented uncompressed-(pypdfbox `save()`)-vs-compressed-(PDFBox `mergeDocuments` default) full-save strategy; compress-saving the pypdfbox merge lands within the deflate-envelope residual (1119 vs 1121 bytes). New `MergeObjectGeometryProbe` + 2 oracle tests; 5 stale pins retargeted + 1 positive gating test. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `multipdf/PDFMergerUtility.java` `appendDocument` L848-850 (`mergeMarkInfo`//`mergeLanguage`//`mergeViewerPreferences`)
  reason: three catalog scalars diverged from upstream's struct-tree-gated merge semantics and one merge arm was invented
- **FIX — JBIG2 `SegmentHeader` holds decoded segment data by a STRONG reference, not a weakref; the last arithmetic arcs land bit-exact.** Upstream (`apache/pdfbox-jbig2` `SegmentHeader.java:76,382`) caches via `SoftReference` — cleared only under memory pressure, i.e. effectively strong for a normal decode; Python's `weakref` dropped the referent the instant the local reference died, so a context-reuse SD's retained coding context was GC'd mid-decode (`base.cx is None` → AttributeError, the wave-1505 deferral). Strong reference is the faithful analog; lazy contract preserved (lazy create on first `get_segment_data()`, explicit `clean_segment_data()` release already called by `JBIG2Page._clear_segment_data()` — no leak). Arcs landed on top: CONTEXT-REUSE (SDCONTEXTUSED=1, 2 imported + 2 new symbols) decodes bit-exact in pypdfbox — golden-pinned, with the bundled jar's own fault documented both-sides (it cannot decode the stream through ANY access shape: `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` in `getToExportFlags` — the export-bounds guard upstream later added is absent from the jar; new probe `Jbig2SymbolDictReuseProbe` proves base-first access faults too); SDHUFF=0 SINGLE-INSTANCE REFINEMENT (IAID//IARDX//IARDY + GenericRefinementRegion template-1) bit-exact vs the jar (identity//changed-target//shifted); AGGREGATE route (IAAI>1 → one-strip arithmetic TextRegion) bit-exact for in-bounds placements. MQ encoder gains `encode_refinement_region_template1` + `Cx.copy()`. 10 new tests (4 round-trip + 6 oracle). (A)
  upstream: `apache/pdfbox-jbig2` `SegmentHeader.java` (SoftReference) + bundled 3.0.7 jar differentials; ITU-T T.88 refinement procedure
  reason: weakref was an unfaithful translation of SoftReference; the remaining encoder-blocked arithmetic arcs needed the refinement-region encoder

## Wave 1505 — stripper accumulator + MQ encoder + multipdf audits + filter fuzz (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — `PDFTextStripper` line-overlap accumulator: the upright unrotated path now tracks the line's MIN user-Y baseline (reset `+inf`, update `<=`) instead of the literal MAX.** A single visual line mixing very different glyph heights (a huge-font run between normal-font anchors) now merges onto one line as upstream does. Root cause proven by transforming upstream `overlap` via `device_y = -user_y`: the y-up mirror of upstream's `maxYForLine = MAX(positionY)` (y-down device frame) is the MIN user-Y, and `_overlaps_line`'s two span clauses were already the matching y-up transform — only the accumulation direction was inverted. Per-axis predicate `min_y_line_axis = not _flip_axes and _page_rotation == 0`: the rotated path (where `_apply_page_rotation` stores upstream's device-frame `getY()`, so the literal MAX IS upstream verbatim) and the flip-axes path keep `-inf`//`>=` — `test_right_angle_rotation_byte_exact[90]/[270]` stays byte-exact. The wave-1504 `font_size_huge` content-fuzz mutant is re-added and matches Java (`LEFTHFSRIGHT` one-line merge). Closes the wave-1504 deferral. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` `writePage` L689-692 (`maxYForLine`) + `overlap` L762-766
  reason: the lite stripper carries Y in user-space y-up; keeping upstream's device-frame comparator literally inverted the accumulation on the upright path
- **FIX — `Overlay.float2String` was not byte-faithful (decimal formatting of the overlay placement matrix).** `_float_to_string` formatted the 64-bit double via `f"{v:.10f}"`; upstream casts to `(float)` (32-bit) and renders the shortest round-trip digits through `new BigDecimal(String.valueOf(floatValue)).toPlainString()` then strips trailing zeros keeping `.0`. Diverged for any non-half-integer matrix entry (`123456.789`→`123456.79`, `66.66666`f→`66.666664`, `1e8`→`100000000` with no trailing `.0`). Now ports the exact pipeline (new `_java_float_to_string` replicates `Float.toString` incl. E-notation thresholds; `Decimal(...)` `"f"` format mimics `toPlainString`; negative-zero normalized as BigDecimal does). Verified 400/400 random float32 values + 26 golden cases against live Java (new probe `Float2StringProbe`, verbatim inline of the package-private method). Rest of the audit: LayerUtility full parity (PAGE_TO_FORM_FILTER, cloneMerge, matrix sequence, OCG append); PDFCloneUtility parity (upstream's HashMap cache keys by identity for dicts/arrays — `id()` keying observationally equivalent; cycle-hardening already pinned). (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `multipdf/Overlay.java` `float2String`
  reason: 32-bit-float shortest-round-trip + `BigDecimal.toPlainString` exponent expansion differ from a fixed-10-digit double format; affects overlay content-stream bytes
- **PARITY (pinned divergence, now documented) — `Overlay.calculateAffineTransform` uses the real lower-left corner (PDFBOX-6048).** pypdfbox centres via `(pageW-overlayW)/2 + pageLLX - overlayLLX` (and the Y analogue); upstream 3.0.7 uses only `(pageW-overlayW)/2` (assumes media-box origin (0,0)). Deliberate 4.0 alignment per CLAUDE.md; the overlay placement stream is therefore intentionally NOT byte-identical to the 3.0.7 oracle for pages/overlays whose media box does not start at the origin. Unit-pinned in `tests/multipdf/test_overlay.py` + `test_overlay_wave1370.py`; was undocumented in CHANGES.md until now. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `multipdf/Overlay.java` `calculateAffineTransform`
  reason: 3.0.7 has the PDFBOX-6048 (0,0)-origin bug; pypdfbox follows the PDFBox 4.0 fix
- **FIX — `Splitter` materialises inherited page geometry; `PageExtractor` delegates to `Splitter`.** (1) `Splitter.process_page` now re-applies `set_crop_box`/`set_media_box`/`set_rotation` after `import_page`, matching upstream `PDDocument.importPage` (PDDocument.java L700-702) which pypdfbox's `import_page` omits — a page that INHERITED `/MediaBox`//`/CropBox`//`/Rotate` from a page-tree node previously fell back to Letter/0 defaults in the split output (reproduced: inherited `[0,0,200,300]` → resolved `[0,0,612,792]`). (2) `PageExtractor.extract` now delegates to `Splitter` (upstream parity — upstream builds a Splitter, sets start/end/splitAtPage, returns `split().get(0)`; the old bespoke page-walk was a wave-708 stop-gap from before Splitter was ported), restoring upstream's `IllegalArgumentException`→`ValueError` on an out-of-document range (start=9,end=9 on a 2-page doc; oracle-confirmed) where the old walk silently returned an empty doc, and picking up Splitter's annotation cloning + struct-tree clone + `/B` removal. New probe `SplitterInheritProbe` + 4 oracle cases pin per-page MediaBox/CropBox/rotation byte-identical; 10 hand-written structural tests; 3 stale pins retargeted. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `multipdf/Splitter.java` + `multipdf/PageExtractor.java` + `PDDocument.importPage` L700-702
  reason: `import_page` deep-copy did not re-materialise inheritable geometry, and PageExtractor reimplemented the page walk instead of delegating
- **PARITY — test-only MQ-arithmetic encoder unlocks the JBIG2 SDHUFF=0 arithmetic decode spans; 4 paths pinned bit-exact vs the jar; 1 latent pypdfbox bug found (deferred).** New `tests/jbig2/helpers/mq_encoder.py`: faithful T.88 Annex E.3 MQ ENCODER (INITENC/ENCODE/CODEMPS/CODELPS/RENORME/BYTEOUT/FLUSH; imports the production `QE` table so encode/decode cannot drift; byte-stuffing/carry model cross-checked against agl/jbig2enc `jbig2arith.cc`) + `ArithmeticIntegerEncoder` (A.2 buckets + sign + OOB; A.3 IAID) — 20 round-trip tests. `jb2_encoder.py` gains template-0 generic-region bitmap encoding + arithmetic SD builders. Oracle-pinned bit-exact: single-symbol, multi-symbol same height-class, multi-height-class, and the arithmetic IMPORT-CHAIN (second SD imports + re-exports a base SD's symbols — `_retrieve_import_symbols`//IAEX over mixed symbols, previously Huffman-only). FOUND, not fixed (test-only surface): the coding-context-reuse arc crashes on cold//by-number decode — `SegmentHeader` caches segment data by WEAKREF (`segment_header.py:92`), so the base SD's retained bitmap CX is GC'd between import-retrieval and `_adopt_retained_coding_contexts` (`base.cx is None` → AttributeError); the bundled jar ALSO faults on the same probe access (`ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` in `getToExportFlags`). Deferred with fix path (strong-hold referred SDs through adoption). (B)
  upstream: ITU-T T.88 Annex E.3 (no upstream encoder — PDFBox ships none); decode targets `jbig2/segments/SymbolDictionary` arithmetic branches vs the bundled 3.0.7 jar
  reason: SDHUFF=0 arithmetic bodies were the last encoder-blocked fixture-starved spans; the MQ encoder removes the blocker
- **FIX — filter-decode fuzz (75 mutants, 7 filters): 3 lenient-decode parity bugs fixed; library-gap divergences characterized.** (1) `FlateDecode.decode` used one-shot `zlib.decompress` (strict Error -5 on truncation), raising where upstream's `FlateFilterDecoderStream` (PDFBOX-1232) keeps already-inflated bytes — the lenient wrapper was already ported but UNUSED; decode now routes through it (truncated→partial, garbage→empty, garbage-tail→full, oracle-confirmed; the raw-deflate-no-zlib-header fallback is retained, deliberately more lenient than PDFBox's 0-byte result). (2) `LZWDecode` raised `OSError` on a corrupt/out-of-range code where upstream `LZWFilter.doLZWDecode` throws `EOFException` caught as a lenient premature-EOF stop keeping partial output — now raises `EOFError` into the existing handler. (3) Predictor `_unpng` raised on an unknown per-row PNG filter tag (>4) where upstream `decodePredictorRow`'s `default: break` passes the row through unchanged (a wrong-/Columns geometry mis-aligns tag bytes) — decode-side passthrough now; encode-side still raises (programming error). 67/75 mutants pinned byte-exact (ok+len+sha), 8 ok-only. Documented (not forced): CCITT libtiff strictness (PDFBox's pure-Java decoder never throws — pre-allocates and fills what it can; new deferral), empty-DCT short-circuit, ASCII85 truncated-final-group harness artifact. 7 stale wrong-direction pins realigned. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `filter/FlateFilter.java` (PDFBOX-1232 decoder stream) + `LZWFilter.doLZWDecode` L119 + `Predictor.decodePredictorRow` (default passthrough)
  reason: malformed-stream PDFs now load identically to PDFBox instead of aborting on recoverable filter defects

## Wave 1504 — type6//7 patches + jbig2 follow-ons + fdf-export + cs-fuzz + pubkey-interop (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **PARITY — FDF/XFDF export path confirmed byte-exact vs PDFBox 3.0.7; new byte-level oracle.** `FDFDocument.write_xml`/`FDFCatalog.write_xml`/`FDFDictionary.write_xml`/`FDFField.write_xml`/`save_xfdf` produce byte-identical XFDF (envelope incl. `<?xml?>` + `xfdf` namespace + `xml:space="preserve"`, `<f>`/`<ids>` (uppercase hex via `to_hex_string`)/`<fields>` body, raw-unescaped field names, repeated `<value>` for lists, `<value-richtext>`, `/Kids` recursion, `\n` separators, UTF-8) to upstream — new `FdfProbe` `xfdfbytes` mode emits the raw `writeXML(Writer)` bytes verbatim for direct comparison. Re-confirmed upstream `FDFDictionary.writeXML` emits no `<annots>` (the wave-1208 carve-out is genuine upstream behaviour) and `FDFAnnotation` has no `writeXML` (no per-annotation serialiser gap). Binary `FDFDocument.save` (`%FDF-` header, `COSWriter(fdf=True)`) at parity. No production change. **Pinned LOW divergence (not fixed):** `escape_xml` for an astral char (> U+FFFF) emits one `&#NNNNNN;` (code point) vs Java's two surrogate-half `&#NNNNN;` entities (UTF-16 code units); BMP identical — the realistic FDF domain. 1 byte-exact oracle + 2 hand-written pins. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/fdf/FDFDocument.java` + `FDFDictionary.java` + `FDFField.java` `writeXML`
  reason: audit — export direction had no byte-level pin; astral-char escape difference documented
- **PARITY — public-key encryption multi-recipient cross-implementation interop pinned both directions; no divergence.** pypdfbox encrypts N recipients with distinct permission masks → Java PDFBox opens with each recipient's own PKCS#12 key, recovers identical content, and surfaces that recipient's OWN AccessPermission mask (`new AccessPermission(accessBytes).getPermissionBytes()` == the 4 bytes pypdfbox wrote via `get_permission_bytes_for_public_key()`, masked 0x00000FFF); `/Recipients` holds exactly N envelopes in policy iterator order. Java→pypdfbox multi-recipient confirmed to hit the same pinned RC2-CBC CMS read-gap (one RC2 envelope per recipient). Upstream-source verifications: `prepareDocumentForEncryption` emits SUBFILTER5 for BOTH case 4 (AES-128) and case 5 (AES-256); `prepareForDecryption` never dispatches on `/SubFilter` (reads `/Recipients` from the default crypt filter), so the wave-1502 s5 shape is self-consistent. No write-path change needed. `PublicKeyDecryptProbe` gained a PERMS projection; new `PublicKeyEncryptMultiProbe` (N recipients, each certDer:permInt, policy order); 6 new oracle tests (AES-128 + AES-256 parametrised). No persistent cert fixture (per-run shared self-signed cert pattern retained). (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/encryption/PublicKeySecurityHandler.java` `prepareDocumentForEncryption` (case 4//5 → `setSubFilter(SUBFILTER5)`) + `prepareForDecryption`
  reason: completes the wave-1502 write-path work with live differential round-trips of the new multi-recipient write shape
- **FIX — content-stream operator fuzz: `PDFTextStripper` lite interpreter now gates text-showing/positioning operators on an open BT…ET text object (67-mutant corpus, 8 divergences → one root cause).** Upstream `ShowText`/`ShowTextAdjusted` skip when `getTextMatrix() == null` and `MoveText` skips when `getTextLineMatrix() == null` (both matrices identity inside BT…ET, null outside); the lite `_dispatch` emitted glyphs and advanced the cursor for `Tj`/`TJ`/`'`/`"`/`Td`/`TD`/`T*` regardless — text stranded outside a text object (a `TJ` between `ET` and the next `BT`, or a `Tj` whose `BT` was lost to truncation) was extracted where Java silently ignores it. New `_TextState.in_text_object` flag (set by `BT` AND by `Tm` — `SetMatrix` makes both matrices non-null even outside BT, faithful to upstream — cleared by `ET`) gates those 7 operators; `"` still sets `Tw`/`Tc` unconditionally before the gate. Corpus: 67 deterministic mutants (seed 1504; raw `/Contents` injection into a hand-emitted template so bytes are identical both sides) over missing/wrong-type operands, unbalanced q//Q + BT//ET, unknown ops ± BX//EX, inline-image corruption, missing resources, truncation, division-shaped edges, byte flips — 60 matched out of the box, 8 fixed by the gate, 1 dropped as out-of-scope (large-font line-merge — a line-grouping geometry edge in the `max_y_for_line` accumulator, new DEFERRED item; flipping it regresses rotated-text byte-exact pins). 3 branch-coverage tests retargeted to open the text object first. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/operator/text/ShowText.java` + `ShowTextAdjusted.java` (textMatrix null-skip) + `MoveText.java` (textLineMatrix null-skip)
  reason: the lite stripper extracted text Apache PDFBox silently ignores — surfaced by the wave-1504 operator fuzz
- **PARITY — Type 6/7 patch-mesh model-layer decode pinned against live PDFBox; no production divergence.** New package-private probe `PatchMeshDecodeProbe` (in `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.shading`, the parse-layer counterpart to wave-1503's `MeshVertexDumpProbe`) dumps the reshaped 4×4 `Patch.controlPoints` grid + `Patch.cornerColor` per decoded patch. pypdfbox matches byte-for-byte across all three edge-flag continuation chains (0=new full patch; 1/2/3=4 reused boundary points + 2 reused corner colours), control-point dequantisation, per-flag colour order, Coons 12→4×4 and tensor 16→4×4 reshapes (interior `square[1][1]=tcp[12]`, `square[2][1]=tcp[15]`), all with non-byte-aligned 12-bit fields. The Types 6/7 NO-per-patch-byte-align contract (unlike Types 4/5) is pinned with a negative control proving a spurious align desyncs the stream. 5 parity + 1 fixture-guard + 1 negative-control tests. Orphan-port note deferred: `PatchMeshesShadingContext.collect_patches` calls a method absent on the concrete types (never instantiated — live renderer uses `parse_patches`). (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `PDMeshBasedShadingType.collectPatches`/`readPatch` + `CoonsPatch`/`TensorPatch.reshapeControlPoints` + `Patch.getFlagNEdge`/`getFlagNColor`
  reason: audit — render-grid oracles can mask a parse-layer divergence; this isolates and pins the decoder
- **PARITY — JBIG2 segment-graph decode paths pinned bit-exact + the SD-aggregate edge-clip divergence root-caused (jar bug, fixed upstream by PDFBOX-6156; pypdfbox correct).** (1) Root cause: the `0x81`-vs-`0x80` aggregate divergence is in `Bitmaps.blit`, not the placement arithmetic (which is byte-identical to the standalone `apache/pdfbox-jbig2` source). `javap -c` proves the bundled 3.0.7 jar's `Bitmaps` has NO `blitByPixel` — it carries the pre-fix shifted-blit that mis-clips the edge pixel of a non-byte-aligned instance overhanging the region edge; pypdfbox ports the corrected PDFBOX-6156 per-pixel fallback (`bitmaps.py:404-413`), so its byte is correct. Pinned both-sides (same shape as the template-1 `referenceDX<0` precedent); root-cause citation recorded in the test docstrings. (2) Encoder extended: cross-SD import-symbols arc (base SD exports 2 symbols → referring SD imports both + adds 1 + re-exports 3; exercises `_retrieve_import_symbols`/imported-export branch) and the type-53 user-supplied custom Huffman code-table arc (text region `SBHUFFFS==3` → `_get_user_table` + `EncodedTable`/`Table` parse) — both byte-for-byte identical jar-vs-pypdfbox. `text_region.py` 80%→82%. Remaining starved spans need an MQ-arithmetic ENCODER (does not exist; the test encoder is Huffman-only) — deferral narrowed. 2 oracle tests. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 bundled jbig2 plugin jar + standalone `apache/pdfbox-jbig2` `Bitmaps.java:573-582` (PDFBOX-6156) + `segments/{SymbolDictionary,TextRegion,Table}` + `decoder/huffman/EncodedTable`
  reason: fixture-starved segment-graph branches now proven at parity; the aggregate divergence is a documented bundled-jar bug, not a pypdfbox defect

## Wave 1503 — incremental-save/fuzz/jbig2/mesh-shading/sign-designer surfaces (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — `PDVisibleSignDesigner` `transform()` now defensive-copies + `zoom`/`width`/`height`/`set_image` formatter-rect casts are float32 bit-exact.** (1) `transform(at)` previously stored the supplied transform by reference; upstream stores `new AffineTransform(at)`. Now a three-tier defensive copy: component-snapshot into a fresh `_IdentityAffineTransform` for any conforming transform (upstream-faithful — covers the rotation path and any `m00..m12`-shaped object), `copy.copy` for other copyable objects, by-reference for uncopyable opaque handles (documented residual; Python can't copy an arbitrary opaque object). Mutation-isolation contract now holds. (2) Upstream stores `imageWidth`/`imageHeight` as 32-bit `Float`, so the `(int)` casts populating `formatterRectangleParameters[2]/[3]` in `zoom`/`width`/`height`/`set_image` are single-precision; the wave-1500 port computed in float64 and rounded the other way at integer boundaries (2M-case random search vs Java: e.g. w=621.1739 p=66.62 → Java 1035, float64 1034). New `_f32` helper narrows stored dimensions + the `zoom` arithmetic chain (incl. the `percent` param) to float32. `VisibleSignDesignerProbe` zoom mode + 8 oracle cases (5 previously-divergent boundaries) pin the rect bit-exact; 12 hand-written tests pin the defensive-copy tiers. `PDVisibleSigProperties` audited clean (pure builder, parity). (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/digitalsignature/visible/PDVisibleSignDesigner.java` (L285 zoom, L360-381 width/height, L453-462 setImage, L477-481 transform)
  reason: transform stored by reference (no defensive copy) + formatter-rect int casts diverged from Java's single-precision rounding at boundaries
- **FIX — appended xref-STREAM dictionary key order now byte-identical to PDFBox on incremental save.** `_do_write_xref_stream_increment` emitted the appended `/Type /XRef` dict as `/Filter /Length /Type /XRef /Size /W /Index /Root /Info /ID /Prev` — valid but structurally divergent. Upstream's insertion-ordered `COSStream` keySet emits `/Length` (constructor-seeded `setInt(LENGTH,0)`) → the `addTrailerInfo` subset `/Info /Root /Encrypt /ID /Prev` IN TRAILER-ITERATION ORDER → `/Type /Size /Index /W` (`getStream`) → `/Filter` (`createOutputStream`, last). Rebuilt the dict in that exact sequence (pre-seeding `/Length 0` since pypdfbox's `COSStream.__init__` doesn't; copying the trailer subset by iterating the source trailer's own key order with the fresh `/Prev` substituted in place). The full appended tail of an incremental save is now byte-for-byte identical to PDFBox 3.0.7 across xref-table and xref-stream sources (only the regenerated `/ID[1]` differs, per ISO 32000-1 §14.4). New `IncrementalTailBytesProbe` pins the whole tail by hex (7 oracle cases over 6 fixtures + `/ID` contract) + 2 oracle-free key-order guards. Signing-path audit clean (space-fill inside `[`…`]` brackets matches upstream `doWriteSignature`; wave-1502 conventions hold). Closes the wave-1499 ordering residual. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/PDFXRefStream.java` `getStream` L67-116 + `addTrailerInfo` L123-133; `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` `doWriteXRefInc` L716-755; `cos/COSStream.java` constructor L81
  reason: pypdfbox built the xref-stream dict in an ad-hoc order; upstream's insertion-ordered keySet fixes a specific emit sequence the appended tail must reproduce byte-for-byte
- **FIX — lenient load-path mutation-fuzz parity: 4 real recovery bugs aligned to upstream (rebaselined Wave 1520).** (1) Brute-force recovery aborted the whole load on per-object body defects (truncated streams, garbled obj keywords) — upstream `COSObject.getObject` catches the IOException and leaves that one object null; `_resolve_recovered_objects` now swallows per-object dereference errors. (2) Inflated object-stream `/N` raised "header truncated" — upstream `PDFObjectStreamParser.privateReadObjectOffsets` breaks at the `/First` boundary; `_read_object_stream_offsets` now does too. (3) A catalog packed inside an object stream was unrecoverable on full brute-force rebuild ("Missing root") — ported `bfSearchForObjStreams` + the second `searchForTrailerItems` pass (`COSParser.bf_search_for_obj_stream_members` + `PDFParser._recover_obj_stream_trailer_items`). (4) Object-number/generation mismatch between an xref offset and the `n g obj` header is rejected by `_load_indirect_object_at`, and Wave 1520 aligned the outer `COSObject.get_object` catch so that failed dereference becomes null like upstream. Plus: truncated/garbled page-tree kids prune instead of aborting (`check_pages_dictionary` + `PDPageTree.get_kids` tolerate failed kid dereference) and the full-rebuild path runs `check_pages_dictionary` like `initialParse` (guarded so FDF still loads). Corpus: 42 mutants from `random.Random(1503)` over 3 inline fixtures; 4 catalog-null cases are xfailed under the existing deferred-`initial_parse`/catalog-synthesis architecture where PDFBox eagerly fails load and pypdfbox opens a 0-page shell. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `COSParser.parseFileObject` L729-734 / `BruteForceParser.{rebuildTrailer,bfSearchForObjStreams,searchForTrailerItems}` / `PDFObjectStreamParser.privateReadObjectOffsets` / `COSObject.getObject` L120 / `COSParser.checkPages`
  reason: pypdfbox diverged from upstream lenient recovery (aborted on recoverable defects, missed objstm-hidden catalogs, silently accepted gen mismatches)
- **PARITY — JBIG2 Huffman fixture-starved spans finally exercised: a test-only minimal JBIG2 encoder unblocked the long-deferred paths; 3 paths bit-exact vs the jar, 1 new divergence pinned both-sides.** The DEFERRED item ("a correct multi-segment Huffman+refinement .jb2 can't be hand-crafted minimally") is now overturned: `tests/jbig2/helpers/jb2_encoder.py` (446-line test-only encoder; segment headers, Annex-B standard Huffman tables, height-class collective bitmaps, run-code symbol-ID tables, refinement instances) builds complete valid streams fed IDENTICALLY to the bundled 3.0.7 jar (reflection probes) and pypdfbox. Bit-exact: SDHUFF=1/SDREFAGG=0 symbol dictionary (collective bitmap + export flags), SBHUFF=1/REFINE=0 text region (run-code table + strip placement), SBHUFF=1/REFINE=1 text region (refined instance, symInRefSize bound + seek). DIVERGES (pinned both-sides, new deferral): SDHUFF=1/SDREFAGG=1 aggregate route with two instances overlapping at the aggregate-region edge — pypdfbox clips the second instance at column width-1 (byte-1 `0x81`), the jar yields `0x80`; identical bits consumed, same final stream position; root-causing needs an instrumented Java probe; exotic edge, no known real-world trigger. Coverage: `symbol_dictionary.py` →82%, `text_region.py` 77%→80%. 5 golden-pin + 4 live-oracle tests; no production change. (C — salvaged after session-limit cut; verified green by orchestrator)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 bundled jbig2 plugin (jar, reflection probes `Jbig2SymbolDictByNrProbe`); ITU-T T.88 §6.4//§6.5 + Annex B
  reason: audit — fixture-starved decode bodies had never run against ground truth; the encoder-construction impossibility claim was wrong
- **FIX — mesh shading (Types 4/5) per-vertex byte alignment + lattice triangle vertex order.** (1) PDF 32000-1 §8.7.4.5.5 mandates each vertex's coord+colour data occupy a whole number of bytes (trailing partial bits are padding); upstream `PDTriangleBasedShadingType.readVertex` enforces it via `getBitOffset()` → `readBits(8 - bitOffset)`. pypdfbox omitted it — with a non-byte-multiple layout (e.g. 12-bit coordinates: vertex = 8+12+12+36 = 68 bits) every vertex after the first was read from the wrong bit offset, corrupting the whole mesh. Existing render oracles all used 8-bit layouts, masking the bug. New `_PatchBitReader.align_to_byte()` invoked per vertex by the Type 4/5 Gouraud readers (patch control points, Types 6/7, are deliberately NOT aligned — matching upstream). (2) Type 5 lattice stitching emitted the second cell triangle as (v2,v4,v3); upstream `PDShadingType5.createShadedTriangleList` emits (v2,v3,v4) — raster-invariant (Gouraud fill is winding-independent) but now structure-identical. Pinned by new model-layer oracle `MeshVertexDumpProbe` (lives in the `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.shading` package to reach the package-private `collectTriangles`/`ShadedTriangle.corner`/`.color`; coords+colours to 1e-4) + 6 hand-written alignment tests; 1 wave-1493 pin retargeted. Types 6/7 patch-geometry parity did not land before the session-limit cut — re-queued. (D — salvaged after session-limit cut; verified green by orchestrator, 3091 passed)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/shading/PDTriangleBasedShadingType.java` `readVertex` + `PDShadingType5.java` `createShadedTriangleList`
  reason: per-vertex byte-alignment was unported (silent mesh corruption on non-8-bit layouts) and the lattice triangle order diverged structurally

## Wave 1502 — full-save residuals + signature/xmpbox/pubkey-write surfaces (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — compressed full-save object-stream packing now mirrors PDFBox's value-hashed `COSObjectPool`.** A direct scalar (string/integer/float/name/boolean) value-equal to a registered indirect scalar is emitted as an `N G R` reference instead of inlined — upstream pools every object in a `HashMap<COSBase, COSObjectKey>` and the scalar COS types are value-hashed, so `COSWriterObjectStream.writeObject`'s `compressionPool.contains(base)` matches by value (acroform.pdf object 77's `[(BMW) 75 0 R (VW) (Audi)]` had regressed to inline `(Porsche)`). `_ObjStmPoolShim` now takes a value-pool (lowest object number wins per value; containers stay identity-matched). Decoded ObjStm bodies are byte-identical (SHA match) to PDFBox on acroform / PDFBOX-3110-poems-beads / unencrypted; full-save byte sizes: acroform 20759==20759 (was 1 off), unencrypted 69446==69446 (held), PDFBOX-3110 41572 vs 41581 — residual is solely the xref-stream deflate envelope (zlib vs `java.util.zip.Deflater`; raw lengths equal, decode identical, `/W`//`/Index` pinned). Oracle decoded-body pin broadened from unencrypted-only to all three fixtures; 3 hand-written value-equality tests. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfwriter/compress/COSObjectPool.java` + `COSWriterCompressionPool.java` + `COSWriterObjectStream.java`
  reason: `_ObjStmPoolShim` matched pool membership by `id()` only, inlining value-equal scalars
- **FIX — digital signatures: `/ByteRange` delimiter convention converged to upstream (writer + reader, real interop fix).** pypdfbox's signing writer emitted `/ByteRange` with the `<`/`>` /Contents delimiters INSIDE the two hashed ranges, while PDFBox's COSWriter (`doWriteSignature`: `beforeLength=signatureOffset`, `afterOffset=signatureOffset+signatureLength`) emits them OUTSIDE — Java's `getContents(byte[])` on a pypdfbox-signed doc dropped the first/last hex nibble, and `get_contents_from_bytes` carried a lenient two-convention gap-walk to read either layout. Aligned the writer (`PDDocument._render_incremental_with_placeholder`, both save_incremental + external-signing paths) and `sig_utils.compute_byte_range` to brackets-EXCLUDED, replaced the reader's gap-walk with upstream's exact `begin = br[0]+br[1]+1; len = br[2]-begin-1` (defensive `<`/`>` strip delegated to `get_converted_contents`), and fixed `set_byte_range` to call `ary.set_direct(True)` mirroring upstream. Two new live-oracle probes pin it: `SignByteRangeConventionProbe` (PDFBox's own signer writes brackets-EXCLUDED) and `SignatureContentsByteRangeProbe` (pypdfbox-signed doc re-sliced by Java's `getContents(byte[])` == embedded COSString == pypdfbox, byte-for-byte). Accessor audit otherwise clean; one pinned intentional divergence retained (`set_byte_range` raises ValueError on len≠4 where upstream no-ops). Cross-library sign/verify 38/38 both directions; 7 stale pins retargeted + 4 reader tests reworked + 2 new oracle pins. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `PDSignature.java` `getContents(byte[])` L354-361 + `getConvertedContents` + `setByteRange` L290; `COSWriter.java` `doWriteSignature` L873-883
  reason: the lenient reader masked a genuine writer divergence; aligning the writer removes a silent first/last-nibble truncation when a pypdfbox-signed doc is read by Java PDFBox
- **FIX — xmpbox `rdf:parseType="Resource"` structured types: parse + serialize divergences closed (the wave-1499 deferral's "no known divergence" was wrong — two real bugs).** PARSE: `DomXmpParser` parsed a single `parseType="Resource"` structured property (`xmpMM:DerivedFrom`/`ManagedFrom`) as an EMPTY STRING instead of a typed `ResourceRefType`; ported upstream `manageStructuredType` + `parseLiDescription` via a new `_TYPED_STRUCT_REGISTRY` + `_try_parse_typed_struct` (strict==lenient, child fields `instanceID`/`documentID`/`renditionClass`/`versionID` surface typed). SERIALIZE: pypdfbox emitted `<stRef:DerivedFrom><stRef:instanceID>…` (field prefix, no wrapper); upstream `serializeFields` emits `<xmpMM:DerivedFrom><rdf:li rdf:parseType="Resource">…</rdf:li></xmpMM:DerivedFrom>` (schema-prefix wrapper) — `_append_field`/`serialize_fields` rewritten as a faithful port incl. `usePrefix`/`resourceNS` semantics. Upstream's known limitation is reproduced deliberately: inner `stRef:*` fields are emitted WITHOUT declaring `xmlns:stRef`, so the packet is not re-parseable by DomXmpParser ("prefix stRef not bound") — pinned, fidelity over correctness. Fixture hand-crafted (raw XMP bytes; the Java public-API path needs `setPropertyName` before `addProperty` to dodge the `normalizeAttributes` NPE — used in the probe's serialize mode). 3 oracle + 7 hand-written tests. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `xmpbox/xml/DomXmpParser.java` (`manageStructuredType`/`parseLiDescription`) + `xmpbox/xml/XmpSerializer.java` (`serializeFields` L185-206)
  reason: single structured (Cardinality.Simple) properties were never wired into the typed parse/serialize paths; serializer namespace omission matches upstream's PDFBOX-2378 shape
- **FIX — public-key handler write path: 2 invented behaviours reverted to upstream `prepareDocumentForEncryption`/`computeRecipientsField`.** (1) `/SubFilter` is `adbe.pkcs7.s5` for BOTH the AES-128 (V=4) and AES-256 (V=5) crypt-filter branches — `adbe.pkcs7.s4` is reserved for the legacy non-crypt-filter RC4 default branch only (pypdfbox's AES-only write path always emits s5; was emitting s4 for V=4). (2) ONE PKCS#7 envelope per recipient, in policy iterator order — upstream allocates `new byte[getNumberOfRecipients()][]` and calls `createDERForRecipient` once per recipient; an earlier wave had invented per-permission-mask grouping (recipients sharing a mask rode a single multi-recipient envelope), violating the no-invented-abstractions rule. `/Recipients` now has exactly N entries in iterator order and the file-key digest hashes them in that order. The 24-byte seed‖perms plaintext, `get_permission_bytes_for_public_key()` packing, and per-recipient round-trip through `prepare_for_decryption` all retained. Stale pins retargeted (per-mask grouping tests → one-envelope-per-recipient, incl. the wave-1367 four-recipient matrix pin). (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/encryption/PublicKeySecurityHandler.java` (`prepareDocumentForEncryption` switch case 4+5 → `setSubFilter(SUBFILTER5)`; `computeRecipientsField`)
  reason: per-mask envelope grouping and the V=4 s4 sub-filter were pypdfbox inventions contradicting upstream's write shape

- **CLEANUP — GsubWorkerForTamil: removed the `getbefore_reph_glyph_ids` parity alias added in wave 1396.** The alias was a camelCase-style parity hack for a method that does not exist anywhere upstream; canonical `get_before_reph_glyph_ids` retained (sibling workers spell the helper `getBeforeRephGlyphIds`). Method-parity surface re-verified against a fresh 3.0.7 checkout: **8783/8783 matched methods (100%), zero classes with missing methods** — the stale `.parity/parity.json` 8752/8766 figure came from a newer upstream tree; `XrefParser`, `GsubWorkerForTamil`, `DebugLogAppender` do not exist in 3.0.7 (Python-only supersets). Alias test file deleted. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 (class absent; siblings `fontbox/ttf/gsub/GsubWorkerFor{Devanagari,Gujarati}.java`)
  reason: alias matched no real Java method and violated the strict-snake_case/no-aliases rule

## Wave 1501 — objstm packing + stripper residue + colour/pubkey/outline audits (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **PARITY — outline-tree mutation pointer rewiring verified byte-for-byte; the cross-parent re-add stale-pointer quirk pinned.** Upstream surface confirmed via javap: `PDOutlineNode` exposes only `addLast`/`addFirst` (+ package-private helpers) and `PDOutlineItem` the two `insertSibling*` — there is NO `removeFromTree` upstream; pypdfbox's `remove_child` family is a documented pypdfbox-only convenience. New probe dumps the raw COS `/First /Last /Next /Prev /Parent` projections (previous pins covered only /Count and post-reload titles) across 7 mutation scenarios — all identical to the jar, including the quirk where a cross-parent re-add of an only child leaves the OLD parent's `/First`/`/Last` dangling at the moved node while BOTH parents take the child-count contribution (pinned so a future refactor can't "fix" it into a divergence). No production change. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/documentnavigation/outline/PDOutlineNode.java` + `PDOutlineItem.java`
  reason: audit — pointer-level mutation surface was unpinned
- **PARITY — CIE/special colour-space audit complete: every remaining space already at parity; Separation tint-cache projection pinned.** Read-and-verified against upstream source: `PDCalRGB`/`PDCalGray` unit-white-point gamma+matrix pipeline vs verbatim passthrough (PDFBOX-2553 hack) — already pinned; `PDLab` 6/29 linear segment, white-point scaling, XYZ<0 floor, and the empirical no-input-clamp finding — already pinned; `PDDeviceN` attributes/NChannel — already pinned. The one gap: `PDSeparation.toRGB` applies NO `/Domain` clamp to the tint ((-0.5)→0.25, 1.5→2.25, clamped only at round-to-255) and caches on the truncating `(int)(tint*255)` key — pypdfbox matches byte-for-byte over all 11 probe lines (DeviceRGB alternate, no CMM). No production change. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/color/PDSeparation.java` (L137 + toRGBMap) + `PDCalRGB.java` (L78) + `PDCalGray.java` (L84) + `PDLab.java` (L122)
  reason: audit — parity confirmed, tint-cache quantisation pinned
- **FIX — public-key handler read path: 3 divergences in `prepare_for_decryption` aligned to upstream.** (1) The decrypted recipient envelope must be EXACTLY 24 bytes (upstream `!= 24` reject, "The enveloped data does not contain 24 bytes") — pypdfbox only checked `< 20` and tolerated longer/shorter; (2) the recovered `AccessPermission` is now locked via `set_read_only()` (upstream L~268) and decoded via signed `int.from_bytes` mirroring the `AccessPermission(byte[])` ctor; (3) key length AND encrypt-metadata now source from the DEFAULT CRYPT FILTER dictionary first (non-zero /Length precedence, upstream L~221-238) with AES detected from the crypt-filter `/CFM` (AESV2/AESV3), not inferred from `V>=4`. 2 pre-existing tests had pinned the lenient non-parity behaviour — retargeted. The documented RC2-CBC envelope library gap (cryptography decrypts AES-CBC only) stays the pinned contract. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/encryption/PublicKeySecurityHandler.java` (`prepareForDecryption` L~221-268, 350)
  reason: lenient envelope length, unlocked permission, /Encrypt-level key sourcing diverged
- **COVERAGE — `pdf_text_stripper.py` 91→95% (15 behavioural pins; no divergences).** Form-XObject `Do` text recursion (own /Resources + non-identity /Matrix, image skip, missing-name/empty-body no-ops, self-referencing depth-50 cap), bare `BMC`, ActualText-suppressed run under ignore-space, `Ts` rise through ignore-space, `_compute_width_of_space` fallbacks, mixed-direction comparator antisymmetry, /Rotate-180 fold prongs. One pragma (structurally-unreachable non-flip `_is_line_break` fallback — sole caller gates on `_flip_axes`); remaining 78 lines are defensive fault-injection + documented carve-out residue, left per the file's convention. Tooling: numpy reimport-under-coverage crash on Python 3.14.5 worked around via a pre-import pytest plugin (measurement-only). (B)
  upstream: n/a (test-only + 1 pragma)
  reason: coverage residue round-out
- **FIX — compressed full save converged onto upstream's object-stream packing; unencrypted.pdf now BYTE-SIZE IDENTICAL to PDFBox (69,446 bytes, was 91,605).** Decompiled contract: `doWriteBodyCompressed` classifies every reachable indirect BEFORE writing (top-level first, then ObjStms, `/Encrypt` last); `addObjectToPool` eligibility = gen 0, not COSStream, not /Encrypt, NOT the /Root catalog; `getIndexEntry` emits sparse `/Index` runs over `{0} ∪ objectNumbers` (no gap free-fill); the xref stream carries no self-entry (`getStream()` precedes its own write). Six root causes fixed in `cos_writer.py`: packed objects double-written as free-standing indirects (new non-emitting `_collect_indirect_objects` pre-pass + actual-id dedup); /Root wrongly packed; gap free-fill rows collapsing /Index dense; self-entry widening /W; early ObjStm emission pushing type-1 offsets past 65535 (now deferred); verbose dict serialization (now the compact `COSWriterObjectStream` format, `<num> <off> ` header byte-for-byte). acroform.pdf within 1 byte (20,758 vs 20,759). Residuals (documented, acceptable): PDFBox's reference-vs-inline choice for shared indirect scalars (the 1 byte) + the deflate envelope (zlib vs java Deflater) on identical uncompressed payloads — ObjStm decoded-body SHA-256 MATCHES. `qpdf --check` rc=0. Xref stream no longer enumerable as a pool object after reparse (located via startxref, matching upstream) — 3 parser pins retargeted. Closes the wave-1499 body-geometry deferral. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` (`doWriteBodyCompressed` bytecode 0-554) + `pdfwriter/COSWriterCompressionPool.java` (`addObjectToPool` 60-117) + `pdfparser/PDFXRefStream.java` (`getIndexEntry` 0-170)
  reason: double-write + /Root packing + dense gap-fill /Index + self-entry diverged from upstream's pool classification

## Wave 1500 — signature geometry + function types 0/4 + objstm packing + colour math + coverage (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — `PDVisibleSignDesigner` rotation/zoom/formatter geometry was API-surface-only stubs; now fully ported and bit-exact.** `adjust_for_rotation()` was a no-op — now the full 90/180/270 coordinate transforms + AffineTransform + width/height swap (PDVisibleSignDesigner.java L216-258, rect deliberately NOT updated, matching Java); `set_image`/`width(w)`/`height(h)` now write `formatterRectangleParameters[2]/[3]` (L453/360/381); `zoom(percent)` updates them height-then-width (L285); `calculate_page_size` raises on `page<1`, sets `imageSizeInPercents=100` and `rotation = get_rotation() % 360` (L195-207); `_IdentityAffineTransform` takes the 6 `java.awt.geom.AffineTransform` operands. 7 live-oracle rotation/geometry cases bit-exact at float32 width (Java builds the transform from 32-bit floats). Also verified at parity: SignatureOptions size guard, PDSignature signed-content/contents slicing on the canonical layout, PDPropBuildDataDict accessors (NonEFontNoWarn default true), SigningSupport handover. 2 LOW structural notes recorded (transform defensive-copy semantics; contents gap-walk leniency). (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `interactive/digitalsignature/visible/PDVisibleSignDesigner.java` (L195-258, 285, 360-381, 453)
  reason: designer geometry was stubbed at the API surface — math never ported
- **FIX — `PDFunctionType0` eval: the invented cubic (/Order=3) Catmull-Rom branch REMOVED — upstream always interpolates linearly.** Upstream `PDFunctionType0.eval` (3.0.7) ignores `/Order` entirely (no cubic branch exists); pypdfbox's spec-cubic implementation produced different outputs for /Order=3 tables than Java's linear interpolation. Per "behaviour over style", eval is now always n-linear over the 2^n surrounding samples; `/Order` is read for diagnostics only (`set_order` stays a pure COS setter). Oracle `FunctionType0OrderProbe` pins /Order 1 vs 3 tables to identical linear outputs. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/function/PDFunctionType0.java` (`eval` — no cubic branch)
  reason: spec-faithful cubic diverged from upstream's /Order-ignoring linear eval
- **FIX — Type 4 numeric-domain edges now emit IEEE-754 specials clamped by /Range instead of raising.** `div` by zero: upstream `ArithmeticOperators$Div` is plain float division — +/-Infinity (NaN for 0/0) absorbed by the trailing /Range clip (jar: `{ 1 0 div }` over /Range [-1000,1000] → 1000.0); pypdfbox raised `OSError`. `exp` with negative base + non-integer exponent: Java `Math.pow` returns NaN; Python `math.pow` raises `ValueError` — now caught → NaN. Oracle `FunctionType4DomainErrProbe` pins the battery. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/operator/...`/`common/function/type4/ArithmeticOperators.java` (`Div`, `Exp`)
  reason: exception-vs-IEEE-special divergence on out-of-domain operands
- **FIX — `PDIndexed.toRGB` index rounding: Java `Math.round` half-UP, not Python banker's round.** Upstream uses `Math.round(value[0])` = `(int) Math.floor(a + 0.5f)` (PDIndexed.java L182), which disagrees with Python's `round()` on EVERY half-integer tint (Java 0.5→1, 2.5→3; Python 0.5→0, 2.5→2) — fractional indices dereferenced the wrong palette slot. Now `math.floor(value[0] + 0.5)`; the existing clamp matches `Math.max(index, 0)`. Oracle `IndexedRoundProbe` pins fractional + out-of-range tints on a DeviceRGB base. (E — agent killed by usage limit mid-audit; in-tree work salvaged + bookkept by orchestrator)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/color/PDIndexed.java` (L182)
  reason: banker's-round vs half-up disagreement on half-integer palette indices
- **COVERAGE — `PDFormContentStream` + `PDPatternContentStream` constructor/close surfaces pinned (wave-1499 ports).** (A — killed by usage limit before reaching the pdf_text_stripper residue; that part re-queued)
  upstream: n/a (test-only)
  reason: new ports were the lowest-coverage files at the wave-1500 checkpoint
- **NOTE — the objstm-packing investigation (agent D) was killed by the usage limit mid-rewrite; its incomplete `cos_writer.py` edit (288 lines, 7 failing packing tests) was REVERTED by the orchestrator and the surface re-queued.** No behavioural change landed from that surface this wave. (D)
  upstream: n/a
  reason: partial work not committable

## Wave 1499 — appearance divergences + full-save xref + MMR fuzz + taggedpdf/xmpbox audits (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **PARITY — xmpbox serializer/parser corners audit: every exercised corner at parity; reverse direction now pinned.** First reverse-direction oracle (xmpbox `XmpSerializer` writes → pypdfbox `DomXmpParser` reads): identical typed values across all 5 standard schemas (DC LangAlt/Seq/Bag, dates incl. +120 offset, AdobePDF, PDF/A-id lowercase `part`/`conformance`, Photoshop). Also pinned: `x-default`-first LangAlt ordering, element-form simple props, byte-identical xpacket wrapper (BOM `begin`, canonical id, `end="w"`), and the about-mismatch merge (same-namespace Descriptions with differing `rdf:about` fold to one schema keeping the FIRST about). New LOW deferral: parseType="Resource" structured-type round-trip blocked by an upstream Java-side NPE in `normalizeAttributes` — pinning gap only, no known divergence. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.xmpbox.xml.XmpSerializer` / `DomXmpParser`
  reason: audit — parity confirmed, reverse direction + merge corner pinned
- **FIX — Squiggly tiling-pattern appearance + Underline typed-colour operator now byte-exact; both prior structural appearance divergences closed.** Ported `PDFormContentStream` + `PDPatternContentStream` (thin `PDAbstractContentStream` subclasses) and a faithful `PDAppearanceContentStream.draw_form` (`/key Do`, no `q/Q`, per PDAbstractContentStream.java L558). `PDSquigglyAppearanceHandler` now paints the sawtooth from an uncolored tiling pattern (`/PaintType 2 /TilingType 3 /BBox [0 0 10 12] /XStep 10 /YStep 13`, cell `1 J 1 j 1 w 10 M 0 1 m 5 11 l 10 1 l S`) wrapped in a form XObject exactly like PDSquigglyAppearanceHandler.java L106-163 — outer stream, form body, pattern cell and dicts all match the jar (modulo resource-key numbering); 72-DPI render gate confirms rasterisation (the enabling uncolored-tiling tint path landed in wave 1385). `PDUnderlineAppearanceHandler` emits typed `/DeviceRGB CS … SC` instead of the `RG` shorthand (PDUnderlineAppearanceHandler.java L99). Sibling check: StrikeOut already converted (wave 1467); Highlight's transparency-group form-XObject spelling remains the one documented divergence in this family. 5 oracle/pin files updated from divergence-encoding to full-parity asserts. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `handlers/PDSquigglyAppearanceHandler.java` (L106-163) + `handlers/PDUnderlineAppearanceHandler.java` (L99) + `pdmodel/PDFormContentStream.java` + `pdmodel/PDPatternContentStream.java`
  reason: tiling-pattern construction was unported; colour-set operator spelling shorthand
- **FIX — `PDStructureElement` `/A`/`/C` revision arrays: stateful parse + live-COSArray mutation aligned to upstream over 5 edge shapes.** `get_attributes()`/`get_class_names()` did a structural array-wrap instead of upstream's stateful pairwise parse (PDStructureElement.java L159-191/L325-355): orphan/leading integers became phantom entries, double-integers-after-dict produced an extra entry instead of last-wins, and a bare-dict `/A` was read at the element's `/R` where upstream reads revision 0 (L188). The six mutators (`add_attribute`/`remove_attribute`/`attribute_changed` + `/C` analogues) cleared the whole slot on empty-out where upstream leaves an orphan `[0]` array (the size-2 collapse never fires at size 1) and collapses `[dict,0]`→bare dict even on a no-op remove (L229-457, unconditional back-pointer clear L283). All 7 probe shapes now equal the jar; 5 unit-test files retargeted from the divergent contract. Role-map transitive resolution + `/K` kid dispatch verified already covered. Pre-existing design note recorded: `Revisions.get_object_at` returns raw COS (callers compensate via `create()`) — restructuring out of scope. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/PDStructureElement.java` (L159-457)
  reason: structural wrap + slot-clearing mutators diverged on malformed/edge revision arrays
- **FIX — MMR `uncompress_2d` raises `IndexError` when the previous line returned EOL (`ref_run_length < 0`), matching Java's uncaught AIOOBE; Python's negative-index wrap previously decoded a BOGUS bitmap silently.** The array init at the top of `uncompress_2d` runs outside the try — Java crashes with `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` (`referenceOffsets[-1]`, MMRDecompressor.java L303-304); Python's list[-1] silently wrapped to the tail and kept decoding. Five adversarial streams now crash equivalently both sides. The dedicated fuzz wave hand-assembled T.6 bitstreams reaching every defensive surface: catch-Throwable→EOF (vertical-left storm → partial zero-filled bitmap byte-identical to jar), mode-table gap `code is None`, EOL/EXT default branch, 1-D EOL fallback ×3 incl. BLACK inner branch, H-mode negative-run terminators, VR2/VL2/VR3/VL3 bodies, RunData buffer-error paths. `mmr_decompressor.py` 77→98%; the 6 remaining lines proven dead by construction — recorded in DEFERRED as a no-action note. (C)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `decoder/mmr/MMRDecompressor.java` (L303-304 + uncompress loop)
  reason: negative-list-index wrap masked an upstream crash contract — silent corruption vs fail-fast
- **FIX — full-save xref-STREAM `/W` widths now computed via the shared `getWEntry` algorithm (closes the wave-1498 deferral).** Disassembled-jar contract: `getWEntry` seeds `long[3]` to 0, takes `Math.max` per column over streamData, then byte-counts (max-0 column → width 0); the implicit object-0 NULL row is written with the computed widths but NOT scanned; in the COMPRESSED full save the xref stream's own self-entry IS registered before serialisation (unlike the incremental arm) so its offset feeds the scan, and type-2 `ObjectStreamXReference` rows put the in-objstm INDEX in field 3. Fix in `_do_write_xref_stream`: self-entry appended to records before the width scan (replacing the old `+4096` over-estimate), `w1/w2/w3` via `_xref_field_width`, free-row generations (65535) excluded from the field-3 scan exactly like upstream's un-scanned NULL_ENTRY (pypdfbox keeps its documented inter-object-gap free-fill body rows), free-row gens packed truncated to the computed `w3`. `/W` third field now matches PDFBox verbatim on all fixtures (`[1 3 2]`→`[1 3 1]`, `[1 2 2]`→`[1 2 1]`); the residual second-field difference on some fixtures is the separate object-stream-packing file-size divergence (3-byte offsets genuinely needed), not a width bug. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/PDFXRefStream.java` (`getWEntry` bytecode 0-126, `writeStreamData` 0-128) + `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` (`doWriteBodyCompressed`)
  reason: hardcoded `w3=2` + offset over-estimate diverged from the shared width algorithm

## Wave 1498 — two LOW deferrals + annotation/merger/pdfwriter audits + coverage (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — full brute-force rebuild now rejects a rootless rebuilt trailer at load time (closes the wave-1497 pdfparser/recovery deferral).** A header-less/catalog-less buffer carrying only decoy `n g obj` tokens previously rebuilt a trailer with no `/Root` and loaded as a silent 0-page shell; upstream `Loader.loadPDF → parse(boolean) → initialParse()` raises `IOException("Missing root object specification in trailer.")`. New `_reject_full_rebuild_without_root()` (mirrors upstream's `/Root` deref) invoked on the full-rebuild path only — the located-xref path keeps the lazy `/Root` resolution contract. Both-sides oracle pin flipped to a real `py == java` parity assert. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/PDFParser.java` (`initialParse`)
  reason: rejection point was deferred past the rebuild — silent empty shell vs upstream raise
- **FIX — JBIG2 `_map_stream` rewinds to the window start before scanning; a file-header-only/zero-segment globals stream re-maps deterministically (0 pages) instead of raising `EOFError` (closes the wave-1497 jbig2 deferral).** Root cause was the re-map's stream POSITION, not `_reached_end_of_stream`: the constructor's first map leaves the SubInputStream at the post-header offset, and `_is_file_header_present` reads from the current position (faithful to upstream, which never seeks) — so the re-map read EOF (-1), skipped header parsing and ran a phantom segment parse. Upstream's logic is byte-identical and equally ill-defined on this degenerate shape; the rewind makes the re-map reproduce the first pass. 9-byte (pages-unknown) and 13-byte (pages-known-zero) shapes pinned. (A)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `JBIG2Document.java` (`mapStream`/`isFileHeaderPresent`)
  reason: re-map ran from a stale stream position on a degenerate input
- **FIX + COVERAGE — `GenericRegion` AT-pixel override negative-shift crash fixed (same Java `>>` masking class as the wave-1494 refinement fix); generic_region 83→92%, huffman nodes to 100%.** The override expressions `result >> (to_shift - gb_at_x[k])` (template 0a/0b) and `result >> (7 - (minor_x + gb_at_x[0]))` (templates 1/2/3) go negative when an AT pixel sits on the CURRENT row (`gb_at_y==0`) far enough right — Java masks the count to the low 5 bits (yields 0); Python raised `ValueError`. Existing override tests all used `gb_at_y<0`, masking the latent crash. New module-level `_java_ushr(value, shift)` routed through all 19 override callsites; 5 new oracle cases (all-AT template 0/ext, same-row templates 1/2/3) byte-identical vs the jar. Huffman `internal_node`/`node` to 100% (duplicate-node arms, abstract-body pragma). `mmr_decompressor` (79%) deliberately left: remaining misses are corrupt-data defensive residue needing hand-assembled adversarial MMR bitstreams — candidate for a dedicated MMR-fuzz wave. (E)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `segments/GenericRegion.java` (AT-override shift semantics)
  reason: Java int-shift count-masking missed at 19 sites; latent crash unreachable from prior fixtures

- **PARITY — PDFMergerUtility deep-mode audit: OCG `/OCProperties` merge + post-merge dest/outline re-resolution confirmed byte-exact; structure-tree merge verified already covered.** OCG merge appends source `/OCGs` with NO name dedup (two sources naming "Shared" both survive → 4 groups), `/D/Name` is first-source-wins, and a dest lacking `/OCProperties` gains the source's wholesale — matching `PDFCloneUtility.cloneMergeCOSBase` exactly. Every named destination + outline item re-resolves to its correct cumulative page index after 2- and 3-source merges (the load-bearing dest→page question was previously un-probed; only surviving-name sets were pinned). Structure-tree merge (ParentTree re-key, StructParents offset, RoleMap conflict, IDTree collision, /Pg rewrite, MCID offsets) found already covered by invariant tests — no redundant probe added. No divergences. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `multipdf/PDFMergerUtility.java` + `multipdf/PDFCloneUtility.java`
  reason: audit — parity confirmed, merge-path OCG/dest projections now pinned
- **FIX — `PDAnnotationLink.construct_appearances` was a no-op (stale "handler not ported yet" docstring) although `PDLinkAppearanceHandler` is fully ported; now wired per upstream.** Upstream `PDAnnotationLink.java` L236-247 instantiates `new PDLinkAppearanceHandler(this, document)` and calls `generateAppearanceStreams()` on the default path; the port fell through to the base no-op so Link annotations never produced an /AP /N. Now byte-exact against the jar over 6 variants (default black `0 G`, red solid, dashed `[3 2] 0 d`, STYLE_UNDERLINE single-edge no-close, /QuadPoints inside /Rect, /QuadPoints outside /Rect → warn + /Rect fallback) via the new `LinkAppearanceProbe`. Audit alongside: all 18 appearance handlers inventoried — Line /LE endings, Polygon/PolyLine, Ink, cloudy /BE borders, Caret, FreeText callout/DA/RD, Highlight blend already token-pinned; Squiggly tiling-pattern + Underline `RG`-shorthand remain the documented structural xfails. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/annotation/PDAnnotationLink.java` (L236-247) + `handlers/PDLinkAppearanceHandler.java`
  reason: default-path handler wiring missing — stale port-status docstring
- **FIX — incremental xref-STREAM append now matches PDFBox `PDFXRefStream` geometry (two rooted-together divergences).** (1) `/W` over-width: pypdfbox hardcoded `w3 = 2` and fed object-0's free head (gen 65535) into the width scan, emitting `/W [1 3 2]`; upstream `getWEntry()` sizes each field from the CHANGED entries only (excluding the implicit object-0 NULL row and the self-entry) — an offset-only edit yields `/W [1 3 0]` (a 0-valued column gets width 0, and `_pack_unsigned(v, 0)` now emits `b""` mirroring `writeNumber`).
