(2) Dangling self-row: pypdfbox appended the xref stream's own entry as a 4th body row the `/Index` never declared; upstream registers the self-entry only AFTER `getStream()` serialises the body. Decoded stream bytes now byte-identical to the jar on a deterministic edit (`/Index [0 1 30 1 32 1]`). Also verified at parity: multi-generation incremental stacking + `/Prev` chains over xref-stream sources, signing `/ByteRange` geometry, `/ID[0]`-kept/`/ID[1]`-regenerated. New LOW deferral: the FULL-save xref-stream path shares the `w3=2` quirk (valid-but-over-wide; unreached by default `save()`), to get the same treatment in a full-save wave. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/PDFXRefStream.java` (`getWEntry`/`getIndexEntry`/`writeStreamData`) + `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` (`doWriteXRefInc`)
  reason: hardcoded width + self-entry ordering diverged from upstream's changed-entries-only geometry

## Wave 1497 — header-garbage recovery + tools CLI completion + color-ops wiring + fontbox audit + coverage (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — a `%PDF-`/`%FDF-` header absent from the 1024-byte scan window no longer aborts the load; lenient parse falls through to brute-force recovery (closes the wave-1496 deferral).** Decompiled-jar contract: `COSParser.parseHeader` does NOT throw on a missing marker (seeks 0, returns false) — only a present marker with an unparseable version throws `IOException("Error getting header version:")`; `PDFParser.parse(boolean)` on header-false logs "Error: Header doesn't contain versioninfo" and continues in lenient mode (strict throws). Port: new `_HeaderNotFoundError(PDFParseError)` sentinel (marker-absent only — the previous code conflated malformed-version into the swallow), `parse()` keeps the COSDocument default version (1.4) and falls into the existing offset-agnostic brute-force rebuild; the "not a PDF" rejection re-derived downstream as "brute-force found zero objects". Oracle: junk 1100/2000/8000 past the window recovers all pages at parity; window-boundary 1019/1024/1030 shapes; total-garbage fails both sides. 2 pins retargeted (their old contracts contradicted upstream). **Narrowed new LOW deferral:** lenient `parse()` doesn't auto-run `initial_parse`, so a recovered document with no resolvable `/Root` yields pages=0 where upstream's eager `initialParse` raises "Missing root" — orthogonal to the fall-through, pinned. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`parseHeader`) + `pdfparser/PDFParser.java` (`parse(boolean)`)
  reason: eager header-throw had no upstream basis; rejection belongs downstream of brute-force

- **FIX — `PDFStreamEngine` colour-operator gating wired into the base dispatch loop; non-renderer engines now suppress colour ops inside d1 charprocs and uncoloured tiling patterns.** The 14 colour-operator classes read `is_should_process_color_operators()` but the base engine set it `True` in `__init__` and never flipped it — only the renderer (own `_type3_ignore_color` gate) suppressed; any other engine subclass leaked d1-charproc/uncoloured-pattern colour ops into its graphics state. Faithful port of upstream `processStreamOperators` (PDFStreamEngine.java L538-578): `_dispatch_tokens` takes the content stream, forces True for the loop, flips False up-front for an uncoloured `/PaintType 2` tiling pattern (L545-551) and on a leading `d1` in a `PDType3CharProc` (L558-562), restores in `finally` (L575-577). Raw-bytes callers (incl. the renderer's Type3 path) pass None → flag untouched, so the two gates never double-apply (verified — independent dispatch paths). Operator audit alongside: pypdfbox covers 100% of upstream operator classes; the inline-image `BI/ID/EI` heuristic verified byte-for-byte (`hasNoFollowingBinData` whitelist, 10-byte window, NUMBER_PATTERN, skipLinebreak) and already oracle-pinned; `sh`/marked-content/`gs` `/Font` already probed. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java` (`processStreamOperators` L538-578)
  reason: setter never called — suppression only existed in the renderer's parallel gate
- **PARITY — fontbox deep audit: cmap formats 2/13 oracle-pinned (were entirely unprobed); GSUB Indic reposition + post 2.5 verified at parity.** New reflective probe drives upstream's package-private `processSubtype2`/`processSubtype13` over hand-built subtable bodies; byte-for-byte parity confirmed, locking in format 2's surprising-but-correct behaviours (char code uses the SUB-HEADER ARRAY INDEX as the high byte — `(i<<8)+firstCode+j` — not the font's lead byte; raw glyph-0 entries are stored, only idDelta application is skipped). Gujarati/Tamil `repositionGlyphs`+`adjustRephPosition` verified against decompiled upstream bytecode; `post` 2.5 signed-byte resolution matches. Recorded notes: Tamil/SMCP/AALT workers + the `Language` enum are intentional ahead-of-upstream extensions (waves 1286/1375, fonts non-redistributable for probe-driving); upstream's unused `processSubtype14` numGlyphs arg deliberately dropped. No divergences. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/ttf/CmapSubtable.java` + `fontbox/ttf/gsub/*` + `PostScriptTable.java`
  reason: audit — parity confirmed, unprobed cmap formats now pinned
- **FIX — `ImportFDF`/`ImportXFDF` tools crashed outright (`AttributeError` escaping the `except OSError` handler); `PrintPDF` raised an uncaught `KeyError` on a bad `-duplex` token.** The import tools loaded via `Loader.load_pdf` (returns a bare `COSDocument`) then called `get_document_catalog()` on it — upstream `Loader.loadPDF` returns a `PDDocument`; fixed by wrapping via `PDDocument.load` (the path the sibling export tools already used). Both now exit 0 with PDFBox-identical imported field `/V` values + COS types (text/choice→COSString, checkbox→COSName, hierarchical kids walk), incl. the in-place no-`-o` overwrite. `PrintPDF` `-orientation`/`-duplex` constrained to the upstream enum values via argparse `choices=` → bad tokens exit 2 matching picocli. Confirmed already at parity: ExportFDF/ExportXFDF (incl. upstream's documented exit-1-vs-0 no-form asymmetry), DecompressObjectstreams (strips /ObjStm, page count preserved, in-place), missing-file exit 4 + missing-option exit 2 surfaces. Tools CLI audit is now COMPLETE across all 23 upstream tool classes. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `tools/ImportFDF.java` / `ImportXFDF.java` / `PrintPDF.java`
  reason: COSDocument-vs-PDDocument loader mismatch; unvalidated enum flags
- **COVERAGE — jbig2 structural tail: 7 files to 100% (35 tests).** `jbig2_page` 77→100, `jbig2_read_param` 79→100, `region` 82→100, `segment_data` 86→100, `dictionary` 88→100, `io/input_stream_factory` 88→100, `jbig2_document` 89→100 — ImageReadParam validation, abstract-interface contracts, hand-built document streams (extended-template flag, known-page-count header, RANDOM organisation, PDFBOX-6147 page-1-missing abort), normal-page compose branches. Genuinely-unreachable residue pragma'd with justification (`global_segments` null guard, `_reached_end_of_stream` IndexError catch, the corpus-absent striped-unknown-height compose path — fixture-starved like the Huffman residue). One degenerate-input divergence flagged → new LOW deferral (file-header-only globals stream EOFError on page-count re-map). (E)
  upstream: n/a (test-only)
  reason: coverage tail round-out

## Wave 1496 — FDF /Root fix + parser/io + tools + rendering audits + coverage (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX — xref-less FDF without `/Type /Catalog` now loads: the brute-force catalog predicate was missing upstream's `containsKey(FDF)` clause (PDFBOX-3639).** Root cause was NOT object-scan registration (both FDF objects were already registered) but the root-candidate test: upstream `BruteForceParser.isCatalog` (L763-767) is `CATALOG.equals(getCOSName(TYPE)) || containsKey(FDF)` — a dictionary carrying `/FDF` is treated as a root catalog even without `/Type /Catalog`; live-oracle instrumentation confirmed `searchForTrailerItems` sets `/Root = 1 0 R` for `nocatalog.fdf` via exactly that clause. Added the `/FDF` containment test to both `cos_parser.rebuild_trailer` (the FDF-driving path) and `brute_force_parser.is_catalog`, and excluded such dicts from the `/Info` heuristic (mirroring the upstream if/else-if). `test_load2_no_catalog` flipped from strict-xfail to a live pass; `withcatalog.fdf` + the parser/pdmodel/io recovery suites stay green. Closes the wave-1495 deferral. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/BruteForceParser.java` (`isCatalog` L763-767, `searchForTrailerItems`)
  reason: catalog predicate omitted the FDF-root clause — /Root never resolved on rebuild
- **HYGIENE — stripped the `Loader.loadFDF` camelCase alias** (`pypdfbox/loader.py`; flagged by agent A's side-note) per the strict-snake_case rule; its alias test retargeted to the canonical `load_fdf` round-trip. (orchestrator)
  upstream: n/a
  reason: no-camelCase-aliases rule
- **COVERAGE — cff_parser + jbig2_decode to 100%; pd_image decode branches; renderer matrix helpers pinned (50 tests).** `cff_parser.py` 95→100 (encoding-resolver helpers; one dead `return None` after an unconditional return removed), `filter/jbig2_decode.py` 95→100 (DecodeParms-array entry, non-stream globals, page-None/OSError-reraise), `pd_image_x_object.py` 91→94 (16-bit RGB/Gray, ICCBased, DeviceN /Decode, color-key rejection legs, SMask /Matte un-premultiply, explicit /Mask polarity), `pdf_renderer.py` rotation/affine helpers deterministically pinned + `# pragma: no cover` on `_normalise_rotation`'s negative-modulus guard (unreachable in Python — `%` of a positive modulus never returns negative; faithful to Java's `% 360` which can). Tooling note recorded: concurrent agents sharing the default `COVERAGE_FILE` produce corrupt under-N% lists — tail sweeps must use a private `COVERAGE_FILE`. (D)
  upstream: n/a (test-only + dead-code removal)
  reason: coverage tail round-out

- **FIX — `RandomAccessRead.rewind(n)` / `skip(n)` no longer reject negative counts; aligned to upstream's sign-free seek delegation.** Upstream (verified against the 3.0.7 bytecode) implements both as bare seek arithmetic — `rewind` = `seek(getPosition() - n)` (negative n seeks FORWARD), `skip` = `seek(getPosition() + n)` (negative n seeks BACKWARD; past-end clamping happens inside `seek`, not via an explicit `min`). The port's `ValueError` guards + clamp had no upstream basis. Confirmed against the live jar (`rewindNeg=NO_THROW pos=4`, `skipNeg=NO_THROW pos=4`); internal callers all pass positive args (zero blast radius); 2 stale guard pins retargeted. **Audit deferral (strict-xfail both-sides):** leading garbage past the 1024-byte header scan window aborts pypdfbox's load (`missing %PDF- header`) where upstream `parsePDFHeader` assigns the default version and falls through to brute-force recovery (oracle: 2000 junk bytes → pages=2) — structural (the eager header-throw is an asserted load contract); fix path documented in the new `## pdfparser/recovery` DEFERRED section. Already-pinned recovery surfaces (brute-force, root recovery, stream-length, hybrid-xref, xref chains) verified probed. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `io/RandomAccessRead.java` (default methods) + `pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`parsePDFHeader`)
  reason: invented sign guards; header-abort vs brute-force fall-through divergence recorded
- **PARITY — tools CLI audit: PDFSplit + OverlayPDF + ExtractXMP confirmed at parity; 3 new live oracles.** PDFSplit: default 1-page-per-file, `-split 2` counts `[2,2,1]`, `-startPage`/`-endPage` matrix and `<prefix>-<n>.pdf` naming match Java across 5 scenarios. OverlayPDF: `-default` + combined `-first/-last/-odd/-even` precedence (FIRST and LAST win over odd) per-page byte-for-byte. ExtractXMP: `-console` packet bytes, "No XMP metadata available" + "Page N doesn't exist" error text and exit-1 codes identical (probe drives the real CLI as a subprocess — upstream exposes only `main()` + `System.exit`, and Java 21 removed the SecurityManager exit-trap). No production change; remaining unprobed CLIs (Import/ExportFDF/XFDF, PrintPDF, DecompressObjectstreams) left for a later round. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `tools/PDFSplit.java` / `tools/OverlayPDF.java` / `tools/ExtractXMP.java`
  reason: audit — no divergence found, contracts now pinned
- **PARITY — rendering pixel-parity sweep: all audited operator families already pinned; Type3 `d1` colour-suppression branch now oracle-covered.** Separable + non-separable blend modes, soft masks (/Luminosity, /Alpha, /BC, /TR), text clip modes 4-7 and stroke geometry (caps/joins/miter/dash) were all found already differential-pinned. `/SA` stroke adjustment + `/RI` rendering intent verified as raster no-ops in BOTH engines (PDFBox's Graphics2D pipeline ignores them; pypdfbox carries the state without pixel effect) — intentionally not pinned. The one gap: no test exercised a d1 glyph attempting to set its own colour — the suppression machinery (`_type3_ignore_color` + `_COLOR_OPS`, mirroring upstream `isShouldProcessColorOperators()`) was correct but unexercised; now pinned per-channel (d1 body running `0 0 1 rg` paints the text-state colour; the identical d0 body paints blue). Side observation (out of scope, no pixel effect): the non-rendering base `PDFStreamEngine._should_process_color_operators` flag is half-wired — readers exist, setter never called; the renderer has its own correct gate. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java` (`isShouldProcessColorOperators`) + `pdmodel/font/PDType3Font` rendering path
  reason: audit — parity confirmed, suppression branch was unpinned

## Wave 1495 — rotation CTM fold + xmpbox strict dates + audit + coverage + upstream test ports (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally)

- **PORT — upstream JUnit gap sweep: only 8 of 186 upstream 3.0.7 test classes were unported; 4 ported, inventory closed.** Cross-referenced all 186 upstream `*Test.java` classes (pdfbox/fontbox/xmpbox at the 3.0.7 tag) against the 241 ported-test PROVENANCE rows. Ported: `PDStreamTest` (filter passthrough incl. PDFBOX-2948), `PfbParserTest` (the 2 bundleable methods), `TestFDF` (+3 upstream fixtures bundled), legacy `TestPDPageContentStream` (colour-range `IllegalArgumentException`→`ValueError`, text-mode `IllegalStateException`→`RuntimeError` guards). Remaining gaps classified: corpus-blocked halves (network-fetched JIRA assets), `Type1LexerTest` (blocked by the documented tuple-token API divergence — its companion test's "upstream ships no Type1LexerTest" claim was stale and is superseded by this inventory), and 2 already-ported classes whose PROVENANCE rows were backfilled (`TestPDDocumentCatalog`, `TestPDPageTree`). **Parity finding (strict-xfail + DEFERRED):** the FDF fallback parser fails to resolve the trailer `/Root` for an xref-less FDF without `/Type /Catalog` (PDFBOX-3639 `nocatalog.fdf`) — upstream loads it via brute-force object search. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 test trees (see PROVENANCE rows)
  reason: PRD §12 ported-upstream-test requirement — gap inventory now exhaustive
- **FIX — `PDCryptFilterDictionary.get_length()` default corrected 5 → 40 (bits).** Upstream `getInt(COSName.LENGTH, 40)` (PDCryptFilterDictionary.java L86) — the entry is the key length in BITS (multiple of 8 per the Javadoc); the port returned a default of 5 with a comment misreading it as a byte count, and the two pinning tests asserted the invented 5 (never oracle-checked). Verified 40 with a new live probe BEFORE changing; blast radius isolated (all key-derivation sites use `PDEncryption.get_length()`, already 40 — the crypt-filter accessor only feeds introspection/round-trip). 3 stale assertions retargeted. Audit also verified at parity with no new pins needed: AccessPermission bit decode + pubkey bytes, AFM parser relaxation/composites/track-kern, CFF FDSelect format-0/format-3 boundary + sentinel returns, NumberFormatUtil float emission. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/encryption/PDCryptFilterDictionary.java` (L86)
  reason: bits-vs-bytes misread; pins were hand-invented rather than oracle-derived
- **FIX — xmpbox `DateConverter` strictness split implemented; `DateType` empty-string semantics corrected (closes the wave-1494 deferral).** New `to_calendar_strict` (+ `DateConverter.to_calendar_strict` classmethod) ports `org.apache.xmpbox.DateConverter.toCalendar` L76-205 1:1 — strips `D:` then runs the pure-numeric `replaceAll("[-:T]","")` fixed-substring parse, so the PDF apostrophe TZ form, SimpleDateFormat locale shapes, slash dates, a misplaced `T` and a <4-char remainder all raise `OSError` (Java IOException); empty/whitespace/None returns `None` (upstream null → downstream NPE, matching the probe). The lenient `to_calendar` (pdfbox.util semantics) is retained for the FDF + document-info callers. xmpbox schema parsing (`type/date_type.py`, upstream `DateType`) routes `is_good_type`/`set_value_from_string` through the strict variant; while there the port was corrected to upstream `DateType` exactly — `isGoodType("")` is True (only catches IOException, never inspects the null result) and `setValue("")` stores a null Calendar, verified vs the live jar. Wave-1494 strict-xfail flipped to a differential parity assert; oracle extended (4 accept + 6 reject + NPE + divergence rows). (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.xmpbox.DateConverter` (L76-205) + `org.apache.xmpbox.type.DateType`
  reason: one lenient parser served two upstream classes with different accept-sets
- **COVERAGE — 6 tail modules to 100% (44 behaviour-anchored tests).** `cos_float.py` 93→100 (f2i/f2l saturating clamps, NaN→0, the three malformed-real repairs `--16.33`/`0.-262`/`-16.-33`, `_float32_or_inf` overflow), `ascii_hex_decode.py` 93→100 (EOF/EOD edge quirks), `otf_parser.py` 94→100 (read_table tag dispatch + legacy forwards), `fdf_option_element.py` 93→100, `free_x_reference.py` 91→100, `native_font_dir_finder.py` 90→100. A second sweep (same agent, parallel run) added 30 more behavioural tests: BER indefinite-length TLV scanner arms in `sig_utils.py`, `type1_char_string` seac/affine/subrs helpers, color-key raw-sample reading + Lab decode in `pd_image_x_object.py` (89→91). No production change; no divergences. (D)
  upstream: n/a (test-only)
  reason: coverage tail round-out
- **FIX — page-rotation text-extraction parity at all four `/Rotate` values; rot90/270 byte-exact (closes the wave-1493 MEDIUM deferral).** Key correction to the deferral's premise: upstream does NOT fold the rotation into the CTM — `PDPage.getMatrix()` is identity (PDPage.java L385-389) and `LegacyPDFStreamEngine.processPage` builds only the cropbox translate; the rotation is carried in each `TextPosition` (`new TextPosition(pageRotation, pageWidth, pageHeight, …)`, LegacyPDFStreamEngine.java L309-313), and the default extraction path groups on the rotation-adjusted `getX()`/`getY()`/`getWidth()` (`getXRot`/`getYLowerLeftRot`/`getWidthRot`, TextPosition.java L293-446, consumed at PDFTextStripper.java L585-591). Fix: `PDFTextStripper._apply_page_rotation` — a per-page post-pass rewriting each run's stored x/y/width into the rotation-adjusted frame — plus per-glyph emission on 90/270 pages (`_emit_per_glyph`) so rotated rows fragment glyph-by-glyph the way upstream's `showGlyph` feeds `writePage`. Byte-exact vs the live jar at all four rotations for both the identity-`Tm` device-frame fixtures (incl. the asymmetric 90-vs-270 fragmentation that follows from `maxYForLine = max(...)` accumulation) AND the common upright-reading rotated case (new `RotatedUprightTextProbe`); rot90/270 both-sides pins flipped to parity asserts; ByArea rotated-region behaviour re-pinned. The `set_should_flip_axes` lite-only toggle stays the separate documented carve-out. Agent died before reporting; code/tests/DEFERRED were complete — bookkeeping merged by the orchestrator. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/TextPosition.java` (rot accessors L293-446) + `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (L585-591) + `contentstream/LegacyPDFStreamEngine.java` (L309-313)
  reason: rotated TextPositions stayed in raw user space — rotation-adjusted grouping frame missing

## Wave 1494 — rotation CTM fold + typed returns + JBIG2 tail + audit round 2 (5-agent wave; first spawn killed by session usage limit, re-run after reset; entries merged incrementally)

- **FIX + COVERAGE — JBIG2 refinement procedure to 100%; latent negative-shift crash fixed; dead code pragma'd with upstream citations.** `_override_at_template0` crashed with `ValueError: negative shift count` when an AT pixel pushed `7 - (minor_x + gr_at_x[0])` negative — Java's `int >>` masks the count to the low 5 bits; the port now masks `& 0x1F` (same pattern used elsewhere in the file): a real latent divergence surfaced by covering the AT-override path. Dead-code audit confirmed against upstream `GenericRefinementRegionDecodingProcedure.java` (`run()` L256-277 dispatches template-1 exclusively per-pixel) that `decodeTypicalPredictedLineTemplate1` + the `case 1:` arms of `decodeOptimized`/`decodeTypicalPredictedLine`/`updateOverride` are structurally unreachable upstream too — port kept faithful, `# pragma: no cover` with citations; same for `Bitmaps._blit_shifted`/`_blit_special_shifted`/`_to_signed_short` (upstream's PDFBOX-6156 guard diverts every shifted blit to `blitByPixel`). +27 tests (template-0 shift bands, AT overrides, TPGR override, `TextRegion._blit` transposed×corner combos, `Bitmaps.blit` arms). refinement-proc 75→100%, bitmaps 84→96%, text_region 69→77%. DEFERRED narrowed to exactly the Huffman/segment-graph fixture-starved spans + the pinned referenceDX<0 jar divergence. (C)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `decoder/GenericRefinementRegionDecodingProcedure.java` + `image/Bitmaps.java` (PDFBOX-6156)
  reason: Java >> count-masking semantics missed at one site; dead dispatch arms mirrored from upstream
- **FIX — `PDTargetDirectory` typed returns aligned to upstream (closes the wave-1493 deferral).** `get_relationship()` → `COSName | None` (via `get_cos_name`); `get_page_number()`/`get_annotation_index()` → upstream's `getInt(key, -1)` contract (`-1` for absent or string-form `/P`//`/A`); `get_named_destination()` → `PDNamedDestination | None`. The None-form string siblings remain for the dual int-vs-name `/P`//`/A` split. The embedded-GoTo `resolve_target` consumer migrated (`rel.get_name()` for chain relationships; `page >= 0` sentinel normalisation into `TargetStep`); the two strict-xfail divergence pins consolidated into a parity assert against `TargetDirectoryProbe`; ~7 stale pins retargeted (parity/wave469/remote-goto round-trip files). Agent hit the session usage limit after the code+oracle work; the final test retargets + bookkeeping were completed by the orchestrator. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/action/PDTargetDirectory.java`
  reason: None-means-absent local contract replaced with upstream's typed sentinels per the parity-first rule
- **AUDIT (partial — agent killed by session usage limit) — xmpbox `DateConverter` differential started; ISO-8601 formatting at parity, `toCalendar` strictness split pinned.** New `XmpDateConverterProbe` covers the *xmpbox* DateConverter (distinct from the already-probed `pdfbox.util` one): `toISO8601` one-/two-arg (colon-separated `±HH:MM` zone offset, milliseconds flag) matches byte-for-byte over fixed-offset instants. Structural divergence pinned strict-xfail + DEFERRED: upstream xmpbox `toCalendar(String)` rejects the PDF `D:` form (IOException) and NPEs on the empty string, while pypdfbox deliberately shares one lenient `to_calendar` across both upstream classes. Remaining audit clusters (fontbox/pdfwriter/encryption) not reached — re-run in a later wave. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `org.apache.xmpbox.DateConverter`
  reason: audit round 2 — one formatting surface verified, one acceptance-shape divergence recorded

## Wave 1493 — last text xfail + rotated-page unification + JBIG2 fixtures + coverage + parity audit (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally as agents report)

- **FIX — `PDTargetDirectory()` no-arg constructor stamped a spurious `/R = C` default; upstream creates an empty dictionary.** Fixed (default stamp + unused `_C` removed): `get_relationship()` on a fresh instance returns `None` and the saved wire form carries no `/R`, confirmed vs the jar (`empty.keys.size=0`, `getRelationship()=null`). Found by the wave-1493 parity audit, which probed the previously un-oracled measurement package (`PDNumberFormatDictionary`/`PDMeasureDictionary`/`PDRectlinearMeasureDictionary`/`PDViewportDictionary` — all defaults at parity, incl. `getMeasure` NOT auto-promoting `/Subtype RL`), `PDActionHide` (`/H` default true — parity) and `PDTargetDirectory`. Typed-return divergences on `PDTargetDirectory` (`-1` sentinels vs `None`, `PDNamedDestination`/`COSName` vs `str`) are structural (the deliberate dual-accessor split) — pinned strict-xfail + new DEFERRED entry. Also re-verified at parity without new pins: PDDocumentInformation, PDPageTree insert count propagation, merger AcroForm collision rename, page-label generators, submit/reset-form flags, optional-content properties. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/action/PDTargetDirectory.java` + the measurement package
  reason: constructor default not present upstream; audit sweep of un-probed clusters
- **COVERAGE — Gouraud mesh decoders + `Do` dispatch pinned deterministically; 3 files to 100%.** `PDShadingType4.collect_triangles` (free-form flag-0/1/2 vertex stitching, bit-packed decode, /Decode interpolation, EOF/unknown-flag termination, degenerate fallbacks incl. missing-colour-range `OSError`), `PDShadingType5.collect_triangles` (lattice `2*(vpr-1)` cell stitching, partial-trailing-row discard, same fallback family) and `invoke_named_xobject`'s dispatch arms (image→`draw_image`, transparency-group, form by classname / `is_form_xobject`, unrecognised-skip, `MissingResourceException`, no-resources noop) — previously reachable only via `@requires_oracle` render tests — now pinned with 39 deterministic tests. `pd_shading_type4.py` 69→100%, `pd_shading_type5.py` 75→100%, `invoke_named_xobject.py` 57→100%. No production change; no divergences found. (D)
  upstream: n/a (test-only)
  reason: behavioural core of mesh shading + Do dispatch was oracle-gated or untested
- **INVESTIGATION (no behavioural change) — flip-axes carve-outs confirmed correct by design; rotated-page parity boundary pinned.** Live-oracle investigation established that `set_should_flip_axes` is a lite-only manual X/Y transpose with NO upstream counterpart (no `flipAxes` symbol anywhere in pdfbox-app-3.0.7.jar) and carries no rotation in the coordinate matrix (`dir == 0`, so `get_x_dir_adj`/`get_y_dir_adj` are identity) — the wave-1489 overlap grouping and wave-1492 paragraph classifier therefore CANNOT be unified onto that path; the legacy prev-only heuristics are retained by design (inline comments now state the real reason). The differential baseline exposed the REAL `/Rotate` gap instead: Java folds `page.getRotation()` into the CTM so rot90/rot270 multi-line content fragments in device-frame line grouping, while the lite `LegacyPDFStreamEngine.process_page` builds only a cropbox translate and emits clean upright text; rot0/rot180 are byte-exact on both engines. New `RotatedMultiLineProbe` + oracle test pin rot0/180 at parity and rot90/270 as a both-sides divergence target; the actionable CTM-fold item is a new MEDIUM deferral. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/LegacyPDFStreamEngine.java` (rotation CTM fold) — gap documented, not yet ported
  reason: carve-out unification premise falsified; real rotation gap localized + pinned
- **FIX — `_is_word_break` gap-separator suppression now ports upstream's gate verbatim; the last open text-extraction xfail flipped.** The lite stripper had two whitespace-suppression checks (`prev.text[-1].isspace()` and `pos.text[0].isspace()`) with no upstream basis; upstream gates the gap-driven separator on a single previous-glyph test — emit unless `lastPosition.getUnicode()` already ends with the configured `wordSeparator` (PDFTextStripper.java L670-674). Under a large Tc over `(AB CD)`, Java emits a gap separator *before* the explicit space glyph AND keeps the glyph (the double space), suppressing only the redundant separator *out of* the space glyph; the lite current-glyph check collapsed `A B  C D` to `A B C D`. Replaced both checks with the verbatim mirror (suppress only when `_word_separator` is non-empty and `prev.text.endswith(separator)`) — also correctly honours custom separators (the `|W|` separator-oracle case stays green). `test_large_char_spacing_intra_run_word_breaks_match_pdfbox` now asserts `py == java == "A B  C D\n"`; every previously byte-exact oracle stayed byte-exact. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (writePage word-separator emission L668-677)
  reason: lite-port whitespace heuristic suppressed the separator Java emits next to an explicit space glyph
- **COVERAGE — JBIG2 decode-body round-out without new fixtures; one real divergence found + pinned.** Exhaustive check of upstream apache/pdfbox-jbig2's unbundled test resources (`001/002/004/007`, `20123110002…010`) proved they are ALL template-0 arithmetic full-page scans — none exercise templates 1-3, Huffman symbol dictionaries, or refinement, so no fixture was bundled (zero coverage gain). Reachable decode bodies covered via crafted segment-byte streams + direct decoder-state driving, byte-parity'd vs the live 3.0.7 jar where drivable: generic-region extended-template (`_decode_template0b` + 12-pixel AT override, 3 oracle cases), refinement template-1 non-TPGR + template-0 byte-blit bit-exact across 11 oracle cases, TPGR template-0 pinned Python-deterministically (the jar NPEs on SLTP via setParameters), SD `_decode_new_symbols`/`_decode_refined_symbol` + TR `_decode_ib` r=1 driven directly. **Divergence (strict-xfail both-sides):** template-1 refinement with referenceDX<0 — the 3.0.7 jar's inlined decoder predates the upstream refactor's negative-remainder fix (`(2 - modReferenceDX) % 8`); pypdfbox follows the refactored upstream (real-reader behaviour). Dead-code finding: template-1's optimized byte-blit bodies are structurally unreachable (`_run` dispatches template-1 per-pixel only) — DEFERRED as removal/pragma candidate. refinement-proc 60→75%, generic_region 71→84%, symbol_dictionary 74→78%. (C)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `GenericRefinementRegion.java` / `GenericRegion.java` / `SymbolDictionary.java` / `TextRegion.java` (vs the bundled 3.0.7 jar bytecode)
  reason: decode bodies were fixture-starved; jar-vs-refactored-upstream split documented

## Wave 1492 — text-layout xfail closures + JBIG2 coverage (5-agent wave; entries merged incrementally as agents report)

- **PARITY — sorted-mode BiDi extraction of mixed-direction bracketed runs now byte-exact; `test_bidi.py::test_sorted` unskipped.** The wave-1409 "sorted-vs-reorder" residual (`BidiSample.pdf`, `'«إختبار ذاتي»'` one codepoint off under `sort_by_position=True`) was already eliminated by the CTM-aware emission (wave 1409) + running vertical-span line-grouping / `computeFontHeight` work (waves 1489-1491): the geometric `TextPositionComparator` re-sort now groups glyphs onto the same logical lines as Java *before* the UAX#9 reorder runs. Verified byte-exact against both the bundled `BidiSample.pdf-sorted.txt` fixture (all 8 lines) and the live Java oracle (`TextSortInlineProbe` SORTED payload); unsorted likewise. No production change — skip removed + a new live sorted/unsorted oracle differential added so the contract is pinned both ways. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (sorted writePage + Bidi normalize)
  reason: stale skip — root cause had been fixed by intervening geometry waves
- **FIX — lite `PDFTextStripper` paragraph-separation indent prong measured against the wrong anchor; hanging-indent + list-item detection missing.** Ported upstream `isParagraphSeparation` (PDFTextStripper.java L1611-1683) as `_classify_paragraph_separation`: the y-gap drop prong still measures vs the prior glyph, but the indent / hanging-indent / list-item prongs now measure vs `lastLineStartPosition` (the previous line's first glyph, carried as a `PositionWrapper`), mutating `set_paragraph_start`/`set_hanging_indent` flags exactly as upstream; the already-ported `LIST_ITEM_EXPRESSIONS` regexes + `match_list_item_pattern` are now wired in. `_emit_group` tracks `last_line_start`/`last_wrapper` and seeds the first page glyph as paragraph+line start (`startOfPage`); the flip-axes (rotated-page) path retains the legacy prev-only heuristic (documented). `with_outline.pdf` paragraph-token placement flipped from strict xfail to a byte-exact pass (upstream wraps every stair-step-indented bookmark heading line in its own paragraph — now reproduced); new list-item + hanging-indent synthetic oracle fixtures; public 2-arg `is_paragraph_separation`/`handle_line_separation` API contracts untouched; `add_more_formatting` + text2html oracles stay green. Closes the wave-1490 indent-prong residual. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (`isParagraphSeparation` L1611-1683, `handleLineSeparation` L1559-1587, `writePage` plumbing L497-503/656-665/709-713)
  reason: indent prong anchored to the immediately-previous glyph instead of the previous line start
- **FIX — `PDFTextStripperByArea` overlapping-region routing CONVERGED with upstream: an overlap glyph now lands in exactly one region (Java's HashMap-order survivor), not every matching region.** Investigation proved Java's surviving region is deterministic: live-oracle experiments over varied name sets + insertion orders ({a,b}→a regardless of order, {r1,r2}→r2, {p,q,s}→p) match a pure `java.util.HashMap` bucket-order computation (`String.hashCode` with the `h ^ (h>>>16)` spread, masked by capacity 16) — JLS-specified and stable across JVMs, so convergence is safe (not brittle) for realistic region counts. Implementation: shared page-wide `_character_list_mapping` (upstream `characterListMapping`, reset per `extract_regions`), `_is_suppressed_duplicate` porting the dedup block of `processTextPosition` (L913-950: `width/len/3.0` tolerance, half-open windows), `_bin_glyph` iterating matching regions in Java-HashMap order via `_hashmap_order`, and the format-time per-bin dedup suppressed for the byArea path (upstream's per-region `writePage` does not re-dedup). With `set_suppress_duplicate_overlapping_text(False)` the glyph lands in every matching region on both sides. Per-region output byte-identical to Java for disjoint / overlap-ON / overlap-OFF / 3-way / four-quadrant; probe gained a `--no-suppress` flag. Closes the wave-1457 deferral as CONVERGED. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripperByArea.java` (L156-164) + `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (`processTextPosition` dedup L913-950) + `java.util.HashMap`/`String.hashCode` iteration order
  reason: independent per-region binning routed an overlap glyph to every region where Java picks one
- **FIX — lite `PDFTextStripper` bead/article-thread reading order: `PDFBOX-3110-poems-beads.pdf` at byte-for-byte parity; strict xfail flipped.** Replaced the naive inside-rect-or-trailing-residual bead bucketing with upstream's full `charactersByArticle` machinery: (1) the `2*N+1` slot scheme (slot `i*2+1` = inside bead `i`, slot `i*2` = the gap before it, last slot = catch-all) with the inside / left / above / left-and-above priority assignment from `processTextPosition` (PDFTextStripper.java L954-1020) + CropBox lower-left adjustment (L410-417) — gap runs now interleave between bead columns so inline stage directions stay adjacent to their text; (2) cross-article line-state carry — `maxYForLine`/`lastPosition`/`lastLineStartPosition` live outside the article loop (L497-503) so a new article's first glyph line-breaks against the previous article's last; (3) the article-start branch of `handleLineSeparation` (L1566-1585): a large vertical jump between columns where the previous glyph is an article start collapses to a bare `writeParagraphStart` (no inter-article line break); only a plain one-row drop into a residual line emits a single line separator. The old xfail's "per-glyph width clipping" concern was a non-issue (no glyphs dropped — the entire residual was the stitch order). Bead bucket ordering pins retargeted (3 tests); 2 new hand-written slot-scheme tests. Closes the last open multi-column/bead reading-order deferral. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (`processTextPosition` bead assignment L954-1020, CropBox adjust L410-417, `writePage` carry L497-503, `handleLineSeparation` article-start branch L1566-1585)
  reason: single-residual bead bucketing lost upstream's gap-slot interleaving + cross-article line state
- **COVERAGE — JBIG2 lowest-coverage round-out; all 15 resampling kernels byte-exact vs the 3.0.7 jar.** `Jbig2ReaderProbe` gained an optional filterType arg (bypasses `readRaster`'s hardwired GAUSSIAN via the `JBIG2Document → JBIG2Page.getBitmap()` pipeline) enabling 60 per-kernel live-oracle cases (4 scale/region cases × 15 `FilterType` members) — every kernel matches byte-for-byte — plus kernel-math pins (support-edge discontinuities, Lanczos float32 truncation, Bessel asymptotic branch), `Bitmaps` helper pins, segment header-parse/flag-validation pins and refinement validation/template pins. `image/filter.py` 51→96%, `bitmaps.py` 75→84%, segment/decoder validation surfaces covered. Remaining uncovered spans are real reachable decode paths (Huffman coding / GBTEMPLATE 1-3 / refinement template-1) that need new binary fixtures none of the 5 bundled `.jb2` files exercise — deliberately NOT marked `no cover`; deferred until fixtures are sourced. Test-only + probe arg; no behavioural change. (C)
  upstream: n/a (test-only; probe extension)
  reason: coverage round-out — kernels and validation branches untested since the wave-1490 port

## Wave 1491 — deferral-closure wave: 4 fixes + 1 falsified hypothesis (get_resources strict-null, vertical writing mode, Std-14 encoding split, PDNamedDestination contract, mask-precedence parity pin) (5-agent wave)

- **FIX — `PDPage.get_resources()` restored to upstream's strict-null contract.** Returns `None` (no back-write) when neither the page nor any ancestor `/Pages` node carries `/Resources` (was an empty-`PDResources` wrapper materialised + back-written). New `PDPage.get_or_create_resources()` factors upstream's inline `if (resources == null) { resources = new PDResources(); page.setResources(resources); }` idiom for call-sites needing a guaranteed-non-null bag. Sibling audit: `PDFormXObject`/`PDPattern`/`PDType3Font`/`PDType3CharProc`/`PDTilingPattern` already returned `None` correctly — only `PDPage` diverged. All production read-paths were already `None`-guarded; one extractor tidied, the create-and-mutate example migrated, ~13 test call-sites migrated to `get_or_create_resources()`. `PageInheritanceProbe` now emits a `res` presence flag so the no-`/Resources` branch is asserted differentially (it was deliberately omitted while the divergence was open). Closes the wave-1454 deferral. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDPage.java` (`getResources`)
  reason: empty-bag fallback + back-write diverged from upstream's null contract on resource-less pages

- **FIX — lite `PDFTextStripper` vertical writing mode (WMode 1) reading order.** The stripper now detects `font.is_vertical()` (Identity-V / WMode-1 encoding CMap) in `_emit` and delegates to a new `_emit_vertical`: each code is read via `font.read_code`, the glyph origin is offset by `font.get_position_vector(code)·fontSize`, one `TextPosition` is emitted per glyph, and the cursor steps DOWN the column by `ty = w1y·fontSize + Tc (+ Tw on code 32)` through the text-matrix scale/shear — porting the vertical branch of upstream `showText`. Each glyph lands on its own baseline so line grouping breaks per glyph (`"A\nB\nC\n"` matching Java, was `"ABC\n"`); shared-baseline columns order left-to-right under `sort_by_position`; `/ActualText` whole-span replacement preserved; horizontal path untouched (gated on `_is_vertical_font`). Strict xfail in `test_vertical_text_strip_oracle.py` flipped to a both-sides parity pin; probe extended with a `sort` arg; two-column default + sorted oracle cases added. Closes the wave-1460 deferral with full parity (single- and multi-column, default and sorted). (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java` (`showText` vertical branch)
  reason: lite text-state machine was horizontal-only — ignored WMode + vertical displacement vector
- **FIX — non-embedded Standard-14 `PDType1Font` toUnicode/width encoding split now mirrors upstream's two construction paths.** A core font parsed from a PDF dict with NO `/Encoding` reads its built-in `Type1Encoding` from the bundled AFM (AdobeStandardEncoding / FontSpecific) for toUnicode — `0xAD`→`guilsinglright` (U+203A), `0x27`→`quoteright`, `0x60`→`quoteleft` (PDType1Font.java L495-498); the direct `new PDType1Font(FontName)` constructor (ported as new `PDType1Font.standard14(base_font)`) writes an explicit `/Encoding /WinAnsiEncoding` for Latin cores (L120) and assigns the FontSpecific `SymbolEncoding`/`ZapfDingbatsEncoding` singletons for the symbol fonts; `PDFontFactory.create_default_font` routes through `standard14`. The previous single-source `WinAnsiEncoding.INSTANCE` return (deliberate wave-1431 choice) conflated the two paths. Std-14 metrics oracle helpers switched to `standard14()` to faithfully mirror the Java probes' direct-constructor builds (the dict-no-/Encoding path genuinely differs in PDFBox too, e.g. ZapfDingbats 128-141 `a89`-`a96` AFM names vs `.notdef`). Soft-hyphen oracle xfail flipped to pass; bonus: the text-field appearance "unencodable eacute" divergence closes (explicit WinAnsi `/Encoding` makes `café` encodable in both engines). 7 stale pins retargeted. Closes the wave-1468 deferral. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDType1Font.java` (ctor L104-148; `readEncodingFromFont` L495-498)
  reason: single encoding source conflated AFM-Standard toUnicode with WinAnsi direct-constructor widths
- **FIX — `PDActionGoTo.get_destination` / `PDActionRemoteGoTo.get_destination` returned a bare `str` for a name/string `/D`; now delegate to `PDDestination.create` (upstream-exact).** A `COSString`/`COSName` `/D` returns `PDNamedDestination` (name via `get_named_destination()`); an explicit array returns the concrete `PDPageDestination` subclass; a malformed `/D` (bare dict/integer) raises `OSError` mirroring Java's `IOException` (was lenient `None`). The other `/D`-bearing surfaces (`PDActionEmbeddedGoTo`, `PDOutlineItem`, `PDAnnotationLink`) were audited and already at parity — only the two GoTo classes diverged. Dead str-rewrap branch removed from `PDOutlineItem.find_destination_page`; stale splitter comment fixed; the print_bookmarks example's named-destination arm is now reachable (pragma removed). New `ActionDestTypeProbe` pins the Java class identity of the four `/D` shapes; ~14 assertions retargeted across 9 pinning test files. Closes the wave-1434 deferral. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/action/PDActionGoTo.java` (`getDestination` L66-69)
  reason: deliberate local-API divergence (bare str) replaced with the upstream typed contract
- **PARITY — `PDImageXObject` mask precedence confirmed (not changed): a color-key `/Mask` array is discarded when `/SMask` is present; the wave-1449 deferral hypothesis was wrong.** Upstream `getImage` does pass `getColorKeyMask()` into `SampledImageReader.getRGBImage` even in the `/SMask` branch (PDImageXObject.java L499), but the wave-1449 disassembly missed the next step: `applyMask` (L619-728) overwrites the alpha band wholesale with the soft-mask samples (`raster.setSamples(..., 3, samples)`, L679) — replacing, not multiplying, the color-key alpha. Net: the SMask wins outright, which is exactly what pypdfbox already did. Verified against live Java 3.0.7 (a DeviceGray image with color-key `[0 60]` + an SMask ramp renders with pure ramp alpha on both halves); an experimental "apply color-key first, multiply" fix produced a worst alpha diff of 241 vs gate 28 and was reverted. Production change is documentation-only (precedence-justification comments in `pd_image_x_object.py` + `_op_do`); behaviour pinned by a new live oracle + 6 hand-written combo tests so any future change on either side trips. Closes the wave-1449 deferral as already-at-parity. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/image/PDImageXObject.java` (`getImage` L499, `applyMask` L619-728)
  reason: deferral hypothesis falsified by live oracle — no divergence existed

## Wave 1490 — 3 bug-fix surfaces (JBIG2 Resizer port, Type0 symbol-cmap encode fallback, writePage paragraph bracketing) + 2 coverage round-outs (5-agent wave)

- **PORT — JBIG2 filtered-zoom resampler: `Resizer` + `Filter`/`FilterType` kernels + `Utils` numeric helpers; `source_render_size` scaling no longer raises.** Closes the wave-1489 residual: `Bitmaps.as_raster`/`as_buffered_image` now take the scaled path when a `JBIG2ReadParam` render size differs from the bitmap size, producing the row-major 8-bit grayscale `DataBufferByte` raster (PIL mode `"L"`; unscaled bilevel mode `"1"` unchanged). `ParameterizedFilter`/`Weighttab`/`BitmapScanline`/`Resizer` consolidated into `pypdfbox/jbig2/image/resizer.py` (only `BitmapScanline` is reachable from the JBIG2 read surface); all 11 resampling kernels ported verbatim into `pypdfbox/jbig2/image/filter.py` (Bessel polynomial tables byte-for-byte; Gaussian is the reader default); `Utils.clamp/floor/ceil/round` fast approximations reproduced exactly (`pypdfbox/jbig2/util/utils.py`). Java 32-bit `int` overflow + arithmetic-shift semantics in the accumulators masked via `_int32`, reproducing the Java bit patterns exactly. Byte-exact vs the bundled 3.0.7 jar via the render-size-extended `Jbig2ReaderProbe` (scaled `readRaster` dumps). (A)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `image/Resizer.java` / `Filter.java` / `FilterType.java` / `ParameterizedFilter.java` / `Weighttab.java` / `Scanline.java` + `util/Utils.java`
  reason: last JBIG2 surface — scaled reads raised NotImplementedError
- **FIX — `PDType0Font.encode` emitted the raw Unicode codepoint for embedded symbolic/subset CIDFontType2 fonts (wrong CID + wrong `get_string_width`).** The embedded-path guard keyed on *having a unicode cmap lookup* (`get_cmap_lookup() is not None`); upstream `PDCIDFontType2.encode` keys on *embedded-ness* and on a cmap miss falls back to the parent `/ToUnicode` CMap's reverse lookup (`getCodesFromUnicode`). A symbolic subset font with only a `(3,0)` Microsoft-symbol cmap (or no cmap) therefore short-circuited to the predefined-CMap path and emitted the codepoint as the CID — eu-001's `JMGKCC+Symbol` encoded U+2022 to 0x2022/advance 1000 where PDFBox emits CID 0x78/advance 459. Now: embedded Identity- fonts always take the embedded path (cmap lookup → `/ToUnicode` reverse fallback → lenient `.notdef` only when both miss, the documented permanent divergence). New live-oracle `Type0EncodeWidthProbe` pins `encode`/`get_width`/`get_string_width` (the WRITE-side complement of the wave-1465 read-width probe) over embedded, symbol-cmap and cmap-less fixtures. Closes the wave-1465 deferral. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDCIDFontType2.java` (`encode` L357-404)
  reason: guard keyed on cmap presence instead of embedded-ness; missing /ToUnicode reverse fallback
- **FIX — lite `PDFTextStripper` missing upstream `writePage` page-body paragraph bracketing + mid-page paragraph-separation emission order reversed.** (1) Upstream emits `writeParagraphStart` once per page on the first glyph of the first non-empty article (`startOfPage` flag) and `writeParagraphEnd` after the last line of *each* article (PDFTextStripper.java:700-724); `_emit_group` now reproduces both, suppressed for subclasses that bracket paragraphs through their own article hooks (`PDFText2HTML`) so markers are not doubled. (2) The mid-page paragraph break now emits `writeLineSeparator → writeParagraphEnd → writeParagraphStart` (upstream `handleLineSeparation`, L1578-1579) instead of end-first — in PDFText2HTML `\n</p>\n<p>` rather than `</p>\n\n<p>`. With the default empty markers both changes are invisible; under `add_more_formatting` (markers promoted to the line separator) the cadence is now byte-exact vs Java over single-line / same-paragraph / paragraph-break / multipage fixtures (new live-oracle `AddMoreFormattingProbe`); Text2HTML oracle extended with the leading/trailing `<P>` bracketing case. Closes the wave-1484 cadence + wave-1489 emission-order deferrals; the text-separator placement xfail narrowed to the per-line indent prong (paragraph start/end at page boundaries now match). 3 cadence pins retargeted. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (`writePage` L700-724, `handleLineSeparation` L1578-1579)
  reason: page-body paragraph markers never emitted; separation order end-first vs upstream's line-sep-first
- **COVERAGE — `rendering/pdf_renderer.py` + `page_drawer.py` + `_aggdraw_compat.py` residual-branch round-out.** Behaviour-anchored tests (painted pixels / return values / gs mutation, not bare line execution) over module helpers (`_first_operator_is_d1`, `_normalise_rotation`), operator-handler defensive guards, shading colour-space/background fall-throughs, stroke-pattern arms (incl. the unknown-type solid fallback), image-mask helpers, per-glyph vertical accessors, `PageDrawer` no-renderer fall-backs and the odd-length-dash duplication arm. Test-only; no production changes. (D)
  upstream: n/a (test-only)
  reason: coverage round-out
- **COVERAGE — `tools/extract_images.py` `write2file` branch round-out to 100%.** Drives the arms unreachable from the wave-1489 oracle fixture: helper exception/`None` fall-throughs, `-noColorConvert` raw-path `get_raw_image` AttributeError arm + >3-channel CMYK→tiff arm, jpg CMYK re-encode, jp2 JPX passthrough + CMYK→jpeg2000, tiff DeviceGray bitonal G4. Oracle-observable branches remain pinned by the wave-1489 live-oracle file. Test-only; no production changes. (E)
  upstream: n/a (test-only)
  reason: coverage round-out

## Wave 1489 — 5 bug-fix surfaces (eu-001 line grouping byte-exact, ExtractImages write2file rewrite, PDFText2HTML article divs, weakref reverse cache, JBIG2ImageReader port) (5-agent wave)

- **FIX — lite `PDFTextStripper` line grouping ported from upstream `writePage`; eu-001.pdf at byte-exact parity.** Replaced the flat prev-only `0.5×fontSize` line-break test with upstream's running `maxYForLine`/`maxHeightForLine` accumulators + the vertical-span `overlap` test (y-up mirror of `PDFTextStripper.java` L497-501/656-707/762-766, incl. the column-break reset) and ported `LegacyPDFStreamEngine.computeFontHeight` (half font-bbox height preferring `/CapHeight` or `(ascent−descent)/2`, cached per font like upstream's `fontHeightMap`) onto a new `TextPosition.height` field — the full-font-size height proxy had over-grouped any baselines within one em. Multi-column tables whose value cells sit on a slightly different baseline than their wrapped row label now group as Java does: `test_eu001_word_spacing_matches_pdfbox` flipped from strict xfail to a byte-exact pass (199/199 lines). Flip-axes (rotated-page) extraction retains the legacy prev-only heuristic (documented inline). 3 over-grouping regressions root-caused to the old height proxy now pass (48pt composite-font titles et al.). poems-beads xfail intact (bead stitching is a separate surface). (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (writePage/overlap) + `contentstream/LegacyPDFStreamEngine.java` (computeFontHeight L324-368)
  reason: prev-only em-scaled line break + font-size-as-height proxy collapsed multi-column reading order
- **FIX — `ExtractImages.write2file` rewritten to upstream's dispatch (3 bugs).** (1) `ImageIOUtil.write_image` was called with inverted argument order — the real image bytes went to a file literally named `png`/`jpg` while the intended `prefix-N.suffix` file was created 0-byte: every extracted image was corrupt. (2) The `-noColorConvert` raw path (Java L358-381: `get_raw_image()` → png, tiff when >3 channels) was missing entirely. (3) The JPEG/JP2 direct-passthrough (raw DCT/JPX stream copied verbatim for DeviceGray/DeviceRGB or `-useDirectJPEG`, Java L387-454) + tiff/DeviceGray bitonal-G4 branch were missing — every image was decoded+re-encoded, destroying upstream's byte parity. Live-oracle confirmed via new `ExtractImagesWrite2FileProbe` (drives the real Java CLI): `.jpg` passthrough byte-identical (sha256 match), `.png` decoded-pixel digests identical, dedup count matches. Upstream's create-empty-file-even-when-decode-fails semantics also restored (2 wave-1319 tests pinned the old bug, corrected). (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `tools/ExtractImages.java` (write2file)
  reason: inverted write args + missing passthrough/raw branches
- **FIX — `PDFText2HTML` article `<div>` wrapper missing + leaked article-start separator.** Upstream emits the per-article `<div>`/`</div>` from `startArticle`/`endArticle`, which the lite stripper never calls (it drives `write_article_start`/`write_article_end`); the port's overrides were dead code, so the nesting `page→article→paragraph` lost its div layer and leaked a stray `\n`. Now byte-identical to `PDFText2HTML.getText` (probe `Text2HtmlProbe`) across escaping (`&quot;`, `&#9;`, control chars, non-ASCII `&#NNN;`), metadata-title extraction, multi-page and multi-line fixtures. One stripper-rooted divergence documented in DEFERRED (mid-page paragraph-separation emission order). (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `tools/PDFText2HTML.java`
  reason: article hooks wired to the wrong stripper extension points
- **FIX — `pd_simple_font._REVERSE_CACHE` re-keyed `id(encoding)` → `weakref.WeakKeyDictionary`.** A GC'd encoding's recycled id could return a stale unicode→code reverse map (same module-cache class as the wave-1487 `_SRGB_CACHE` leak). Auto-evicting weak keys; encoding classes verified weakref-able; identity-equality semantics preserved. Sibling audit: only `Type1Mapping.__hash__`'s in-object `id(charstring)` remains (correct — identity hash of a live member, not a cache key). (A)
  upstream: n/a (port-internal cache)
  reason: id-recycling stale-map risk
- **PORT — JBIG2 `javax.imageio` surface: `JBIG2ImageReader` + `JBIG2ImageReaderSpi` + `JBIG2ReadParam`** (+ reader-facing `Bitmaps.as_buffered_image/as_raster/subsample/subsample_x/subsample_y`). `read` returns a PIL Image (the repo's BufferedImage analogue); `read_raster` returns packed inverted-polarity raster bytes; source-region + subsampling math byte-exact vs the bundled 3.0.7 jar across 9 live-oracle cases (`Jbig2ReaderProbe`). Deviations (documented): subsample dimensions use integer division matching the 3.0.7 jar bytecode (upstream master switched to `Math.ceil`), incl. reproducing 3.0.7's X-only/Y-only dimension bug (IndexError mirroring AIOOBE); scaling via `source_render_size` raises NotImplementedError (needs the AWT Resizer port — rendering-wave concern). Closes the last open JBIG2 item. (C)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `JBIG2ImageReader.java` / `JBIG2ImageReaderSpi.java` / `JBIG2ReadParam.java` / `image/Bitmaps.java`
  reason: API-surface parity for the imageio entry points

## Wave 1488 — 5 bug-fix surfaces (button on-value stream filter, real per-glyph stripper advances, axial extend domain quirk, JBIG2 truncated remainder, oracle-package collision) + 2 surfaces at parity (7-agent wave)

- **FIX — `PDButton.get_on_value_for_widget` / `PDCheckBox.get_on_value` accepted any non-`/Off` `/AP /N` key.** Now route through `widget.get_appearance().get_normal_appearance().get_sub_dictionary()` — upstream's `PDAppearanceEntry.getSubDictionary()` only surfaces COSStream-valued state keys, so a non-stream placeholder (plain dict or name) under `/N` contributes no on-value. Oracle-confirmed over stream/dict/name/mixed/all-placeholder shapes (probe `ButtonOnValueFilterProbe`). 9 stale test files retargeted (their on-state placeholders upgraded from `COSDictionary()` to `COSStream()`); appearance-generator blast radius zero (it never calls the discovery helpers). Closes the wave-1487 deferral. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/form/PDButton.java` (`getOnValueForWidget` L353) + `PDCheckBox.java` + `pdmodel/interactive/annotation/PDAppearanceEntry.java` (`getSubDictionary`)
  reason: raw-key iteration bypassed the stream filter
- **FIX — lite `PDFTextStripper` used font-wide-average advances; now threads real per-glyph widths.** `_emit` decodes the show-text bytes code-by-code via the font's `read_code` (1–4-byte Type0/CID, single-byte simple) and advances by the true per-code width (`PDFont.get_width` /Widths-or-program, oracle-verified wave 1408) + Tc (+ Tw on single-byte code 32), composed through textMatrix×CTM; the average path survives only as a malformed-font fallback. `_is_word_break` recalibrated to upstream's `min(widthOfSpace×spacingTolerance, averageCharWidth×averageCharTolerance)` (0.5/0.3 defaults; legacy `font_size×1.5` kept for font-less runs); runs subdivide on intra-run Tc-widened gaps (`_split_run_on_word_gaps`, ActualText/ligature runs exempt); `TextPosition` gains `individual_widths`; `PDFTextStripperByArea` routes boundary glyphs at true cumulative offsets. 4 strict xfails flipped to passing (word-gap + 3 horiz-scaling Tz cases); with_outline.pdf intra-line X drift eliminated (~0.005pt, now asserted at parity); 4 stale pins retargeted. Residual xfail narrowed: the double space Java emits around an explicit space glyph under large Tc (whitespace-collapse rule). Closes the by-area exact-split + word-break-calibration deferrals. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `PDFStreamEngine.showText` / `PDFTextStripper.writeString` (PDF 32000-1 §9.4.4)
  reason: average-advance estimate diverged on per-glyph positions, word breaks, and region splits
- **FIX — axial shading `/Extend` substituted the spec-literal 0/1 for out-of-axis pixels; upstream substitutes the raw `/Domain` endpoint.** `AxialShadingContext.getRaster` feeds `domain[0]`/`domain[1]` (NOT 0/1) into `key=(int)(inputValue*factor)` on the extend branches, so for a non-default domain like `[0.25 0.75]` the extended pixels take the colour at ramp *fraction* 0.25/0.75 — pypdfbox's `u=0/1` clamp returned the endpoint colour (left-extend pixel diverged (191,0,64) vs Java (159,0,95)). Now mirrors the quirk (radial confirmed different upstream: 0/1, already at parity). Ramp index switched from `round()` to upstream's truncating `int()`. INTENTIONAL DIVERGENCE pinned oracle-free: for `/Domain` outside [0,1] + extend, Java throws `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` (verified: index 284 / length 143 at `AxialShadingContext.getRaster:238`); pypdfbox clamps the ramp index and renders. (D — agent lost to a server error before reporting; work verified + adopted by the orchestrator)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/AxialShadingContext.java` / `RadialShadingContext.java` (`getRaster`)
  reason: spec-literal extend clamp missed the domain-endpoint quirk
- **FIX — JBIG2 generic refinement region used Python floored `%` where Java truncates toward zero.** `referenceDX % 8` with a negative refinement reference offset (`(RDW>>1)+RDX < 0`, arising on large symbol dictionaries) selected the wrong context-shift branch (`-1%8`: Python 7 → zero branch, Java -1 → shift branch), desyncing the shared arithmetic stream — first surfacing as a symbol id 866 > 805 IndexError on `20123110001.jb2` text-region instance #33 (the 805 dictionary symbols themselves decode bit-exact; localized via new `Jbig2SymbolDictProbe`). `_java_mod` applied at the three negative-capable `%` sites + Java's `<<` low-5-bit shift-count mask on `modBitsToTrim`. Page now decodes bit-exact vs PDFBox 3.0.7 (2550×3345). Closes the wave-1426 deferral. (F)
  upstream: apache/pdfbox-jbig2 `GenericRefinementRegionDecodingProcedure.java`
  reason: remainder-sign semantics diverged on negative reference offsets
- **FIX (test-infra) — oracle test-package namespace collision.** `tests/pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/outline/` and `.../destination/` lacked `__init__.py`, so under pytest's `prepend` import mode their `oracle/` subpackages were rooted as a bare top-level `oracle` package — two such dirs in one run collide in `sys.modules` (`ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'oracle.<test>'`). Added the two missing `__init__.py`; whole-tree audit shows no other broken package chains; all 4098 oracle-dir tests now collect together. Closes the wave-1482 deferral. Also from the same agent: the three documented fontbox PYTHONHASHSEED flakes did NOT reproduce across 16+ seed/ordering sweeps and their logic audits clean (fixed cipher seeds, per-instance caches, fresh ttLib objects) — left documented; one latent same-class bug found and deferred: `pd_simple_font._REVERSE_CACHE` is keyed by `id(encoding)`, which GC can recycle (stale-map risk). (G)
- 2 surfaces at parity:
  - `PDCIDFontType2` non-embedded (`/FontFile2`-less) model-layer contract — isEmbedded, descriptor program absence, `/CIDToGIDMap` kind, `/DW`, codeToCID sweep, getWidth all match (probe `CidSubstituteGidProbe`; fixture built deterministically by stripping `/FontFile2` from an embedded DejaVuSans Type0). INTENTIONAL DIVERGENCE documented: upstream `codeToGID` resolves substitute GIDs via the machine-dependent platform FontMapper; pypdfbox keeps substitute-font GID resolution in the renderer, so the substitute GID is unpinnable/not pinned. (B)
  - NoRotate (`/F` bit 5) + transparency-group appearance compositing under `/Rotate` 0/90/180/270 — pypdfbox folds the PDFBOX-4744 counter-rotation into the user-space CTM *before* painting the group canvas, geometrically equivalent to upstream's rebuild-then-counter-rotate buffer order; per-pixel maxdiff=0 across group-alpha overlap/backdrop fixtures. Closes the wave-1484 deferral. (E)

## Wave 1487 — 5 bug-fix surfaces (FontDescriptor lazy caches + bbox pad, PDButton strict check_value, get_font typed-PDFont, image resources/caches, Float.toString E-notation) + 2 surfaces at parity (7-agent wave)

- **FIX — `PDFontDescriptor` lazy caches + `/FontBBox` leniency.** `get_flags`/`get_cap_height`/`get_x_height` now lazily cache the dict value in instance fields on first read (sentinels -1 / -inf) and the setters overwrite the cache with the *raw* value, matching upstream — a direct dict mutation after the first read is not observed, and `set_cap_height(-100)` re-reads as -100 (the PDFBOX-429 `abs()` workaround fires only on the cache-miss dict read). `get_font_bounding_box` now zero-pads a malformed short `/FontBBox` (and coerces non-numeric entries to 0) instead of returning None, mirroring `new PDRectangle(rect)` → `Arrays.copyOf(toFloatArray(), 4)`. 5 stale tests retargeted. Re-run of the wave-1486 session-limited surface; salvage at /tmp/wave1486_interrupted_B verified line-by-line (probe + oracle test applied as-is; the bbox fix re-derived — the salvaged diff would have raised TypeError on non-numeric entries where upstream coerces to 0). (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDFontDescriptor.java` (fields L46-48, getFlags L388, getCapHeight L522, getXHeight L552, getFontBoundingBox L411) + `PDRectangle.java` (L143-160)
  reason: every-read dict access + every-read abs() + short-bbox None all diverged from the cached/lenient upstream contract
- **FIX — `PDButton` strict `check_value` + empty-string on-values (5 bugs).** `get_on_values` no longer skips empty on-values (AP-less widgets contribute "") and returns an insertion-ordered `dict_keys` view (upstream LinkedHashSet); `set_value`/`set_default_value` route through the strict `check_value` (IllegalArgumentException → ValueError, no permissive fall-through); `PDCheckBox.check()` sets the discovered on-value ("" when none — the "Yes" fallback is gone) and `is_checked` is `get_value() == get_on_value()`; `PDPushButton.get_on_values` returns an empty ordered view. Appearance regeneration (pypdfbox extension) now runs before the strict check so `regenerate_appearance=True` still works. ~24 stale tests retargeted across 13 files. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/form/PDButton.java` (`setValue`/`checkValue`/`getOnValues`), `PDCheckBox.java`, `PDPushButton.java`
  reason: permissive value scaffold + empty-on-value skip diverged from the strict upstream contract
- **FIX — `PDResources.get_font` returned the raw `COSDictionary` for DIRECT font entries.** Now always wraps via `PDFontFactory.create_font` and returns a typed `PDFont` (None for missing/non-dict); the resource cache is consulted/populated for indirect entries only, exactly matching upstream. Removes the legacy cluster-#1 raw-dict surface that crashed the free-text handler in wave 1486. Full caller audit (13 call sites): the orphan-widgets fixup + appearance helper now route typed fonts through `put` correctly; the debugger/examples call sites that expected typed fonts were previously broken for direct entries and now work. Sibling accessors (`get_x_object`, `get_color_space`, `get_pattern`, `get_shading`, `get_ext_gstate`, `get_property_list`) audited — all already wrap both entry kinds; `get_font` was the sole divergent one. 4 stale tests retargeted; oracle pin extended (`ResourceLookupProbe` `font_F0_class`). (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDResources.java` (`getFont`)
  reason: direct-entry raw-dict legacy contract vs upstream's always-typed getFont
- **FIX — `PDImageXObject` lost the upstream `resources` linkage + per-instance caches.** `PDXObject.create_x_object` now threads its `resources` into `PDImageXObject(stream, resources)` (upstream constructor parity): a *named* `/ColorSpace` resolves against the page's `/Resources/ColorSpace` and an indirect colour-space ref consults/populates the document ResourceCache (PDFBOX-4022). Orchestrator completed the surface after the agent was interrupted: removed an invented `set_image` method (upstream has no such API), added the per-instance `colorSpace` cache (built once, reset by `set_color_space`, which also invalidates the image cache — Java L945-951), and implemented the real `cachedImageSubsampling` rule (full-region renders cached at ANY subsampling level, preferring the lowest seen; region renders never cached — Java L472-519, not the "subsampling==1 only" approximation the agent shipped). (E + orchestrator)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/image/PDImageXObject.java` (ctor L77-101, getColorSpace, getImage L463-519, setColorSpace L945-951) + `pdmodel/graphics/PDXObject.java` (`createXObject`)
  reason: dropped constructor param severed named-CS resolution + both upstream caches; set_image was fabricated API
- **FIX — `Matrix.toString`/`Vector.toString` lost Java's scientific notation; `COSFloat` mis-rendered the smallest subnormals.** Added `pypdfbox.cos.cos_float.float_to_string` — a byte-for-byte `Float.toString` port (decimal form for 1e-3 ≤ |v| < 1e7, scientific otherwise; shortest round-trip mantissa with Java's two-significant-digit floor, so `Float.MIN_VALUE` → `1.4E-45`, not `1E-45`) — and rewired `Matrix`/`Vector` toString onto it (a 1e8 cell now renders `1.0E8`, was `100000000.0`). `format_float32` now derives from `float_to_string` + `BigDecimal.toPlainString` semantics, which also closed the previously-documented subnormal divergence on the `COSFloat.write_pdf` path (`Float.MIN_VALUE` serializes byte-exact); the now-obsolete subnormal exclusion was removed from the COS self-write oracle. In-window plain-decimal output is byte-identical to the wave-1485 pins. Verified over the full `FloatToStringProbe` edge sweep (boundaries, powers of ten, subnormals, MAX_VALUE, ±0.0). (G)
  upstream: JDK `java.lang.Float#toString` as consumed by PDFBox 3.0.7 `util/Matrix.java`/`Vector.java` + `cos/COSFloat.java`
  reason: plain-decimal-everywhere formatting diverged outside the [1e-3, 1e7) window and on subnormals
- **Test-isolation fix (orchestrator, gate-caught):** the ICC-CMYK image oracle test failed order-dependently under full-tree collection — `test_pd_output_intent_icc_wave668.py` monkeypatches `ImageCms.createProfile`/`ImageCmsProfile` and its autouse fixture cleared the module-level ICC caches only BEFORE each test, so the file's last fake profile leaked into `pd_icc_based`'s one-slot `_SRGB_CACHE` for every test collected later ("Invalid type for Profile"). Both that fixture and `test_pd_iccbased_v4_wave1386.py`'s now clear after as well. Pre-existing (reproduced on the wave-1486 commit in a pristine worktree), invisible to the pre-push gates because the full-suite run ignores `*/oracle/*`.
- 2 surfaces at parity:
  - AcroForm fixup orphan-widget rebuild + generate-appearances arm (PDFBOX-4985: `/NeedAppearances` + empty `/Fields` → field-tree rebuild from page widgets, parent-chain dedup, missing-`/DA` font fallback, `/AP` generation, `/NeedAppearances` clear) — differentially pinned over 3 raw-bytes fixtures, byte-faithful; probe `AcroFormOrphanFixupProbe`. (D)
  - `PDNumberTreeNode`/`PDNameTreeNode` half-populated `/Limits` padding — pypdfbox pads the unset slot with `COSNull.NULL` where upstream holds a Java-null element; the live oracle (`NumberTreeLimitsPadProbe`, reflection into the private setters) confirms both serialize to the identical token (`[5 null]` / `[null 9]`) and both resolve to None via `getObject`. Non-observable; parity pinned, no code change. (F)

## Wave 1486 — 6 bug-fix surfaces (annotation /Subtype COSString, button COSName-only values, destination create fall-through, COSFilterInputStream close semantics + PDDocument RuntimeError guards, page-label setStyle verbatim, free-text font wrap) + 1 surface at parity (7-agent wave; B's FontDescriptor surface session-limited, salvaged for re-run)

- **FIX — `PDAnnotation` factory + `get_subtype()` read `/Subtype` via `get_name` (COSName-only).** Upstream uses `COSDictionary.getNameAsString`, which decodes COSName AND COSString — a `/Subtype (Link)` string dispatches to `PDAnnotationLink` and reads back "Link"; pypdfbox fell to `PDAnnotationUnknown`/None. Now `get_name_as_string`. All 19 no-arg constructor dict shapes + markup/base accessor defaults confirmed at parity (probe `AnnotFactoryProbe`). (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/annotation/PDAnnotation.java` (`createAnnotation` L111, `getSubtype` L245)
  reason: COSString subtypes mis-dispatched to Unknown
- **FIX — `PDButton.get_value`/`get_default_value` returned stored COSStrings.** Upstream reads only an `instanceof COSName` token: COSString (or any non-name) `/V` → "Off", `/DV` → "". 3 stale tests retargeted. Permissive `set_value` scaffold + empty-string on-value skip remain documented divergences (deferred). (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/form/PDButton.java` (`getValue` L105, `getDefaultValue` L205)
  reason: non-COSName value tokens leaked through instead of the Off/empty defaults
- **FIX — `PDDestination.create` raised bespoke messages for malformed destination arrays.** Upstream gates the array branch on `size() > 1 && item[1] instanceof COSName`; failing arrays fall through the else-if chain to IOException "Error: can't convert to Destination ..." — pypdfbox raised "Destination array too short"/"...must be a name" inside the branch. Now mirrors the gated fall-through (OSError, prefix-exact). XYZ short-array `getZoom` bounds-safety pinned as intentional divergence (upstream throws IndexOutOfBounds; pypdfbox returns None). 2 stale tests retargeted. (F)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/documentnavigation/destination/PDDestination.java` (`create`)
  reason: bespoke malformed-array messages replaced upstream's fall-through contract
- **FIX — `COSFilterInputStream` raised `ValueError` on read-after-close; upstream keeps reading.** Upstream extends `FilterInputStream`; the byte[] constructor wraps a `ByteArrayInputStream` whose `close()` is a no-op — read-after-close returns the next in-range bytes (oracle: `COSFilterInputStreamClosedProbe`). pypdfbox now mirrors: byte[]-backed streams stay readable after close; caller-supplied streams propagate close. Also: `PDDocument` precondition guards ("save_incremental requires a loaded document…" et al.) now raise `RuntimeError` mirroring upstream IllegalStateException (was ValueError). Closes both wave-1485 deferrals. (A — verified by orchestrator; agent session-limited before reporting)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/COSFilterInputStream.java` + `pdmodel/PDDocument.java` (L1089 family)
  reason: fabricated closed-read ValueError; IllegalStateException guards used the wrong type
- **FIX — `PDPageLabelRange.set_style` validated against a style allow-list.** Upstream `setStyle` stores any non-null string verbatim via `setName` (the label generator falls back to decimal for unrecognised codes); null removes `/S`. pypdfbox raised ValueError for unknown codes. Validation removed (use `is_valid_style` to pre-validate); write-side number-tree shape + computed labels pinned (probes `PageLabelsWriteProbe`/`PageLabelRangeAccessorProbe`). (G — verified by orchestrator; agent session-limited before reporting)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/PDPageLabelRange.java` (`setStyle`)
  reason: invented validation upstream doesn't perform
- **FIX (orchestrator, gate-caught) — fixup-injected direct `/Helv` crashed the free-text appearance handler.** `PDResources.get_font` preserves the raw COSDictionary surface for DIRECT entries (legacy cluster-#1 contract; upstream always wraps via PDFontFactory) — the wave-1484 `AcroFormDefaultFixup` injects `/Helv` as a direct entry, so `PDFreeTextAppearanceHandler._resolve_font` handed a COSDictionary to `set_font` (TypeError). The handler now wraps via `PDFontFactory.create_font`. The `get_font` direct-entry contract itself is a real divergence — DEFERRED. Also gate-caught: 3 form-oracle test files compared a fixed-up pypdfbox read against the probes' deliberate `getAcroForm(null)` — py side retargeted to `get_acro_form(None)` to mirror (the wave-1484 wiring changed the no-arg form's meaning); form+annotation oracle trees now 209/209 green.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDResources.java` (`getFont`) + `pdmodel/fixup/processor/AcroFormDefaultsProcessor.java`
  reason: direct-entry raw-dict contract met the fixup's injected fonts
- 1 surface at parity: `PDTilingPattern`/`PDShadingPattern`/`PDAbstractPattern` model accessors — dispatch messages, raw XStep/YStep (0→bbox substitution is rendering-layer only), stream-backed `getShading`, matrix defaults; new shading-pattern differential pin (probe `ShadingPatternProbe`) (E).
- PARTIAL: agent B (PDFontDescriptor accessor sweep) was session-limited with 4 stale pins unretargeted; its partial work is salvaged at /tmp/wave1486_interrupted_B and reverted. Re-run in a future wave.

## Wave 1485 — 5 bug-fix surfaces (closed-state OSError family, appearance-stream 4-digit, Std14 average-width AFM-first, public-key permission bytes, Matrix/Vector float32) + 2 surfaces at parity (7-agent wave)

- **FIX — closed-state guards in `COSStream`/`COSWriter`/`PDDocument`/`FDFDocument` raised a fabricated `ValueError("operation on closed …")`.** Upstream: `COSStream.checkClosed()` → IOException "COSStream has been closed and cannot be read. Perhaps its enclosing PDDocument has been closed?" (3 redundant pypdfbox guards replaced with `check_closed()`); `PDDocument.save` → IOException "Cannot save a document which has been closed" (oracle-confirmed; applied to `save`/`save_incremental`/`save_incremental_for_external_signing`; pypdfbox-only `split`/`extract_pages` → "PDDocument has been closed"); `COSWriter` → "COSWriter already closed" per the io-class convention; `add_signature` second call → `RuntimeError` mirroring upstream IllegalStateException "Only one signature may be added in a document" (was ValueError). 14 stale ValueError pins retargeted across 11 files. Closes the wave-1483 deferral. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSStream.java` (`checkClosed`) + `pdmodel/PDDocument.java` (L1027, L316)
  reason: fabricated ValueError family had no upstream counterpart; types + messages now IOException→OSError aligned
- Note (A): `FDFDocument.save`/`save_xfdf` keep a defensive closed-guard (`OSError "Cannot save a document which has been closed"`) where upstream has NO guard (oracle: NO_THROW) — intentional symmetry with `PDDocument`.
- **FIX — `PDAppearanceContentStream` emitted 5 fractional digits; upstream emits 4.** Threaded an instance-level `_max_fraction_digits` through `PDPageContentStream._write_operands`/`set_dash_pattern`/`show_text_with_positioning` (default 5, page writer byte-identical) and overrode to 4 in `PDAppearanceContentStream.__init__` (upstream extends the 4-digit `PDAbstractContentStream` base). Listbox-highlight now emits `0.6 0.7569 0.8431 rg` like PDFBox (oracle: real upstream appearance stream). 3 stale 5-digit pins retargeted. Closes the wave-1483 deferral. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDAbstractContentStream.java` (ctor L112) + `interactive/annotation/PDAppearanceContentStream.java`
  reason: appearance streams inherited the page writer's 5-digit override instead of the 4-digit base default
- **FIX — `PDType1Font.get_average_font_width()` let `/Widths` win over the Standard-14 AFM.** Upstream returns the AFM average whenever `getStandard14AFM() != null`, ignoring `/Widths` entirely (Helvetica with `/Widths` 999/888/777 → 542.7714, oracle-confirmed); only non-Standard-14 fonts fall through to the `/Widths` mean. Reordered AFM-first. 1 stale test split/retargeted. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDType1Font.java` (`getAverageFontWidth` L474-484)
  reason: precedence inverted — /Widths mean returned where upstream uses the AFM average
- **FIX — `PublicKeySecurityHandler` embedded raw `/P` bytes in recipient PKCS#7 envelopes.** Upstream `computeRecipientInfo` packs `getPermissionBytesForPublicKey()` (bit 1 forced on, bits 7/8 + 13-32 cleared — default permissions wire as 3901, not 0xFFFFFFFC). The wrong bytes fed the file-key SHA-1 hash and read-back permissions, breaking cross-vendor public-key interop. Both write sites fixed; helper constants oracle-pinned (`PermProbe` new `pubkey` mode). 2 stale tests retargeted. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/encryption/PublicKeySecurityHandler.java` (`computeRecipientInfo` L449)
  reason: raw permission bytes used where upstream applies the public-key mask
- **FIX — `util.Matrix`/`Vector` stored float64 where upstream stores 32-bit floats.** No narrowing anywhere: `getRotateInstance` at non-trivial angles, multiply/concatenate chains, sheared `getScalingFactorX/Y` (PDFBOX-4148 `(float)Math.sqrt` + `Float.compare` -0.0-aware shear test), `transformPoint`/`transform(Vector)`, `Vector.scale`, and `toString` all diverged. Added `f32()` narrowing at every store AND in the float-typed intermediate arithmetic (left-to-right, matching Java accumulation so chained drift is identical); overflow → ±inf like `(float)` casts; `toString` via `format_float32`. 2 stale tests retargeted. Blast radius: util+rendering+contentstream+pdmodel (10,702 tests) green + full rendering oracle suite re-run. (F)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `util/Matrix.java` (L237-495) + `util/Vector.java`
  reason: float64 storage/arithmetic diverged from upstream float32 in every derived value
