- 2 surfaces confirmed at parity / pinned (no production change): `PDFCloneUtility` identity semantics — 17-observation oracle table (scalar sharing, identity cache, clone-of-clone, COSObject resolution, same-doc clone) + a deliberate hardening divergence pinned: pypdfbox pre-registers in-progress ARRAY clones so indirect array cycles terminate where upstream StackOverflows (probe `CloneArrayCycleOverflowProbe`) (C); `PDXObject.createXObject` factory dispatch + `PDFormXObject`/`PDTransparencyGroupAttributes` accessors incl. PDFBOX-4372 broken-resources fallback — two Java/Python idiom message differences pinned exactly ("Invalid XObject Subtype: None", short class name in "Unexpected object type") (G).
- Note (E): `AccessPermission(-1)` literal-int construction stores `~3`, so `getPermissionBytesForPublicKey` yields 3901 vs upstream 3903 on that path only — the default-constructor path (all real flows) is identical.

## Wave 1484 — 14 bugs fixed (AcroFormDefaultFixup wiring, NoRotate annotations, action get_next dispatch, ExtGState malformed-overwrite, stripper bead/article markers, incremental xref-stream append, PDStream encode-on-embed, axial/radial Background+roots) — re-run of the 8 wave-1483 session-limited surfaces (7-agent wave)

- **FIX — `AcroFormDefaultFixup` was ported but never wired; `/Helv`/`/ZaDb` injection silently no-op'd.** Upstream no-arg `getAcroForm()` is `getAcroForm(new AcroFormDefaultFixup(document))`. pypdfbox's `pypdfbox/pdmodel/fixup/` classes existed but `PDDocumentCatalog.get_acro_form()` never invoked them; additionally `AcroFormDefaultsProcessor._ensure_font` passed a font-name string to `PDType1Font` (expects a COSDictionary), swallowed by a bare except — so `/DA` seeding and `/DR` font injection never happened. Now: no-arg `get_acro_form()` applies a fresh `AcroFormDefaultFixup` (sentinel distinguishes no-arg from explicit `None` = upstream `getAcroForm(null)`, no fixup); processor builds the standard-14 Type1 font dicts. Oracle: `/DA`="/Helv 0 Tf 0 g ", `/DR` fonts Helv+ZaDb, fresh wrapper per no-arg call (`a is not b`). Closes the wave-1482 merger byte-delta gap. `get_acro_form_or_create`/`import_page` route via `get_acro_form(None)`. 1 stale test retargeted. (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDDocumentCatalog.java` (`getAcroForm`) + `pdmodel/fixup/AcroFormDefaultFixup.java`/`processor/AcroFormDefaultsProcessor.java`
  reason: default fixup never applied; font-dict construction bug made the defaults processor a no-op
- **FIX — NoRotate (`/F` bit 5) annotations rendered rotated on rotated pages.** Upstream `PageDrawer.showAnnotation` counter-rotates a NoRotate annotation around its rect's upper-left corner when the page has `/Rotate` (PDFBOX-4744). Ported the AffineTransform sequence into `_render_annotation`; oracle-pixel-confirmed at `/Rotate` 90/180/270 + unrotated no-op + non-identity appearance `/Matrix`. Closes the wave-1482 deferral. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/PageDrawer.java` (`showAnnotation`)
  reason: NoRotate counter-rotation was not implemented in the lite renderer
- **FIX — `PDAction.get_next()` used the lenient `PDAction.create` dispatcher and dropped malformed slots.** Upstream `getNext()` routes through `PDActionFactory.createAction` (unknown/missing `/S` → null) and preserves the `/Next` array length. pypdfbox returned `PDActionUnknown` wrappers and silently dropped non-dict members. Now returns `list[PDAction | None]` with length == `/Next` array length. Secondary accessors (RemoteGoTo `/F`/`/D`/`/NewWindow`, Launch + `PDWindowsLaunchParams.get_operation` default "open", GoToE `/T`, Hide `/H` default true, Named `/N`, full factory dispatch) confirmed at parity. 2 stale tests retargeted. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/action/PDAction.java` (`getNext`) + `PDActionFactory.java`
  reason: wrong dispatcher produced wrappers instead of null slots and collapsed the list length
- **FIX — `PDExtendedGraphicsState.copy_into_graphics_state` skipped present-but-malformed entries.** Upstream calls each target setter unconditionally per present key: malformed `/D`/`/TR`/`/TR2`/`/RI` push null (clearing the seeded value); malformed `/OPM` pushes the spec default 0. pypdfbox's `_copy_value` skipped `None`, leaving stale seeded values. Oracle-confirmed across 6 edge cases; all other accessors (`/LW`/`/ML`/`/FL`/`/SM`/`/CA`/`/ca`, `/LC`/`/LJ` sentinels, `/OP`/`/op` fallback, `/Font`, `/BM` normalization, SMask CTM-clone) at parity. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/state/PDExtendedGraphicsState.java` (`copyIntoGraphicsState`)
  reason: malformed gs entries silently skipped instead of overwriting the target state
- **FIX — `PDFTextStripper` bead handling: hardcoded inter-bucket line separator, page-level article wrap, inert `add_more_formatting`.** Upstream wraps each `charactersByArticle` bucket in `startArticle()`/`endArticle()` (default markers "") — adjacent buckets concatenate directly; pypdfbox injected `write_line_separator` between buckets and wrapped articles once per page. And `setAddMoreFormatting` promotes `paragraphEnd`/`pageStart`/`articleStart`/`articleEnd` to the line separator — pypdfbox stored the flag but never used it. All three fixed; oracle byte-match on the bead fixture. 1 stale test retargeted. Byte-exact AMF cadence remains in the documented average-advance carve-out family. (D)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (`writePage` L533/725 + `writeText` L243-250)
  reason: bead buckets separated by a hardcoded newline; article markers mis-scoped; AMF flag inert
- **FIX — incremental save over an xref-stream source emitted a malformed table/XRef hybrid; parser never tagged xref-stream documents.** Upstream `COSParser.parseXref` sets `isXRefStream` from the resolver's xref type; `COSWriter.doWriteXRefInc` appends a real cross-reference STREAM for stream sources. pypdfbox always wrote a classic table while the appended trailer kept `/Type /XRef` + leftover `/W`/`/Index`/`/Filter` keys. Now: parser tags via `COSDocument.set_is_xref_stream`; new `_do_write_xref_stream_increment()` (free head + changed entries only, self-entry out of `/Index`, document-wide `/Size`, `/Prev`, `/ID[1]` refresh). Object selection confirmed already at parity (page edit `[33]`, parent chain `[29,32,33]`, add-annot page+annot only); `/Index`/`/Size`/`/Prev` byte-match the oracle. (E)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`parseXref`) + `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` (`doWriteXRefInc`) + `PDFXRefStream`
  reason: xref-stream sources got a classic-table increment with stale XRef trailer keys
- **FIX — `PDStream(doc, input, filter)` stored input bytes verbatim while stamping `/Filter` (corrupt embedded streams).** Upstream writes the input through `createOutputStream(filters)` — encoding it. pypdfbox `set_raw_data`'d the raw payload tagged `/FlateDecode`, so `create_input_stream()` raised on decode; every font embedder (`/FontFile2` TrueType/CIDType2/Type1) and `PDEmbeddedFile` hit this path and emitted un-decodable streams. Now encodes via `create_output_stream(filters)` (verbatim only when `filters is None`); oracle byte-identical incl. raw deflate sha256 (CPython zlib == Java Deflater on pinned payloads). 9 stale tests retargeted. (G)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/PDStream.java` (ctor L130-142)
  reason: embed constructor stamped /Filter without encoding, corrupting every embedder output
- **FIX — axial/radial shading: `/Background` ignored, radial root selection unfaithful, degenerate cone crash.** (1) Out-of-extent non-extended pixels painted white instead of `/Background` (upstream `getRaster` paints `getRgbBackground()`). (2) The "larger non-negative root" heuristic mis-colored the r0==r1 cylinder band edge and asymmetric `/Extend[T,F]` inward region — replaced with a faithful port of `RadialShadingContext.calculateInputValues` + the two-root/extend/background ladder. (3) `|c1−c0|==|r1−r0|` (denom 0) raised `ZeroDivisionError` and painted nothing — Java float division yields ±Inf/NaN and `(int)(NaN*factor)==0` → start color; added IEEE-754 division + NaN-propagating max + NaN→index-0. Also: `AxialShadingContext`/`RadialShadingContext` accept both the upstream COSArray `/Extend` and pypdfbox's tuple shape (latent crash). Per-channel diff ≤2 across all probed cases. Orchestrator follow-fix: F's blanket NaN→start-color mapping over-painted the COINCIDENT-circle case (c0==c1, r0==r1 → both roots 0/0=NaN) — upstream's both-NaN early-out (`getRaster` L188) skips the pixel entirely (no paint, extend ladder not consulted), oracle-confirmed white even inside the circle; only a NaN selected *through* an extend branch (the ±Inf cone) reaches `(int)(NaN*factor)==0`. Both-NaN early-out added; permanent `radial_same_circle` differential pin added. 3 stale rendering tests retargeted (wave481 origin-cone-FF expected the pre-1484 linear-root gradient — upstream paints nothing; wave702 denom-0 cone with extend now start-color; wave671 same-circle white pin kept, passing again). (F)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/AxialShadingContext.java` + `RadialShadingContext.java` (`getRaster`, `calculateInputValues`)
  reason: background fill missing; root-selection heuristic diverged on degenerate geometry; denom-0 cone crashed

## Wave 1483 — 6 bugs fixed (COSArray typed-setter no-grow, abstract content-stream float32/4-digit, io sibling closed-OSError, FunctionType2 /N default, Type3 d1 operand guard, page-tree /Count trust) + 6 surfaces at parity (PARTIAL — 12 of 20 agents completed, 8 session-limited, re-run as wave 1484)

- **FIX — `COSArray.set_int`/`set_string` (and pypdfbox-only `set_float`/`set_boolean`) auto-grew on out-of-range index.** Upstream `setInt`/`setString` delegate to `set(index, …)` → `List.set` (throws, no grow). All four typed mutators now route through `self.set(...)`, raising `IndexError`; `set_string` also gains upstream's `None` → `set(index, null)` branch. The only index-based caller (`PDNumberTreeNode` limit setters) already pre-pads, matching upstream. Completes the wave-1480 `set_name` fix; 9 stale tests retargeted (incl. 6 artifact-marked-content tests caught by the full-suite gate, the same family wave 1480 hit). (A)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSArray.java` (`setInt`/`setString`→`set`)
  reason: typed index-mutators auto-grew instead of throwing like upstream `List.set`
- **FIX — `PDAbstractContentStream._format_decimal` used float64 `%.Nf` at a wrong 5-digit default.** Upstream's shared base constructor sets `setMaximumFractionDigits(4)` (the 5 is a `PDPageContentStream`-specific override) and routes through float32 `NumberFormatUtil.formatFloatFast`. `_format_decimal` now delegates to the wave-1480 float32 helper (generalized to `_format_number(value, max_fraction_digits)`) at the correct default of 4; byte divergences like `12345.6789`→`12345.6787`@4 and `0.000005`→`0` fixed. Oracle-confirmed via new `AbstractContentStreamFormatProbe` (real upstream `PDAppearanceContentStream`@4 + `PDPageContentStream`@5). Closes the wave-1480 deferral. (B)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDAbstractContentStream.java` (ctor + `writeOperand(float)`) + `util/NumberFormatUtil.java`
  reason: abstract content-stream operands formatted via float64 at 5 digits instead of float32 formatFloatFast at 4
- **FIX — `RandomAccessReadMemoryMapped`/`RandomAccessWriteBuffer`/`ScratchFile` closed-state ops raised `ValueError`; `ScratchFileBuffer.length()` over-guarded.** Upstream closed-checks throw `IOException` → now `OSError` with upstream-exact messages ("RandomAccessReadMemoryMappedFile already closed" — upstream simple-name preserved, "RandomAccessBuffer already closed", "Scratch file already closed"); memory-mapped negative-seek message aligned to "Invalid position N"; `ScratchFileBuffer.length()` no longer checks closed (upstream omits the guard — oracle `lengthBufClosed=NO_THROW`). Extends the wave-1482 alignment; closes that deferral. 7 stale test files retargeted. (C)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `io/RandomAccessReadMemoryMappedFile.java` + write-buffer base + `io/ScratchFile.java`/`ScratchFileBuffer.java`
  reason: closed-state errors used ValueError; length() carried a close guard upstream doesn't have
- **FIX — `PDFunctionType2` with a missing `/N` defaulted the exponent to `1.0`; upstream is `-1.0`.** Upstream caches `exponent = getFloat(COSName.N)` and single-arg `COSDictionary.getFloat` returns `-1` on a missing key, so a no-`/N` Type 2 evaluates as `x**-1` (oracle: C0=0/C1=1, eval(0.25)=4.0) and `getN()`/`toString()` report `-1.0`. `get_n()` now defaults to `-1.0`; `eval`/`__str__` follow. `/N` is required by §7.10.3 so only malformed input is affected. Type 3 stitching (bound-exact strict `<`, zero-width subdomains, repeated bounds, reversed Encode, nesting) + rest of Type 2 (N=0, negative-base NaN, C0/C1 min-sizing, no input clip) confirmed at parity. 4 stale tests retargeted. (I)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/function/PDFunctionType2.java` + `cos/COSDictionary.java` (`getFloat(COSName)`)
  reason: missing-/N exponent defaulted to 1.0 instead of upstream's getFloat -1 sentinel
- **FIX — `PDType3CharProc.get_glyph_b_box` accepted `d1` with more than six operands.** Upstream computes the bbox only when the first operator is `d1` AND `arguments.size() == 6`; a 7-operand `d1` returns null. pypdfbox's `< 6` guard wrongly computed `(0,0,500,700)` from `750 0 0 0 500 700 900 d1` (oracle: null) — corrupting the `generate_bounding_box` charproc-union plan-B. Guard now `!= 6`. (M)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDType3CharProc.java` (`getGlyphBBox`)
  reason: glyph-bbox guard accepted over-long d1 operand lists upstream rejects
- **FIX — `PDPageTree.get(index)`/`__getitem__` ignored the stored `/Count` (full-walk resolution).** Upstream `get(pageNum, node, encountered)` trusts each node's `/Count` to decide descent — observable whenever `/Count` lies. Faithful recursive port with upstream exception contract: undercount → `IndexError("1-based index out of bounds: N")`, overcount-with-short-Kids → `RuntimeError("1-based index not found: N")` (`IllegalStateException`), `/Kids` cycle → `RuntimeError("Possible recursion found…")`; `get_count()` stays raw O(1); Pythonic negative indexing preserved as a documented extension. (Q)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDPageTree.java` (`get(int,COSDictionary,int)` L308-379)
  reason: page lookup walked the real tree instead of trusting /Count like upstream
- 6 surfaces confirmed at parity / divergences pinned (no production change): outline `/Count` open/close propagation incl. closed-ancestor absorption + always-open root (G, probe `OutlineCountProbe`); `PDNameTreeNode` setter-writers — only ONE divergence on the string tree (single-armed `set_kids` on non-root; root `/Limits` + empty-map behavior AGREE unlike the integer tree; sort order COSString `/Limits` at parity) (E, probe `NameTreeSetterProbe`); named-destination resolution — tree-over-legacy precedence, dict-with-`/D` vs bare array, string→string one-hop-then-null (PDFBOX-5975) (H, probe `NamedDestEdgeProbe`); optional-content WRITE paths — `add_group` shape, `set_group_enabled` over ALL same-named groups, BaseState-OFF interaction, OCMD `/P` round-trip (K, probe `OcmdProbe`); CCITTFax K==0 MH-RLE sniff fallback byte-exact vs pypdfbox's always-T.4 path incl. white-padding + truncated `/Rows` (L); `PDCIDFontType2` `/CIDToGIDMap` edges — odd-length trailing-byte drop, empty-stream all-GID-0, non-Identity name as bounded identity (N).
- Note (E): name-tree non-root `set_kids` stays single-armed (clears `/Names`) where upstream clears only on a root — intentional §7.9.7-conformant divergence, consistent with the wave-1482 number-tree pins.
- PARTIAL: agents D (AcroFormDefaultFixup), F (NoRotate annotations), J (action factory), O (stripper knobs), P (incremental-save selection), R (ExtGState sweep), S (axial/radial extend), T (PDStream filters) were session-limited before reporting; their partial work was salvaged to /tmp/wave1483_interrupted and reverted from the tree. Re-run as wave 1484.

## Wave 1482 — 5 bugs fixed (merger JOIN-mode rename, FunctionType0 32-bit signed cast, inline-image COSNull two-key, date UTC +00'00', io closed-OSError) + 5 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — `PDFMergerUtility` `JOIN_FORM_FIELDS_MODE` appended colliding AcroForm fields verbatim.** In PDFBox 3.0.x `acroFormJoinFieldsMode` is a thin delegate to `acroFormLegacyMode` (`PDFMergerUtility.java:1278`), so JOIN and LEGACY are identical — a destination field-name collision is renamed to `dummyFieldNameN`. pypdfbox's JOIN path instead concatenated the source field verbatim, leaving two same-named fields. `_acro_form_join_fields_mode` now delegates to `_acro_form_legacy_mode`. Oracle-confirmed (`MergeFormFieldsModeProbe`): both modes emit `dummyFieldName1` + `name` on a single collision; three `A` forms → `A` + `dummyFieldName1` + `dummyFieldName2`. `/DR`/`/DA`/`/Q`/`/NeedAppearances` confirmed NOT reconciled by `mergeAcroForm` upstream (parity). 5 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `multipdf/PDFMergerUtility.java` (`acroFormJoinFieldsMode` → `acroFormLegacyMode`)
  reason: JOIN mode concatenated colliding fields verbatim instead of delegating to legacy-mode rename
- **FIX — `PDFunctionType0` treated 32-bit `/BitsPerSample` sample codes as unsigned.** Upstream `getSamples()` stores each code via `(int) readBits(bitsPerSample)` — for `bits == 32` a code ≥ 2³¹ truncates to a *negative* signed int before the `/Decode` mapping, clamping output to `/Range[min]` (upstream's own "will this cast work properly for 32 bitsPerSample" TODO). pypdfbox returned the unsigned value, producing large positive outputs (oracle example: codes `[0, 0xFFFFFFFF]`, `Range=[0,1]` → PDFBox eval = 0.0, pypdfbox was 1.0). `_read_sample` now sign-extends 32-bit codes; `bits < 32` lossless, unaffected. 3 stale parametrizations retargeted. Size==1 collapse, truncated-stream zero-fill, Encode/Decode defaults, Domain/Range clipping, 1-/2-bit widths all confirmed at parity (probes `FunctionType0DegenerateProbe`/`FunctionType0Bit32Probe`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/function/PDFunctionType0.java` (`Rinterpol.getSamples()`)
  reason: 32-bit sample codes were unpacked unsigned instead of upstream's signed-int truncation
- **FIX — `PDInlineImage` two-key getters short-circuited on raw short-key presence.** `get_width`/`get_height`/`get_bits_per_component`/`is_stencil`/`get_interpolate` used `contains_key(short)` to pick the short key, so a `COSNull` short-form value returned the default. Upstream `COSDictionary.getInt/getBoolean(first, second, default)` route through `getDictionaryObject(first, second)`, which resolves a `COSNull` first key to null and falls back to the long-form key (`/W`=COSNull + `/Width`=13 → PDFBox 13, pypdfbox was -1; same for `/BPC`, `/IM`, `/I`). `_two_key_int`/`_two_key_boolean` now resolve via `get_dictionary_object(short, long)` then type-check. Short-wins-when-both-present, long-form fallback, and wrong-type default confirmed at parity (probe `InlineImageKeyResolveProbe`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSDictionary.java` (`getInt`/`getBoolean(COSName, COSName, default)` via `getDictionaryObject(COSName, COSName)`)
  reason: COSNull short-form value returned the default instead of falling back to the long-form key
- **FIX — PDF date write-side rendered UTC as a bare `Z`/`Z00'00'` instead of `+00'00'` (4 helpers).** Upstream `DateConverter.toString`/`formatTZoffset` always render `(+|-)HH'mm'` — "For offset of 0 millis, the String returned is +00'00', never Z" (`DateConverter.java:234`) — and every write path (`COSDictionary.setDate`, `PDDocumentInformation.setCreationDate`, `PDSignature.setSignDate`, `PDAnnotation.setModifiedDate(Calendar)`, `PDEmbeddedFile.setCreationDate/setModDate`) routes through it. Four private pypdfbox `_format_pdf_date` helpers emitted `Z` (cos) or `Z00'00'` (pdmodel/annotation/embedded-file) for a zero offset. The annotation + embedded-file formatters now delegate to the shared `pd_document_information._format_pdf_date`; naive datetimes are treated as UTC (`+00'00'`). Parse side unchanged (still accepts `Z` and `+00'00'`); xmpbox-level `DateConverter.to_string` was already correct. Live-oracle-confirmed via `DateConvertProbe` format mode. 6 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `util/DateConverter.java` (`toString`/`formatTZoffset`)
  reason: UTC offsets serialized as `Z`/`Z00'00'` instead of upstream's invariant `+00'00'`
- **FIX — `RandomAccessReadBuffer`/`RandomAccessReadBufferedFile` closed-state + seek error semantics (3 divergences).** (1) Operations after `close()` raised `ValueError`; upstream `checkClosed()` throws `IOException` → now `OSError` with upstream messages ("RandomAccessBuffer already closed" / "org.apache.pdfbox.io.RandomAccessReadBufferedFile already closed"). (2) Negative seek raised "invalid seek position N"; upstream is "Invalid position N" (siblings `SequenceRandomAccessRead`/`RandomAccessReadView` already matched). (3) `RandomAccessReadBufferedFile.length()` raised after close; upstream `length()` omits the close guard and returns the cached file length. Oracle-confirmed via `RandomAccessReadSemanticsProbe`. 6 stale tests retargeted. (Sibling `RandomAccessReadMemoryMapped`/`RandomAccessWriteBuffer`/`ScratchFile` still raise `ValueError` — DEFERRED.)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `io/RandomAccessReadBuffer.java` (`checkClosed`, `seek`) + `io/RandomAccessReadBufferedFile.java` (`checkClosed`, `seek`, `length`)
  reason: closed-state errors used ValueError and a non-upstream seek message; length() over-guarded after close
- 5 surfaces confirmed at parity / divergences pinned (no production change): `PDNumberTreeNode` setter-writers — 3 intentional spec-side divergences pinned (PDFBox writes root `/Limits`, `/Limits [null null]` on an empty map, and leaves `/Nums`+`/Kids` both present after `setKids`; pypdfbox stays §7.9.7-conformant — probe `NumberTreeSetterProbe`) (A); predefined CMaps — Identity-H/V metadata + full-range `to_cid`, 90ms-RKSJ-V `usecmap` override precedence, GBK-EUC-H mixed-width codespace decode + out-of-codespace rewind recovery, oracle-free pin added (F); direct raw-`.pfb` FontBox parse path — `create_with_pfb`/`create_with_segments` equivalence, name/matrix/BBox/Subrs/charstring-set/encoding counts across 4 fixtures (probe `Type1PfbDirectProbe`) (G); annotation `/AP` rendering under page `/Rotate` 90/180/270 composed with HIDDEN/NOVIEW/INVISIBLE flags + `/AS` state selection + zero-BBox skip (H, no renderer change); embedded-files name-tree write shape — pypdfbox auto-splits >64 names into a balanced `/Kids` tree where upstream `setNames` always writes flat `/Names`; auto-split output verified fully readable by live PDFBox at 100 attachments (I).
- Note (A): `PDNumberTreeNode` root-`/Limits` drop, empty-map `/Limits` omission, and single-armed `set_kids` are intentional pypdfbox divergences (pypdfbox is the more §7.9.7-conformant side; consistent with `PDNameTreeNode`).
- Note (I): name-tree auto-split write shape is an intentional divergence; gate/opt-out only if byte-for-byte `setNames` output ever becomes a requirement.

## Wave 1481 — 3 bugs fixed (Type3 d1 uncolored-mask, TrueType get_width gid-0, OTF hasPostScriptTag) + 7 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — Type3 `d1` glyphs honored color operators inside the charproc.** Per PDF §9.6.5.3, a `d1` (SetCharWidthAndBoundingBox) glyph is an uncolored mask: upstream `PDFStreamEngine.isShouldProcessColorOperators()` returns false for it, so any color op in the charproc is ignored and the glyph paints in the surrounding text-state color. pypdfbox's renderer ran color ops unconditionally, so a `d1` glyph with e.g. `1 0 0 rg` painted red where PDFBox painted the text-state green. The renderer now gates `RG/rg/G/g/K/k/CS/cs/SC/sc/SCN/scn` while rendering a `d1` charproc (detected by scanning to the first operator); `d0` glyphs keep their own color.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java` (`isShouldProcessColorOperators`) + `rendering/PageDrawer`
  reason: `d1` uncolored-mask color-op gating was missing — `d1` glyphs used their own color instead of the text-state color
- **FIX — `TrueTypeFont.get_width(name)` special-cased gid 0 → `0.0`.** Upstream `getWidth(String)` is unconditionally `getAdvanceWidth(nameToGID(name))` — a name resolving to `.notdef`/unknown (gid 0) must report gid 0's advance, not `0.0` (e.g. `get_width("bogus")` on LiberationSans = 1536, not 0). Removed the zero special-case. 3 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/ttf/TrueTypeFont.java` (`getWidth(String)`)
  reason: unresolved-name width returned 0.0 instead of gid 0's advance
- **FIX — `OTFParser.parse()` never set the SFNT version, so `hasPostScriptTag` stayed false.** `set_version` was only wired into the unused slow path; the normal `_parse_data_stream` path left an `OTTO` `OpenTypeFont` with `hasPostScriptTag=false`, so `get_glyph_table()` returned `None` instead of raising on a PostScript font (upstream `OpenTypeFont.getGlyph()` gates on the raw post-script-tag flag). Now seeds `set_version` from the SFNT scaler type.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/ttf/OTFParser.java` / `OpenTypeFont.java` (`getGlyph`)
  reason: OTTO fonts reported `hasPostScriptTag=false`, so `get_glyph_table` did not raise on a PostScript font
- 7 surfaces confirmed already at parity (regression pins, no production change): `COSDictionary` insertion-order + typed/raw getters (A); `/EncryptMetadata false` reader — plaintext metadata stream, RC4-128 + AES-256 (B); API `protect()`+`save()` `/Encrypt` dict round-trip (C); `PDPageLabels` decimal/Roman/letter(Z→AA)/prefix/start label strings (D); article threads `PDThread`/`PDThreadBead` chain traversal (F, pypdfbox skips non-dict `/Threads` entries where upstream casts — intentional leniency); separable blend modes HardLight/SoftLight/Difference/Exclusion/ColorDodge/ColorBurn per-channel incl. special-case branches (H); external-signing signature-dict identity fields + `/Contents` placeholder (I); Overlay default-position stamp (J, pinned wave 1466).
- Note: `PDDocumentCatalog.get_threads` skips non-dictionary `/Threads` entries; upstream casts unconditionally (`ClassCastException` on a non-dict entry, wraps a `COSNull` as `PDThread(null)`). pypdfbox is intentionally more lenient on malformed input (consistent with the project's lenient-parsing stance).

## Wave 1480 — 4 bugs fixed (COSArray.set_name no-grow, content-stream float formatting, role-map single-hop, CFF Format1Encoding) + 6 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — `COSArray.set_name(index, value)` auto-grew the array on an out-of-range index.** It called `grow_to_size(index+1)` then assigned. Upstream `COSArray.setName` delegates to `set(index, COSName)` → `List.set`, which throws `IndexOutOfBoundsException` and does NOT grow (live-oracle confirmed). `set_name` now routes through `self.set(...)`, raising `IndexError` on out-of-range. 4 internal artifact tests that built an empty array via `set_name(i, …)` (relying on the old auto-grow) now `grow_to_size(n)` first, matching the upstream `COSArrayTest.testGetSetName` idiom. (Sibling `set_int`/`set_string` likely share the divergence — DEFERRED.)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSArray.java` (`setName`→`set`)
  reason: out-of-range `set_name` auto-grew instead of throwing like upstream `List.set`
- **FIX — `PDPageContentStream` float operands formatted via `format(f, ".5f")` on the float64 value.** Upstream narrows every operand to a Java 32-bit `float` and runs `NumberFormatUtil.formatFloatFast` (single-precision + a truncating half-up round on the narrowed fraction). `_format_number` now replicates that (float32 narrow via `struct`, integer half-up truncation, trailing-zero strip, negative-zero preserved), fixing byte-level divergences (`0.000005`→`0`, `0.000025`→`0.00002`, `0.123455`→`0.12346`, `12345.6789`→`12345.67871`, `-0.000005`→`-0`). Confirmed `PDPageContentStream` sets `MaximumFractionDigits=5`. 3 stale tests retargeted. (`pd_abstract_content_stream._format_decimal` likely shares the divergence — DEFERRED.)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDPageContentStream.java` + `util/NumberFormatUtil.java` (`formatFloatFast`)
  reason: content-stream operand formatting used float64 `.5f` instead of upstream's float32 formatFloatFast
- **FIX — `PDStructureElement.get_standard_structure_type` did a recursive multi-hop `/RoleMap` walk.** Upstream `getStandardStructureType` reads `/S`, does ONE `roleMap.get(type)`, and returns the mapped value only when it is a name (else returns `/S` unchanged) — no recursion (bytecode-verified). pypdfbox chased the chain up to 16 hops, so `Foo→Bar→P` over-resolved to `P` where upstream stops at `Bar`. Replaced with a single lookup. 6 stale tests (which codified the multi-hop behavior) corrected.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/PDStructureElement.java` (`getStandardStructureType`)
  reason: role-map resolution recursed multi-hop instead of upstream's single lookup
- **FIX — embedded CFF `/Encoding` always surfaced as `Format0Encoding`.** In the fontTools-backed `CFFParser` path, `_resolve_top_dict_encoding` hard-wired every embedded encoding to `Format0Encoding` (fontTools discards the format byte). A font with an on-disk Format1 (range-based) encoding reported `Format1Encoding` in PDFBox but `Format0Encoding` in pypdfbox. Now re-reads the on-disk encoding format byte at the raw Encoding offset and wraps as `Format1Encoding` when base format == 1. Code→name mapping was already correct; only the class identity diverged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/cff/CFFParser.java` (`readEncoding`) + `CFFType1Font.getEncoding`
  reason: embedded CFF encoding class identity was always Format0, never Format1
- 6 surfaces confirmed already at parity (regression pins, no production change): linearization param dict `/L`/`/T`/`/E`/`/H` (B); compressed-write `/Type /XRef` trailer invariants — `/Root`/`/Size`/`/W`/`/Index`, qpdf-valid (C); marked-content `BMC`/`BDC`/`EMC`/`MP`/`DP` + `PDFMarkedContentExtractor` tree (E, retroactive PROVENANCE rows added); annotation `/AP /N` §12.5.5 BBox→Rect with offset BBox + non-identity Matrix (H); `Splitter` boundary intervals — interval 1/N/>pages (I); FlateDecode `/Predictor` (TIFF 2 + PNG 10-15) over multi-byte pixels (J).

## Wave 1479 — 1 bug fixed (xref /Prev free-entry shadowing) + 9 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — a `free` xref entry in a newer `/Prev` section could shadow an `in-use` entry from an older section, dropping the object.** `XrefTrailerResolver.get_xref_table()` merged cross-section entries oldest→newest with a plain `dict.update`, so a newer free entry (`compressed_index == -1`) overwrote the older in-use entry; `populate_document` then skipped it, dropping the object from the pool (one short, unresolvable). Upstream `COSParser.parseXrefTable` registers ONLY in-use (`n`) entries into the byte-offset map — free records never enter it and so can't shadow an older in-use entry. `get_xref_table()` now skips a free entry during the cross-section merge when an in-use entry already exists for the same key (a free entry survives only when no in-use entry exists anywhere in the chain). Headline value-shadowing (object redefined across revisions → newest wins) was already correct; this free-over-in-use case was the bug.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`parseXrefTable`) + `XrefTrailerResolver`
  reason: newer free entry shadowed an older in-use entry, dropping the object from the pool
- 9 surfaces confirmed already at parity (regression pins, no production change): stream `/Length` recovery — indirect ref + wrong/missing length via endstream scan (A, already pinned); `COSWriter.writeString` literal-vs-hex + escaping across the full 0x00-0xFF byte sweep (C); `PDDocumentCatalog`/`PDViewerPreferences` accessors incl. absent-key + unrecognised-name + COSString-valued defaults (D); optional-content groups re-read from PDFBox-authored bytes — enumeration, `/BaseState`, `is_group_enabled` (E); end-to-end `PDFTextStripper` over Type0 `/ToUnicode` surrogate-pair + multi-char destinations (F); TrueType `glyf` composite-glyph assembly (G, pinned wave 1458); mesh shadings ShadingType 4/5/6/7 (H, already pinned); text-field `/AP /N` `Tj` byte-payload + `Tf` size (I); `PDFToImage`/`renderImageWithDPI` float32-floor pixel-dimension rounding + cross-DPI content (J).

## Wave 1478 — 2 bugs fixed (COSNumber overflow sentinel, brute-force last-occurrence) + 8 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — body-parser integer literals exceeding `Long.MAX_VALUE` produced a valid unbounded `COSInteger` instead of the overflow sentinel.** `COSParser._wrap_number` wrapped the integer branch with `COSInteger.get(int(text))`, so e.g. `9223372036854775808` parsed to a valid arbitrary-precision int. Upstream `COSParser` inherits `BaseParser.parseCOSNumber`, which routes through `COSNumber.get` → on overflow returns the `OUT_OF_RANGE_MAX/MIN` `COSInteger` sentinel (value clamped to `Long.MAX/MIN_VALUE`, `isValid()==false`, PDFBOX-5176). `_wrap_number` now dispatches the integer branch through `COSNumber.get(text)` (the content-stream `PDFStreamParser` path already did); float branch unchanged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/BaseParser.java` (`parseCOSNumber`) + `cos/COSNumber.java` (`get`)
  reason: Long-overflow integer in a PDF body diverged — valid unbounded int vs upstream clamped invalid sentinel
- **FIX — brute-force xref recovery kept the FIRST occurrence of a duplicated object header.** `COSParser.bf_search_for_objects` used `offsets.setdefault(key, num_start)` (first-wins). Upstream `BruteForceParser.bfSearchForObjects` records every `n g obj` header via an unconditional `Map.put`, so the LAST (higher-offset) definition wins — when a body redefines an object, recovery must resolve the later copy. Changed to `offsets[key] = num_start` (last-wins). Confirmed on a fixture defining obj 5 twice (xref deleted to force rebuild): PDFBox + pypdfbox now both resolve the later copy. 3 stale unit tests (which pinned first-wins) corrected.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/BruteForceParser.java` (`bfSearchForObjects`)
  reason: recovery resolved the stale earlier copy of a redefined object (first-wins vs upstream last-wins)
- 8 surfaces confirmed already at parity (regression pins, no production change): document-outline traversal — first/last-child, next/prev-sibling, signed `/Count`, `findDestinationPage` (C); text rendering mode `Tr` 0-7 incl. mode-3 invisible + clip modes 4-7 (D, already pinned); `setSortByPosition` two-column reading order, sorted + unsorted (E); fontbox CMap mixed 1-/2-byte codespace ranges — variable-length decode + CID resolution + fallback (F); continuous-tone luminosity `/SMask` (`luminance * coverage` partial alpha, MAD 0.00) (G); `PDAnnotationLink` destinations — explicit/named `/Dest` + `/A /GoTo`, 8 forms (H); `PDTilingPattern` typed accessors + spec defaults (I); `WriteDecodedDoc` CLI — `/Filter` removed + `/Length` rewritten + text/qpdf parity, with a new `/Length`-parity assertion (J).

## Wave 1477 — 4 bugs fixed (hex-string leniency, incremental trailer-only /Info, TextStripperByArea per-glyph binning, all-zero dash) + 6 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — hex-string `<...>` parsing was strict where upstream is lenient.** `BaseParser.read_hex_string` (shared by the content-stream + document parsers) raised `PDFParseError` on any non-hex, non-whitespace character. Upstream `BaseParser.parseCOSHexString` discards a dangling half-pair on a stray char, then skips to the closing `>`, decoding only the clean leading pairs (e.g. `<4865ZZ6C6C6F>` → `He`, `<G>` → empty). `read_hex_string` now mirrors that skip-to-`>` recovery; odd-length pad + whitespace-skip unchanged. 2 stale unit tests (which pinned the strict-raise behavior) corrected.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/BaseParser.java` (`parseCOSHexString`)
  reason: hex-string recovery diverged — hard raise vs upstream lenient skip-to-`>`
- **FIX — incremental save silently dropped a trailer-only synthesised `/Info` dict.** When a source PDF had no `/Info`, `get_document_information()` mints a new direct dict and wires it into the trailer but not the COS object pool; `COSWriter._do_write_increment` walked only the pool, so the new dirty `/Info` (and any title edit) appended zero bytes and vanished. The increment path now offers the trailer's `/Root`/`/Info`/`/Encrypt` entries to `_add_object_to_write` (mirroring the full-save `_do_write_body`); the incremental dirty-filter still skips un-dirtied entries, so only a freshly-dirtied trailer dict is minted. Matches PDFBox `saveIncremental` (same new object number, `/Info N 0 R`, `/Prev`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` (`doWriteIncrement`/`doWriteBody`)
  reason: incremental writer only walked the object pool, dropping a trailer-reachable synthesised dict
- **FIX — `PDFTextStripperByArea` mis-routed runs straddling a region boundary.** Because the lite `PDFTextStripper` emits one `TextPosition` per show-text run (not per glyph), `process_text_position` binned a whole run by its start origin — a run physically crossing a region boundary landed entirely in the region containing its first glyph (oracle: PDFBox split 5 glyphs left / 3 right; pypdfbox put all 10 left). Now splits each multi-glyph run into per-glyph sub-positions and routes each by its own origin via a `_bin_glyph` helper (same `Rectangle2D.contains` half-open semantics). 2 internal tests updated for per-glyph binning. Exact straddle split point still bounded by lite-mode average-advance widths (DEFERRED).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStripperByArea.java`
  reason: per-run (not per-glyph) binning mis-routed boundary-straddling runs
- **FIX — an all-zero line-dash array rendered as a solid line instead of invisible.** `_op_set_dash` collapsed an all-zero dash array (e.g. `[0 0] 0 d`) to `None` (solid), the opposite of PDFBox: `PageDrawer.getStroke` detects `isAllZeroDash` and returns a stroke whose `createStrokedShape` is an empty `Area` — it paints nothing. `_op_set_dash` now preserves the all-zero tuple, and the `S`-path stroke chokepoints skip painting when no dash interval is > 0. An empty array (`[] 0 d`) remains a solid line.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/PageDrawer.java` (`getStroke`/`isAllZeroDash`)
  reason: all-zero dash conflated with empty array → solid instead of invisible
- 6 surfaces confirmed already at parity (regression pins, no production change): object-stream `/Extends` chain — compressed-object lookup driven by the type-2 xref entry, not an `/Extends` walk (B); page-tree inheritable `/MediaBox`/`/CropBox`/`/Rotate`/`/Resources` incl. `/Rotate > 360` normalization (D); inline-image `BI`/`ID`/`EI` param-dict parsing — abbreviated keys/CS/filter names, key order, raw data length (E); fontbox Type1 `getWidth` for seac composites + hsbw (G); AcroForm `PDChoice`/`PDListBox`/`PDComboBox` `/Opt`/`/V`/`/I` round-trip (I); xmpbox Dublin Core round-trip — title LangAlt, creator Seq, subject Bag, date Seq with ± offsets (J).

## Wave 1476 — 4 bugs fixed (sc/SC invalid color, Type3 text advance, Separation image /Decode, type-4 roll) + 6 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — `sc`/`SC` (and the device `g`/`rg`/`k`/`G`/`RG`/`K`) color operators silently kept the colorspace's initial color on a non-numeric operand.** Upstream `SetColor.process` (PDFBOX-5851, verified via 3.0.7 bytecode) instead calls `setColor(new PDColor(new float[0], null))` — an invalid empty-component, null-colorspace color — for a non-numeric operand in a non-Pattern color space. pypdfbox left the color at the space's initial value (e.g. RGB `[0,0,0]`). Both stroking + non-stroking operators now set `PDColor([], None)`, matching upstream (the `scn`/`SCN` N-variants already did). 2 stale unit tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/operator/color/SetColor.java` (`process`)
  reason: non-numeric `sc`/`SC` operand handling diverged — silent skip vs upstream invalid-color
- **FIX — Type3-font text-extraction cursor advances mis-scaled by a hard-coded `1/1000`.** `PDFTextStripper._compute_avg_advance` divided the average `/Widths` value by 1000 (correct only for the implicit `[0.001 …]` FontMatrix of simple fonts). A Type3 font's `/Widths` are in its explicit `/FontMatrix` glyph space, so a 500-unit-em font's advances came out 2× too short, mis-anchoring second-and-later show-text runs on a line (e.g. x=107.40 vs PDFBox x=114.40). Now scales by the FontMatrix x-scale for `PDType3Font`, mirroring `PDType3Font.getDisplacement`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDType3Font.java` (`getDisplacement`)
  reason: Type3 avg-advance used a fixed 1/1000 instead of the real FontMatrix x-scale
- **FIX — Separation/DeviceN image `/Decode` array was ignored.** `decode_pdimage_to_pil` sent Separation/DeviceN rasters straight to the tint transform without applying `/Decode`, so an inverted `/Decode [1 0]` produced the same RGB raster as the default `[0 1]`. Upstream `SampledImageReader` maps each raw sample through `decode[0] + sample/maxVal*(decode[1]-decode[0])` into the component range *before* the tint transform. Added `_apply_devicen_decode` (identity fast-path for absent/`[0 1]` decode) ahead of `_decode_devicen_to_rgb`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/image/SampledImageReader.java` (decode) + `PDSeparation.toRGBImage`
  reason: `/Decode` not applied for Separation/DeviceN images — inverted decode produced identical output
- **FIX — `PDFunctionType4` `roll` reduced the rotation count modulo the window size.** pypdfbox did `j %= n`; upstream `StackOperators$Roll` (verified via bytecode) does NOT — for `|j| > n` it pops past the window and throws `EmptyStackException`, and `n < 0` is a rangecheck error. `roll` now raises `OSError` for both overflow conditions instead of silently rotating with a mod-reduced count; in-range output is byte-identical. 1 stale unit test retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/function/type4/StackOperators.java` (`Roll`)
  reason: `roll` mod-reduced an out-of-range count instead of mirroring upstream's stack-underflow/rangecheck error
- 6 surfaces confirmed already at parity (regression pins, no production change): CFF CIDFont per-FD local-subr bias selection (A); annotation `/BS` typed-accessor defaults incl. the `/W`-as-name → 0 quirk (B); Encrypt CLI `/U`/`/O`/`/P`/`/R`/`/Length` round-trip across RC4-40/128 + AES-256 (F, prior failure was a test-harness missing-password mistake); `Tw` word-spacing applied only to single-byte code 32, not multi-byte CID codes (G); `PDFMergerUtility` AcroForm field-collision rename via live `/Fields` lookup (I); TrueType `cmap` subtable selection order + format-4 segment-gap GID lookup (J).

## Wave 1475 — 4 surfaces confirmed at parity (0 bugs, session-limit-interrupted wave)

- No production change. A 10-agent wave was launched but hit the session limit mid-run; only 4 agents completed and all 4 confirmed their surface already matches PDFBox 3.0.7 (regression pins added). The 4 confirmed-parity surfaces:
  - **Zero-width (hairline) stroke** renders as a single device-pixel line matching PDFBox (agent A, `ZeroWidthStrokeProbe`).
  - **Xref-stream `/Index` with multiple subsections** resolves the same object→offset map as PDFBox (agent E, `XrefIndexSubsectionsProbe`).
  - **Page append ordering** + kids-tree shape match PDFBox (agent G, `PageAppendModeProbe`).
  - **TrueType `post`-table GID→name** lookups match PDFBox (agent H, `PostTableGidProbe`).
- The 6 interrupted surfaces (CFF FDArray local-subrs, annotation `/BS` typed accessors, `scn`/`SC` operand-count handling, Type3 text, Separation `/Decode` images, Encrypt CLI round-trip) were re-queued; their partial/unverified edits and failing test stubs were reverted/removed, leaving the tree at parity.

## Wave 1474 — PDNameTreeNode.get_names non-recursive (1 bug fixed) + 9 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — `PDNameTreeNode.get_names()` recursively flattened the whole sub-tree.** Upstream `PDNameTreeNode.getNames()` is NON-recursive (verified via jar bytecode): it reads only the current node's own `/Names` `COSArray` and returns `null` on an intermediate/root node that carries only `/Kids`. pypdfbox walked `/Kids` and merged every leaf, so `get_names()` on a kids-only `/Dests`/`/JavaScript` root returned the flattened set where upstream returns nothing. Made `get_names()` upstream-exact (own `/Names` leaf only, `None` on a kids-only node), added `flatten()` to materialise the whole sub-tree via a `/Kids` walk (the documented multi-level view), and routed `merge()` through `flatten()` so its whole-tree contract is preserved. Confirmed upstream consumers already do their own kid-walk (`ExtractEmbeddedFiles`, `Splitter`, `PDFMergerUtility.getIDTreeAsMap`). 3 fallout tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/PDNameTreeNode.java` (`getNames`)
  reason: `getNames()` exposes only the node's own leaf array; callers descend `/Kids` themselves.
- 9 surfaces confirmed at parity (no production change): ExtGState `/SMask /BC` backdrop (runtime-neutral — `removeBackdrop` strips the `/BC` fill); fontbox `hhea` table (17 fields); `PDChoice` multi-select + editable combo; inline-image `ID...EI` false-EI binary scan; `COSBoolean`/`COSNull` singletons + serialisation; GlyphList AGL name→Unicode (battery extended — upstream's strict len-5/7 `u`/`uni` gating confirmed, brief's astral-`u` expectation rejected); `PDOutputIntent` `/S`+`/N`; `PDLab.toRGB` no input-domain clamp; `ExtractText` CLI page-range + `-sort`. New probes per agent.

## Wave 1473 — non-Device shading + q/Q error triage + text rise + embedded-Type1 encoding (6 bugs fixed) + 6 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

Four production changes; two broad (`operator_exception` triage on every stream-engine error path; renderer shading). Combined state verified across the full non-oracle suite + the rendering oracle suite.

- **FIX — shading ignored a non-Device `/ColorSpace` + missing function-output clamp (2).** `PDFRenderer._evaluate_shading_rgb` only handled DeviceGray/RGB/CMYK by name; a Separation 1-tint output fell into the DeviceGray branch (grey ramp instead of the tint colour) and an Indexed index was treated as grey. Also, it evaluated the raw `/Function` without the PDF §7.10.2 clamp-to-[0,1], so an Indexed shading swept past `hival`. Added `_convert_shading_output` routing the (clamped) function output through the typed colour space's `to_rgb` (mirroring `ShadingContext.shadingColorSpace.toRGB`), gated so the Device paths are untouched.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/` shading contexts (`AxialShadingContext` etc.), PDF §7.10.2
  reason: a shading's colour-table conversion must use its declared colour space, on clamped function output.
- **FIX — `operator_exception` aborted on an unbalanced `Q`.** The default operator-error policy demoted only `MissingOperandException` + `Do` failures; the since-shipped `EmptyGraphicsStackException` (raised by `Restore` on an empty stack, PDFBOX-161) re-raised, aborting the content stream. Disassembled upstream `PDFStreamEngine.operatorException` (3.0.7) and mirrored its triage verbatim: swallow `MissingOperand`/`MissingResource`/`MissingImageReader`/`EmptyGraphicsStack`, swallow when the op is `Do`, swallow `DataFormatException`-caused (Python `zlib.error`), else re-raise. 5 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java` (`operatorException`)
  reason: an unbalanced `Q` (and the other listed exceptions) is a lenient warning, not a stream-fatal error.
- **FIX — `PDFTextStripper` ignored the text-rise operator (`Ts`).** The lite stripper had no `Ts` handler, so super/subscript runs collapsed onto the baseline (Y unshifted). Added `text_rise` to `_TextState` (persists across `BT`), a `Ts` dispatcher case, and folded the rise into the text-rendering matrix as the parameter-matrix f-translation (`[1,0,0,1,0,rise] × Tm × CTM`, upstream `showText`). Inline extraction (no spurious newline) was already at parity.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java` (`showText` rise fold), `text/TextPosition`
  reason: `Ts` shifts the glyph baseline; extracted per-glyph Y must reflect it.
- **FIX — embedded-Type1 encoding class + `has_glyph` `.notdef` (2).** `PDType1Font.read_encoding_from_font` wrapped an embedded Type1 program's built-in encoding in `BuiltInEncoding`; upstream builds `Type1Encoding.fromFontBox` for the embedded-Type1 branch (`BuiltInEncoding` is TrueType/Type3 only). And `has_glyph` had a non-upstream `.notdef`→False short-circuit; upstream is exactly `genericFont.hasGlyph(getNameInFont(name))`, so a program carrying a `.notdef` charstring reports True. Both fixed; 2 tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDType1Font.java` (`readEncodingFromFont`, `hasGlyph`)
  reason: embedded-Type1 built-in encoding is a `Type1Encoding`; `has_glyph` must not special-case `.notdef`.
- 6 surfaces confirmed at parity (no production change): empty/zero-contour glyf glyph; signature `/Lock`+`/SV` accessors; `COSInteger` interning (-100..256 cache, verified empirically); catalog `/Metadata` XMP stream wire facts; 16-bpc image decode (linear-rounding down-sample, `0x00FF→1`); WriteDecodedDoc. New probes per agent.

## Wave 1472 — CTM stroke width + OTF name_to_gid/post + comb appearance + form recursion + DeviceN toString (7 bugs fixed) + 5 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

Five production changes; three broad (renderer stroking, font GID resolution, stream-engine recursion). Combined state verified across the full non-oracle suite + the rendering oracle suite.

- **FIX — CTM-transformed stroke width was double-scaled.** `_draw_via_aggdraw` set the CTM on the skia canvas (scaling the pen) AND pre-multiplied the pen width by `sqrt(|det(CTM)|)`, so device width = `lineWidth × scale²`. Worse, even single-scaled, letting the canvas CTM scale the pen yields skia's true *elliptical* pen under an anisotropic CTM, whereas PDFBox `PageDrawer.transformWidth` collapses it to an isotropic scalar `lineWidth × sqrt((x²+y²)/2)`. Reworked stroking into device space (`_stroke_path_device_space` + `_transform_width_scale`): path transformed to device coords, stroked under identity transform with the scalar device width (1px hairline floor + dash intervals scaled by the same scalar). Fills still transform under the canvas CTM.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/PageDrawer.java` (`transformWidth`, §8.5.3.1)
  reason: stroke pen width is an isotropic CTM-scaled scalar, applied once.
- **FIX — `TrueTypeFont` name→GID + post-table glyph names (2).** `name_to_gid` had a non-upstream `glyphOrder.index(name)` fallback after post/cmap/`g\d+`, so a name miss returned a synthetic GID instead of 0; and `get_post_script` copied glyph names for *every* `post` format, so a format-3.0 `post` (nameless upstream) reported names (feeding the first bug). Removed the fallback (returns 0 like FontBox `nameToGID`) and gated name population to `post` formats 2.0/2.5/4.0 (matching `PostScriptTable.read`). 1 test retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/ttf/TrueTypeFont.java` (`nameToGID`), `PostScriptTable.java` (`read`)
  reason: a name with no post/cmap entry is `.notdef` (0); a format-3.0 post carries no names.
- **FIX — comb text-field `/AP` geometry.** `_emit_comb_text` diverged from upstream `insertGeneratedCombAppearance` on every axis: baseline (now ascent-centred `(bboxH − ascent/1000·fontSize)/2`), per-cell positioning (now the incremental `xOffset += prevCharW/2 − currCharW/2` scheme with `fontSize/2` half-width + `initialOffset = (combW − firstCharW)/2`), glyph widths (now real `get_string_width`, not the average estimate), and `/Q` (now shifts `initialOffset` for values shorter than `/MaxLen`). Rewrote line-for-line; per-cell `Td` offsets match within 0.05 units.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/form/AppearanceGeneratorHelper.java` (`insertGeneratedCombAppearance`)
  reason: comb cells are laid out by the incremental ascent-centred algorithm, not a cell-centre estimate.
- **FIX — stream-engine form-recursion level double-count.** `PDFStreamEngine.process_stream` bumped the recursion level around every nested dispatch, but upstream manages the level *solely* in `DrawObject.process` (`increaseLevel` then `getLevel() > 50` cap). The double-count halved the effective cap, so a self-referencing / cyclic form XObject terminated at 26 operators where PDFBox yields 51. Removed the bump from `process_stream` (resource save/restore stays); 5 stale tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/operator/DrawObject.java`, `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java`
  reason: the recursion cap counts form-XObject `Do` depth only, owned by `DrawObject`.
- **FIX — `PDDeviceNAttributes` toString + getColorants self-population (2).** `__str__` rendered each `/Colorants` entry as `"name": cs.get_name()` without the per-entry trailing space (upstream appends the full `PDSeparation.toString()` + a space after every entry); and `get_colorants()` returned `{}` without inserting an empty `/Colorants` dict (upstream's self-population side effect, so a later `has_colorants()` becomes true). Both fixed to match upstream; the process-CS leg keeps a stable `get_name()` rather than upstream's non-deterministic JVM-hashcode `Object.toString`. 2 tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/color/PDDeviceNAttributes.java` (`toString`/`getColorants`)
  reason: toString renders full Separation strings with trailing spaces; getColorants self-populates an empty dict.
- Stroke + 4 more surfaces confirmed at parity: COSString text→bytes encoding (67 cases); gap-driven word-separator (sub-threshold + very-large at parity; mid-size = existing DEFERRED xfail); page+catalog `/AA` additional-actions; Standard-14 synthesised descriptor; `Splitter` content preservation. New probes per agent.

## Wave 1471 — '/" operator re-entry + /Root recovery + text reading-order + CCITT /Height + merge CLI (6 bugs fixed) + 4 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

Six production changes across disjoint trees (renderer / parser / text / ccitt / merge-tool ×2); two are broad (parser core, text reading-order) — combined state verified across the full non-oracle suite.

- **FIX — `'` and `"` text operators bypassed engine re-entry.** `PDFRenderer` decomposed `'` (≡ `T*` `Tj`) and `"` (≡ `Tw` `Tc` `'`) via direct Python method calls, where upstream `ShowTextLine`/`ShowTextLineAndSpace` re-enter the engine via `processOperator("T*"/"Tj"/...)` (confirmed by `javap` of the 3.0.7 jar). An engine-level observer saw one event per `"` instead of the nested constituents, and the `process_operator` dispatch/knockout machinery never fired for them. `_op_show_text_line`/`_op_show_text_line_with_spacing` now re-dispatch through `process_operator`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/operator/text/ShowTextLine.java`, `ShowTextLineAndSpace.java`
  reason: composite text operators must re-enter the engine so observers/dispatch see their constituents.
- **FIX — catalog `/Root` recovery (2 bugs).** (1) `PDFParser.parse()` only brute-force-rebuilt the trailer when the xref was unlocatable; a located xref whose trailer *omitted* `/Root` was left rootless (0 pages). Upstream `COSParser.retrieveTrailer` rebuilds when `trailer.getItem(ROOT) == null` (lenient) — scans all objects for `/Type /Catalog` and repairs `/Root`. Added the missing-`/Root` branch + `_rebuild_trailer_for_missing_root()` (merges recovered objects the located xref omitted; only the full no-xref rebuild eager-resolves everything). (2) `initial_parse()` never called `check_pages()`, so a `/Root` mis-pointed at the `/Pages` node silently yielded 0 pages instead of raising "Page tree root must be a dictionary"; now wired in upstream order. A dangling/non-catalog `/Root` still surfaces the error rather than rebuilding.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`retrieveTrailer`/`rebuildTrailer`), `PDFParser.java` (`initialParse`/`checkPages`)
  reason: a located-but-rootless trailer must recover the catalog by `/Type /Catalog` scan; the page-tree-root check must run on initial parse.
- **FIX — text reading-order sort used a naive `(-y, x)` key.** `PDFTextStripper._format_positions` sorted non-rotated glyphs by raw Y, so two runs on a *visually shared* line differing by sub-line-height baseline jitter (y=700 vs y=701) were reordered by Y (and the X-gap word-break test then misfired). Upstream `TextPositionComparator` treats runs that share a baseline (ΔY < 0.1) or whose vertical extents overlap as the same line and orders left-to-right by X, comparing Y only for vertically-disjoint runs. Replaced the key with a `cmp_to_key(_compare_reading_order)` mirroring that, in the stripper's y-up frame. Rotated path unchanged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/TextPositionComparator.java`
  reason: shared-baseline runs must order by X, not raw Y, or sub-pixel jitter corrupts reading order + word breaks.
- **FIX — CCITTFaxDecode ignored the stream-dict `/Height`.** `decode` read `/Rows` from `/DecodeParms` only and never consulted `/Height` (`/H`), mis-sizing the decoded buffer when `/Rows` was absent or differed. Upstream `CCITTFaxFilter.decode`: both present → `/Height` wins outright; else the non-zero one. Applied that exact rule + a fixed `rows*rowBytes` footprint (pad polarity per `/BlackIs1`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `filter/CCITTFaxFilter.java` (`decode`)
  reason: the decoded CCITT row count is reconciled `/Height`-over-`/Rows`, not `/Rows` alone.
- **FIX — merge CLI `-i` dropped all but the last input on a repeated flag.** Upstream picocli `PDFMerger` declares `-i` as a repeatable single-value option (`-i a -i b -i c`); pypdfbox's `merge`/`pdf_merger` used argparse `nargs="+"`, which on `-i a -i b -i c` silently kept only `['c']`. Switched to `action="append"`+`nargs="+"` + flatten — accepts upstream's repeatable form AND the `-i a b c` shorthand.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `tools/PDFMerger.java` (picocli `-i=<infile> [-i=<infile>]...`)
  reason: the merge CLI must accept the canonical repeatable-`-i` invocation without dropping inputs.
- Annotation `/AP /N` `/AS` appearance-state substream selection during page render confirmed at parity. New `AnnotApStateProbe` + `test_annot_ap_state_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `Type1Font` `/FontInfo` + top-level metadata confirmed at parity (15 fields × 4 PFB fonts). New `Type1FontInfoProbe` + `test_type1_font_info_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDAcroForm` form-level accessors (`/DA`/`/NeedAppearances`/`/DR`/`/XFA`/`/SigFlags`/`/CO`/`/Q`) confirmed at parity. New `AcroFormAccessorProbe` + `test_acro_form_accessor_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDPageTree` `indexOf`/iteration/round-trip confirmed at parity. New `PageTreeIndexProbe` + `test_page_tree_index_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `/ToUnicode` surrogate-pair/multi-char/bfrange-array decoding confirmed at parity. New `ToUnicodeSurrogateProbe` + `test_to_unicode_surrogate_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1470 — shading-pattern matrix + glyph width-sentinel + stream recovery + Type0 GID + color-key mask + render float32 (6 bugs fixed) + 4 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

Three of the six fixes touch `pdf_renderer.py` (shading-pattern matrix, glyph width guard, render float32) — concurrent edits merged intact (module imports clean; all three markers present) and verified together across the full non-oracle + rendering-oracle suites.

- **FIX — shading-pattern (`/PatternType 2`) fill ignored the pattern `/Matrix`.** `_paint_pattern_fill`/`_paint_pattern_stroke` dispatched a `PDShadingPattern` to `_paint_shading` without its `/Matrix`; the sub-painters map device pixels back to shading space via the *current* CTM (correct for the `sh` operator, wrong for a shading pattern whose space is `patternMatrix × initialPageCTM`, §8.7.3.1). Added a `pattern_matrix` override to `_paint_shading` (+ `_pattern_matrix` helper), threaded at fill+stroke; the `sh` path passes `None`, unchanged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/` shading-pattern painting (PDF §8.7.3.1)
  reason: a shading pattern's gradient is positioned by `patternMatrix × page CTM`, not the fill-time CTM.
- **FIX — `_draw_glyph` placeholder overwrote an explicit width matching the 500.0 sentinel.** When an embedded-glyph draw fell through to the placeholder branch, the substitute-upgrade guard only protected an explicit width of *exactly 0*; a font whose genuine `/W`/`/Widths` advance equalled the `500.0` hard-default sentinel got replaced by a FontMappers substitute width (e.g. 278). Generalised the guard from `explicit_zero` to `has_explicit` — any explicit width is honoured verbatim (matching upstream `getWidth(code)/1000`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/PageDrawer` glyph advance
  reason: an explicitly-declared width must never be replaced by a substitute metric.
- **FIX — `PDFParser._load_indirect_object_at` discarded streams recovered by the endstream-scan.** When a wrong/missing `/Length` forced the recovery scan to truncate at an embedded `endstream` token, the cursor landed mid-body and the parser raised `PDFParseError` on the non-`endobj` trailing keyword, discarding the whole object. Upstream `COSParser.parseFileObject` only *warns* in lenient mode (the default) and keeps the object, raising only in strict mode. Added `_check_endobj` mirroring that lenient/strict split; routed all 3 closing-keyword sites through it. 2 wave tests that pinned the strict-throw-in-lenient behaviour retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/COSParser.java` (`parseFileObject`)
  reason: a non-`endobj` trailing keyword is a lenient-mode warning, not a fatal error.
- **FIX — `PDType0Font.code_to_gid` assumed GID==CID for CIDFontType0.** The parent reimplemented CID→GID inline and, lacking a `cid_to_gid` (the `PDCIDFontType2`-only attribute), fell back to `gid = cid`. For a CIDFontType0 (CID-keyed CFF) the GID is the embedded CFF charset index of `cidNNNNN` (CID 40→GID 10), not the CID. Now delegates to the descendant's charset-based `code_to_gid` (matching upstream `descendantFont.codeToGID`); the Type2 path is unchanged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDType0Font.java` (`codeToGID` → `descendantFont.codeToGID`)
  reason: CIDFontType0 maps CID→GID via the CID-keyed CFF charset, not identity.
- **FIX — color-key `/Mask` evaluated against converted sRGB pixels on non-RGB images.** `_apply_color_key_mask` compared the sRGB output against the range pairs and required ≥6 entries, so a 1-pair range (DeviceGray, Indexed) was silently ignored (image stayed opaque) and DeviceCMYK keyed the wrong components. Per §8.9.6.4 (and `SampledImageReader.applyColorKeyMask`), the comparison must be against the raw native colour-component samples (one `[min,max]` pair per component, in `[0, 2^bpc−1]`), before conversion; Indexed keys on the palette *index* honouring its `/Decode`. Rewrote to read raw samples (`_read_color_key_samples`) and key per-component; plain RGB falls back to the prior sRGB path (wave-1456 behaviour unchanged).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/image/SampledImageReader.java` (`applyColorKeyMask`)
  reason: color-key masking keys raw native samples per component, not converted sRGB.
- **FIX — `render_image_with_dpi` raster dimensions used double precision.** pypdfbox computed `int(width_pt * dpi/72.0)` in 64-bit double; upstream `PDFRenderer.renderImage` does it in 32-bit Java float (`Math.floor(widthPt * (dpi/72f))`), so 792pt@150dpi is `1650.0` in double but `1649.999…` in float → 1649. Now computes pixel dims in `numpy.float32` to mirror Java exactly; the device CTM + downstream geometry keep double precision (painted content unchanged).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/PDFRenderer.java` (`renderImage`)
  reason: raster pixel dimensions must match upstream's single-precision floor at exact-boundary DPIs.
- CFF subr-bias glyph-path assembly (local/global subr nesting) confirmed at parity. New `CffSubrPathProbe` + `test_cff_subr_path_oracle.py` + synthetic fixture. No production change.
- `PDRadioButton` `/Opt` export-values group confirmed at parity. New `RadioGroupProbe` + `test_radio_group_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDOptionalContentProperties` name-keyed accessors confirmed at parity. New `OcGroupNamesProbe` + `test_oc_group_names_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Predefined-CMap metadata (Registry/Ordering/Supplement/WMode) confirmed at parity. New `PredefCMapInfoProbe` + `test_predefined_cmap_info_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1469 — simple-font /Widths index-shift + rotated-text getDir/sort + non-terminal field value (4 bugs fixed) + 6 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

One broad fix (font-width list across 7 files); combined state verified across the full non-oracle suite.

- **FIX — `PDFont.get_widths` skipped non-numeric `/Widths` entries (index shift).** A `null`/non-`COSNumber` entry in `/Widths` was dropped, shortening the list and shifting the index of every later entry — so `getWidth(code)` returned the wrong width for codes after the bad slot (e.g. 1000 instead of 800) and trailing codes fell out of the `/FirstChar`..`/LastChar` window. Upstream `PDFont.getWidths()` delegates to `COSArray.toCOSNumberFloatList()`, which appends `null` *in place* (preserving length + index alignment), and `getWidth` reads a `null` slot back as `0.0`. `PDFont`/`PDSimpleFont.get_widths` now return `list[float | None]` with `None` for non-numeric slots; every array consumer (`PDFont.get_width`/`get_space_width`/`get_average_font_width`, `PDSimpleFont`, `PDType1Font`, `PDType1CFont`, `PDTrueTypeFont`, `PDType3Font.get_width_for_code`, and the Type3 renderer advance) null-guards a `None` slot as `0.0` (and skips it in positive-mean averages). `has_explicit_width` unchanged (size-based). 5 stale tests that pinned the old skip retargeted to the `None`-in-place contract (2 wave-279 by the agent; 3 more — `test_wave1398`/`test_wave1399`/`test_wave1400` in `tests/coverage_boost/` + `tests/fontbox/` — caught by the full-suite gate).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDFont.java` (`getWidths` → `COSArray.toCOSNumberFloatList`)
  reason: a non-numeric `/Widths` slot must hold its index (read as 0.0), not be dropped.
- **FIX — `TextPosition.getDir()` was hardcoded 0; `sortByPosition` ignored direction.** The lite `PDFTextStripper` never derived text direction from the text matrix, so every glyph reported `dir=0`, and `sortByPosition` sorted purely by `(-y, x)` device coords — interleaving runs at different rotations. Added `_text_dir(trm)` (snaps `atan2(shear_y, scale_x)` of the text-rendering matrix to the nearest right angle mod 360, matching upstream `TextPosition.getDir()`), passed `dir=` at both `TextPosition` construction sites, and made `dir % 360` the primary sort key (matching `TextPositionComparator`). Unrotated docs (all dir=0) order unchanged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/TextPosition.java` (`getDir`), `text/TextPositionComparator.java`
  reason: text direction must come from the text matrix and drive reading-order grouping.
- **FIX — `PDNonTerminalField.get_value_as_string` decoded the `/V` payload.** Upstream `PDNonTerminalField.getValueAsString` is uniformly `getValue() != null ? getValue().toString() : ""` — `COSBase.toString()`, not a decoder (PDFBox emits `COSString{…}`, `COSName{…}`, `COSInt{…}`, etc.). pypdfbox special-cased each COS type into a decoded/comma-joined payload. Now `item = get_value(); return "" if item is None else item.to_string()`; 4 tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/form/PDNonTerminalField.java` (`getValueAsString`)
  reason: a non-terminal field's value-as-string is the raw COS value's `toString()`, not a decoded payload.
- Uncolored tiling pattern (`/PaintType 2`, scn-supplied tint) confirmed at parity. New `UncoloredTilingProbe` + `test_uncolored_tiling_oracle.py`. No production change.
- CFF `/charset` Format 0/1/2 + predefined ISOAdobe (name- + CID-keyed) confirmed at parity. New `CffCharsetProbe` + `test_cff_charset_oracle.py` + 3 synthetic fixtures. No production change.
- Marked-point `MP`/`DP` engine dispatch (incl. unresolved named `/Properties` → no callback) confirmed at parity. New `MarkedPointDispatchProbe` + `test_marked_point_dispatch_oracle.py`. No production change.
- COSStream write wire format (§7.3.8 framing, direct `/Length`, no `/DL`) confirmed byte-identical. New `StreamWireFormatProbe` + `test_stream_wire_format_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Page-label string formatting (roman subtractive, ≥4000 m-per-thousand, alpha doubling) confirmed at parity. New `PageLabelFormatProbe` + `test_page_label_format_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDExtendedGraphicsState.getFontSetting` (typed `PDFontSetting`) + `/BM` COSArray fallback confirmed at parity. New `ExtGStateFontSettingProbe` + `test_ext_gstate_font_setting_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `ImageToPDF` single-image build (page count, Letter MediaBox, image XObject, qpdf-clean) confirmed at parity. New `ImageToPdfProbe` + `test_image_to_pdf_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1468 — isolated transparency-group clip-composite premultiplied colour (1 bug fixed) + 9 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — `PDFRenderer._paint_through_clip` premultiplied colour on a transparency-group canvas.** The no-blend clip-commit path used PIL `paste(rgb, (0,0), combined)`, which blends source RGB toward the *destination* RGB weighted by the mask. Onto an isolated group's transparent `(0,0,0,0)` RGBA canvas that yields premultiplied RGB (cyan `(0,255,255)` at `/ca 0.55` → `(0,140,140,140)`) while recording the right alpha; the premultiplied colour then darkened when the group composited onto the page (isolated cyan-over-orange rendered `(115,146,89)` vs the spec `(115,209,152)`). Now `alpha_composite`s a straight-alpha source layer when the base is RGBA; the RGB-base branch keeps `paste` (exact there). Matches PDFBox 3.0.7 across the isolated/non-isolated/knockout matrix. (Non-isolated + knockout were unaffected — their canvas is seeded with opaque parent pixels.)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/` (PageDrawer group compositing)
  reason: a group canvas must carry straight (un-premultiplied) colour so it composites correctly onto the backdrop.
- Form-XObject transparency-group compositing (isolated×knockout) confirmed at parity (after the fix). New `TransparencyGroupCompositeProbe` + `test_transparency_group_composite_oracle.py`.
- FontBox `post` table header fields (italicAngle/underline/isFixedPitch/memType) confirmed at parity. New `PostHeaderProbe` + `test_post_header_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Push-button `/MK` icon refs (`/I`/`/RI`/`/IX` form XObjects) confirmed at parity. New `WidgetIconProbe` + `test_widget_icon_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Inline-image filter abbreviations (`/AHx`/`/A85`/`/LZW`/`/Fl`/`/RL` + chain) confirmed at parity (filters verbatim, decoded bytes byte-exact). New `InlineFilterAbbrevProbe` + `test_inline_filter_abbrev_oracle.py`. No production change.
- COSDictionary/COSArray serialization framing confirmed byte-identical. New `CosCompositeWriteProbe` + `test_cos_composite_write_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Soft-hyphen (U+00AD) extraction confirmed at parity (preserved verbatim in ordinary text). New `TextSoftHyphenProbe` + `test_soft_hyphen_oracle.py`. Surfaced + DEFERRED a Standard-14 no-`/Encoding` toUnicode divergence (StandardEncoding-from-AFM vs WinAnsi). No production change.
- `PDDocument` top-level accessors (numberOfPages/getPage/isEncrypted/access-permission/signature counts) confirmed at parity. New `DocumentAccessorsProbe` + `test_document_accessors_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDFontDescriptor` `/Flags` bit-predicates + numeric metrics confirmed at parity (32 cases). New `FontDescFlagsProbe` + `test_font_desc_flags_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDIndexed.toRGBImage` with a stream `/Lookup` (+ hival clamp) confirmed at parity. New `IndexedStreamImageProbe` + `test_indexed_stream_image_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Decrypt CLI (AES-128 V4 + AES-256 R6 → unencrypted, qpdf-clean) confirmed at parity. New `DecryptToolProbe` + `test_decrypt_tool_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1467 — Type4-fn stack + graphics Do dispatch + COSWriter /ID + StrikeOut/Squiggly colour (4 bugs fixed) + 6 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

Two of the four fixes are broad (Type-4 function eval; graphics `Do` dispatch); combined state verified across the full non-oracle suite.

- **FIX — `PDFunctionType4.eval` returned the entire residual stack.** When a Type-4 PostScript program leaves more values on the stack than `/Range` declares — which any *pure-stack* program does (it leaves the original N inputs below the M computed outputs) — pypdfbox returned the whole residual stack and let `clip_output` clamp only the first M entries (the untouched inputs). Upstream `PDFunctionType4.eval` fills the output from `popReal()` off the stack TOP, taking only the top N and discarding the surplus below (verified via bytecode + a live oracle: body `{0.1 0.2 0.3}` with 1-output `/Range` → `[0.3]`). Now slices `stack[-declared:]` before clipping. This corrupted every pure-stack Type-4 tint transform / shading function (e.g. a hexachrome DeviceN orant mapped onto garbage CMYK). One stale test that encoded the buggy pass-through retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/common/function/PDFunctionType4.java` (`eval` → `popReal`)
  reason: a Type-4 function returns the top `/Range`-many stack values, not the full residual stack.
- **FIX — graphics `Do` operator (`InvokeNamedXObject`) was a no-op stub.** The `PDFGraphicsStreamEngine`-registered `Do` handler validated the operand and stopped at a debug-log no-op — it never resolved the named XObject nor dispatched to `draw_image`/`show_form`/`show_transparency_group`. So `ExtractImages` extracted ZERO images and `PageDrawer` (which subclasses `PDFGraphicsStreamEngine`) could not draw image XObjects reached via `Do`. Implemented `InvokeNamedXObject.process` to mirror upstream `contentstream/operator/graphics/DrawObject`: resolve `resources.get_x_object(name)`, raise `MissingResourceException` on an unresolved name, dispatch image→`draw_image` / transparency-group→`show_transparency_group` / form→`show_form`. (The main `PDFRenderer` uses its own `_op_do` and is unaffected; the text-extraction `operator/draw_object.py` correctly skips images and is a separate handler.)
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/operator/graphics/DrawObject.java`
  reason: the graphics-engine `Do` must resolve + dispatch the XObject, not no-op.
- **FIX — COSWriter `/ID` synthesis truncated the digest to 16 bytes.** `_ensure_document_id` used `sha256(seed).digest()[:16]`; upstream `COSWriter` uses the full 32-byte SHA-256 for both `/ID` halves (identical on a fresh doc — verified by bytecode + oracle: `id0_len=32`, `id1_len=32`, `id0_eq_id1=true`). Dropped the `[:16]` slice. Affects only docs saved without a pre-existing `/ID`; the incremental path already used the full digest, and the encryption-prep `/ID` (key-derivation input) was intentionally left at 16 bytes.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfwriter/COSWriter.java` (`/ID` synthesis)
  reason: a freshly-synthesised `/ID` half is the full 32-byte SHA-256 digest.
- **FIX — StrikeOut + Squiggly markup appearance emitted device colour shorthand.** Both handlers passed a bare float list to `set_stroking_color` (→ `RG`); upstream passes a typed `PDColor` (→ `/DeviceRGB CS … SC`). Wrapped `/C` via the shared `_pd_color_from_components` (waves 1463/1466 pattern). StrikeOut `/AP /N` now byte-identical; Squiggly's colour + `/BBox` match (squiggly's tiling-pattern form-XObject construction was ported faithfully in wave 1499 — now byte-exact).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/PDStrikeoutAppearanceHandler.java`, `PDSquigglyAppearanceHandler.java`
  reason: typed-`PDColor` stroke must serialise as colourspace+`CS`+`SC`, not shorthand.
- Page `/Rotate` 90/180/270 rasterisation confirmed at parity (dims W/H-swapped + grid). New `PageRotateRenderProbe` + `test_page_rotate_render_oracle.py`. No production change.
- FontBox `vhea`/`vmtx` vertical metrics (header fields + per-GID advanceHeight/TSB incl. trailing-TSB compression) confirmed at parity. New `VheaVmtxProbe` + `test_vhea_vmtx_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Inline-image colorspace resolution (abbreviated/full keys + abbreviated CS names + named-resource `/CS`) confirmed at parity (10 cases). New `InlineCsResolveProbe` + `test_inline_cs_resolve_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDFTextStripper.setSortByPosition` intra-line (same-baseline) re-sort confirmed at parity. New `TextSortInlineProbe` + `test_text_sort_inline_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDPage.getUserUnit` + boundary-box default cascade / clip-to-MediaBox confirmed at parity (7 cases). New `PageUserUnitBoxProbe` + `test_page_user_unit_box_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDType3Font.getCharProc`/`getResources` accessors confirmed at parity. New `Type3CharProcAccessorProbe` + `test_type3_char_proc_accessor_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1466 — CFF DICT defaults + Caret colour + ICCBased image + XMP prolog + PDFTextStripper Do-descent (6 bugs fixed) + 4 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

Six production changes across disjoint trees (cff / annotation / image / xmpbox / text); combined state verified across the full non-oracle suite + the image/color oracle suites.

- **FIX — CFF `get_top_dict`/`get_private_dict` did not materialise operator defaults.** Apache FontBox's `CFFParser.parseFont` stamps DICT defaults into the maps exposed by `getTopDict`/`getPrivateDict`; pypdfbox returned only fontTools' `rawDict` (physically-present operators), so absent operators returned `None` (`FontMatrix`/`FontBBox`/`CharstringType`/`defaultWidthX`/`nominalWidthX`) and a present-but-equals-default `CharstringType 2` (which fontTools drops) reported `None`. Added `_TOP_DICT_DEFAULTS`/`_PRIVATE_DICT_DEFAULTS` tables stamped in the getters (gated on a parsed fontset, so present operators override + synthetic fonts stay undecorated); hint operators (`BlueValues`/`StdHW`/`StdVW`) correctly carry no default.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/cff/CFFParser.java` (`parseFont`/`addValueToTopDict`/`addToPrivateDict`)
  reason: the resolved DICT maps must expose the spec defaults upstream stamps at parse.
- **FIX — `PDCaretAppearanceHandler` emitted device colour shorthand.** Like the wave-1463 Ink fix: upstream calls `setStrokingColor(getColor())` with a `PDColor` → `/DeviceRGB CS … SC`; pypdfbox passed raw `list[float]` → `RG`/`rg` shorthand. Added a shared `PDAbstractAppearanceHandler._pd_color_from_components` and wrapped Caret `/C` in a `PDColor`; caret `/AP /N` now byte-exact.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/PDCaretAppearanceHandler.java`
  reason: typed-`PDColor` stroke/fill must serialise as colourspace+`CS`+`SC`, not shorthand.
- **FIX — ICCBased image XObjects returned `None` from `decode_pdimage_to_pil`.** The helper had Device/Indexed/Lab/Separation/DeviceN branches but no `ICCBased` branch, so an ICCBased image fell through to `return None` (verified: the new N=4 CMYK test fails without the fix).
