Added an `ICCBased` branch — decode N-channels-per-pixel keyed on the profile's `/N` (1/3/4), apply the component-wise `/Decode`, hand to `PDICCBased.to_rgb_image` (which converts through the embedded profile or `/Alternate` fallback) — mirroring the wave-1462 Lab fix. CMYK CMM tail stays in the documented divergence tier.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/image/PDImageXObject.java` (`getImage` → `PDICCBased.toRGBImage`)
  reason: an `[/ICCBased <dict>]` image must decode through `PDICCBased.toRGBImage`, not return null.
- **FIX — `XmpSerializer` emitted a spurious `<?xml?>` prolog.** Apache xmpbox's `XmpSerializer.save` sets `OMIT_XML_DECLARATION="yes"`, so the packet starts with `<?xpacket?>` — a prolog before the xpacket PI is malformed XMP. pypdfbox used `Document.toxml(encoding=…)` which always prepends the prolog; now renders each top-level node via `node.writexml(...)` (no prolog) and encodes. 3 tests that pinned the prolog retargeted.
  upstream: XmpBox 3.0.7 `xml/XmpSerializer.java` (`save` → `OMIT_XML_DECLARATION`)
  reason: the serialized XMP packet must begin with `<?xpacket?>`, never an `<?xml?>` declaration.
- **FIX — `PDFTextStripper` did not descend into form XObjects (`Do`).** The lite stripper's operator dispatch had no `Do` handler, so text painted via a form XObject (e.g. content placed by `multipdf.Overlay`) was never extracted. Upstream `PDFTextStripper` extends `PDFStreamEngine` (`DrawObject`→`showForm` replays the form body). Added a `Do` case: resolve the named XObject, skip images, concat the form `/Matrix` onto the CTM, push the form `/Resources` (+ fresh font/cmap cache for colliding names), replay the form body recursively (depth-capped 50); font/cmap/property lookups now route through a new `_current_resources()` override.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `contentstream/PDFStreamEngine.java` (`showForm`) via `text/PDFTextStripper`
  reason: text inside a `Do`-invoked form XObject must be extracted, matching upstream.
- Text rendering-mode clip variants (`Tr` 4/5/6 — paint + add glyph outlines to the clip, committed at `ET`) confirmed at parity. New `TextClipModeProbe` + `test_text_clip_mode_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `COSObject` lazy indirect resolution (pool dedup identity, null-on-missing dangling ref, self/cyclic-ref termination) confirmed at parity. New `CosLazyResolveProbe` + `test_cos_lazy_resolve_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDPageContentStream.draw_image` (3 overloads → `cm` matrix + `/XObject` registration) confirmed at parity. New `DrawImageProbe` + `test_draw_image_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Embedded simple Type1C font (`PDType1CFont`) per-code name/GID/width + matrix/encoding confirmed at parity. New `Type1CSimpleFontProbe` + `test_type1c_simple_font_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1465 — Line annotation rotation CTM + text Tz scaling (2 bugs fixed) + 4 surfaces at parity (re-run of wave-1464 interrupted surfaces)

Re-ran the 6 surfaces the user interrupted in wave 1464; all 6 completed. The 2 production changes (`pd_line_appearance_handler.py`, `pdf_text_stripper.py`) are clean re-derivations of the files reverted last time; combined state verified across the full non-oracle suite.

- **FIX — `PDLineAppearanceHandler` rotation CTM used a rotate-around-point translation.** Upstream `Matrix.getRotateInstance(angle, x1, y1)` is literally `(cos, sin, -sin, cos, x1, y1)` — the raw endpoint as the translation row. pypdfbox computed a rotate-*around*-point translation (`x1 - x1·cos + y1·sin`, `y1 - x1·sin - y1·cos`), so every rotated `cm` (line body + start/end angled line-endings) was shifted, displacing the whole line/ending geometry (e.g. `cm` translation `216.363 20.45` where PDFBox emits `60 520`). Fixed all 3 transform call sites to emit `(ca, sa, -sa, ca, x1, y1)` / `(…, x2, y2)`. After the fix geometry + Helvetica-metric caption `Td` offsets are byte-exact. Full `tests/pdmodel/interactive/annotation` tree green (2296).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/PDLineAppearanceHandler.java` (via `Matrix.getRotateInstance`)
  reason: the line/ending rotation translation must be the raw endpoint, not a rotate-around-point offset.
- **FIX — `PDFTextStripper` ignored horizontal text scaling (`Tz`).** The lite stripper never registered the `Tz` operator and scaled the show-text advance only by the text-matrix `tm_a`, so run positions were identical for `Tz` 50/100/200 — diverging from PDFBox, where `Tz` scales glyph displacement / `Tc` / `Tw` horizontally by `Tz/100` (§9.3.4) and thus affects word segmentation in `getText`. Added `horizontal_scaling` to `_TextState` (default 1.0, persists across `BT`/`ET`), added the `Tz` handler, and multiplied the run width, emitted run/space widths, and `TJ` numeric adjustment by `Tz/100`. Full `tests/text` green (1029 passed). A mid-size-`TJ`-gap word-break-threshold divergence (space-relative vs `font_size*1.5`) surfaced — orthogonal to `Tz` (reproduces at Tz 100) — pinned strict-`xfail` + DEFERRED.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStreamEngine.java` / `text/PDFTextStripper.java` (horizontal-scaling displacement)
  reason: extraction spacing/word-segmentation must honour the `Tz/100` horizontal-scale factor.
- `PDFRenderer.renderImageWithDPI(page, dpi, ImageType)` confirmed at parity: exact raster dims across 36/72/96/150 DPI (incl. fractional-mediabox `(int)` floor) + exact channel layout (RGB/ARGB/GRAY/BINARY); RGB/ARGB grid within MAD<6/MAXDIFF<60. GRAY/BINARY luminance VALUES intentionally not gated — PDFBox's AWT TYPE_BYTE_GRAY/BINARY applies a gamma-2.2 ICC TRC at paint time vs Pillow Rec.601 (documented CMM-family divergence). New `RenderImageTypeProbe` + `test_render_image_type_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `COSName` write-escaping (§7.3.5, `#XX` for non-printable-allowlist bytes) confirmed byte-identical across 152 cases (every ASCII byte + multi-byte UTF-8). New `NameWriteEscapeProbe` + `test_name_write_escape_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Parent `PDType0Font` READ-side decode + width (`codeToCID`/`codeToGID`/`getWidth`/`readCode`) confirmed at parity across 6 Identity-H CIDFontType2 fixtures. New `Type0ReadWidthProbe` + `test_type0_read_width_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `TextToPDF.createPDFFromText` (default Helvetica 10pt / 40pt margins / 1.05 leading / Letter) multi-page build confirmed at parity (page count, extracted text, font). New `TextToPdfProbe` + `tests/tools/oracle/test_text_to_pdf_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1464 — fontbox head/maxp + tokenizer comments/BX-EX + catalog page enums + Separation All/None (4 surfaces at parity, 0 bugs; partial wave — user-interrupted)

Partial wave: 10 agents launched; the user interrupted the batch (to free the shell) after 4 completed. The 4 surfaces below landed complete + verified (35 tests, ruff clean); no production code changed (all confirmed-parity regression pins). The 6 interrupted surfaces (render DPI/ImageType, Line annotation appearance, COSName write-escaping, Tz horizontal scaling, PDType0Font encode/width, TextToPDF) left partial/unverified files — their two partial production edits (`pd_line_appearance_handler.py`, `pdf_text_stripper.py`) were reverted and their untracked probes/tests removed; to be re-run cleanly in a later wave.

- FontBox `head` (HeaderTable) + `maxp` (MaximumProfileTable) tables confirmed at parity across LiberationSans + DejaVuSansMono (incl. `created`/`modified` epoch-millis and the Java `Float.toString` 32-bit-shortest repr for `version`/`fontRevision`). New `HeadMaxpProbe` + `test_head_maxp_oracle.py`.
- Content-stream tokenizer comment-skipping (`%`→EOL across LF/CRLF/bare-CR) + `BX`/`EX` compatibility operators (unknown ops preserved as `Operator`) confirmed at parity. New `CommentCompatTokenizeProbe` + `test_comment_compat_tokenize_oracle.py`.
- Catalog `/PageMode` (6 members) + `/PageLayout` (6 members) enum round-trip confirmed at parity. New `CatalogPageEnumProbe` + `test_catalog_page_enum_oracle.py`.
- Separation `/All` + `/None` special colorants confirmed at parity (colorant name verbatim incl. `""` default-ctor, 1 component, initial colour `[1.0]`, `toRGB` exact on DeviceGray). New `SeparationAllNoneProbe` + `test_separation_all_none_oracle.py`.

## Wave 1463 — Ink appearance colour + ASCII85 encode framing (2 bugs fixed) + 8 surfaces at parity + 2 latent stale-test fixes (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — Ink annotation `/AP /N` emitted the stroking colour as device shorthand `RG`.** Upstream `PDInkAppearanceHandler` calls `setStrokingColor(PDColor)`; `PDAppearanceContentStream` only overrides the `float[]` overload (`RG`/`G`/`K`), so the `PDColor` overload resolves to `PDAbstractContentStream.setStrokingColor(PDColor)` which always writes `/<cs> CS` + components + `SC` (`SCN` only for Pattern/Separation/DeviceN/ICCBased) — never device shorthand. pypdfbox routed Ink's `/C` through the float shorthand, emitting `1 0.5 0 RG` where PDFBox emits `/DeviceRGB CS 1 0.5 0 SC`. Fixed `PDPageContentStream._emit_pd_color` (PDColor path) to mirror upstream exactly (colour-space-stack tracking, `CS`/`cs`, then `SC`/`scn` by space kind), made `PDAppearanceContentStream.set_(non_)stroking_color` dispatch a `PDColor` argument to that path, and wrapped Ink's `/C` in a device `PDColor`. Resolves the wave-1414 documented Ink colour divergence. Several existing oracle/content-stream tests that hand-normalised `RG`↔`CS SC` simplified to exact comparison.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDAbstractContentStream.java` (`setStrokingColor(PDColor)`), `.../annotation/handlers/PDInkAppearanceHandler.java`
  reason: a typed-`PDColor` stroke must serialise as colourspace+`CS`+`SC`, not device shorthand.
- **FIX — ASCII85 encode framing diverged from upstream.** `ASCII85Decode.encode` bypassed pypdfbox's own `ASCII85OutputStream` and did `base64.a85encode(..., adobe=True)[2:]` — no 72-column line-folding, no trailing LF after `~>`, and emitted `~>` for empty input. Upstream `ASCII85Filter.encode` wraps its destination in `ASCII85OutputStream`, whose contract (verified against the 3.0.7 bytecode) is: a running line-break counter emitting `\n` every 72 output columns (spanning the trailing partial group, so a newline can land immediately before `~>`), an unconditional trailing LF after `>`, and `flushed=true` at construction so empty input emits zero bytes. Rewrote `ASCII85OutputStream` as a faithful byte-for-byte port and routed `ASCII85Decode.encode` through it; encoded output is now byte-identical to PDFBox across input sizes 0–300 (and the `[/ASCII85Decode /FlateDecode]` chain `/Length` matches, 118==118). Two prior "documented divergence" oracle tests upgraded to byte-identity.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `filter/ascii85/ASCII85OutputStream.java`, `filter/ASCII85Filter.java`
  reason: ASCII85 encoded output must match upstream's line-folding + EOD framing byte-for-byte.
- **Latent stale-test fixes (not new bugs — wave-1458/1461 verified-correct behaviour, tests missed because they live outside the trees prior targeted regressions ran):**
  - `tests/filter/test_filter_tail_wave784.py` — pinned the pre-wave-1458 pypdfbox-specific `COSFloat("1-2-3")` message `"misplaced '-'"`; upstream raises `"Error expected floating point number actual='1-2-3'"` (wave 1458 aligned it). Retargeted the `pytest.raises` match.
  - `tests/test_wave1359_residue_close_to_100.py` — asserted `PDResources.get_color_space("Missing") is None`; wave 1461 made `get_color_space` raise `MissingResourceException` for an unresolvable non-device name (matching upstream). The production `copy_needed_resources_to` is unaffected (it guards on `has_color_space`); retargeted the test's assertion to `not has_color_space(...)`.
- Type 6 Coons / Type 7 tensor patch-mesh flag-driven edge sharing confirmed at parity (per-channel RGB). New `PatchMeshFlagProbe` + `test_patch_mesh_flag_oracle.py`. No production change.
- fontbox OS/2 Windows metrics table (all 37 accessors + PANOSE + v1/v2 gating) confirmed at parity (GPOS had no upstream FontBox surface to mirror → pivoted). New `Os2MetricsProbe` + `test_os2_metrics_oracle.py`. No production change.
- OCG render-time visibility gate (image-XObject `/OC`, OCMD `/VE`, nested `/OC`) confirmed at parity OFF+ON. New `OptionalContentRenderProbe` + `test_optional_content_render_gate_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `Tw` word-spacing applicability (§9.3.3, single-byte-32-only incl. Identity-H 2-byte-32 exclusion) confirmed at parity. New `WordSpacingCodeProbe` + `test_word_spacing_code_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Interactive-action secondary accessors (factory dispatch, URI `/IsMap`, GoToR/Launch `OpenMode`, Launch `/Win` params, `/AA` placement) confirmed at parity. New `ActionAccessorProbe` + `test_action_accessor_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDTrueTypeFont` non-symbolic embedded simple-TTF `codeToGID`/`getWidth`/`hasGlyph` confirmed at parity over codes 0..255. New `SimpleTtfMapProbe` + `test_simple_ttf_map_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDCalRGB`/`PDCalGray` component count + initial colour + `toRGB` gamma/matrix confirmed at parity (CMM tail within documented tolerance). New `CalComponentsProbe` + `test_cal_components_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Encryption restricted-permission WRITE round-trip (AES-128 V4 + AES-256 R6, signed `/P` -2336 byte-identical both directions) confirmed at parity. New `PermWriteProbe` + `test_perm_write_roundtrip_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1462 — COSArray accessors, CIDFontType0 CID→GID, Lab image decode (3 bugs fixed) + 7 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

Combined state verified across 32162 non-oracle + 303 oracle tests (0 failures); the 3 production changes touch different files (cos_array / pd_cid_font_type0 / pd_image_x_object).

- **FIX — `COSArray.get_int`/`get_name`/`get_string` dereferenced indirect entries.** Upstream `getInt`/`getName`/`getString` read the RAW entry (`objects.get(index)`, verified via bytecode — no `getObject` call) and check `instanceof`, so an indirect `COSObject` element falls through to the default rather than resolving its target. pypdfbox dereferenced via `get_object(index)`. Fixed all three to read `self._items[index]` raw (`get_int` still coerces any `COSNumber`; `get_name`/`get_string` still match `COSName`/`COSString` only). `get_float`/`get_boolean` left dereferencing — pypdfbox-only additions with no upstream `COSArray` equivalent. 1 stale test retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSArray.java` (`getInt`/`getName`/`getString`)
  reason: these accessors read the raw array slot, not the dereferenced target.
- **FIX — `PDCIDFontType0.get_type2_char_string` double-resolved CID→GID.** It pre-resolved the CID to a GID via `charset.index("cidNNNNN")`, then passed that GID into `CFFCIDFont.get_type2_char_string`, which resolves CID→GID *again* (`gid_for_cid`) — looking up `"cid0000<gid>"`, collapsing to GID 0. So the returned charstring carried `gid=0`, `has_glyph(code)` was `False` for every real glyph, and `get_path`/rendering resolved the wrong outline. Upstream `PDCIDFontType0.getType2CharString(cid)` passes the CID straight through (verified against 3.0.7 bytecode). Fixed to pass the CID unchanged for the `CFFCIDFont` branch. 3 test fakes (which had encoded the buggy GID-in assumption) made faithful to the real CID-in contract.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/PDCIDFontType0.java` (`getType2CharString`)
  reason: `CFFCIDFont.getType2CharString` takes a CID and does its own CID→GID; pre-resolving double-applied it.
- **FIX — Lab colour-space image XObjects never rendered.** `decode_pdimage_to_pil` (`pd_image_x_object.py`) had branches for DeviceRGB/Gray/CMYK/Indexed/Separation/DeviceN but no `/Lab` branch, so a Lab image fell through to `return None`. Upstream renders it via `PDLab.toRGBImage`. Added a `"Lab"` branch passing the RAW 8-bit samples to `color_space.to_rgb_image(...)` (no `/Decode` pre-pass — upstream `PDLab.toRGBImage` consumes the raw raster and does its own L*a*b* scaling, confirmed by disassembly). `PDLab.to_rgb`/`to_rgb_image` math was already at parity (the only residual is the documented XYZ→sRGB CMM model difference, same class as CalRGB/CalGray).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/color/PDLab.java` (`toRGBImage`)
  reason: a `[/Lab <dict>]` image XObject must decode through `PDLab.toRGBImage`, not return null.
- Type 4 free-form Gouraud mesh shading with flag-1/flag-2 vertex continuation confirmed at parity (per-channel RGB). New `MeshGouraudFlagProbe` + `test_mesh_gouraud_flag_oracle.py`. No production change.
- TrueType cmap format 14 (UVS) confirmed at parity — FontBox 3.0.7 `processSubtype14` is a no-op (logs+ignores), so a format-14 subtable is inert under `getGlyphId` and does not corrupt neighbours. New `CmapFormat14Probe` + `test_cmap_format14_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Polygon/PolyLine annotation `/AP /N` appearance (operand-level token stream incl. PolyLine `/LE` OpenArrow/Diamond endings) confirmed byte-exact. New `PolyAppearanceProbe` + `test_poly_appearance_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Form XObject `/Do` draw — `/Matrix` concatenation onto CTM + `/BBox` clip (incl. a content-cropping case) confirmed at parity. New `FormXObjectProbe` + `test_form_xobject_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `suppressDuplicateOverlappingText` confirmed at parity with suppression ON (default) — coincident glyphs collapse byte-for-byte. New `DuplicateOverlapProbe` + `test_duplicate_overlap_oracle.py` (+ fixture). Suppression-OFF is the documented run-vs-glyph granularity carve-out (strict xfail). No production change.
- `PDPage.get_annotations()` typed-subclass factory dispatch + order preservation + filter overload confirmed at parity. New `PageAnnotRetrievalProbe` + `test_page_annot_retrieval_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `LosslessFactory.create_from_image` gray/gray+alpha/indexed encode confirmed at parity (round-trip bit-exact; pins two pre-existing deliberate divergences: indexed→`/Indexed` vs upstream `/DeviceRGB`, LA→`/DeviceGray` vs upstream `/ICCBased`). New `LosslessEncodeProbe` + `test_lossless_encode_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1461 — PDResources color-space, COSDictionary get_string, text ToUnicode fallback, FontMapper aliases (4 bugs fixed) + 6 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

All 4 production changes touched different files; the combined state was verified across 33371 non-oracle tests + 377 oracle tests (the "PDFontDescriptor font_name=None" failures some agents saw in mid-flight full-suite runs were transient concurrent-edit artifacts, not real — the final tree is green).

- **FIX — `PDResources.get_color_space` returned `None` for an unresolvable non-device name.** Upstream `getColorSpace` → `PDColorSpace.create(name, resources)` raises `MissingResourceException("Missing color space: <name>")` for a name that is neither a device shortcut nor a resolvable resource entry (verified against the 3.0.7 jar). pypdfbox fell back to the permissive no-resources `PDColorSpace.create(name)` (returns `None`). Replaced `_get_default_color_space` with `_color_space_for_bare_name`, mirroring upstream's COSName branch exactly: `Default*` override for `Device*` refs, device singleton for `Device*`/`Pattern`, else raise `MissingResourceException`. Dropped the `G`/`RGB`/`CMYK` short forms (upstream's `PDInlineImage#toLongName` expands those before any lookup). 4 tests that codified the old `None` behaviour retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDResources.java` (`getColorSpace`), `pdmodel/graphics/color/PDColorSpace.java` (`create`)
  reason: an unresolvable non-device color-space name must raise, not return null.
- **FIX — `COSDictionary.get_string` coerced a `COSName` value to text.** Upstream `getString` (Java 718/750) decodes only a `COSString`; name coercion is the separate `getNameAsString` (653/689). pypdfbox's `get_string`/`has_string` also accepted `COSName`, conflating the two. Fixed `get_string`/`has_string` to `COSString`-only and gave `get_name_as_string` a real implementation (`COSName`→name, `COSString`→string, else default). Migrated the 12 production callers that upstream resolves via `getNameAsString` (`PDFontDescriptor.get_font_name`/`get_font_stretch`, `PDSignature`/`PDSeedValue` filter, `PDSeedValueCertificate.get_url_type`, `PDViewportDictionary.get_name`, `PDCIDSystemInfo.get_registry`/`get_ordering`, `PDMarkedContent.get_language`, `PDUserProperty.get_name`, `PDRendition`/`PDMediaClip` subtype, `PDDocumentInformation.to_dict`). Callers whose keys are genuine text strings upstream stayed on `get_string`. 3 tests retargeted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSDictionary.java` (`getString` vs `getNameAsString`)
  reason: `getString` is COSString-only; conflating name coercion diverged from upstream and risked silently mis-decoding name-valued keys.
- **FIX — `PDFTextStripper` dropped glyphs whose code is absent from a simple font's `/ToUnicode` CMap.** `_decode_text_via_cmap` walked bytes through the `/ToUnicode` CMap only and skipped any unmapped code. Upstream `PDFTextStreamEngine.showGlyph` emits `font.toUnicode(code)` per glyph (ToUnicode-first, then encoding + glyph-list fallback). `PDFont.to_unicode` was already correct; only the stripper's CMap-only loop was wrong. Now falls back to `font.to_unicode(code)` when the CMap returns `None`, dropping the code only if that also yields `None`. Java extracted `"ZBCffi \n"` where pypdfbox had `"Zffi\n"`; now byte-identical.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `text/PDFTextStreamEngine.java` (`showGlyph`) / `pdmodel/font/PDFont.java` (`toUnicode`)
  reason: extraction must honour the full ToUnicode→encoding fallback chain, not drop CMap-unmapped codes.
- **FIX — `FontMapperImpl.__init__` skipped upstream's Acrobat alias substitute-expansion loop.** Upstream `FontMapperImpl()` runs `for baseName in Standard14Fonts.getNames(): if getSubstitutes(baseName).isEmpty(): addSubstitutes(...)`, so `getSubstitutes("Arial"/"ArialMT"/"TimesNewRoman"/"CourierNew"/`*-MT` variants)` return the canonical mapped font's substitute list. pypdfbox populated only the canonical table, so those alias names returned `[]`. Added the expansion loop driven off a verbatim pin of upstream's exact `Standard14Fonts.ALIASES` key set (`_STANDARD14_ALIAS_NAMES`), so only the names upstream registers get substitutes.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/FontMapperImpl.java` (constructor)
  reason: alias font names must resolve to the canonical Standard-14 substitute list, matching Acrobat's font-mapping table.
- Rendering stroke geometry (line caps `J` 0/1/2, joins `j` 0/1/2, miter limit `M`, dash `d`) confirmed at parity (MAD<6/MAXDIFF<60). New `StrokeGeometryProbe` + `test_stroke_geometry_oracle.py`. No production change.
- FontBox GSUB substitution extraction (Lookup Type 1/3/4 single/alternate/ligature) confirmed at parity across 3 bundled fonts. New `GsubSubstitutionProbe` + `test_gsub_substitution_oracle.py`. No production change.
- FreeText annotation `/DA` parse + `/RD` bbox + `/AP /N` token generation confirmed at parity. New `FreeTextDaRdProbe` + `test_free_text_da_rd_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDDocumentInformation` accessor + save→reload round-trip confirmed at parity (typed getters, `getMetadataKeys`, raw `D:…+HH'mm'` date byte form). New `InfoAccessorRoundTripProbe` + `test_info_accessor_round_trip_oracle.py`. No production change.
- ICCBased-on-image `/Alternate` fallback for an unparseable embedded profile (N=3→DeviceRGB, N=1→DeviceGray) confirmed at parity. New `test_icc_alternate_fallback_oracle.py` (reuses `RenderProbe`). No production change.
- Incremental save appending a NEW object (text annotation) confirmed at parity (prefix preservation, `/Prev` chain, object-number minting above source max, `/ID[0]`-stable/`/ID[1]`-regenerated, qpdf-clean). New `IncrementalAddAnnotationProbe` + `test_incremental_add_annotation_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1460 — image-mask compositing in get_image + AFM reduced-dataset (2 bugs fixed) + 3 at parity + 1 divergence pinned (re-run of wave-1459 interrupted surfaces)

This wave re-ran the 6 surfaces a usage limit interrupted in wave 1459; all 6 completed.

- **FIX — `PDImageXObject.get_image()` returned the opaque raster instead of compositing masks.** Upstream `getImage()` returns an ARGB `BufferedImage` once a `/SMask` or `/Mask` is present; pypdfbox previously decoded only the colour samples ("mask compositing is rendering-cluster work and not yet folded in here"). `get_image()` now applies, in upstream's precedence order, the `/SMask` soft mask (§8.9.5.4, grayscale → alpha, scaled to the base dims honouring the SMask's own `/Interpolate` + `/Decode`, with `/Matte` un-pre-multiply), then an explicit-mask `/Mask` 1-bit stencil (§8.9.6.3, `/Decode [1 0]` polarity, nearest-neighbour scale), then a color-key `/Mask` range (§8.9.6.4). Each step is best-effort (a mask that fails to decode leaves the opaque raster). `get_opaque_image()` was correspondingly changed to NEVER composite masks, matching upstream `getOpaqueImage()` (previously it delegated to `get_image()`, so the two coincided). The main renderer path is unaffected (it uses `to_pil_image()` + its own soft-mask compositing, not `get_image()`); verified no double-masking via the image mask oracles + 2242 image/rendering unit tests.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/image/PDImageXObject.java` (`getImage`/`getOpaqueImage`/`applyMask`)
  reason: `getImage()` must return the masked ARGB raster; `getOpaqueImage()` the colour samples alone.
- **FIX — `Standard14Fonts.get_afm` / `afm_loader.load_standard14` parsed the full AFM dataset.** Upstream `Standard14Fonts.getAFM` parses bundled Core-14 AFMs with `AFMParser.parse(true)` (reduced dataset — kern-pair + composite blocks skipped), so `FontMetrics.getKernPairs()` is empty for the Core-14 faces even though the files ship thousands of `KPX` lines. pypdfbox parsed the full dataset, returning 2705 kern pairs for Helvetica vs Java's 0. Now `parse(reduced_dataset=True)`; char metrics + header values unchanged. 3 enriched tests that pinned the pre-fix behaviour retargeted (with a regression test asserting `load_standard14("Helvetica").get_kern_pairs() == []`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/font/Standard14Fonts.java` (`getAFM`)
  reason: bundled Standard-14 AFMs must parse with the reduced dataset to match upstream's empty kern-pair contract.
- COSDocument `/Prev`-chained multi-section trailer resolution confirmed at parity (`/Root`/`/Info`/`/ID`/`/Size`/`/Encrypt` — newest section wins, omitted keys fall back down `/Prev`). New `oracle/probes/TrailerResolveProbe.java` + `test_trailer_resolve_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDPageTree` structural mutation/traversal confirmed at parity on an unbalanced 3-level `/Kids` tree (`add` appends to root; `insert_before`/`insert_after`/`remove` re-target `/Parent` + propagate `/Count` up the chain; all `qpdf --check` clean across 9 cases). New `oracle/probes/PageTreeMutateProbe.java` + `test_page_tree_mutate_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Tagged-PDF `/ParentTree` reverse MCID→element mapping confirmed at parity (leaf classification, per-slot standard-type/role-map resolution, `/StructParents` linkage). New `oracle/probes/StructParentTreeProbe.java` + `test_struct_parent_tree_oracle.py`. No production change.
- **DIVERGENCE PINNED (not fixed) — vertical writing mode (WMode 1) text extraction.** Java steps each `/Identity-V` glyph down its column (`PDFTextStripper` reads `CMap.getWMode()`) → `"A\nB\nC\n"`; pypdfbox's horizontal-only lite stripper → `"ABC\n"`. The vertical font-metric API is already at parity (wave 1428); the stripper's consumption of it is unimplemented — a deferred layout feature (same class as eu-001 / poems-beads). Pinned both sides + strict `xfail` in `test_vertical_text_strip_oracle.py`; tracked in `DEFERRED.md`.

## Wave 1459 — Type4 op edges + Type3 d0/d1 + kern table + highlight blend (4 surfaces at parity, 0 bugs; partial wave — session limit)

Partial wave: 10 agents launched, but a session usage limit interrupted 6 of them before they produced output. The 4 surfaces below landed complete and verified; no production code changed (all confirmed-parity regression pins). The interrupted surfaces (AFM Standard-14 metrics, page-tree mutation ops, vertical-writing-mode text, structure tree, COSDocument trailer resolution, image `/SMask` alpha) are unstarted/incomplete and were dropped — to be re-run in a later wave.

- Type 4 PostScript-calculator operator edge cases confirmed at parity: `not`-on-int negation (`5 not → -5`, not bit-inverse), `or`/`xor`/`and`/`bitshift`, `idiv`/`mod` negative-operand sign semantics, `round` ties, `ceiling`/`floor`/`truncate`/`cvi`/`cvr` on negatives, `cos`/`log`/`neg`/`abs`/`atan`-wrap, relational `eq`/`ne`/`le`/`ge`; + Type 2 leaves out-of-domain inputs un-clipped. New `oracle/probes/Type4OpEdgeProbe.java` + `test_type4_op_edge_oracle.py`.
- Type 3 glyph char-proc metric operators `d0`/`d1` (set width / set width + bbox) confirmed at parity. New `oracle/probes/Type3CharProcProbe.java` + `test_type3_char_proc_oracle.py`.
- TrueType `kern` table (format-0 horizontal kerning subtable selection + per-pair lookup + coverage flags) confirmed at parity. New `oracle/probes/KernTableProbe.java` + `test_kern_table_oracle.py`.
- Highlight text-markup annotation `/AP /N` (multiply-blend appearance from `/QuadPoints` + `/C`, PDF §11.3.5) confirmed at parity. New `oracle/probes/HighlightBlendProbe.java` + `test_highlight_blend_oracle.py`.

## Wave 1458 — blend+alpha + content-stream formatting + COSNumber boundary (9 bugs fixed) + 7 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — blend mode silently reset by a `/ca`-only `gs`; source alpha double-applied.** (1) `PDFRenderer._set_graphics_state` updated the blend mode unconditionally from `ext_gstate.get_blend_mode()` (which returns `Normal` for an absent `/BM`), so a second `gs` carrying only `/ca` wiped an earlier `/BM`. Now guarded on the ExtGState explicitly carrying `/BM` (PDF §8.4.5). (2) The blend paste folded the combined clip/soft-mask/`/ca` alpha into the blend *and* pasted the result through that same alpha again, double-weighting source alpha; now pasted through the blended layer's own over-alpha, so Multiply at `/ca`=0.5 renders the §11.3.6 midpoint `0.5·Cb+0.5·B` (was `0.75·Cb+0.25·B`).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `rendering/` (PageDrawer blend compositing), `pdmodel/graphics/state/PDExtendedGraphicsState.java`
  reason: an ExtGState updates only the parameters it carries; alpha-aware blend compositing must follow §11.3.6 exactly.
- **FIX — `PDPageContentStream`/`PDAbstractContentStream` numeric formatting + dash spacing.** Numeric operands were formatted with 4 fraction digits; PDFBox's `formatDecimal` uses `NumberFormat.getNumberInstance(Locale.US)` with `setMaximumFractionDigits(5)` (grouping off, HALF_EVEN). Set to 5 digits (Python `format(f, ".5f")` is also half-to-even, no grouping). Dash-array elements were space-*joined* (`[3 2] 1 d`); upstream `setLineDashPattern` writes each via `writeOperand` (always trailing space) → `[3 2 ] 1 d`. Both aligned; 5 hand-written tests pinning the old bytes corrected.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/PDPageContentStream.java`, `pdmodel/PDAbstractContentStream.java`
  reason: generated content-stream bytes must match upstream's number formatter + per-element operand spacing.
- **FIX — `COSNumber.get`/`COSFloat` boundary behaviour (5 divergences).** Verified against 3.0.7 bytecode: (1) `COSNumber.get("")` returned `COSInteger.ZERO` — upstream returns `OUT_OF_RANGE_MAX` (empty string passes the `\d*` overflow branch); (2) uppercase-`E` literals (`"1E2"`) returned `COSFloat` — upstream's `isFloat` checks only `.`/lowercase-`e`, so they route to the integer branch and raise; (3) multi-`+` (`"++5"`) returned `COSInteger(5)` — `lstrip("+")` stripped all signs; now strips one; (4) `COSFloat(String)` malformed-recovery (PDFBOX-2990/-3500) preserved raw bytes — upstream leaves `valueAsString=null` on the repair path so the value reformats (`--16.33`→`-16.33`); (5) `intValue`/`longValue` used unbounded `int(float)` and `-0.0` kept its sign — now saturate like JVM `f2i`/`f2l` and `coerce` flushes `-0.0`→`+0.0`. Pinned by `CosNumberProbe` over 47 literals; 5 tests retargeted, 1 obsolete white-box test deleted.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `cos/COSNumber.java`, `cos/COSFloat.java`
  reason: COSNumber parse/format/truncation must match upstream branch-for-branch including the overflow and malformed-recovery paths.
- Blend-mode compositing combined with constant `/ca` confirmed at parity for the remaining separable + non-separable modes (after the two fixes above). New `oracle/probes/BlendAlphaProbe.java` + `test_blend_alpha_oracle.py`.
- FontBox `glyf` composite-glyph assembly (scale/2×2 transforms, `MORE_COMPONENTS` chain) confirmed byte-exact across 13 DejaVuSans composites (366 lines). New `oracle/probes/CompositeGlyphProbe.java` + `test_composite_glyph_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Cloudy-border (`/BE /S C`) appearance generation confirmed at parity — Square byte-exact (full 263-op curl-Bezier stream + `/Rect`/`/BBox`/`/Matrix`/`/RD`); Circle meets the geometry contract (curl count diverges only via the documented `flatten_ellipse` Ellipse2D difference). New `oracle/probes/CloudyBorderProbe.java` + `test_cloudy_border_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Path fill rules (nonzero `f` vs even-odd `f*` on self-intersecting/nested paths) + `W`/`W*` clip-then-fill confirmed at parity within MAD<6/MAXDIFF<60 (with a discrimination guard). New `oracle/probes/PathFillClipProbe.java` + `test_path_fill_clip_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDStream.createInputStream(stopFilters)` partial filter-chain decode confirmed at parity (stop-at-first→raw, prefix-only decode, null/empty/absent stop list, null-slot `/DecodeParms` alignment). New `oracle/probes/StopFilterProbe.java` + `test_stop_filter_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDCIDFont` `/W` width-array (both `c [w…]` list + `c_first c_last w` range forms) + `/DW` default-width fallback confirmed at parity across 6 Type0 fixtures. New `oracle/probes/CidWidthProbe.java` + `test_cid_width_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDNameTreeNode`/`PDNumberTreeNode` `getValue` `/Limits` binary descent confirmed at parity on sparse-leaf hole keys, touching/adjacent ranges, boundary keys, and negative/zero-spanning integer keys. New `oracle/probes/NameNumTreeRangeProbe.java` + `test_name_number_tree_range_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `/DCTDecode` Adobe APP14 transform dimension confirmed at parity — CMYK JPEG with the marker stripped + a YCCK (transform byte 2) JPEG; PDFBox un-inverts stored CMYK regardless of the marker and Pillow's reader matches. Reuses `oracle/probes/JpegImgProbe.java`; new `test_dct_adobe_transform_oracle.py` + 2 synthesized fixtures. No production change.

## Wave 1457 — parser literal/name decode + list-box highlight + xmpbox lang-alt (4 bugs fixed) + 5 at parity + 1 divergence pinned (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — `BaseParser.read_literal_string` normalised bare CR/CRLF to LF.** Upstream `parseCOSString` writes unescaped EOL bytes verbatim — only the *backslash + EOL* line continuation is special. `(a\rb)` must decode to `a\rb`, not `a\nb`. Removed the CR-normalisation branch; all non-escape bytes now stored raw. Two hand-written tests pinning the old behaviour retargeted to raw-byte expectations.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/BaseParser.java` (`parseCOSString`)
  reason: literal-string bytes must round-trip verbatim except for the documented escape sequences.
- **FIX — `BaseParser.read_name_bytes` peek-based `#XX` handling diverged on EOF / single-hex-digit.** Upstream reads both following bytes via `read()`: a `#` whose hex pair hits premature EOF is discarded (`/AB#`→`AB`); a `#` followed by a non-hex pair rewinds the second byte, writes a literal `#`, and re-processes the first. pypdfbox's `peek`-based version kept the dangling `#` and mishandled the single-digit case. Rewrote to mirror the upstream `read()`-based algorithm + `is_end_of_name` loop exactly.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/BaseParser.java` (`parseCOSName`)
  reason: name `#XX` hex-escape decoding must match upstream's read-and-rewind byte handling.
- **FIX — list-box `/AP /N` selection-highlight fills were gated on the visible scroll window.** `PDAppearanceGenerator._regenerate_listbox_widget` only emitted a highlight rect when a selected index was `>= /TI`. Upstream `insertGeneratedListboxSelectionHighlight` loops the entire selected-index set and positions each rect at `upperRightY - highlightBoxHeight*(selectedIndex - topIndex + 1) + 2`; selections scrolled above the window still emit a fill that lands off the top and is clipped by the `/Tx` clip path. With `/TI`=2 and options 0+2 selected, PDFBox emits 2 fills, pypdfbox emitted 1. Now iterates `sorted(highlighted)` with the off-window position (no gate, no y-clamp). Row-text loop (which legitimately skips scrolled-out rows) unchanged.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/interactive/form/AppearanceGeneratorHelper.java` (`insertGeneratedListboxSelectionHighlight`)
  reason: every selected option must emit a highlight fill positioned relative to `/TI`, regardless of the visible window.
- **FIX — `DomXmpParser` reordered lang-alt entries on parse.** Apache xmpbox's `manageLangAlt` appends each `rdf:li` via raw `addProperty` and preserves source document order; pypdfbox's parser forced `x-default` to the front (via `set_unqualified_language_property_value` → `_reorganize_alt_order`, and re-sorting `_build_*_lang_alt` helpers). For `dc:title` languages `[en, fr, x-default, ja]`, xmpbox returns that order; pypdfbox returned `[x-default, en, fr, ja]`. `_assign` now deposits the lang map directly into `schema._properties` (source order), and the four `_build_*_lang_alt` helpers (DublinCore, XMPRightsManagement, Exif, Tiff) emit children in stored order. The x-default-first reorganization remains confined to the schema *setters* (unchanged). Two hand-written tests retargeted.
  upstream: XmpBox 3.0.7 `xml/DomXmpParser.java` (`manageLangAlt`)
  reason: the parser must preserve `rdf:li` document order; x-default-first is a setter-API convenience, not a parse contract.
- Spaced tiling-pattern fill (`/XStep`/`/YStep` > `/BBox`, gaps showing page background) confirmed at parity (MAD 0.00/MAXDIFF 0, symmetric + asymmetric steps). New `oracle/probes/SpacedTilingProbe.java` + `test_spaced_tiling_oracle.py`. No production change.
- FontBox Type1 charstring interpreter (program-level `getPath` via `createWithPFB`) confirmed at parity across 4 Type1 programs incl. the `seac` eacute composite; the `CurvedFlexType1` `o` malformed-flex divergence is documented + pinned (FontBox drops the invalid flex → 4 cubics, the wrapped fontTools `T1OutlineExtractor` keeps it → 6; pypdfbox follows fontTools per the library-first rule, shapes visually identical). New `oracle/probes/Type1GlyphPathProbe.java` + `test_type1_glyph_path_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Content-stream text-state + text-positioning operator evolution (text-rendering-matrix + glyph displacement across `BT`/`Tf`/`TL`/`Td`/`TD`/`Tm`/`T*`/`Tc`/`Tw`/`Tz`/`Ts`/`Tj`/`TJ`/`'`/`"`) confirmed identical to PDFBox 3.0.7 across 16 glyphs. New `oracle/probes/TextStateMatrixProbe.java` + `test_text_state_matrix_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `PDFMergerUtility` `/PageLabels` number-tree merge (range-key re-basing by destination page count + dest-without-labels fallback) confirmed at parity (`qpdf --check` clean). New `oracle/probes/MergePageLabelsProbe.java` + `test_merge_page_labels_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Embedded CMap stream parsing confirmed at parity: mixed-width codespace `readCode` byte-length disambiguation, `begincidrange`/`begincidchar`, `/Identity-H usecmap` inheritance, `/CMapType 2` ToUnicode stream. New `oracle/probes/EmbeddedCMapProbe.java` + `test_embedded_cmap_oracle.py`. No production change.
- `AccessPermission` owner-vs-user split + `/Encrypt /P` wire integer confirmed at parity (owner→`-4`/`isOwnerPermission`/`!isReadOnly`; user→wire `/P`/`isReadOnly`; wire `/P` identical under both passwords). New `oracle/probes/PermOwnerUserProbe.java` + `test_perm_owner_user_oracle.py`. No production change.
- **DIVERGENCE PINNED (not fixed) — `PDFTextStripperByArea` overlapping regions.** Upstream's page-wide `suppressDuplicateOverlappingText` dedup (default on, backed by one `characterListMapping`) routes a glyph in two overlapping regions to exactly one region (the first in `regionArea`'s `HashMap` iteration order); pypdfbox's lite stripper bins each region independently with per-region dedup, so the glyph lands in every containing region. Disjoint/four-quadrant/empty regions remain byte-parity. Faithful convergence would require replicating Java's non-spec `HashMap` order — brittle, out of scope. Pinned both sides in `test_text_multi_region_oracle.py`; tracked in `DEFERRED.md`.

## Wave 1456 — colour-raster float32 + hmtx guards + gs-merge setters + sub-byte predictor (6 bugs fixed) + 4 surfaces at parity (10-agent wave)

- **FIX — Separation/DeviceN raster→RGB done in double precision (per-pixel off-by-one).** PDFBox's `PDSeparation.tintTransform` and `PDDeviceN.toRGBWithTintTransform(WritableRaster)` do the `/255` pre-scale and `*255` post-scale + truncating `(int)` cast in 32-bit `float`; pypdfbox did it in 64-bit `double`. For tint outputs like `1 - 127/255`, double lands exactly on `128.0` (→128) while float lands on `127.999…` (→127) — a consistent off-by-one per pixel. `PDSeparation.tint_transform` now wraps the scaling in `numpy.float32`; `PDDeviceN` gained a dedicated `to_rgb_image` mirroring `toRGBWithTintTransform(WritableRaster)` (truncate in float) instead of delegating to the generic rounding base path. New `oracle/probes/ColorImageRgbProbe.java` + `test_color_image_rgb_oracle.py` (Indexed raster confirmed already at parity).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/color/PDSeparation.java`, `PDDeviceN.java`
  reason: raster tint scaling/truncation must be 32-bit float to match upstream's `float` arithmetic exactly.
- **FIX — `HorizontalMetricsTable` carried non-upstream defensive guards.** `get_advance_width`/`get_left_side_bearing` had a trailing-LSB bounds check + `gid<0` short-circuit (returning 250/0). Upstream indexes the arrays unchecked and throws `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException` for an out-of-range GID. Removed the guards so an out-of-range/negative GID raises `IndexError` (mirroring the Java throw); two hand-written regression tests retargeted to the upstream behaviour. New `oracle/probes/HmtxLsbProbe.java` + `test_hmtx_lsb_oracle.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `fontbox/ttf/HorizontalMetricsTable.java`
  reason: out-of-range GID lookups must throw (not silently clamp), matching upstream's unchecked array access.
- **FIX — `PDExtendedGraphicsState.copy_into_graphics_state` silently dropped `/CA`/`/ca`/`/OP`/`/op` and stored `/RI` as a raw string.** The `gs` ExtGState→PDGraphicsState merge targeted setter names (`set_alpha_constants`/`set_non_stroke_alpha_constants`/`set_stroking_overprint_control`/`set_non_stroking_overprint_control`) that do not exist on `PDGraphicsState` (whose actual methods are `set_alpha_constant`/`set_non_stroke_alpha_constant`/`set_overprint`/`set_non_stroking_overprint`), so stroking/non-stroking alpha + overprint were silently lost; `/RI` was copied via `get_rendering_intent()` (raw string) instead of the typed `RenderingIntent` enum. Both arms aligned to upstream; 3 hand-written pins retargeted. New `oracle/probes/GraphicsStateApplyProbe.java` + `test_graphics_state_apply_oracle.py`.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdmodel/graphics/state/PDExtendedGraphicsState.java` (`copyIntoGraphicsState`)
  reason: every mergeable ExtGState parameter must land on the graphics state with the upstream setter and the typed enum for `/RI`.
- **FIX — sub-byte TIFF `/Predictor 2` un-filtering zeroed padding bits + lacked the bpc==1/colors==1 path.** PDFBox's `Predictor.decodePredictorRow` for TIFF predictor 2 rewrites each sample window in place (`getBitSeq`/`calcSetBitSeq`), leaving trailing padding bits of a non-byte-aligned row untouched, and has a dedicated `bpc==1 && colors==1` whole-buffer bit walk. pypdfbox rebuilt each row from a zeroed buffer (zeroing padding) and had no 1-bit/1-colour path, diverging byte-for-byte on every non-byte-aligned sub-byte row. Rewrote `_untiff_bits` to port the in-place loop verbatim, added `_untiff_1bit_1color`, and rewrote the encode inverses so streams round-trip. New `oracle/probes/PredictorProbe.java` + `test_predictor_subbyte_oracle.py` (52 cases).
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `filter/Predictor.java` (`decodePredictorRow`)
  reason: predictor un-filtering must preserve non-byte-aligned padding bits exactly as upstream does.
- ExtGState `gs` external-signing `/ByteRange` byte-offset arithmetic + `/Contents` placeholder bracketing on `save_incremental_for_external_signing` confirmed at parity (computed ByteRange parsed identically by PDFBox; signed-content length and `<…>` delimiter bracketing match). New `oracle/probes/ByteRangeProbe.java` + `test_byte_range_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Classic xref free-list (`f` entry) + generation-number reuse across a `/Prev` chain confirmed at parity: the object pool is keyed by `(number, generation)`, a freed gen-0 object coexists with a reused gen-1, and `6 1 R` resolves to the rev2 marker (matching PDFBox 3.0.7 — pages=1, object_count=7, identical text). New `oracle/probes/XrefFreeListProbe.java` + `test_xref_free_list_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Sub-byte (1/2/4 bpc) image-XObject `get_image()` decode confirmed at parity (DeviceGray ramps with per-row padding, `/Decode` inversion, Indexed palette lookup + index reversal) within MAD<6/MAXDIFF<60. New `test_sub_byte_image_oracle.py` (reuses `ImageExtractProbe`). No production change.
- Built-in Symbol/ZapfDingbats encoding on non-embedded Standard-14 fonts confirmed at parity: code→glyph-name (`getEncoding().getName`) and code→Unicode (`toUnicode`) over codes 0..255, including the Zapf U+27xx glyph-list-vs-AGL selection (Symbol 65→Alpha→U+0391, ZapfDingbats 65→a10→U+2721, 225→a172→U+27A1). New `oracle/probes/SymbolEncodingProbe.java` + `test_symbol_encoding_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Full-save object-number assignment order on a fresh in-memory `PDDocument` confirmed at parity: catalog=1, page-tree root=2, page leaves=3..N+2 (add order), identical to PDFBox 3.0.7 when both save uncompressed (probe forces `CompressParameters.NO_COMPRESSION` to isolate the numbering surface from PDFBox's default ObjStm/XRef-stream packing — an already-documented intentional divergence). New `oracle/probes/SaveObjectOrderProbe.java` + `test_save_object_order_oracle.py`. No production change.
- PDFTextStripper word/line/page separator placement confirmed byte-identical (sentinel-token probe); paragraph-token placement pinned as a strict `xfail` (upstream `isParagraphSeparation` indent/hanging-indent detection is a deferred layout feature). New `oracle/probes/TextSeparatorProbe.java` + `test_text_separator_oracle.py`. No production change.

## Wave 1455 — content-stream hex-string round-trip (1 bug fixed) + 4 surfaces at parity

- **FIX — `PDFStreamParser` force-hex flag corrupted content-stream string round-trip.** `COSParser._read_cos_hex_string` (shared base) calls `s.set_force_hex_form(True)` — correct and intentional for the *document* writer round-trip (pinned by `test_cos_parser_wave554`/`wave584`), but upstream `BaseParser.parseCOSString` on the *content-stream* path never calls `setForceHexForm` (it is invoked nowhere in PDFBox 3.0.7 main source). pypdfbox therefore re-emitted an ASCII string parsed from a content stream as hex `<…>` where Apache PDFBox's `ContentStreamWriter` emits literal `(…)` (escaping `(`/`)`/`\`), diverging on 3 of 10 page fixtures (any content stream storing UTF-16BE text as hex-string operands). `PDFStreamParser` now overrides `_read_cos_hex_string` to build the `COSString` without setting force-hex, matching upstream's content-stream behaviour; the document parser's force-hex round-trip preservation is untouched. New `oracle/probes/ContentStreamWriterProbe.java` + `test_content_stream_writer_oracle.py` (13 cases); broader regression 4771 passed / 22 skipped across contentstream + pdfparser + pdfwriter.
  upstream: PDFBox 3.0.7 `pdfparser/PDFStreamParser.java`, `pdfwriter/ContentStreamWriter.java`
  reason: content-stream string operands must carry the default `forceHexForm=false` so the writer prefers literal form for ASCII, as upstream does.
- ExtGState `/SMask` soft-mask with a mask transparency-group `/BBox` smaller than / offset from the masked-paint region (PDF 32000-1 §11.6.5.2-3) confirmed at parity (MAD 0.00 / MAXDIFF 0) across `/Luminosity` (`/BC` white/black/grey, origin-narrow & offset-narrow bboxes) and `/Alpha`: outside the mask group's bbox the mask alpha is 0 (coverage-modulated) regardless of `/BC` — a white-`/BC` backdrop does NOT bleed across the device area to open the masked paint everywhere. New `oracle/probes/SoftMaskBBoxProbe.java` + `test_soft_mask_bbox_oracle.py` (7 cases). No production change.
- FontBox `Type2CharStringParser` token stream confirmed byte-exact vs PDFBox 3.0.7 across all operand encodings (1-byte 32–246, 2-byte 247–254, `28` short-int, `255` 16.16 fixed), `callsubr` inline unrolling with bias + trailing-RET trim, and the stem-count→`getMaskLength` byte-skip for `hintmask`/`cntrmask`. Pins the parser's intermediate token stream (upstream-distinct from the existing glyph-path / advance-width oracles where a decoder slip can cancel out invisibly). New `oracle/probes/CffType2ParseProbe.java` + `test_cff_type2_parse_oracle.py` + synthetic `type2_parse.cff` fixture. No production change.
- Widget `/MK` appearance-characteristics read path confirmed at parity: `/RC` rollover caption, `/AC` alternate caption, the colour-arity dispatch in `get_border_colour()`/`get_background()` (1/3/4 components → DeviceGray/RGB/CMYK; arity-2 and empty arrays → `None`, matching upstream's private `getColor(COSName)`), and `/R` rotation. Fixture authored in Apache PDFBox so the differential is purely pypdfbox's read path. New `oracle/probes/WidgetMkProbe.java` + `test_widget_mk_oracle.py` (3 cases). No production change.
- Object-stream (`/Type /ObjStm`) internal parsing confirmed at parity: `PDFObjectStreamParser.read_object_numbers` (`/N`/`/First` header offset-table decode) and `parse_all_objects` (offset-sorted body walk + PDFBOX-4927 duplicate-object-number collapse) across in-order pairs, out-of-order offsets, duplicate numbers, and `/First` whitespace padding. Comparison is on content (dict-equality) not iteration order — upstream `readObjectNumbers`/`parseAllObjects` both return unordered `HashMap`; the internal offset `TreeMap` is only a walk aid and not a behavioural contract. New `oracle/probes/ObjStmParseProbe.java` + `test_obj_stream_parse_oracle.py` (8 cases). No production change.

## Wave 1454 — Type3 displacement matrix translation + catalog OpenAction dispatch (2 bugs fixed) + 4 surfaces at parity

- **FIX — `PDType3Font.get_displacement` ignored `/FontMatrix` translation.** Upstream `PDType3Font.getDisplacement` calls `Matrix.transform(Vector(width, 0))` which folds the matrix translation `(e, f)` into the result — for a 6-element `[a b c d e f]` and width `x` the displacement is `(a*x + e, b*x + f)`. pypdfbox previously returned `(a*x, b*x)`, dropping translation, which corrupts glyph positioning for any Type3 font whose `/FontMatrix` carries a non-zero `e`/`f`. Now mirrors upstream byte-for-byte. Extended `oracle/probes/Type3FontProbe.java` with `BBOX`/`DISP` emissions; new `test_type3_displacement_with_translating_matrix` oracle case (e=0.5, f=0.25 pins the parity).
- **FIX — `PDDocumentCatalog.get_open_action` accepted invalid `/OpenAction` shorthands.** Upstream `PDDocumentCatalog.getOpenAction()` switches on `COSDictionary`/`COSArray` only — names/strings are rejected, and a `/OpenAction` dict that lacks `/S` returns `null` even if `/D` is present (PDActionFactory rejects). pypdfbox previously delegated to the looser `PDDestinationOrAction.create` factory which (a) treated `COSName`/`COSString` as a `PDNamedDestination` shorthand and (b) treated `/D`-only dicts as implicit `PDActionGoTo`. Both arms now return `None` at the catalog boundary, matching upstream. The looser factory remains available for callers that want the legacy shorthand. New `oracle/probes/ViewerPrefsAndOpenActionProbe.java` + `test_viewer_prefs_extra_oracle.py` (27 cases) cover the 4 boundary getters (`/ViewArea`/`/ViewClip`/`/PrintArea`/`/PrintClip` — default `CropBox`), `/PickTrayByPDFSize`/`/NumCopies`/`/PrintPageRange`/`/Enforce`, and open-action subtype dispatch across XYZ/Fit/GoTo/URI/dict-without-S/COSName/COSString.
- Page-tree attribute inheritance (PDF §7.7.3.4) confirmed at parity across 7 spec cases: `/MediaBox` inherited from root `/Pages`, intermediate override, US-Letter default when absent everywhere, `/CropBox` defaults to resolved `/MediaBox`, `/Rotate` sanitisation (multiples-of-90 normalised via `((angle%360)+360)%360`), `/Resources` inheritance from root, negative case (sibling does not bleed). New `oracle/probes/PageInheritanceProbe.java` + 7 focused tests added to `test_page_inheritance_oracle.py`. Investigation surfaced a real but high-blast-radius divergence: `PDPage.get_resources` returns an empty `PDResources` wrapper while upstream returns `None` when no ancestor carries `/Resources` (50+ test call-sites depend on the empty-bag fallback). Deferred to a dedicated sweep wave; tracked in `DEFERRED.md`.
- PDF §12.3.2.2 destination types confirmed at parity across all 8 explicit array syntaxes (`/XYZ`, `/Fit`, `/FitB`, `/FitH`, `/FitBH`, `/FitV`, `/FitBV`, `/FitR`) and both name-resolution surfaces (modern `/Names /Dests` name-tree + legacy `/Dests` catalog dict). `PDDestination.create` dispatches to the correct subclass and `get_left()/get_top()/get_right()/get_bottom()/get_zoom()` return identical numeric values, with the XYZ-null "retain current viewer value" sentinel normalised between PDFBox's `-1` int and pypdfbox's `None`. New `oracle/probes/DestinationTypesProbe.java` + `test_destination_types_oracle.py` (9 cases).
- Document outline (PDF §12.3.3) confirmed at parity across 5 differential cases: `add_last` + `open_node` `/Count` propagation into open AND closed parents (signed magnitude = subtree size); destination resolution across explicit `/Dest`, `/Names/Dests`-named `/Dest`, `/A GoTo` with explicit dest, `/A GoTo` with named-string dest, and the no-dest orphan; title decoding (PDFDocEncoding vs UTF-16BE) on ASCII/Latin/Greek/CJK/control/empty/quote+backslash; malformed cyclic `/Next` chain terminates via visited-set guard; catalog without `/Outlines` emits JSON `null`. New `oracle/probes/OutlineTreeProbe.java` + `test_outline_tree_oracle.py`. No production change.
- Embedded-file attachments (PDF §7.11.4 + §14.13) confirmed at parity across 3 multi-attachment cases: catalog `/Names /EmbeddedFiles` name tree sorted by name, dual `/EF/F` + `/EF/UF` streams on the same filespec, non-ASCII `/UF` filenames, `/Desc` round-trip, `/AFRelationship` enum round-trip, Flate-compressed streams decoded byte-identically, kid-tree structural shape (leaf count / kid count / total names) preserved with explicit `/Kids`, and catalog `/AF` array linkage. New `oracle/probes/EmbeddedFilesMultiProbe.java` + `test_embedded_files_multi_oracle.py` (5 cases). No production change.

## Wave 1453 — exp-notation reals in arrays (1 bug fixed) + xref-stream `/W` field-width parsing + JPX `/SMaskInData` confirmed at parity

- **Exponential-notation real numbers inside arrays raised `PDFParseError` where PDFBox tolerates them (1 bug FIXED).** Found by the new `ExpNotationProbe` differential. PDF 32000-1 §7.3.3 defines real numbers as `[+-]?(\d+|\d*\.\d+|\d+\.\d*)` — exponential notation (`1e3` / `1.5e-2`) is OUT OF SPEC, but real-world PDF writers emit it. PDFBox's two number-parser branches diverge here in a way pypdfbox must mirror: (a) `PDFStreamParser.parseNextToken` inlines its own number reader that rejects `e`/`E` — `1.5e2 Tf` at the TOP LEVEL of a content stream tokenises as `1.5(COSFloat), e(operator), 2(COSInteger), Tf(operator)` on both engines (the operator chain downstream is garbage, but no throw); (b) `BaseParser.parseCOSNumber` (called by `parseDirectObject` for numbers inside arrays / dictionaries) ACCEPTS `e`/`E` plus an optional exponent sign — `[-1.5e3] TJ` parses cleanly as `[COSFloat(-1500.0)] TJ`. Pypdfbox's `pypdfbox/pdfparser/base_parser.py::read_number` matched only the spec grammar (no exp marker), so an exp-notation real INSIDE an array raised `PDFParseError("unexpected byte 0x65 ('e')")` from `COSParser.parse_direct_object`; `PDFStreamParser` then caught the array failure and closed the source, leaving the WHOLE token stream EMPTY. Fix: `read_number` now accepts `e`/`E` followed by an optional `+`/`-` sign and strips a trailing `e`/`E` with no following exponent digit (rewinding the source) — byte-for-byte mirror of PDFBox's accept set (verified via `javap` against the pinned 3.0.7 jar — bytecode offsets 21-55 for the accept set, 95-149 for the trailing-strip-and-rewind). The top-level `PDFStreamParser._parse_number_token` is unchanged (it already matches PDFBox's strict top-level reader). 5 cases (clean control, top-level positive exp, top-level negative exp, in-array exp, in-array mixed-case exp) all pin token-sequence + page-count + extracted-text byte-equal between pypdfbox and PDFBox 3.0.7. New probe `oracle/probes/ExpNotationProbe.java`; new tests `tests/pdfparser/oracle/test_exp_notation_oracle.py`.



- JPX `/SMaskInData` (PDF 32000-1 §8.9.7.5 Table 89) accessor + dict-level read confirmed at parity with the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle across every legal value the spec defines. Per §8.9.7.5, `/SMaskInData` is a JPX-only optional integer hint to the decoder (0 = no embedded SMask information / default; 1 = the JPX stream carries premultiplied colour samples + alpha; 2 = the JPX stream carries an opacity / matte channel used as a soft mask). PDFBox 3.0.7's `PDImageXObject` has NO public `getSMaskInData()` accessor (added on a later upstream branch; 3.0.7 reads the entry inline inside `getImage()` to forward via `DecodeResult.getJPXSMask()`), so the differential target is PDFBox's raw `image.getCOSObject().getInt(COSName.getPDFName("SMaskInData"), 0)`. pypdfbox's forward-compat `PDImageXObject.get_smask_in_data()` / `set_smask_in_data()` (which already existed before this wave — `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/pd_image_x_object.py:461`) match the same lookup byte-for-byte across `absent` → 0 (the spec default), explicit `0`, `1` (premultiplied), and `2` (matte); the setter's `[0, 1, 2]` legal-value guard mirrors PDF 32000-1 Table 89's enumeration. Render parity is intentionally out of scope: the pinned `pdfbox-app-3.0.7.jar` registers no JPEG 2000 `ImageReader` (verified — the new probe emits `grid none`), so there is no Java raster to differential against, and modes 1/2 require a decoder that understands embedded alpha which neither side ships. Accessor parity confirms the pypdfbox forward-compat getter is COS-equivalent to upstream's inline read. New probe `oracle/probes/SMaskInDataProbe.java` (emits `smid page <p> name <n> w <w> h <h> bpc <bpc> accessor <int> raw <int> filter <name-or-[arr]-or-null> grid <none-or-256-ints>`); new tests `tests/pdmodel/graphics/image/oracle/test_smask_in_data_oracle.py` (5 cases — 4 parametrised legal values absent / 0 / 1 / 2 plus a dedicated explicit-0 round-trip pin proving the entry survives save/load with its physical presence preserved as a regression guard against a future "drop default 0 on write" optimisation). Each fixture authors a one-page JPX image XObject (Pillow / OpenJPEG-encoded `DeviceRGB` 32×24 gradient) and asserts pypdfbox's `get_smask_in_data()` bit-equals PDFBox 3.0.7's raw COS read. No production code change required — surface confirmed at parity.

- Xref-stream `/W` field-width parsing per PDF 32000-1 §7.5.8.3 confirmed at parity with the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle across every width tuple a real-world producer emits. Every record in a cross-reference stream has three fields (type / offset-or-objnum / gen-or-index) whose byte widths are declared via the stream-dictionary's `/W` array, and PDFBox tolerates any reasonable combination — pypdfbox must match across the whole range. `pypdfbox/pdfparser/pdf_xref_stream_parser.py::parse_value` (the big-endian unpacker `value += (data[i+start] & 0xFF) << ((length - i - 1) * 8)`, Python's unbounded-int shifts naturally handling the 56-bit case) is byte-for-byte equivalent to the verified 3.0.7 bytecode across `[1 2 1]` (compact — small-file shape PDFBox emits for documents under ~64 KiB; free-list-head gen clamped to 0xFF because W2=1 only fits 8 bits), `[1 4 2]` (standard — PDFBox's default), and `[1 8 2]` (large-file — 8-byte offset required when any offset exceeds 2^32; exercises the parser's 56-bit shift arithmetic end-to-end even when the high 4 bytes are zero — a small-int truncation bug like `int.from_bytes(buf[:4], "big")` or a fixed-width shift mask would mis-align the per-record byte accounting and surface as wrong page count / wrong xref-table size / unreadable text). Three byte-level fixtures hand-authored (catalog + pages + page + content stream + Helvetica font + self-referenced xref stream, `/Index [0 7]`, zlib-deflated record block); each width tuple round-trips `pages` / `object_count` / `text` byte-equal between pypdfbox and PDFBox. New probe `oracle/probes/XrefWFieldsProbe.java` (`facts` mode — emits `pages` / `object_count` / `text` for the loaded fixture); new tests `tests/pdfparser/oracle/test_xref_w_fields_oracle.py` (4 cases — 3 parametrised width tuples + 1 dedicated `[1 8 2]` page-text pin). No production code change required — surface confirmed at parity. The `[1 4 2]` standard tuple is also exercised by `test_xref_chain_oracle.py` (wave 1450, as one column in a mixed-form xref-chain walk); this wave's dedicated three-width sweep is the headline pin for the compact and large arms.

- Multiple sequential incremental saves (PDF 32000-1 §7.5.6) confirmed at parity. `pypdfbox/pdfwriter/cos_writer.py::_do_write_increment` + `_do_write_trailer_increment` correctly compose a chain of `PDDocument.save_incremental` calls — each appended trailer's `/Prev` targets the previous revision's `startxref`, every earlier revision's bytes survive verbatim, PDFBox walks the chain backwards to recover the LATEST `/Info` values, qpdf-validity holds at every intermediate revision. New probe `oracle/probes/IncrementalChainProbe.java`; new tests `tests/pdfwriter/oracle/test_incremental_chain_oracle.py` (6 cases). No production code change required.
- Standard-14 `PDType1Font(Helvetica)` with explicit `/Encoding /MacRomanEncoding` override (PDF §9.6.2.4) confirmed at parity. The AFM-fallback width path (wave 1431) uses the MacRoman name not the WinAnsi default on every lookup: 0xA5→`bullet`/350 not `yen`/556; 0xAA→`trademark`/1000 not `ordfeminine`; the full 23-code MR-vs-WA divergent set matches PDFBox bit-equal, and `PDFTextStripper` extracts the MacRoman→AGL→Unicode chain identically. New probe `oracle/probes/MacRomanStd14Probe.java`; new tests `tests/pdmodel/font/oracle/test_macroman_std14_oracle.py`. No production code change required.
- OCG `/AS` auto-state (PDF §8.11.4.4) confirmed at parity. `PDOptionalContentConfiguration.get_as_array` reads + `PDOptionalContentProperties.compute_visible_ocgs(destination="View")` category-driven override of `/BaseState` match PDFBox 3.0.7 on `/AS` array length, per-entry `/Event` + `/Category` + `/OCGs` count. New probe `oracle/probes/UsageAsProbe.java`; new tests `tests/pdmodel/graphics/optionalcontent/oracle/test_usage_as_oracle.py` (7 cases). No production code change required.

## Wave 1452 — linearization hint-table decoder (2 bugs fixed) + 5 surfaces at parity

- **Linearization Page-Offset hint table decoded incorrectly (2 bugs FIXED).** Found by the new `HintTableProbe` differential against qpdf's `--show-linearization` reference reader (PDFBox 3.0.7 ships no hint-table decoder, so qpdf 12.3.2 is the cross-tool oracle). `pypdfbox/pdfparser/linearization_hint_table.py`: (1) `parse_page_offset_hint_header` read a 32-byte fixed header, but PDF 32000-1 Table F.3 specifies **36 bytes** — Items 12 + 13 (`bits_for_fraction_numerator` u16 at offset 32 and `fraction_denominator` u16 at offset 34) are part of the fixed header, not an afterword. The old decoder bled those 4 bytes into the per-page bit stream, garbling every delta. Now reads the full 36-byte header. (2) `parse_page_offset_hint_table` decoded the per-page block ROW-major (all 5 fields of page 0, then page 1, …) but §F.3 + qpdf's `readHPageOffset` specify **COLUMN-major with `skipToNextByte` between columns**: all `delta_nobjects`, then all `delta_page_length`, then all `nshared_objects`, then per-page shared identifiers + numerators, then `delta_content_offset`s, then `delta_content_length`s — each section byte-aligned at its tail. Same column-major fix applied to `parse_shared_object_hint_table` (§F.4 / qpdf `readHSharedObject`). Per-page hint values now match qpdf's reference output byte-exact. 2 stale round-trip tests updated. Pinned in `tests/pdfparser/oracle/test_hint_table_oracle.py`.

- AES-256 `/EncryptMetadata` WIRE-BYTES facet confirmed at parity with the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle. Per PDF 32000-1 §7.6.4.4.1, when the `/Encrypt` dictionary carries `/EncryptMetadata false`, the catalog's `/Metadata` XMP stream MUST be left CLEARTEXT on the wire (the bytes between `stream\n` and `\nendstream` are the literal XMP packet, no AES applied — search-engine-indexable encrypted PDF pattern). Wave 1427's `test_crypt_filter_oracle.py` exercised the *decoded* metadata payload via `PDMetadata.exportXMPMetadata`; this wave's `EncryptMetadataWireProbe` reads the raw file bytes and isolates the on-disk stream payload directly, bypassing PDFBox's automatic stream-decrypt pass to SEE whether the writer enciphered the metadata or not. `pypdfbox/pdfwriter/cos_writer.py::visit_from_stream` (wave 1367 `skip_metadata_encrypt` branch — exempts `/Type /Metadata` streams from encryption when the active handler's `is_encrypt_metadata()` is False) and `pypdfbox/cos/cos_stream.py::set_security_handler` (symmetric read-side exemption — leaves the on-disk metadata bytes alone when the handler reports `/EncryptMetadata=false`) are byte-for-byte correct: the first 32 wire bytes of an AES-256 `/EncryptMetadata false` document start with `<?xpacket` (0x3c), the entire XMP packet appears verbatim on disk, and PDFBox confirms `isEncryptMetaData()==false`. The symmetric default case (`/EncryptMetadata true` — pypdfbox writes) enciphers the metadata under AES-CBC so the on-disk prefix does not look like XML and the XMP packet does not appear verbatim. PDFBox 3.0.7's `StandardProtectionPolicy` has no `setEncryptMetadata`, so the WRITE side always emits R6 with metadata enciphered; pypdfbox's R6 reader recovers the original XMP byte-for-byte. New probe `oracle/probes/EncryptMetadataWireProbe.java` (`inspect` — emits `ENCRYPT_METADATA:<true|false|absent>` + `METADATA_OBJ` + `METADATA_RAW_LEN` + first-32-byte `METADATA_RAW_HEX` + `METADATA_LOOKS_XML` heuristic, by locating the catalog `/Metadata` object's number via PDFBox then scanning the raw file bytes for the `<N> <G> obj ... stream ... endstream` block); new tests `tests/pdmodel/encryption/oracle/test_encrypt_metadata_wire_oracle.py` (3 cases — pypdfbox `/EncryptMetadata false` leaves XMP cleartext on disk, pypdfbox `/EncryptMetadata true` enciphers under AES, PDFBox-encrypted R6 default-metadata file enciphered on the wire + pypdfbox recovers the original XMP via its R6 decrypt path). No production code change required — wave 1367's writer + reader exemptions are correct on the wire.
- Page `/Trans` transition dictionary (PDF 32000-1 §12.4.4.1) confirmed at parity with the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle across the full `PDTransition` accessor surface. `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/pagenavigation/pd_transition.py` is byte-for-byte equivalent to the verified 3.0.7 bytecode (`getStyle` defaults to `R`, `getDimension` to `H`, `getMotion` to `I`, `getDuration` and `getFlyScale` to `1`, `isFlyAreaOpaque` to `false`; `getDirection` returns the raw `COSBase` with `COSInteger.ZERO` as the absent default; `setDirection` writes the integer degrees or `COSName.NONE` sentinel). Six-page fixture exercises Split-H-inward, Blinds-V, Dissolve+/D 2.5, Wipe+/Di 90, Fly+/SS 1.5+/B true (the only style that honours both), and the no-`/Trans` default page; canonical per-page accessor report agrees line-for-line. New probe `oracle/probes/PageTransProbe.java` (emits `page <i> trans <yes|no>` + optional `page <i> S <style> D <dur> Dm <dim> M <motion> Di <degrees|None> SS <scale> B <true|false>`); new tests `tests/pdmodel/oracle/test_page_trans_oracle.py` (16 cases — canonical-report equality, all 12 spec styles round-trip, absent-dict defaults, `/None` direction-sentinel round-trip, `PDPage.set_transition` / `clear_transition` round-trip). No production code change required — surface confirmed at parity.
- Page `/Rotate` value normalisation (PDF 32000-1 §14.8.4) confirmed at parity with the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle on every interesting out-of-spec bucket. Wave 1430's `test_page_inheritance_oracle.py` already covered a single `/Rotate -90` field as one column in a multi-attribute walk; this wave dedicates a probe + test pair to the full normalisation sweep. New probe `oracle/probes/RotateNormProbe.java` (emits `page <i> rotate <PDPage.getRotation()>` per page). New tests `tests/pdmodel/oracle/test_rotate_norm_oracle.py` (2 cases: canonical-report equality + per-row decomposition naming the exact malformed input on regression). Raw `/Rotate` inputs and their expected normalised return are `-90 → 270` (negative-mod-360 wraparound), `450 → 90` (value above 360), `720 → 0` (exact multiple of 360 collapses), `45 → 0` (non-multiple-of-90 rejected per the upstream `intValue % 90 == 0` gate; pypdfbox snaps to `0` matching `((v % 90) != 0) ? 0` upstream), `-180 → 180` (the `+360` arm of the wrap-positive idiom). `pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_page.py::PDPage.get_rotation` is byte-for-byte equivalent to the verified PDFBox 3.0.7 bytecode (`((v % 360) + 360) % 360` with the `% 90 != 0 → 0` gate) so no production code change was required. The negative-mod-360 + the non-multiple-of-90 cases are the high-value pins (Python's `%` already returns a positive result for negative operands so the C/Java `+360` correction is a no-op in pypdfbox — but matching the *value* still requires the multiple-of-90 gate to be in place, and it is).

- Parser leniency on the `N G obj ... endobj` envelope confirmed at parity: extra whitespace + EOL between header and dict, `%`-comment inside object, missing `endobj` (next `n g obj` arrives directly), extra CRLF + garbage bytes between `endobj` and next object — pypdfbox's `BaseParser.skip_whitespace` treats `%` as whitespace and `COSParser.bf_search_for_objects` rescues every mutation that shifts the next object's byte offset. 5 cases match PDFBox's `pages/objects/root/info/text` fingerprint exactly. New probe `oracle/probes/ObjHeaderLenientProbe.java`; new tests `tests/pdfparser/oracle/test_obj_header_lenient_oracle.py`.
- Annotation appearance-stream marked-content `/OC` suppression confirmed at parity. Wave 1430 wired `BMC`/`BDC`/`EMC` dispatch into the page content-stream dispatch table; this verifies the same dispatch path fires inside `_process_form_bytes` (which `_render_annotation` calls to walk an appearance stream) — `BDC /OC <prop>` resolves against the appearance's own `/Resources`, so an OC-marked appearance block is suppressed identically to an OC-marked page block. New tests `tests/rendering/oracle/test_annot_appearance_oc_oracle.py` (3 cases). No production code change required.
- Non-separable HSL blend modes (Hue / Saturation / Color / Luminosity, PDF 32000-1 §11.3.5) confirmed at parity vs the live oracle. The HSL decomposition + per-component blend in `pypdfbox/rendering/` matches PDFBox exactly within the gate. New tests `tests/rendering/oracle/test_nonseparable_blend_oracle.py`.

## Wave 1451 — /StrF default-Identity crypt-filter routing (1 bug fixed)

- `/Info` `/Producer` + `/ModDate` + `/CreationDate` behaviour across `PDDocument.save` confirmed at parity with the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle — neither library auto-stamps a vendor `/Producer` or refreshes `/ModDate` to "now" on a clean save. `PDDocument.save` (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/pd_document.py`) + `COSWriter.write` (`pypdfbox/pdfwriter/cos_writer.py`) are faithful serialisers of whatever the in-memory `/Info` dict carries, matching PDFBox 3.0.7's contract verbatim. New probe `oracle/probes/ProducerSaveProbe.java` (modes `empty` / `resave` / `mutate`); new tests `tests/pdfwriter/oracle/test_producer_oracle.py` (3 cases — fresh `PDDocument()` + save → empty `/Info` on both sides; load + save without mutation → every standard entry round-trips byte-identical; load + `set_author` + save → new `/Author` lands while pre-existing `/Producer` / `/ModDate` / `/CreationDate` stay untouched). Dates compared as UTC epoch milliseconds so the assertion is Calendar/timezone repr-independent. No production code change required.
- **`/StrF` (and symmetrically `/StmF`) defaults to `/Identity` when absent — pypdfbox was mis-enciphering the Identity-defaulted slot under the document-level AES cipher (FIXED).** `pypdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/standard_security_handler.py::_populate_routing_table`. Per PDF 32000-1 §7.6.4.4 Table 20, when a V=4/V=5 `/Encrypt` dictionary declares `/CF /StdCF` + `/StmF /StdCF` but omits `/StrF` (or vice versa), the absent slot must default to `/Identity` (cleartext pass-through). PDFBox 3.0.7's `PDEncryption.getStringFilterName` coerces an absent `/StrF` to `IDENTITY` (verified against the 3.0.7 bytecode — the getter substitutes Identity, then `SecurityHandler.decryptString` short-circuits on `IDENTITY.equals(stringFilterName)`). pypdfbox's routing table built the slot as `None` instead of `Identity`, which caused `decrypt_string` / `encrypt_string` to fall back to the legacy single-algorithm path inherited from `SecurityHandler` and dispatch AES under `self._aes=True`. Net effect: pypdfbox encrypted strings even when `/StrF` was absent on the wire, so PDFBox (which honours the Identity default) couldn't recover the string. `_populate_routing_table` now substitutes the Identity CFM for an absent `/StmF` or `/StrF` whenever per-object routing is in effect (i.e. `/CF` is present OR an explicit `/StmF` / `/StrF` is set) — handlers built ad-hoc without `/CF` and without either slot still fall back to the legacy single-algorithm path so the existing handler-only round-trip tests keep their semantics. Found wave 1451 by the new `StrFDefaultProbe` oracle differential. New probe `oracle/probes/StrFDefaultProbe.java` (`encrypt-strf-absent` / `inspect`); new tests `tests/pdmodel/encryption/oracle/test_strf_default_oracle.py` (6 cases — pypdfbox writes both `/StrF`-absent and `/StmF`-absent files for AES-128 + AES-256, PDFBox reads each back to `STMF:StdCF / STRF:Identity` (and the symmetric pair) and recovers the marker bytes byte-equal to pypdfbox; two read-side routing-table unit pins verify `/EFF` follows the absent `/StmF` to Identity per §7.6.5).

- Five surfaces confirmed at parity (no bug): filter encode → PDFBox decode round-trip (38-case cryptographic verification across ASCIIHex / ASCII85 / Flate without predictor / Flate+Predictor=12 / RunLength / LZW); `/Filter` shape leniency (bare `COSName` vs single-element `COSArray` vs multi-name chain — all decode identically); Indexed over a non-Device base (`CalRGB` / `CalGray` / `ICCBased` — base routing, per-entry slicing, index clamp all correct, residual Cal-base CMM divergence inherited from the established `test_cal_color_oracle.py` D65-vs-D50 PCS gap); `/Producer` + `/ModDate` + `/CreationDate` on save (PDFBox 3.0.7 does NOT auto-stamp a vendor `/Producer` or refresh `/ModDate` to "now" — both libraries are faithful serialisers; the task-brief premise was a misconception about PDFBox's behaviour); `PDPushButton` field + `/MK` captions (`isPushButton` `/Ff` bit + click-only empty `/V` + `/CA`/`/RC`/`/AC` accessors all match upstream).

## Wave 1450 — 6 surfaces confirmed at parity (0 bugs — parity asymptote signal)

- All six fresh surfaces probed this wave returned clean against the live PDFBox 3.0.7 oracle, with no production code change required. This is the first **all-parity** wave of the campaign — a meaningful indicator that the oracle-reachable surface is approaching the parity asymptote.
- Surfaces (no bug): rotated/sheared text via `Tm` (45°/90°/shear matrices apply to glyph outline AND advance); outline `/A` action vs `/Dest` destination accessor independence when both are present; cross-reference STREAM with `/Prev` chained to a classic xref+trailer of an earlier revision (PDF §7.5.8.3 mixed-form walk); CFF Top DICT metadata strings (`/version`/`/Notice`/`/Copyright`/`/FullName`/`/FamilyName`/`/Weight` resolved via predefined SIDs 0..390 + per-font String INDEX); line-dash `phase` operand of `d` (skia `DashPathEffect` honours the offset); page `/Thumb` thumbnail accessor (`get_thumb()` wraps as `PDImageXObject` with matching dims/bpc/colour-space, absent `/Thumb` → `None`). New probes `OutlineActionDest`, `XrefChain`, `CffMetadata`, `PageThumb` (rotated-text + dash-phase reuse `RenderProbe`); 23 oracle tests; 3 synthetic CFF fixtures + generator.

## Wave 1449 — TrueType `name` table Macintosh decode → ISO-8859-1 (1 bug fixed)

- **TrueType `name` table Macintosh platform decoded with `mac_roman`; upstream uses `ISO_8859_1` (FIXED).** `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/naming_table.py::get_charset` and `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/true_type_font.py::get_naming`. Upstream PDFBox 3.0.7 `NamingTable.getCharset` (NamingTable.java) handles platform=3 (Windows enc 0/1/10) → UTF-16, platform=0 (Unicode) → UTF-16, platform=2 (ISO) enc 0/1 → US_ASCII / UTF-16BE, and **every other case — including platform=1 (Macintosh) — defaults to `StandardCharsets.ISO_8859_1`**. pypdfbox originally chose `mac_roman` for `(plat=1, enc=0)` and `shift_jis` for `(plat=1, enc=1)`, which is the "correct" character-set choice per the OpenType spec but diverges from PDFBox at the byte level (Mac-Roman 0xAA = `™`, Latin-1 0xAA = `ª`). Surfaces visibly in NID 10 (Description) of `LiberationSans-Regular.ttf` (`Arial™` vs `Arialª`) and in any Mac-platform record carrying 0xA0..0xFF bytes. `get_charset` now mirrors PDFBox's fall-through to ISO-8859-1; `get_naming()` (the fontTools-backed builder used by `TrueTypeFont.from_bytes`) decodes the raw byte payload from `ft_record.string` through `NamingTable.get_charset` / `_decode_string` instead of fontTools' `toUnicode()` (which dispatches MacRoman for platform=1). Behaviour is now byte-equal to PDFBox across every platform / encoding / language combination present in the bundled `LiberationSans-Regular.ttf` and `DejaVuSans.ttf` `name` tables. Found wave 1449 by the new live-oracle `NameTableProbe` differential. New probe `oracle/probes/NameTableProbe.java` (records + 4-arg priority lookup per canonical PDFBox tuple + family / sub-family / PostScript-name accessors); new tests `tests/fontbox/ttf/oracle/test_name_table_oracle.py` (3 cases — 2 parametrised parity + dedicated NID 6 PostScript-name pin, all green).
