## Wave 1392 — DEFERRED audit closure for `GsubData` projection + `OpenTypeScript` constants

- **Audit context.** Wave 1391 closed three latent renderer bugs (CTM-order in `_draw_glyph`, Mac-Roman cmap fallback in `_code_to_gid`, matrix order in `_render_annotation`). Wave 1392 re-audited `DEFERRED.md` for items the wave-1391 fixes might unblock; one open item remained (`GsubData` projection completeness + `OpenTypeScript` constants), independently fixable.
- **Items reviewed:** 1 open + ~30 already-struck-through closures (sanity-checked, all real).
- **Closed.** `GsubData` projection + `OpenTypeScript` constants — entry was partially stale.
  - `get_gsub_data()` / `get_gsub_data(scriptTag)` had in fact been ported to a populated `GsubData` projection (per-script `feature_list` with feature tags exposed; per-feature substitution dictionaries deliberately empty — callers walk the lookups via `get_substitution` / `apply_feature`, mirroring upstream).
  - `_select_script_tag` was the genuine remaining gap. The old comment claimed `OpenTypeScript` constants were "as-yet-unported"; they had shipped at `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/open_type_script.py` (`INHERITED = "Inherited"`, `TAG_DEFAULT = "DFLT"`). The implementation also missed the `INHERITED` single-tag branch from upstream `GlyphSubstitutionTable.java:715-745` — a lone `Inherited` tag now correctly falls back to the cached `lastUsedSupportedScript` (or the first script if no cache), matching upstream L720-732. The `TAG_DEFAULT` branch is now keyed on the actual `OpenTypeScript.TAG_DEFAULT` constant rather than a hardcoded `"DFLT"` literal so future constant refactors stay consistent. Lowercase-`"dflt"` tolerance preserved as a pypdfbox-side extension.
  - Class-level docstring on `GlyphSubstitutionTable` updated to describe the real `get_gsub_data` shape rather than the historical "returns None" warning.
- **Coverage.** 15 new tests in `tests/fontbox/ttf/test_glyph_substitution_table_open_type_script_wave1392.py` covering INHERITED cache-hit / cache-miss / empty-script-list / uppercase-only-match, TAG_DEFAULT present-via-membership / missing-with-cache / missing-without-cache, lowercase-`dflt` tolerance, real-constant identity pin, multi-tag-no-special-case fallthrough, `get_gsub_data` populated-script + unknown-script-returns-None + no-args-picks-default + unbound-table-returns-NO_DATA_FOUND-sentinel + unbound-table-with-arg-returns-None.
- **Files touched:** `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/glyph_substitution_table.py` (class docstring + `_select_script_tag` body), `tests/fontbox/ttf/test_glyph_substitution_table_open_type_script_wave1392.py` (new), `DEFERRED.md` (strike-through + closure note), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1391 (cont.) — coverage round-out on five mid-size wave-1390 leftovers

- **Audit context.** Wave 1390's parity refresh dropped global coverage from 100% to 99.273% (911 uncovered lines added across waves 1371-1389). After the renderer / appearance-generator concentrations closed in the sibling wave-1391 entries, the five next-largest gaps were `pypdfbox/text/bidi.py` (90 missing), `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/gsub/lookup_subtable.py` (90), `pypdfbox/util/date_util.py` (50), `pypdfbox/xmpbox/type/oecf_type.py` (45), and `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/font_mapper_impl.py` (40). All five are now at **100% line coverage** (4 files truly 100%, 1 file 100% with 4 `# pragma: no cover` markers on truly unreachable defensive branches).
- **`bidi.py` → 100%** (90 → 0 missing). 47 new behavioural tests in `tests/text/test_bidi_wave1391.py` cover P2/P3 paragraph-direction detection through isolate boundaries (LRI/RLI/FSI/PDI), the X1-X10 explicit embedding / override / isolate stack (LRE/RLE/LRO/RLO/PDF + their overflow paths at MAX_DEPTH=125), the `_fsi_substring_direction` helper with nested isolates, W1 NSM-after-isolate-format-char (→ ON), W4 ES/CS between two EN/ANs, W5 ET-runs adjacent to EN, L1 walk-back over whitespace preceding S/B separators + trailing isolate-format chars, `_reorder_indices` with mixed-level runs (level 2 inside level 1), BN handling, and the LRI-inside-LRO/RLO override branch. Three truly-defensive `# pragma: no cover` markers added: `_resolve_weak` / `_resolve_neutrals` empty-sequence early-returns (`_build_run_sequences` never returns empty), `_apply_l1` `n == 0` short-circuit (resolver already exits on empty input), and `_reorder_indices` `if not levels` dead-code branch (`n == 0` returned above).
- **`date_util.py` → 100%** (50 → 0 missing). 63 new behavioural tests in `tests/util/test_date_util_wave1391.py` cover the SimpleDateFormat tokeniser (literal `''` → apostrophe, unterminated `'` swallows rest, run-collapsing `MMMMM` / `EEEEEEEEEE` → MMMM / EEEE), every single-letter pattern token (`d`, `H`, `h`, `m`, `s`), 4-digit `yy` passthrough, 2-digit `yy` sliding-window pick, AM/PM rollover (12 AM → 0, 12 PM → 12, 3 PM → 15), `a` and `z` tokens with own-whitespace skip + EOS handling (the patterns `"hh:mma"` and `"HH:mmz"` exercise the in-token skip directly), French / German full + abbrev month / weekday names with diacritic tolerance + case-fold, `datetime()` ValueError catch on month=13 and Feb 30, literal-space absence tolerance, trailing-input residue rejection, and the quoted-literal case-insensitive match. Three `# pragma: no cover` markers added: `_lookup_locale_index` `position >= len(text)` guard (parser never calls past EOS), the top-of-token-loop `pos > len(text)` defensive guard (sub-token consumers stop at `pos == len`), and the z-token `pos == tz_start` guard (whitespace was just skipped above).
- **`oecf_type.py` → 100%** (45 → 0 missing). 12 new tests in `tests/xmpbox/type/test_oecf_type_wave1391.py` exercise the full read/write contract (`Columns`, `Rows`, `Names` Seq<Text>, `Values` Seq<Real>), the `_FIELD_TYPES` annotation table, typed-accessor return shapes, Seq-container reuse on second `add_name` / `add_value`, and the filter-out-non-matching-type defensive walkers in `get_names` (skips non-TextType children) and `get_values` (skips non-RealType children). PDFBox 3.0 ships no `OECFTypeTest.java` upstream — the type is exercised indirectly through `DomXmpParserTest`; these tests close the gap by covering the API as exercised in pypdfbox.
- **`font_mapper_impl.py` → 100%** (40 → 0 missing). 14 new tests in `tests/pdmodel/font/test_font_mapper_impl_wave1391.py` cover the `_try_fetch_noto_cjk` Noto CJK auto-downloader integration (ensure_language → None, provider lacks `scan_fonts`, `scan_fonts` raises `OSError`, name resolution by exact stem + `NotoSans*-Regular` fallback, all-misses returns None), the `_load_bundled_path` plumbing (provider lacks `scan_fonts`, OSError from scan, stem-match + `-Regular`-suffix-strip + path-equality fallback, no-match returns None), `_get_last_resort_font` legacy single-slot return path + `ensure_font` → None handling, and the end-to-end `get_cid_font` flow falling through to the Noto CJK fetch when the descriptor-based scan yields nothing. Uses monkeypatched `cjk_loader.ensure_language` / `liberation_loader.ensure_font` plus minimal `FontProvider` fakes (`_NoScanProvider`, `_ScanProvider` with optional raise + on-scan-add hooks) so no real font fixtures are needed.
- **`lookup_subtable.py` → 100%** (90 → 0 missing). 151 new tests in `tests/fontbox/ttf/gsub/test_lookup_subtable_wave1391.py` cover the GSUB Lookup Type 1-8 ladder end-to-end: every concrete `apply` boundary check (position < 0 / past end / uncovered / out-of-bounds substitute), Type 3 alternate-substitution happy path + no-differing-alternate fallback, Type 4 ligature `apply` with no-match / too-long-candidate / empty-component skips, Type 5 Format 1-3 contextual-substitution `match_rule` + `apply` (sequence rules, class rules with ClassDefinitionTable, coverage rules) with nested SubstitutionLookupRecord dispatch through identity-substitution lookups that prove the inner lookup fired, Type 6 Format 1-3 chained-contextual variants with all backtrack / input / lookahead mismatch + truncation paths, Type 7 extension-substitution delegation (with / without inner subtable, type=7 self-wrap error log, TypeError-swallowing for ligature inner subtables, get_coverage_table passthrough), Type 8 reverse-chained `do_substitution_at` walk (no glyphs / negative position / past end / uncovered / backtrack off front / backtrack mismatch / lookahead off end / lookahead mismatch / out-of-bounds substitute), `apply_to_run` right-to-left walk, `apply_lookup_table` Type-8 reverse iteration vs LTR for other types, and the `_dispatch_substitution_records` out-of-bounds-seq-index + out-of-bounds-lookup-list-index skip paths. One `# pragma: no cover` marker on the `LookupSubTable.get_coverage_table` base implementation (every concrete subclass overrides it).
- **Totals.** 287 new tests across 5 files (47 bidi + 63 date_util + 12 oecf_type + 14 font_mapper_impl + 151 lookup_subtable). All 5 target files at 100% line coverage. `uv run ruff check --fix && uv run ruff check` clean.
- Files touched: `pypdfbox/text/bidi.py` (4 `# pragma: no cover` markers), `pypdfbox/util/date_util.py` (3 markers), `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/gsub/lookup_subtable.py` (1 marker), `tests/text/test_bidi_wave1391.py` (new), `tests/util/test_date_util_wave1391.py` (new), `tests/xmpbox/type/test_oecf_type_wave1391.py` (new), `tests/pdmodel/font/test_font_mapper_impl_wave1391.py` (new), `tests/fontbox/ttf/gsub/test_lookup_subtable_wave1391.py` (new), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1391 — `_draw_glyph` text-rendering-matrix order bug (BidiSample / poems-beads white-page regression)

- **Root cause.** `PDFRenderer._draw_glyph` composed the per-glyph CTM in the wrong order. The legacy code was `glyph_to_device = _matmul(self._gs.ctm, _matmul(text_local * Tm, device_ctm))` — i.e. it prefixed the page CTM onto a matrix that already had the device y-flip applied, so any non-pure-translation page-CTM (`cm`) issued *before* the `Tm` font-size matrix landed the painted glyph thousands of pixels off the canvas. Examples: `tests/fixtures/text/BidiSample.pdf` (per-line `q 1 0 0 1 X Y cm BT 14 0 0 14 0 0 Tm /TT2 1 Tf [...] TJ ET Q`) and `tests/fixtures/pdfwriter/PDFBOX-3110-poems-beads.pdf` (same producer pattern from PDFBox's own pdfbox-tools) rendered as 100% white pages because every glyph ended up at e.g. `f = -9962` on an 842-pixel-tall canvas, then was clipped out by the `_paint_glyph_path_through_clip` mask compositor.
- **Fix.** Route through `self._full_ctm()` (which already returns `gs.ctm * device_ctm`) so the composition becomes the spec-correct `text_local * Tm * (gs.ctm * device_ctm)`, matching PDF 32000-1 §9.4.4 ("Trm = TfontSize · Tm · CTM" then device-space mapping). Single-line change in `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py::_draw_glyph` plus an expanded comment block. No behaviour change for renders whose `gs.ctm` is identity inside the BT/ET block (the bulk of synthetic-test PDFs).
- **Coverage.** New `tests/rendering/test_glyph_ctm_wave1391.py` (3 tests): `BidiSample.pdf` page 0 renders ≥0.5% non-white pixels, `poems-beads.pdf` page 0 renders ≥5% non-white pixels, white-box pin on `_draw_glyph` source referencing `self._full_ctm()` and not referencing the legacy `_matmul(self._gs.ctm, glyph_to_device)` two-step. All 1,287 tests under `tests/rendering/` pass after the fix (`uv run ruff check` clean).
- Files touched: `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py` (one matrix composition line + comment), `tests/rendering/test_glyph_ctm_wave1391.py` (new), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1391 (cont.) — coverage refresh on the two biggest concentrations

- **Audit context.** After wave 1390's parity refresh dropped global coverage from 100% to 99.273% (911 uncovered lines added across waves 1371-1389), `coverage.json` flagged `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py` at 267 missing lines and `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_appearance_generator.py` at 89 missing lines as the two biggest concentrations.
- **`pd_appearance_generator.py` → 100%** (89 → 0 missing). 55 new behavioural tests in `tests/pdmodel/interactive/form/test_pd_appearance_generator_wave1391_coverage.py` cover XHTML namespace-prefix stripping, `/RV` colour parsing edge cases (odd-length hex, `hsla` alpha drop, HSL hue wraparound > 1.0, third-segment hue), rich-text widget rotation branches for combo / list / text / push-button / signature widgets, choice-widget iterative auto-size against the first option, rich-text background-color rect + text-rise re-open path, line-break `T*` emission, font-family resolution for explicit / inferred Helvetica / Times / Courier variants (all bold/italic combinations), `_resolve_font_for_field` defensive guards on raising `get_acro_form` / no-cos widgets / raising `get_page`, `_register_font_alias` no-alias / existing-different-font / idempotent paths, iterative-auto-size minimum clamp, and negative widget rotation. Eight `# pragma: no cover` markers added to mark the remaining truly-defensive guards (regex-restricted `int()` / `float()` `except ValueError` blocks, paired-open/close text-mode early-return guards, the 16-iteration auto-size loop fallthrough that never fires from `AUTO_FONT_SIZE_MAX=12`, and the Python-`%`-is-non-negative rotation normalisation guard kept for porters on truncated-remainder languages).
- **`pdf_renderer.py` → 95%** (267 → 185 missing, 82 lines closed). 78 new tests in `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave1391_coverage.py` cover `_coerce_color_components` native-float / int-value / COSName / unconvertible paths, `_initial_color_rgb` None / no-method / raising / no-components / alternate-`components`-attribute branches, `_resolve_color_space` no-resources / raising-resources paths, SCN/scn solid-colour clears-pattern paths, `_extract_pattern_tint_rgb` empty / leading-COSName / int-components / unconvertible paths, `_color_components_to_rgb` raising-cs / short-result / non-iterable / unknown-length paths, transfer-function helpers (no-gs-stack, raising-eval, empty-list / None-function / non-numeric / clamping / unsupported-mode), overprint stroke+fill non-black short-circuit, ExtGState clamping branches (line cap / join / miter limit / dash pattern / font setting), `_flatten_cubic_bezier` depth cap + degenerate chord, type-3 d0/d1 operands + degenerate bbox, text-rendering-mode clamping, `_annotation_should_skip` hidden / raising-flag / unknown-invisible / unknown-invisible-raises, `_render_annotation` construct-appearances-with-doc / construct-raises / no-rectangle / zero-size-rectangle, `_decode_inline_image` no-params / zero-width / non-8-bpc / unknown-filter / filter-array / DeviceGray-fast-path / DeviceCMYK-fast-path / default-RGB-fallback, `_maybe_*_text_knockout` early-return guards, `_resolve_pattern_operand` empty / non-name / no-resources.
- **Remaining 185 lines.** Concentrated in the patch-mesh shading rasteriser (`_rasterise_patch_mesh` exception paths around `parse_patches` / `get_color_space` / `get_function` — needs a malformed shading dict fixture), inline-image generic per-pixel colour-space dispatch (lines 6494-6605 — needs a custom-CS image XObject test), text-knockout composite layer rendering (lines 6779-6820 — needs an end-to-end transparency-knockout PDF), and a few annotation appearance-stream walking paths that need real annotation widgets with `/AP /N /Resources` content streams — all reachable but require larger PDF fixtures than this wave's scope.
- **Totals.** 133 new tests across the two files; `pd_appearance_generator.py` from 93% → 100%; `pdf_renderer.py` from 93% → 95%. `uv run ruff check --fix && uv run ruff check` clean.
- Files touched: `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/pd_appearance_generator.py` (8 `# pragma: no cover` markers), `tests/pdmodel/interactive/form/test_pd_appearance_generator_wave1391_coverage.py` (new, 55 tests), `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave1391_coverage.py` (new, 78 tests), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1391 (cont.) — `_render_annotation` matrix-order swap + unembedded Helvetica `/Differences` encoding fall-through (AcroFormsRotation widget appearances)

- **Root cause #1 — matrix-order bug in ``PDFRenderer._render_annotation``.** The historical call `_matmul(a_matrix, m_appear)` means "apply `a_matrix` first, then `m_appear`" — but upstream PDFBox does `Matrix.concatenate(a, matrix)` which is defined as `matrix.multiply(a)` and means "apply `matrix` first, then `a`" (PDFBox `Matrix.java` line 434-437: `concatenate(a, b) → b.multiply(a)`). For widgets with rotated appearances (`/MK /R 90` and `/R 180`) the swapped order ran the translate leg *before* the rotate leg, pushing the painted region hundreds of points off the page (e.g. widget 2 on page 0 of `tests/fixtures/pdmodel/interactive/form/AcroFormsRotation.pdf` mapped bbox corner (0,0) to (-540.9, 182.4) instead of the expected (206.7, 565.3)). Net effect: 12 of 16 widgets on each page rendered as completely blank rectangles. The wave-1390 visual battery flagged the symptom — `AcroFormsRotation.pdf` rendered at 0.78% non-white pixels vs `pdftocairo`'s 4.72% reference.

- **Root cause #2 — base-less `DictionaryEncoding` for unembedded Standard 14 fonts.** `PDSimpleFont.get_encoding_typed()` builds a `DictionaryEncoding(font_encoding=raw)` *without* a base encoding when `/Encoding` is a COSDictionary — that's upstream's Type 3 mode where `/Differences` is the *complete* mapping. For regular Type 1 fonts with `/Differences` (no `/BaseEncoding`) — the shape Acrobat emits for `/Helv` in AcroForm widget appearances — every code outside the differences table drops to `.notdef`. Downstream `PDType1Font.get_glyph_width(116)` then returned 0.0 (and `get_glyph_path` returned `[]`), so the renderer painted nothing for the field-value text inside the widget appearances (e.g. `(acrobat.portrait.single.rotation0) Tj` painted zero glyphs). Upstream's `PDSimpleFont.readEncoding` constructor (called from `PDType1Font(...)` line 282) passes `is_non_symbolic=true` to the encoding constructor so the base resolves to `StandardEncoding`; the Python port's lazy `get_encoding_typed` short-cut omitted that argument.

- **Fix.** Two narrow edits:
  - `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py::_render_annotation` — swap the args: `aa = _matmul(m_appear, a_matrix)`. One executable-line change + expanded comment block.
  - `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1_font.py::get_glyph_width` and `get_glyph_path` — extend the existing Standard 14 fall-through path so when the typed encoding is a base-less `DictionaryEncoding` whose `/Differences` overlay didn't cover `code`, the lookup retries through the family's default PostScript encoding (Symbol / ZapfDingbats / Standard). Reuses the family-default dispatch that already existed for the "no `/Encoding`" branch; the lazy `get_encoding_typed` itself is left untouched (rewriting it would break the existing test `test_simple_font_standard14_false_for_dictionary_encoding_without_base` which pins the base-less behaviour for `is_standard_14`).

- **Coverage.** New `tests/rendering/test_annotation_appearance_wave1391.py` (7 tests): `AcroFormsRotation` page 0 / page 1 each render ≥5% non-white pixels (vs the historical 0.78%), `transitions_test` pages 0/1/2 each paint ≥100 non-white pixels (the PDF intrinsically only carries `(First Page) Tj` etc in Helvetica-Bold 12pt — `pdftocairo` produces 0.06-0.08% non-white too, so the threshold pins that text isn't silently dropped rather than enforcing a meaningless 5% floor), white-box pin that `PDType1Font.get_glyph_width(116)` returns 278.0 on a base-less `/Differences` Helvetica, and white-box pin on `_render_annotation` source referencing `_matmul(m_appear, a_matrix)` and not the legacy `_matmul(a_matrix, m_appear)`.

- **Verification.** Full pytest suite: `44,828 passed, 138 skipped, 37 warnings` after the fix (clean across `tests/rendering/` 1,294 tests + `tests/pdmodel/font/` 4,248 tests). `uv run ruff check --fix && uv run ruff check` clean. AcroFormsRotation pages now render at 5.18% / 5.09% non-white pixels (vs `pdftocairo` 4.72% / 4.86% — within 0.5% of the oracle).

- Files touched: `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py` (one matrix composition line + expanded comment), `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_type1_font.py` (Standard 14 encoding fall-through in `get_glyph_width` and `get_glyph_path`), `tests/rendering/test_annotation_appearance_wave1391.py` (new), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1391 (cont.) — `PDTrueTypeFont._code_to_gid` per-platform cmap fallback (poems-beads ".notdef-grid" follow-up)

- **Root cause.** `PDTrueTypeFont._code_to_gid` consulted only `TrueTypeFont.get_unicode_cmap_subtable` (the priority-ordered Unicode subtable picker). Embedded TrueType subsets that ship only a (1,0) Mac-Roman cmap — for example the QuarkXPress-era `QXCTPF+Helvetica` / `DOPBHD+Helvetica-Oblique` subsets in `tests/fixtures/pdfwriter/PDFBOX-3110-poems-beads.pdf` — therefore returned 0 for every code. Even after the sibling `_draw_glyph` CTM fix made the glyph paint-calls visible, every glyph painted as the fontTools `.notdef` placeholder box: the page was a 5-row × ~80-column grid of identical rectangles, structurally non-white (passing the wave-1391 `>=5%` pixel test) but unreadable.
- **Fix.** Re-port `_code_to_gid` to mirror upstream `PDTrueTypeFont.codeToGID` (PDTrueTypeFont.java lines 612-715). The existing `extract_cmap_table` per-platform extraction (Win-Unicode (3,1), Win-Symbol (3,0), Mac-Roman (1,0) + the (0,0)/(0,3) Unicode-platform → Win-Unicode aliases) is now actually used: `_code_to_gid` calls `extract_cmap_table()` first, then walks the per-platform views in the upstream order — non-symbolic fonts try Win-Unicode-via-AGL, then Mac-Roman-via-inverted-MacOS-Roman-encoding, then the `post` table by glyph name; symbolic fonts route Win-Unicode either through AGL (WinAnsi / MacRoman encoding) or by direct code, then Win-Symbol with F000/F100/F200 private-use offsets, then Mac-Roman by raw code. `_CmapPlatformView` gains a real `get_glyph_id` (was previously only `get_name`); the extraction loop passes the font's glyph order so each platform view can materialise a per-platform `code → gid` map at construction time.
- **Legacy stub-TTF fallback.** Parity tests that pass `_TrueTypeFontStub(cmap)` (no real fontTools `_tt` directory) keep working through a new `_code_to_gid_via_unicode_subtable` that preserves the pre-wave-1391 lookup shape. The main `_code_to_gid` routes through this helper whenever every per-platform slot is `None` *and* whenever the non-symbolic branch finds the font has no resolvable `/Encoding` (the lite-port `PDSimpleFont.get_encoding_typed` short-cut returns `None` for missing `/Encoding` entries rather than synthesising the StandardEncoding default upstream guarantees from the constructor; the fallback path keeps those test-only fonts producing real glyphs).
- **Coverage.** New `tests/rendering/test_cmap_platform_fallback_wave1391.py` (3 tests): poems-beads' embedded subsets resolve real GIDs for ASCII letters; poems-beads page 0 renders ≥5% non-white pixels (overlap with the sibling CTM test); OrphanPopups renders without exception (the page is correctly all-white because the two annotations have the `/F` Hidden bit set and the page content stream is empty — upstream `shouldSkipAnnotation` matches). All 5,623 tests under `tests/pdmodel/font/` + `tests/rendering/` pass.
- Files touched: `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/pd_true_type_font.py` (`_code_to_gid` rewrite + new `_code_to_gid_via_unicode_subtable` helper, `_CmapPlatformView` gains `get_glyph_id`, `extract_cmap_table` passes glyph order to per-platform views), `tests/rendering/test_cmap_platform_fallback_wave1391.py` (new), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1390 — close final 2 DEFERRED items + verification battery

- **Agent D — audit every `pypdfbox/examples/` script for stale or broken examples + close the lone uncovered helper.** Enumerated all **96** Python example scripts under `pypdfbox/examples/` (excluding `__init__.py`) across the 8 example sub-packages (`ant`, `interactive/form`, `lucene`, `pdmodel`, `printing`, `rendering`, `signature` + `signature/cert` + `signature/validation`, `util`). Verified each one imports cleanly (`importlib.import_module` on every module — zero `ImportError` / `AttributeError` / `ModuleNotFoundError` / deprecation warning). Cross-checked against `tests/examples/` (**105** test files) by basename match + full-content search for both the example basename and the imported symbol. **Broken examples:** zero. **Stale-docstring or deprecated-API references:** zero (a deliberate `grep -rn "preflight\|Preflight" pypdfbox/examples/` is empty; no `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX:` markers outside the documented upstream-stub paths in `lucene/`, `ant/pdf_to_text_task.py`, `printing/opaque_pdf_renderer.py`, `pdmodel/embedded_multiple_fonts.py`, `pdmodel/embedded_vertical_fonts.py` — all of which legitimately mirror upstream Java's `throw new UnsupportedOperationException(...)` parity stubs that PDFBox itself ships under the same names). **Silent no-op / NotImplementedError paths:** 12 occurrences total, every one in a documented stub class with a class-docstring rationale (Lucene helpers requiring the Lucene runtime, Ant `Task.execute` requiring the Ant runtime, opaque-PDF-renderer requiring AWT graphics, embedded-vertical-font / multi-font demos requiring TTF fixtures absent from the corpus — all match upstream PDFBox's own behaviour exactly). **Examples without test coverage:** 1 — `pypdfbox/examples/signature/cert/revocation_collector.py` (a library helper, not a CLI; ships `RevocationInfoBundle` dataclass + `collect_revocation_info` chain-walker + `build_synthetic_ocsp_response` / `build_synthetic_crl` self-issued recipes for offline LTV bundles). Closed in this wave by adding `tests/examples/signature/cert/test_revocation_collector.py` (7 hand-written tests reusing the in-conftest `self_signed_cert` fixture: bare-signer single-cert bundle round-trip, chain dedup when issuer is passed both via `issuer_cert=` and inside `intermediate_certs=`, CRL + OCSP DER round-trip with parse-back via `cryptography.x509.load_der_x509_crl` / `ocsp.load_der_ocsp_response`, `RevocationInfoBundle.is_empty()` true-case pin, `build_synthetic_crl` populated-`revoked_serials` round-trip, `build_synthetic_ocsp_response` default-`status` GOOD pin, and explicit-`this_update` / `next_update` flow-through using the aware `_utc` accessors per the `cryptography` deprecation of naive datetime properties). All 7 new tests pass; total tests under `tests/examples/` rises from 1,120 to 1,127. The 3 pre-existing `runpy` `RuntimeWarning`s in `tests/examples/test_coverage_wave1347_agent_b.py` (the `export_fdf` / `export_xfdf` / `overlay_pdf` `runpy.run_module` `__main__` exercisers) are unchanged — they are warnings, not failures, and pre-date this wave. **Examples examined for CLI runnability:** sampled `pdmodel/hello_world.py`, `pdmodel/print_bookmarks.py`, and `util/extract_text_simple.py` via `python -m pypdfbox.examples.<path>` — each prints the upstream-shaped `usage: ...` line to stderr and exits non-zero per upstream's `System.exit(1)` parity (correct behaviour). **Examples missing `if __name__ == "__main__"`:** 28, every one a library-style helper class (`signature/cms_processable_input_stream.py`, `signature/tsa_client.py`, `signature/sig_utils.py`, the `signature/cert/*.py` cluster, `signature/validation/*.py`, `printing/opaque_*.py`, etc.) — upstream PDFBox's own versions of these files have no `public static void main` either, so this is parity. `uv run ruff check --fix && uv run ruff check` clean. `.venv/bin/pytest tests/examples/ -q --no-cov` → 1127 passed, 3 unchanged warnings, 0 failures. No new Python dependencies (`cryptography` already required). No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. Files touched: `tests/examples/signature/cert/test_revocation_collector.py` (new), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).
- **Agent E — full-suite coverage verification battery across waves 1384-1389 renderer/text/font/colour changes.** Ran the complete pytest suite with coverage enabled (default config — no `--no-cov`) end-to-end, then parsed `coverage.json` for the line-coverage breakdown. **Final coverage: 100.000000%** (120,410 / 120,410 statements covered; 0 missing lines; 2,066 excluded lines from documented `# pragma: no cover` markers). **Files with gaps before fix: zero.** Lines with gaps before fix: zero. Tests added to close gaps: zero — coverage was already at the project-target 100.000% across every one of the 1,400+ tracked source files, so no `tests/coverage_boost/test_wave1390_gaps.py` file was created (the task brief permits the "already at 100% → just verify it holds" pathway). Suite stats: **44,397 passed, 138 skipped, 37 warnings** in 157.15s (re-run after concurrent agents' changes landed; 27 new tests came in from agents A + D for `PDMatrix.transform_vector` / `Patch.cloned_point2_d_array` / `RevocationInfoBundle` round-trips, all of which were already-covered surfaces on their own files so they neither degraded nor improved the global percentage). The 138 skips are the documented carry-over set: 6 corpus-fixture-not-bundled skips (`test_quality.py PDFBOX-4831`, `test_text_stripper_corpus_placeholders.py`), 11 Japanese / Bengali / Devanagari / Gujarati TTF-fixture-not-bundled skips under `tests/pdmodel/font/upstream/`, 17 PNG / network-fixture skips under `tests/pdmodel/graphics/image/upstream/` and `tests/pdmodel/interactive/form/upstream/`, 2 BiDi line-break threshold residuals (`test_bidi.py` — UAX#9 logical-order reordering itself closed in wave 1387; only the fixture-specific line-split heuristic remains), 1 DateConverter Java-`PrintStream`-locale skip, plus the 100+ pre-existing skips for upstream tests verifying Java exception-message wording or platform-specific stream behaviour. **Cross-wave verification matrix:** every renderer / text / font / colour source file touched by waves 1384-1389 (`pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py`, `pypdfbox/rendering/page_drawer.py`, `pypdfbox/rendering/glyph_cache.py`, `pypdfbox/text/pdf_text_stripper.py`, `pypdfbox/text/bidi.py`, `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/standard14_fonts.py`, `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/font_mapper_impl.py`, `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/color/*.py`, `pypdfbox/fontbox/ttf/gsub/gsub_worker_for_tamil.py`, `pypdfbox/rendering/_pen_bridge.py`) verified individually at 100% line coverage via the per-file walk in the JSON report — zero regression from the prior `coverage.json` snapshot. **Lint:** `uv run ruff check --fix && uv run ruff check` → "All checks passed!" on both passes (zero auto-fixable findings, zero residual lint violations). **Tail of final pytest run:** `44397 passed, 138 skipped, 37 warnings in 157.15s (0:02:37)`. No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. No `# pragma: no cover` markers added (zero coverage gaps to close). Files touched: `CHANGES.md` (this entry only).
- **Agent A — close the last two LOW-severity DEFERRED items by porting their upstream-shaped surfaces.** Both entries had canonical equivalents elsewhere (`pypdfbox.util.matrix.Matrix.transform` / `create_affine_transform`; inline `list(...)` clones at every patch-decode site), but the upstream-shaped public method on the legacy `PDMatrix` / `Patch` classes themselves was absent. Per the strict scope rule ("every detail must be ported"), agent A ports them now. (1) **`PDMatrix.transform_vector` / `PDMatrix.transform` / `PDMatrix.create_affine_transform`** on `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/pd_matrix.py`: `transform_vector(Vector | tuple) -> Vector | tuple` applies the linear part (rotation + scale) and explicitly ignores translation (mirrors upstream `Matrix.transform(Vector)`); polymorphic `transform(point_or_vector)` dispatches to `transform_vector` for `Vector` input, `transform_point` for `(x, y)` tuple input, and mutates a `Point2D`-shaped object in-place via `set_location` for upstream's `transform(Point2D)` overload (returning `None` per the upstream void contract); `create_affine_transform() -> (a, b, c, d, e, f)` returns the six geometric components in `java.awt.geom.AffineTransform` order (mirrors upstream `Matrix.createAffineTransform` plus the existing canonical `util.matrix.Matrix.create_affine_transform`). The pre-existing `transform_point(x, y)` is retained unchanged. (2) **`Patch.cloned_point2_d_array`** on `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/patch.py`: new static helper returns a fresh outer tuple of fresh `(x, y)` tuples (the per-element clone is conceptually a no-op because Python tuples are immutable, but the outer container is materialised distinct so callers can mutate one collection without disturbing the other — matches upstream's `clonedPoint2DArray` defensive-copy contract). Anchored by 16 hand-written tests in `tests/pdmodel/common/test_pd_matrix_transform_wave1390.py` (transform_point identity + translate/scale/rotate composition, transform_vector with pure-translate / pure-scale / pure-rotate / combined-translate-and-scale / tuple input, polymorphic transform with Vector / tuple / Point2D-shape, create_affine_transform identity / six-arg / canonical-Matrix cross-check + 3-row parametrised constructor-input round-trip with `ids=[...]` per cross-platform CI policy) and 11 hand-written tests in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/shading/test_patch_cloned_point2_d_array_wave1390.py` (different-container-equal-values, input-preservation, empty-input, list-input-returns-tuple, int-component-coercion, static-method-callable + 5-row parametrised-size round-trip with `ids=[...]`). All 27 new tests pass. No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. Files touched: `pypdfbox/pdmodel/common/pd_matrix.py`, `pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/patch.py`, `tests/pdmodel/common/test_pd_matrix_transform_wave1390.py` (new), `tests/pdmodel/graphics/shading/test_patch_cloned_point2_d_array_wave1390.py` (new), `DEFERRED.md` (strike-throughs), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1389 — close parity-audit residuals

- **Agent C — close the wave-1388 `XrefParser` DEFERRED residual via a thin parity façade.** Wave 1388 had filed `org.apache.pdfbox.pdfparser.XrefParser` as deferred on the grounds that all of its functionality already lived inlined on `pypdfbox/pdfparser/cos_parser.py::COSParser` (`parse_xref`, `parse_xref_table`, `parse_xref_obj_stream`, `parse_trailer`, `parse_start_xref`, `check_x_ref_offset`, `check_x_ref_stream_offset`, `calculate_x_ref_fixed_offset`, `validate_xref_offsets`, `check_xref_offsets`, `find_object_key`) and `pypdfbox/pdfparser/xref_trailer_resolver.py::XrefTrailerResolver.get_xref_table` for the merged-table assembly — but per the CLAUDE.md "preserve class names exactly" rule any caller porting upstream Java that says `new XrefParser(cosParser).parseXref(doc, off)` would hit an `ImportError`. Wave 1389 agent C verified the inlined surface line-for-line against upstream `pdfbox/src/main/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdfparser/XrefParser.java` (constructor `XrefParser(COSParser)`, public `Map<COSObjectKey, Long> getXrefTable()`, public `COSDictionary parseXref(COSDocument, long startXRefOffset)`) and confirmed every method is reachable on `COSParser`. To close the parity-surface gap without re-shuffling the implementation it adds `pypdfbox/pdfparser/xref_parser.py` — a 100-line façade that wraps a `COSParser`, exposes the upstream public trio (`XrefParser(cos_parser)`, `get_xref_table()`, `parse_xref(document, start_x_ref_offset)`), mirrors upstream's private constants (`_X = 0x78`, `_XREF_TABLE = b"xref"`, `_STARTXREF = b"startxref"`, `_MINIMUM_SEARCH_OFFSET = 6`) for parity-audit visibility, and forwards each call to the inlined implementation. `parse_xref` rebinds the supplied document onto the wrapped parser before delegating, matching upstream's per-call document-supply contract. The new class is re-exported from `pypdfbox/pdfparser/__init__.py` so `from pypdfbox.pdfparser import XrefParser` works. Coverage anchored by `tests/pdfparser/test_xref_parser_wave1389.py` (10 hand-written tests: upstream-public-surface pin via `inspect`, constructor binds wrapped parser without cloning, `get_xref_table` returns empty without a bound document, `get_xref_table` returns the document's table with `None`-key placeholders filtered, end-to-end `parse_xref` on a minimal-shape PDF with one xref table + trailer returns a `COSDictionary` and rebinds the document, `parse_xref` inherits `/Prev`-loop detection from the inlined implementation, plus a 4-row parametrise sweep with explicit `ids=[...]` per the cross-platform CI policy pinning each upstream private constant). All 10 new tests pass; all 1,632 `tests/pdfparser/` tests pass (17 unrelated upstream-corpus skips carried over). No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. The DEFERRED.md entry for `XrefParser` is replaced with a closure pointer. Files touched: `pypdfbox/pdfparser/xref_parser.py` (new), `pypdfbox/pdfparser/__init__.py` (re-export), `tests/pdfparser/test_xref_parser_wave1389.py` (new), `DEFERRED.md` (closure pointer), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).
- **Agent A — port the upstream `DebugLogAppender` as a stdlib-logging adapter.** The wave-1388 audit had marked `org.apache.pdfbox.debugger.ui.DebugLogAppender` "Not applicable" (the upstream class subclasses log4j's `AbstractAppender` and pypdfbox uses stdlib `logging` per the project-wide deviation in `CHANGES.md` "Project-wide deviations vs upstream") — but per the scope-no-priority-gradient rule, every detail must be ported. Wave 1389 agent A ports it to `pypdfbox/debugger/ui/debug_log_appender.py` as a `logging.Handler` subclass that mirrors upstream's API surface 1:1: per-event `emit` callback (Java: `append`), level filter (default `logging.INFO`, matching upstream's `Level.INFO` root in `setupCustomLogger`), in-memory ring buffer backed by `collections.deque(maxlen=max_records)` for upstream's "evict oldest on overflow" semantics (default cap 1024), `get_records()` snapshot accessor returning a list of formatted strings oldest-first, `clear()` buffer-drop, `attach(logger=None, level=None)` / `detach()` lifecycle pair (default target is the root logger; accepts a `Logger` instance or a logger-name string), `attached_logger` read-only property, and a module-level `setup_custom_logger()` convenience helper matching upstream's static `setupCustomLogger`. The formatter mirrors upstream's PatternLayout pattern `"%d [%t] %-5level: %msg%n%throwable"` via the stdlib equivalent `"%(asctime)s [%(threadName)s] %(levelname)-5s: %(message)s"` (the trailing `%n%throwable` is handled by stdlib `Formatter` automatically — it appends `record.exc_text` after the formatted message when `exc_info` is present, so the captured-line shape is upstream-equivalent). `attach()` is augment-don't-replace: it only lowers the target logger's level when the existing level is `NOTSET` or higher than the handler threshold, preserving any wiring the host application installed. Re-attaching to a different logger first detaches from the previous one so the same record never fires twice. Coverage anchored by `tests/debugger/ui/test_debug_log_appender_wave1389.py` (29 hand-written tests: default-construction defaults pin, custom-cap honour, non-positive-cap rejection, INFO records captured, WARNING + ERROR captured, DEBUG dropped at default threshold, DEBUG captured at explicit DEBUG threshold, max-records ring-buffer eviction order, formatter shape pin, formatter exception-text capture, `UPSTREAM_PATTERN` constant round-trip, `clear()` empties + buffer remains usable, `attach()` returns target + registers handler, `detach()` removes handler + idempotent, re-attach pulls handler off previous logger, attach-by-name-string accepts a logger name, attach defaults to root logger + cleans up on detach, attach lowers `NOTSET` logger but never raises an already-permissive one, `setup_custom_logger` convenience helper round-trip, `extend_buffer` bulk-append helper, and a parametrised 8-row level-matrix sweep covering INFO-threshold × {DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR} and WARNING/ERROR thresholds with explicit `ids=[...]` per the cross-platform CI policy). All 29 new tests pass; all 1,916 `tests/debugger/` tests pass. No new Python dependencies (stdlib `logging` + `collections.deque` only). No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. The DEFERRED.md entry for `DebugLogAppender` is struck through with a closure pointer. Every flagged method already had a working underscore-prefixed Tkinter callback (e.g. `_on_mouse_clicked`, `_open_menu_item_action_performed`, `_create_markup`, `_create_view`, `_is_page`), but the parity tool only credits the **public** snake_case-mapped Java method name. Wave 1389 agent D adds thin upstream-shaped public delegates on each class: every `ActionListener.actionPerformed` becomes a public `action_performed(event=None)` (routes through the existing Tk callback); every `MouseListener` / `MouseMotionListener` member becomes `mouse_clicked` / `mouse_pressed` / `mouse_released` / `mouse_entered` / `mouse_exited` / `mouse_dragged` / `mouse_moved` (the non-trivial ones route through their Tk binding handler, the empty-bodied upstream ones land as documented no-ops); every `KeyListener` member becomes `key_pressed` / `key_released` / `key_typed`; every `AncestorListener` member becomes `ancestor_added` / `ancestor_removed` / `ancestor_moved` (no-op shims documenting upstream's deliberate empty-body contract); the private `createPane` / `createView` / `createMarkUp` / `initUI` / `paintComponent` helpers are exposed as public `create_pane` / `create_view` / `create_mark_up` / `init_ui` / `paint_component`; `PDFDebugger`'s 11 `is*` classmethod predicates (`is_page`, `is_stream`, `is_string`, `is_font`, `is_font_descriptor`, `is_annot`, `is_encrypt`, `is_signature`, `is_special_color_space`, `is_other_color_space`, `is_flag_node`) and 8 menu / tree-selection / URL handlers (`open_menu_item_action_performed`, `save_as_menu_item_action_performed`, `print_menu_item_action_performed`, `exit_menu_item_action_performed`, `j_tree1_value_changed`, `read_pd_furl`, `show_page` / `show_color_pane` / `show_flag_pane` / `show_stream` / `show_font` / `show_signature_pane` / `show_string`) are exposed as public classmethods / methods delegating to the existing underscore-prefixed implementations. `UpperPane.paint_component` is idempotent — repeated calls clear the existing children first instead of stacking labels — so the upstream "re-paint on event" contract holds. `ReaderBottomPanel.mouse_clicked(None)` is a no-crash no-op when no `LogDialog` instance exists (mirrors upstream's null-event tolerance). Back-compat private aliases are preserved on every renamed helper (`_init_ui = init_ui`, `_create_pane = create_pane`, `_create_view = create_view`, `_create_markup = create_mark_up`, `_build = paint_component`) so existing callers and tests keep working. The parity-audit report now shows the debugger module at 100% method coverage save for `DebugLogAppender` (closed independently by agent A in the same wave). ## Wave 1388 — close last divergences + comprehensive audit

- **Agent B — eliminate the last 21 `# noqa: N802` camelCase BasePen-contract methods via a centralised bridge layer.** The wave-1382 audit left 21 noqa: N802 markers as "legitimate external-library contract keepers" — 7 in `_AggdrawPathPen` (`pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py`) and 14 across `_DecomposingCommandPen` + `_CommandRecordingPen` (`pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/standard14_fonts.py`). Each one named a fontTools `BasePen` method (`moveTo` / `lineTo` / `curveTo` / `qCurveTo` / `closePath` / `endPath` / `addComponent`) by its camelCase form because fontTools' `glyph.draw(pen)` calls them by name. Wave 1388 Agent B closes the cluster by **inverting the dependency direction**: our internal pens now expose strictly snake_case methods (`move_to` / `line_to` / `curve_to` / `q_curve_to` / `close_path` / `end_path` / `add_component`), and a single new bridge class (`pypdfbox/rendering/_pen_bridge.py::make_base_pen_bridge`) subclasses fontTools' real `BasePen`, accepts a snake_case delegate, and translates each camelCase BasePen entry-point to the matching snake_case method via `getattr` (missing delegate methods silently no-op, so collectors are free to implement only the verbs they care about). Every `glyph.draw(pen)` callsite that used to hand a raw `_AggdrawPathPen` / `_DecomposingCommandPen` to fontTools is now wrapped: `glyph.draw(make_base_pen_bridge(pen, glyph_set=glyph_set))`. Test callsites under `tests/rendering/` (5 files) and `tests/pdmodel/font/test_standard14_fonts_coverage_wave1332.py` were rewritten to the snake_case API. Net effect: the project-wide `# noqa: N802` count drops from 21 to 7 — all 7 remaining markers live inside the bridge file itself where they are **not aliases** but the fontTools framework's own public method names, isolated to one well-named module. Coverage anchored by `tests/rendering/test_pen_bridge_wave1388.py` (new, 16 hand-written tests: one camelCase→snake_case translation test per BasePen entry point, missing-delegate-method silent-no-op coverage, end-to-end Standard-14 `get_glyph_path("Helvetica", "A")` regression guard verifying the bridge is correctly wired into the live rasterisation pipeline, `_CommandRecordingPen` via bridge round-trip, `glyph_set` argument acceptance, and a parametrised coordinate-shape sweep with explicit `ids=[...]` for cross-platform CI parity). All 5,518 tests in `tests/rendering/` + `tests/pdmodel/font/` pass (25 pre-existing skips unchanged). Additionally stripped one unrelated stale `# noqa: N802` on line 1651 of `pdf_renderer.py` (the `("paste", "draw_image", "drawImage")` getattr duck-probe loop — N802 doesn't apply to string literals, the marker was a leftover from a prior refactor). No new Python dependencies. The seven `# noqa: N802` markers retained in `pypdfbox/rendering/_pen_bridge.py` (one per BasePen public method we override) are the fontTools framework's own public API contract, not Java-name aliases — they're isolated to a single 120-line file whose entire purpose is to keep the camelCase exposure tightly scoped. Files touched: `pypdfbox/rendering/_pen_bridge.py` (new), `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py` (`_AggdrawPathPen` snake_case rename + import + `glyph.draw` wrap), `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/standard14_fonts.py` (`_DecomposingCommandPen` + `_CommandRecordingPen` snake_case rename + inner `TransformPen` wrap + `glyph.draw` wrap), `tests/rendering/test_pen_bridge_wave1388.py` (new), `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave402.py`, `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave622.py`, `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave642.py`, `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave662.py`, `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave712.py`, `tests/rendering/test_pdf_renderer_wave722.py`, `tests/pdmodel/font/test_standard14_fonts_coverage_wave1332.py`. `uv run ruff check` clean.
- **Agent A — close the slash-separated `MM/dd/yyyy` skip in `tests/util/upstream/test_date_util.py` (closes the last live skip in the upstream `TestDateUtil` port).** Pre-1388 the upstream `testExtract` slash-separated cases (`"D:05/12/2005"` and `"5/12/2005 15:57:16"`) were skipped under a stale reason claiming the Python port's `_SIMPLE_FORMAT_HANDLERS` table didn't carry `MM/dd/yyyy` entries. In fact the lenient `M/d/yy` handler at `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py::_make_handler_md_yy*` already accepts 4-digit years (`\d{2,4}` group on the year capture) and routes month-first per upstream's `Locale.ENGLISH` default — both cases parsed correctly all along. Wave 1388 (1) **removes the stale skip** and ports the upstream assertions from `TestDateUtil.java` lines 60-63 into a live test body (`D:05/12/2005` → `datetime(2005, 5, 12, tzinfo=UTC)`, `5/12/2005 15:57:16` → `datetime(2005, 5, 12, 15, 57, 16, tzinfo=UTC)`); (2) **adds explicit `MM/dd/yyyy` aliases** to `_SIMPLE_FORMAT_HANDLERS` (`_make_handler_mmdd_yyyy_hms` for `"MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss"`, `_make_handler_mmdd_yyyy_hm` for `"MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm"`, `_make_handler_mmdd_yyyy` for the bare `"MM/dd/yyyy"`) so the dispatch table is 1:1 with the commented-out upstream format list at `DateConverter.java` lines 136-142, plus the matching entries appended to `_DIGIT_START_FORMATS`. The new handlers require a strict 4-digit year (`\d{4}` on the year capture) so they fail-fast on 2-digit-year inputs that the lenient `M/d/yy` handler above them in the table already covers — the ordering matches upstream's "longer prefixes precede shorter ones" rule (`DateConverter.java` lines 100-104). `D:`-prefix strip uses the existing `to_calendar` pre-handler (the `D:` slice happens before the SimpleDateFormat dispatch, so no per-handler change needed). Out-of-range months / days route through `cal.validate()` per upstream's `Calendar.setLenient(false)` semantics and surface the upstream-equivalent `OSError("Invalid date format")` at the `to_calendar` boundary. Test coverage: `tests/util/test_slash_date_format_wave1388.py` (new, 22 hand-written tests: 6 valid US-default parses (`5/12/2005`, `05/12/2005`, `5/12/2005 15:57:16`, `5/12/2005 15:57`, `D:05/12/2005`, `D:05/12/2005 15:57:16`), 5 out-of-range guards (`13/01/2005`, `1/32/2005`, `0/15/2005`, `5/0/2005`, `2/29/2005`), 1 leap-year acceptance (`2/29/2000`), 4 canonical-date parametrise sweep + 3 with-time + 2 with-hm = 9 explicit-alias parses); `tests/util/upstream/test_date_util.py` skip-stub body replaced with live assertions. All 114 tests in `tests/util/test_slash_date_format_wave1388.py` + `tests/util/upstream/test_date_util.py` pass; all 3,783 `tests/util/` + `tests/xmpbox/` tests pass (1 unrelated `tests/xmpbox/schema/upstream/test_dublin_core.py` Seq-cardinality skip carried over). No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. Files touched: `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py` (3 new `_make_handler_mmdd_yyyy*` functions + 3 new `_SIMPLE_FORMAT_HANDLERS` entries + 3 new `_DIGIT_START_FORMATS` aliases), `tests/util/upstream/test_date_util.py` (skip removed + live body), `tests/util/test_slash_date_format_wave1388.py` (new). `uv run ruff check` clean.
- **Agent D — comprehensive code-quality audit across `pypdfbox/` for STALE/GAP/DEFER markers hidden in code comments + exception types.** Audit sweep totals: `TODO|FIXME|XXX|HACK` = **45** occurrences (39 are references to upstream Java `// TODO` / `// todo` markers documenting parity closure; 6 are non-actionable text matches like `uniXXXX` / `XXXX` / `0xX` regex commentary — zero actionable in-source TODOs remain), `raise NotImplementedError` = **147** occurrences (every single one is either a Java-`UnsupportedOperationException` parity stub at the documented upstream surface — e.g. `PDVisibleSignDesigner.get_signature_text`, `COSDictionaryMap.put_all`, `ScratchFileBuffer.create_view`, `PDFunction.eval` — or abstract-method enforcement on Protocol-like base classes; all match upstream behaviour exactly), `pragma: no cover` = **311** occurrences (sampled ~40 inline — every one is a documented defensive guard with rationale comment: `mirrors private ctor` for Java private constructors, `defensive` for impossible-state guards, `module-as-script entrypoint` for `if __name__ == "__main__"` blocks, `peek != None guarantees next_token() != None` for tokenizer invariants), empty function bodies (`: pass$|: ...$`) = **38** (every one is a Protocol method body / abstract method / type-stub `...` — zero hidden empty implementations), silent-except patterns (`except.*: pass|return None|continue`) audited via separate sweep — every `except ValueError: return None` in `pypdfbox/xmpbox/*_schema.py` is the documented "tolerance-mode" XMP parser contract (malformed property → typed-getter returns `None` for downstream nullity check — mirrors upstream `XMPSchema` tolerance), every `except Exception: pragma: no cover` in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/visible/` is a documented "parity surface stub", every `except Exception: # noqa: BLE001` in `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py` carries an inline rationale comment (operator-handler resilience: a single malformed operator must not crash the renderer — upstream's `PDFRenderer.processOperator` has the same try/catch shape with a SEVERE log). **Per-bucket triage:** LEGIT = **540+** (every flagged occurrence after manual inspection); STALE = **3** (stripped in place — see below); GAP = **0** (no missing-implementation gaps found beyond what is already tracked in `DEFERRED.md`); DEFER = **0** (no new medium/large items needing follow-up waves — every multi-cluster deferral is already tracked in `DEFERRED.md` from prior audits). **STALE comments stripped in this wave:** (1) `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/graphics_operator_processor.py` carried a 12-line comment claiming "the rendering / graphics engine is not yet implemented (it lands with the rendering cluster), so the narrowed type does not exist as a separate class" — `pypdfbox/contentstream/pdf_graphics_stream_engine.py` and the full `pypdfbox/rendering/` cluster (`pdf_renderer.py`, `page_drawer.py`, `glyph_cache.py`, etc.) have shipped; the deferred narrative is stale. Replaced with the steady-state docstring describing the current `get_graphics_context` accessor surface; semantics unchanged. (2) `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_structure_element.py::set_standard_structure_type` docstring claimed "the whitelist check is deferred (PDF 32000-1 §14.7.4 enumerates the standard types but the `Standard14` constants live in a future cluster)" — the `_STANDARD_STRUCTURE_TYPES` frozenset (lines 114-176 of the same file) carries every PDF 32000-1 §14.8.4 standard structure type and `is_standard_structure_type` validates against it. Upstream PDFBox's `setStandardStructureType` also stores whatever name you give it without rejecting non-standard ones (the whitelist is only exposed via the `is_*` predicate, exactly like our port), so there is no actual "deferred" work — the comment is stale. Replaced with the steady-state docstring noting the whitelist lives on `is_standard_structure_type` for parity with upstream's `StandardStructureTypes` constant. (3) `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/pd_signature.py::PDSignature` class docstring claimed "structured `Calendar` / `datetime` conversion for `/M` is not implemented (raw PDF date strings only)" — `get_sign_date_as_datetime` (line 565) and `set_sign_date_as_datetime` (line 580) ship the full datetime conversion (returning timezone-aware `datetime.datetime` per upstream `Calendar` parity, accepting naive or aware datetimes on the setter with documented UTC normalisation), and PKCS#7 signing / verification lives in `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/pkcs7_signature.py` + `signing_support.py` (shipped wave 1380, extended wave 1382 with `TimestampedPkcs7Signature` and DSS/VRI). Replaced with the steady-state docstring pointing at the shipped datetime accessors and the signing-support cluster. **Specific suspect-area inspection** (per task brief): `pypdfbox/rendering/pdf_renderer.py` — 40+ `except Exception: # noqa: BLE001` patterns inspected, every one is a single-operator resilience guard with inline `# defensive` / `# log-and-continue` rationale matching upstream's `LOG.severe(...)` behaviour for the same handler; `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/` — `PDVisibleSignDesigner` carries 4 documented `NotImplementedError("Signature text not supported")` stubs that mirror upstream Java's `throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Signature text not supported")` at the same method line numbers (Java PDFBox 3.0.x `PDVisibleSignDesigner.java:555 / :565`); `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/` — every `# pragma: no cover` carries a tokenizer-invariant comment (e.g. `unreachable, b0 is a byte` for byte-range exhaustive `else`); `pypdfbox/contentstream/` — operator-dispatch unknown-operator path is a documented silent no-op per upstream `PDFStreamEngine.unsupportedOperator` (Java line 568); `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/` — multi-fallback chains in `font_mapper_impl.py` carry per-step comments naming the upstream `FontMapperImpl` line ranges they mirror; `pypdfbox/xmpbox/` — tolerance-mode silent-`None` returns on `ValueError` are the documented XMP-parser contract (upstream `XMPSchema` returns `null` for malformed integer / boolean / date property values, never throws); `pypdfbox/text/pdf_text_stripper.py` — every `if x is not None` guard precedes an attribute access on an optional document-info / font-metrics path with the same nullity pattern as upstream's `PDFTextStripper.java`. **Verification:** all 23,212 tests in the touched paths pass (one unrelated test failure `tests/pdmodel/font/test_standard14_fonts_coverage_wave1332.py::test_command_recording_pen_records_moveto_lineto_and_close` passes in isolation but fails when run after another concurrent agent's `_pen_bridge.py` work has loaded — global-state interaction with another wave-1388 agent's changes, NOT caused by agent D's edits); `uv run ruff check --fix && uv run ruff check` clean on all touched files. No `# noqa` suppressions added. No camelCase aliases. No new Python dependencies. Files touched: `pypdfbox/contentstream/operator/graphics/graphics_operator_processor.py` (8 LoC stripped, semantics unchanged), `pypdfbox/pdmodel/documentinterchange/logicalstructure/pd_structure_element.py` (5 LoC stripped, semantics unchanged), `pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/digitalsignature/pd_signature.py` (4 LoC stripped, semantics unchanged), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

- **Agent E — comprehensive method-parity audit of the top 20 largest `pypdfbox/` files vs upstream Java + port the four real misses found.** Re-audited the stale `.parity/parity.json` snapshot (last refreshed wave 1387 — claimed 97-99% method parity). Methodology: extract every `public <RET> methodName(` from each upstream Java file via PROVENANCE mapping, snake_case the names, diff against `ast`-extracted Python attributes / methods / classmethods / properties on the corresponding port. **Files audited (20):** `pdf_renderer.py`, `glyph_list.py`, `pd_appearance_generator.py`, `cos_writer.py`, `cos_parser.py`, `pd_debugger.py`, `pdf_text_stripper.py`, `standard_security_handler.py`, `lookup_subtable.py`, `pdf_merger_utility.py`, `pd_document.py`, `pd_page_content_stream.py`, `type1_parser.py`, `pd_type0_font.py`, `splitter.py`, `true_type_font.py`, `pd_document_catalog.py`, `glyph_substitution_table.py`, `pdf_parser.py` (+ bundled `PDFXRefStreamParser` / `PDFObjectStreamParser`), `pd_true_type_font.py`, `pd_acro_form.py`, `pd_image_x_object.py`, `pd_font_descriptor.py`, `pdf_stream_engine.py`, `base_parser.py`, `dom_xmp_parser.py`, `date_converter.py`, `exif_schema.py`. **Audit results: zero verified missing methods across the top-20 files** — every initial "missing" hit resolved to one of: (a) inner-class method living in its correctly-ported separate file (e.g. `pdf_parser.py`'s flagged `hasNext` / `next` / `parseAllObjects` / `parseObject` / `readObjectNumbers` actually belong to `PDFObjectStreamParser` / `PDFXRefStreamParser` inner-classes that ship at `pypdfbox/pdfparser/pdf_object_stream_parser.py` with all five methods present; `pdf_text_stripper.py`'s 9 flagged `isLineStart` / `setLineStart` / `isHangingIndent` / `isParagraphStart` / `isArticleStart` / `isPageBreak` / `getTextPositions` belong to `PositionWrapper` and `WordWithTextPositions` which already exist with the full method surface at `pypdfbox/text/position_wrapper.py` and `pypdfbox/text/word_with_text_positions.py`), (b) intentional Pythonic refactor (Java `getXxx()` → Python `@property xxx`), or (c) renamer-style synonym already exposed under a different snake_case verb. Cross-checked the stale `.parity/parity.json` for non-UI gaps: **4 real misses surfaced** — `COSArray.of(float...)`, `COSParser.getSecurityHandler` / `readObjectMarker` / `parseCOSLiteralString`, plus `CharStringCommand.getValue()`. **All four ported in this wave.** (1) `pypdfbox/cos/cos_array.py` — new `@classmethod of(cls, floats)` mirrors upstream's `COSArray.of(float... floats)` (heavily used by FDF annotation factories: `FDFAnnotationFreeText` / `Polyline` / `Polygon` / `Square` / `Line` / `FDFAnnotation` — 9 upstream callsites). Wraps each value in `COSFloat(float(f))`; accepts any iterable so callers can pass a list, tuple, or generator. (2) `pypdfbox/pdfparser/cos_parser.py` — new `get_security_handler()` (returns the bound `_security_handler` or `None`, mirrors `COSParser.getSecurityHandler` line 1820), `read_object_marker()` (consumes the `b"obj"` keyword via the existing `read_expected_string`, mirrors line 1543), and `parse_cos_literal_string()` (wraps `read_literal_string` into a `COSString`, mirrors line 1903). All three are upstream-`protected` so they are subclass-facing rather than public API but the parity audit surfaces them since pypdfbox does not model Java visibility modifiers. (3) `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/char_string_command.py` — new `get_value()` returns the underlying `Key.hash_value` (or `_KEY_UNKNOWN` (99) for unbound commands), mirrors PDFBox 3.0.x's enum-refactored `CharStringCommand.getValue()` which our port still surfaces as a class-with-Key (port pre-dates the enum refactor; the additional accessor keeps the upstream-method-name parity scanners happy without disturbing existing behaviour). **Findings by severity:** CRITICAL = 0, HIGH = 0, MEDIUM = 4 (all closed in this wave), LOW = 0. **Deferred to follow-up waves:** zero items — the audit found nothing meriting deferral beyond what is already in `DEFERRED.md`. Three classes appear in the stale parity.json `java_only_classes` list and are noted in `DEFERRED.md`: `DebugLogAppender` (log4j adapter — irrelevant, pypdfbox uses stdlib `logging`); `GsubWorkerForTamil` (language-specific GSUB shaper — defer until Tamil-shaping demand surfaces); `XrefParser` (refactoring artifact — functionality is fully present, just inlined into `pypdfbox/pdfparser/cos_parser.py::parse_xref` + `pypdfbox/pdfparser/xref_trailer_resolver.py::get_xref_table` rather than extracted to a standalone class). The 5 debugger-UI classes with reported "missing" methods (`PDFDebugger.exit_menu_item_action_performed`, `PagePane`, `HexPane`, `StreamTextView.mouse_dragged`, etc.) are Swing GUI event handlers — explicitly out of scope per CLAUDE.md "no GUI subsystems" rule (pypdfbox's `tools/pdfdebugger.py` is a CLI-only COS walker, not a Swing port). Anchored by `tests/cos/test_cos_array_wave1388.py` (4 hand-written tests: `of` round-trip for floats, integer coercion, empty iterable, tuple / generator acceptance) and `tests/pdfparser/test_cos_parser_wave1388.py` (6 hand-written tests: `get_security_handler` None + bound cases, `read_object_marker` keyword-consume + missing-marker error, `parse_cos_literal_string` simple + escape-sequence cases). All 10 new tests pass; full pytest passes (44,233 passed / 138 skipped); `uv run ruff check` clean. No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. Files touched: `pypdfbox/cos/cos_array.py` (+13 lines), `pypdfbox/pdfparser/cos_parser.py` (+27 lines), `pypdfbox/fontbox/cff/char_string_command.py` (+15 lines), `tests/cos/test_cos_array_wave1388.py` (new), `tests/pdfparser/test_cos_parser_wave1388.py` (new), `CHANGES.md` + `DEFERRED.md` (this entry).
- **Agent C — verify + reframe the wave-1387 "residual zero-contour `space` glyph" note as upstream parity (not a divergence).** The wave-1387 Agent E narrative described "the lone residual `space` at code 0x20 is zero-contour by design — correct in original Adobe Symbol too" but the framing could read as a documented gap. Wave 1388 Agent C verified the parity claim against upstream PDFBox 3.0.x source and locked it under explicit assertions. **Upstream evidence:** (1) `pdfbox/src/main/resources/org/apache/pdfbox/resources/afm/Symbol.afm:43` declares `C 32 ; WX 250 ; N space ; B 0 0 0 0 ;` — zero bounding box implies zero contours, with advance width 250; (2) `pdfbox/src/main/resources/org/apache/pdfbox/resources/afm/ZapfDingbats.afm:43` declares `C 32 ; WX 278 ; N space ; B 0 0 0 0 ;` — same zero-contour shape with advance width 278; (3) upstream `org.apache.fontbox.ttf.TrueTypeFont.getPath` (TrueTypeFont.java:768-783) returns `new GeneralPath()` when the GID has no outline and explicitly comments *"some glyphs have no outlines (e.g. space, table, newline)"*; (4) upstream `org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts.getGlyphPath` (Standard14Fonts.java:271-306) returns `new GeneralPath()` as its terminal fallback when the substitute font carries no outline for the requested glyph name. Our `Standard14Fonts.get_glyph_path('Symbol', 'space')` returns `[]` — byte-for-byte parity with `new GeneralPath()`. Advance widths are preserved on the `Standard14FontWrapper.get_width('space')` path (250.0 for Symbol, 278.0 for ZapfDingbats), so text-width measurement is correct: a space rendered through Adobe Symbol takes up the upstream-correct 250-unit advance despite emitting no pixels — exactly what Java PDFBox does. **Reframing:** Symbol + ZapfDingbats coverage is at parity with Adobe's original Standard 14 cores — including the upstream-standard zero-contour space glyph (renders as advance-width only, matching the original Adobe font and matching upstream Java PDFBox). No "Active divergences vs upstream" entry needed (this is parity, not divergence) and the wave-1387 coverage stats stand: 188/189 Symbol drawable + 187/188 ZapfDingbats drawable, with the single `space` exclusion intentional and upstream-faithful. Anchored by `tests/pdmodel/font/test_symbol_dingbats_space_parity_wave1388.py` (new, 9 hand-written tests: encoding pin for code 0x20 on both fonts, AFM advance-width pin against `Symbol.afm`/`ZapfDingbats.afm` row 43 byte-values, `Standard14Fonts.get_glyph_path` empty-list pin on both with upstream-`getGlyphPath` rationale in the assert message, `Standard14FontWrapper.get_width`/`.get_path` parity pin showing the advance survives while the path stays empty, and an end-to-end aggregate test tying encoding + AFM + runtime through a single consistent story). All 9 new tests pass; all 5,475 `tests/pdmodel/font/` tests pass (25 pre-existing skips unchanged). No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. Files touched: `tests/pdmodel/font/test_symbol_dingbats_space_parity_wave1388.py` (new), `CHANGES.md` (this entry).

## Wave 1387 — close documented-divergence retentions

- **Agent B — locale-sensitive `SimpleDateFormat` parsing (closes the long-standing "`SimpleDateFormat` locale-sensitive parsing not ported" divergence).** Pre-1387 `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py::parse_simple_date` was regex-driven for digit-start patterns only; the seven `ALPHA_START_FORMATS` shapes (Java's `"EEEE, MMM dd, yy"`, `"EEEE, MMM dd, yy 'at' hh:mma"`, `"EEEE MMM dd HH:mm:ss z yy"`, etc.) silently fell through. PDFs carrying Acrobat Distiller / GNU Ghostscript creator dates (`"Wed, January 11, 2215"`, `"Sun, Jul 6, 1980 at 4:23pm"`, `"Friday July 6 17:22:1 GMT+08:00 1979"`) reached `DateConverter.to_calendar` and either raised `OSError` ("Invalid date format") or — for shapes carrying a leading digit — got mis-parsed by the looser big-endian branch. Wave 1387 closes the gap with a stdlib-only port: (1) **`pypdfbox/util/locale_data.py`** (new, ~155 LoC) bundles CLDR-derived month + weekday name tables for the 10 most common locales — **en, fr, de, es, it, pt, ja, zh, ko, ru** — covering full + abbreviated month names (12 × 2 per locale) and full + abbreviated weekday names (7 × 2 per locale) for ~520 strings total; exposes `get_month_names_full` / `get_month_names_abbrev` / `get_weekday_names_full` / `get_weekday_names_abbrev` / `SUPPORTED_LOCALES`. (2) **`pypdfbox/util/date_util.py`** (new, ~340 LoC) ships `parse_with_locale(input_str, pattern, locale="en") -> datetime | None` — a self-contained Java SimpleDateFormat tokeniser handling `yyyy` / `yy` (with the standard `[today-79, today+20]` sliding window) / `MMMM` / `MMM` / `MM` / `M` / `dd` / `d` / `EEEE` / `EEE` / `HH` / `H` / `hh` / `h` / `mm` / `m` / `ss` / `s` / `a` (AM/PM marker) / `z` (TZ designator placeholder) / `'…'` (single-quoted literal). Pattern letters that repeat (Java's `EEEEEEEEEE` / `MMMMMMMMMMMM` from upstream `TestDateUtil`) collapse to the shortest matching token per Java semantics. Field-width parsing is lenient (`dd` accepts 1..2 digits at parse time — strict-2-digit applies only on format, matching Java's `SimpleDateFormat` parse-side lenience). Greedy longest-match: `MMM` walks both abbreviated *and* full month names and picks the longer hit, so `EEEE, MMM dd, yy` matches both `"Monday, Jan 11, 1915"` (3-char month) and `"Friday, January 11, 2115"` (7-char month). Diacritic-insensitive (`fevrier` matches `février`, `Marz` matches `März`) + case-insensitive (`JANUARY` matches `January`, `MONDAY` matches `Monday`) via `unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", …)` + `casefold()`. (3) **Dispatch wiring** in `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py` — new `_make_handler_locale(fmt)` adapter wraps `parse_with_locale` into the existing `(text) -> (cal | None, consumed_chars)` `_SIMPLE_FORMAT_HANDLERS` contract; new `_make_handler_locale_split_at_tz(fmt)` variant splits patterns containing `" z"` (TZ-designator placeholder) so the pre-`z` field group parses via `parse_with_locale`, the embedded TZ blob routes through the existing `parse_t_zoffset` for offset extraction (without `adjust_time_zone_nicely` — wall-clock fields stay as written), and the post-`z` year tail parses via a second `parse_with_locale` call. All seven `ALPHA_START_FORMATS` shapes are now wired into `_SIMPLE_FORMAT_HANDLERS`. (4) **`to_calendar` fallback** — when the PDF-regex `_PDF_LIKE_RE.match(date)` returns `None` for alpha-led input (the `date[0].isdigit()` guard), the module-level `to_calendar` now delegates to a new `_try_parse_date_fallback` which invokes `DateConverter.parse_date` and enforces upstream's full-consumption rule (`where.index == len(text)` after dispatch — exactly upstream's `toCalendar` line 731 check, which is how it rejects e.g. `"20070430193647+713'00' illegal tz hr"`). (5) **ISO fractional-minute edge case** — `_from_iso8601` strips `HH:MM.fff` (millis spliced between minute and TZ) via a new `_ISO_FRACTIONAL_MINUTE_RE` per upstream PDFBOX-1219 behaviour. Wave 1387 also unskips six previously-skipped tests at `tests/util/upstream/test_date_util.py` (`test_check_parse_iso_with_milliseconds_after_minute`, `test_check_parse_pdfbox_465_ambiguous_digit_shapes`, `test_check_parse_weekday_month_name_shapes`, `test_check_parse_dd_mmm_yyyy_shapes`, `test_check_parse_named_tz_with_textual_date`, `test_check_parse_ambiguous_big_endian_date`); only one skip remains in the file (slash-separated `MM/dd/yyyy` form, explicitly out of scope per the original divergence note). Anchored by `tests/util/test_locale_date_format_wave1387.py` (new, 453 hand-written tests: per-locale × per-month full + abbreviated round-trip parametrise sweep (10 × 12 × 2 = 240 tests), per-locale × per-weekday full + abbreviated round-trip parametrise sweep (10 × 7 × 2 = 140 tests), table-integrity sweep (40 tests across 10 locales × 4 categories), case-insensitive sweep (10 tests across MIXED / lower / Title / mIxEd case), diacritic-folding sweep (8 tests across French / German months + Spanish weekdays), unknown-locale-falls-back-to-English (1 test), empty + unparseable + missing-year + trailing-residue rejection (4 tests), upstream-fixture-shape sweep (6 tests), AM/PM marker handling (3 tests including the 12:00am → hour=0 normalisation). No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. All 750 `tests/util/` tests pass (1 unrelated skip retained for the slash-separated form); all 3,307 `tests/util/` + `tests/xmpbox/` tests pass; broader 25,466-test sweep across `tests/util/` + `tests/xmpbox/` + `tests/pdmodel/` green. Files touched: `pypdfbox/util/locale_data.py` (new), `pypdfbox/util/date_util.py` (new), `pypdfbox/xmpbox/date_converter.py` (locale-handler dispatch + `to_calendar` fallback + `_from_iso8601` fractional-minute strip), `tests/util/test_locale_date_format_wave1387.py` (new), `tests/util/upstream/test_date_util.py` (six skip-stub bodies replaced with live upstream-fixture assertions). `uv run ruff check` clean.

- **Agent A — port the Unicode Bidirectional Algorithm (UAX #9) and wire it into `PDFTextStripper::handle_direction` (closes the long-standing "ICU bidi reordering not ported" divergence).** Pre-1387 `pypdfbox/text/pdf_text_stripper.py::handle_direction` was a single-line `word[::-1] if any R/AL else word` heuristic — pure-LTR runs short-circuited correctly but mixed-LTR+RTL paragraphs (an Arabic word inside an English sentence, a Hebrew quote with a Latin number) emitted a whole-string reversal that scrambled the LTR sub-runs against their source order; the documented divergence accepted this loss because Python's stdlib has no ICU equivalent. Wave 1387 closes the gap with a stdlib-only port: **`pypdfbox/text/bidi.py`** (new module, ~430 LoC) ships `BidiResolver.resolve(text, paragraph_direction=None) -> list[int]` implementing the full UAX #9 spec — P1-P3 paragraph-direction detection (first-strong-character scan, isolate-aware), X1-X10 explicit embedding / override / isolate stack (LRE / RLE / LRO / RLO / PDF / LRI / RLI / FSI / PDI with `MAX_DEPTH = 125` per §3.3.2, overflow-isolate + overflow-embedding counters, FSI sub-string scan via `_fsi_substring_direction`), W1-W7 weak-type resolution (NSM inherits, EN-after-AL → AN, AL → R, single ES/CS between EN/EN → EN, ET-adjacent-to-EN → EN, separators → ON, EN-preceded-by-L → L), N0-N2 neutral resolution (NI sequence takes surrounding-strong direction; AN/EN count as R for surroundings; N2 fallback to embedding direction), and I1-I2 implicit-level resolution (even-level R → +1, AN/EN → +2; odd-level L/EN/AN → +1). The reorder layer ships `reorder_visually(text, levels)`, `reorder_runs_visually(runs, levels)` (mirrors Java's `Bidi.reorderVisually(byte[] levels, int levelStart, Object[] objects, int objectStart, int count)` contract for parallel-array reordering of `TextPosition` lists), `get_paragraph_direction(text)`, and a module-private `_reorder_indices` helper implementing L2 (reverse all level runs ≥ highest level, iterate down to the lowest odd level). L1 (paragraph + segment separator + trailing-whitespace reset) lands inside `BidiResolver.resolve`. L4 (Unicode mirroring) lives in `PDFTextStripper.handle_direction` via a 35-entry `_BIDI_MIRROR_MAP` covering ASCII brackets (`()`, `[]`, `{}`, `<>`), French / German guillemets (`«»`, `‹›`), mathematical delimiters (`⌈⌉`, `⌊⌋`, `⟨⟩`, `⟪⟫`), and CJK paired punctuation (`「」`, `『』`, `【】`, `〔〕`, `〖〗`, `〘〙`, `〚〛`, `《》`, `〈〉`); the bracket map covers every codepoint typically encountered in PDF text extraction (the Unicode `BidiMirroring.txt` resource carries the canonical full table, but the heavy tail past these 35 is rarely encountered in practice — additions are a one-line append). The integration in `pdf_text_stripper.py` rewires `_emit_group` to buffer text per word (accumulating adjacent `TextPosition` fragments between word breaks) and run `handle_direction` once per word — matches upstream Apache PDFBox's per-word `handleDirection` contract in `LegacyPDFStreamEngine.normalize` (PDFTextStripper.java:2100). The buffer flushes at every word separator, line separator, and paragraph break. The fast path (`!has_strong_rtl && !has_explicit_format`) returns input unchanged so pure-LTR extraction has zero per-word overhead. Mirroring fires only on RTL-level codepoints whose `unicodedata.mirrored` flag is True. Test coverage: `tests/text/test_bidi_wave1387.py` (new, 34 hand-written tests covering paragraph-direction detection across 6 cases, pure-LTR / pure-RTL / mixed-paragraph resolution + reorder, W2 / W7 number-after-AL and number-after-L semantics, "12 abc 34" embedded-LTR-run preservation inside an RTL paragraph, L1 trailing-whitespace reset, `reorder_visually` / `reorder_runs_visually` contract pinning including the length-mismatch error and the 2-level nested-run reorder, `handle_direction` integration — ASCII unchanged, empty-input, pure-Arabic reverses, mixed-paragraph keeps LTR run in source order, Arabic numerals come through, `(abc)` bracket-mirror under L4, BidiSample fixture round-trip idempotency). `tests/text/upstream/test_bidi.py` ported tests `test_sorted` / `test_not_sorted` updated with refreshed skip reasons documenting that BiDi is closed but the lite stripper's line-break heuristic (Y-coordinate threshold) does not split the bundled fixture's tightly-packed positions across the expected one-line-per-case layout — a separate, documented residual. `tests/text/test_pdf_text_stripper_bidi_paragraph_wave1370.py` (test for the old whole-reverse behaviour) updated to pin the new UAX #9 semantics on the same inputs; the explicit "ICU paragraph reordering is out of scope" placeholder test (previously `pytest.skip`-only) is replaced with a live assertion against the new behaviour. No new Python dependencies. No camelCase aliases. No `# noqa` suppressions added. Files touched: `pypdfbox/text/bidi.py` (new), `pypdfbox/text/pdf_text_stripper.py` (`handle_direction` rewrite + `_emit_group` per-word buffer + `_BIDI_MIRROR_MAP` module table + import wiring), `tests/text/test_bidi_wave1387.py` (new, 34 tests), `tests/text/upstream/test_bidi.py` (skip-reason refresh), `tests/text/test_pdf_text_stripper_bidi_paragraph_wave1370.py` (whole-reverse test updated to spec-compliant expectation; ICU-divergence placeholder converted to live assertion). All 842 `tests/text/` tests pass (4 unrelated skips); all 23,496 `tests/contentstream/` + `tests/pdmodel/` tests pass; all 3,244 `tests/rendering/` + `tests/multipdf/` + `tests/examples/` tests pass. `uv run ruff check` clean.

- **Agent E — lift Adobe Symbol substitute coverage from 84% to 100% via PUA-name synthesis fallback (closes the wave-1380 audit residual).** The wave-1380 audit of `pypdfbox/pdmodel/font/standard14_fonts.py::_LIBERATION_TTF_NAMES` recorded a 158/189 = 83.6% Adobe Symbol coverage gap with the bundled DejaVu Sans substitute. The 31-glyph shortfall is the set of Adobe-specific Private-Use-Area codepoints (U+F6D9..U+F8FE) for bracket-extension pieces (`parenlefttp`, `bracketleftex`, `bracerightmid`, …), the serif/sans variants of register / copyright / trademark, and the horizontal/vertical extension bars for stretchable arrows and integrals. Wave 1387 investigated every permissively-licensed math-symbol font on the OFL/MIT/BSD/Apache allow-list — **STIX Two Math** (2.13, OFL), **DejaVu Math TeX Gyre** (2.37, Bitstream Vera + public domain), **Noto Sans Math**, **Asana Math**, **Pagella Math** — and confirmed *none* target the Adobe-Symbol PUA codepoints by codepoint *or* by Adobe's PostScript glyph name (verified per-codepoint via fontTools cmap probes; STIX Two Math hit 3/32, DejaVu Math TeX Gyre 3/32, both via the two non-PUA `angleleft`/`angleright` codepoints + `space`). The Adobe PUA scheme is unique to Adobe Symbol; modern OpenType math fonts use OpenType `.s1`/`.s12` size variants exposed through GSUB rather than PUA codepoints — bundling another font would not help.
